FIRST TIMOTHY

INTRODUCTION

[Timothy is the well-known young man of Acts 16:1--2, whose mother ]{.class_3527}["was a Jewess"]{.class_3528}[ ]{.class_3526}[and whose father ]{.class_3527}["was a Greek."]{.class_3528}[ He was circumcised for the sake of ]{.class_3527}[testimony ]{.class_3526}[(1 Cor. 9:20--22), not salvation (Gal. 6:15), as Paul's position on "circumcision" was well-known (Gal. 5:3--5). His name is mentioned about twenty-four times in the Scriptures, the last time being in Hebrews 13:23. He followed Paul around as a sort of ]{.class_3527}["son" ]{.class_3528}[(1 Tim. 1:2), since he was one of Paul's converts. From this came the peculiar, pagan, Roman Catholic blasphemy that it was all right to call a Catholic priest "father" since Paul claimed to have "begotten" his converts as a "father" (see 1 Cor. 4:15 for confirmation).]{.class_3527}

[The Catholic blasphemy was constructed on the additional heresy that the "Church" was the MOTHER of all Christians (]{.class_3527}[notwithstanding the direct statement by the Holy Spirit in Gal. 4:26 that she was NOT]{.class_3526}[), and that, therefore, the Baalite priest who SPRINKLED THE BABY INTO ITS "MOTHER" (or out of its mother or whatever) was entitled to be called "Father" (]{.class_3527}[notwithstanding the direct denial of this teaching by the Holy Spirit in Matt. 23:9]{.class_3526}[). The relationship in the Bible is that of a soulwinner who leads a sinner to Christ, thus "begetting him" (see 1 Cor. 4:15---a passage that no "TULIP" Calvinist would even list in a debate!) and causing him to enter a family where the ]{.class_3527}[soulwinner ]{.class_3526}[is his spiritual "father." Of course, his real "Father" was GOD (1 Cor. 8:6).]{.class_3527}

[Philemon 10 gives us the exact situation. Paul did not sprinkle Onesimus, and Paul would as soon have said "HEIL HITLER" as "Catholic." ]{.class_3527}[No "early Christian" in the New Testament mentions such a silly word.]{.class_3526}

[Timothy's mother was a Jewess named ]{.class_3527}[Eunice]{.class_3526}[, and he is called a ]{.class_3527}["disciple"]{.class_3528}[ in Acts 16:1, so he was led to Christ before this time, probably during Paul's stay in Lystra in Acts 14:6. Timothy's mother and grandmother (]{.class_3527}[Lois]{.class_3526}[) took the pains to get him into the "Scripture" at this time (2 Tim. 3:15), and since ]{.class_3527}[neither of them had a copy of the "plenary, verbally inspired, original autographs" ]{.class_3526}[of ONE SINGLE BOOK they taught him (Genesis to Malachi), they gave him the next best thing: SCRIPTURES THAT WERE ]{.class_3527}["GIVEN BY INSPIRATION OF GOD"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Tim. 3:16). At least, that is the exact New Testament doctrinal truth ]{.class_3527}[as recorded by the Holy Spirit and preserved in every set of manuscripts that has the passage;]{.class_3526}[ notwithstanding the objections of 10,000 Greek professors and Bible teachers in the Alexandrian Cult!]{.class_3527}

[Timothy accompanied Paul in his tour through Macedonia, but when the apostle went forward to Athens, Timothy and Silas remained in Berea (Acts 17:14--16). We find his name joined with Paul's in the addresses of both the epistles to the Thessalonians, as he was sent back there by Paul to visit them. Timothy ministered to Paul during his lengthy stay at Ephesus (Acts 19), and from there Paul sent him into Macedonia and to Corinth (1 Cor. 4:17, 16:10). On Paul's return to Asia, through Macedonia, he went forward and waited for him at Troas (Acts 20:3--5). He was with Paul while Paul was in prison at Rome (Col. 1:1; Phil. 1:1), and he himself was imprisoned and set free about the same time, according to Hebrews 13:23. In the "Pastoral Epistles," he is evidently at Ephesus as a PASTOR. The last notice we have of him, chronologically, is when Paul requests for him to ]{.class_3527}["come before]{.class_3528}[ ]{.class_3527}[winter" ]{.class_3528}[(2 Tim. 4:21).]{.class_3527}

[We are not going to burn the reader's eyes out with the mass of critical material that has been assembled by Vincent (]{.class_3527}[Word Studies in the New Testament]{.class_3526}[), Holtzman, Von Soden, Samuel Davidson, Wiezsacker, and Edwin Hatch against the "Pauline authorship" of the two epistles. We assume from the start that scholars like these don't have the spiritual faculties of a blind mosquito, and when it comes to finding Biblical truth, they couldn't find a bowling ball in a bathtub. You must understand the frame of mind of the Alexandrian discipline. There is an "Alexandrian mentality" that always goes way past the "lunatic fringe." It is lunacy itself---the very personification of BLIND IGNORANCE. We illustrate. Here are the three "reasons" for saying that Paul could not have written two epistles that bear his name, teaching, warnings, advice, counsel, blessings, admonitions, and instructions:

[I. Since "Gnosticism" was not a threat to Christian theology until AFTER A.D. 100, Paul could not have been warning about it ]{.class_3527}[before it showed up ]{.class_3526}[(1 Tim. 6:20 and 2 Tim. 2:16).]{.class_3527}

World War I and World War II are warned about 1900 years before they show up (Matt. 24:6--7).

The United Nations is warned about before the League of Nations is formed (Zeph. 3:8).

White, Eerdmans, 1961, p. 67).]{.class_3527}

You cannot have "elders," "deacons," and "bishops" before Paul dies. "In view of these FACTS \[don't you know, Vince baby!\] it seems unlikely that these Epistles are the work of Paul" (Vincent, p. 197).

[a. The letter is a ]{.class_3527}[pastoral ]{.class_3526}[epistle to a ]{.class_3527}[pastor.]{.class_3526}

[b. Why wouldn't it contain detailed instructions on the ]{.class_3527}[local church]{.class_3526}[ found nowhere else?]{.class_3527}

[d. If Paul ordained elders wherever he went (Acts 14:23) and called them together to give the instructions for the bishops (which he ]{.class_3527}[did:]{.class_3526}[ see Acts 20:17) more than two years before he went to prison the first time (see Acts 20--27), what is all this deal about advanced "second-century church polity"? Someone has "flipped their lid," and we don't have to flip a coin to see who it is.]{.class_3527}

No, we will never (no, never!) be sidetracked from the truth by a pile of lazy, irreverent, theoretical guesswork, proposed by men who have not learned yet how to THINK or how to READ. Let them retire to the "rubber room."

[As for Paul ]{.class_3527}[not having enough time ]{.class_3526}[to write these epistles before he had his head cut off, we simply notice that he spoke of being RELEASED from the imprisonment of Acts 28:30 in Philippians 1:24--26, and when he spoke of coming to see Timothy, he wrote with uncertainty of how long he might be detained from coming to him (1 Tim. 3:14--15).]{.class_3527}

[The fact that he said]{.class_3527}[ "Erastus abode at Corinth"]{.class_3528}[ in 2 Timothy 4:20 shows that he was writing ]{.class_3527}[after ]{.class_3526}[his first imprisonment. Paul had not been in Corinth for several years before his first imprisonment, and in the interval, Timothy had been with him so that he did not need to write subsequently about that visit. He must, therefore, have been ]{.class_3527}[liberated ]{.class_3526}[from the first imprisonment (in Heb. 13:23--24, he claimed to be at liberty in ITALY) and have resumed his apostolic journeys. He was imprisoned again ]{.class_3527}[later]{.class_3526}[, and when he wrote 2 Timothy, ]{.class_3527}[he did not say one word about a future release or the possibility of seeing anyone again.]{.class_3526}[ Compare this with his "open arrest" in Acts 28:30, and it is perfectly apparent to anyone that the two imprisonments are NOT the same; that is apparent to anyone but Holtzman, Salmon, Wiezsacker, et al.]{.class_3527}

[The author is the apostle Paul, and all of the blather about why he would write to an intimate friend like Timothy in the formal style of 2 Timothy 1:11 and 1 Timothy 1:15--16 is just so much Nicaean baloney out of the deep freeze (see ]{.class_3527}[The History of the New Testament Church, ]{.class_3526}[Vol. I, chap. 8). Paul is giving instructions to a young preacher "for life." He is giving many of them on the eve of his own death. In such a situation, it is absolutely necessary to be brief, pointed, exact, sober, specific, and imperative. That is how the two epistles are written. They are instructions for a young minister, and their lack of "theological content" (see the mangled mess of tripe and chittlins given us by Vincent on p. 193 concerning the "central truths" of justification and the "vital union of the believer with Christ" and "the central position of faith," etc.) is due to the fact that the theological revelations God gave to Paul ]{.class_3527}[had already been given and were already being circulated all over the Roman Empire, being copied by hand.]{.class_3526}[ The epistles to Timothy are composed of MINISTERIAL CHARGES given to a young man concerning the MINISTRY.]{.class_3527}

It is true that Vincent, Holtzman, Pfleiderer, Bauer, Goodspeed, Schleirmacher, Sabatier, Julicher, Hatch, McGiffert, Hesse, and Davidson could not grasp this simple fifth-grade concept, but since not one man in the bunch was ever called by God to do anything but demonstrate his ignorance, we wouldn't expect them to "grasp the concept." Any Bible-believing pastor in North Carolina with a high school education would have no problem with such a matter.

[As to the ]{.class_3527}[canonicity ]{.class_3526}[of the pastoral epistles, the only real evidence against them would be "the oldest and best manuscripts" (dead orthodox cliché, used by all members of the Alexandrian Cult on all occassions). They do not have them: they are missing from ]{.class_3527}[Papyrus 46 ]{.class_3526}[(Chester Beatty). We assume Jehudi was head of the Bible Department when P]{.class_3527}[46 ]{.class_3538}[was written.]{.class_3527}

[Clement of Rome]{.class_3526}[ (A.D. 95) quotes 1 Timothy 6:1; 1 Timothy 5:17; 1 Timothy 2:9; Titus 2:4; 1 Timothy 6:12; et al.]{.class_3527}

[Ignatius]{.class_3526}[ (A.D. 110) cites Titus 1:14 and 3:9; 1 Timothy 1:1; 2 Timothy 2:25 and 4:5; Titus 3:1; et al.]{.class_3527}

[Polycarp ]{.class_3526}[(A.D. 110) cites 1 Timothy 6:7 and 10; 2 Timothy 2:12 and 4:10; 1 Timothy 1:1 and 2:2; et al.]{.class_3527}

[The Epistle to Barnabas]{.class_3526}[ (A.D. 120) contains citations from 2 Timothy 4:1; 1 Timothy 3:14, 16; and ]{.class_3527}[The Epistle to Diognetus ]{.class_3526}[(A.D. 150) contains references to 1 Timothy 3:16; Titus 3:4; and 1 Timothy 3:16 again.]{.class_3527}

You are to believe, after that, that some "ghost writer" wrote the epistle between A.D. 110 and 120.

How did "Clement" get a hold of the copy five years before it was written?

The same way "Fatty Fatima" prophesied the Vatican would go Marxist if Russia ever got the upper hand over the United States and did this before World War II, when the Vatican was 100 percent Fascist.

[Having now cleared the ground of the underbrush, we look at the Scriptural text, ]{.class_3527}["GIVEN BY INSPIRATION OF GOD,"]{.class_3528}[ according to the writer who wrote the epistles (2 Tim. 3:16).]{.class_3527}

Here is the kiss of God on the soul of lost mankind! Here is the Monarch of the Books! Let him roar, and let the shepherds tremble (Isa. 31:4)! Here is the Book of Books towering above the loosely scattered (and lost) collection of "original autographs" like Mount Everest above Mount Blanc. Ο βιβλιος. The Book"!

Proverbs 22:17 Bow down thine ear, and hear the words of the wise, and apply thine heart unto my knowledge.

18 For it is a pleasant thing if thou keep them within thee; they shall withal be fitted in thy lips.

19 That thy trust may be in the LORD, I have made known to thee this day, even to thee.

20 Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,

21 That I might make thee know the certainty of the words of truth; that thou mightest answer the words of truth to them that send unto thee?

[Our ears are "bowed" (v. 17). Our hearts are ready to ]{.class_3527}["apply"]{.class_3528}[ (v. 17). We desire these words to be on our lips (v.18). We acknowledge that they are ]{.class_3527}["excellent things in counsels and knowledge"]{.class_3528}[ (v. 20), and if not one faculty member in one Christian college, seminary, or university on the face of this earth is "certain" about them (vs. 21), WE ARE. We are ready to ]{.class_3527}["ANSWER THE WORDS OF TRUTH" ]{.class_3528}[to the reader. There will be no ]{.class_3527}["uncertain sound"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Cor. 14:8), no ]{.class_3527}["strange children"]{.class_3528}[ (Hos. 5:7), no ]{.class_3527}["good words and fair speeches"]{.class_3528}[ to]{.class_3527}[ "deceive the hearts of the simple"]{.class_3528}[ (Rom. 16:18). There will only be plain, clear, pure, holy TRUTH from the words of truth (John 17:17), given by the One who is THE ]{.class_3527}["TRUTH"]{.class_3528}[ (John 14:6) incarnate.]{.class_3527}

"[SPEAK, LORD; FOR THY SERVANT HEARETH"]{.class_3528} (1 Sam. 3:9)!

CHAPTER ONE

This Epistle has six chapters, 113 verses, and 2,269 words. It was written somewhere between A.D. 62 and A.D. 65. The writer is obviously Paul (see the introduction to this volume for a discussion of these matters), and he is writing to a young man whom he led to the Lord and who was called into the "fulltime ministry," as a pastor, after having ministered to Paul for nearly ten years.

1:1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the commandment of God our Saviour, and Lord Jesus Christ, which is our hope;

2 Unto Timothy, my own son in the faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.

3 As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus, when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine,

4 Neither give heed to fables and endless genealogies, which minister questions, rather than godly edifying which is in the faith: so do.

[Before beginning an exposition of the text, notice that the book of Timothy is written in the form of a "ministerial charge," and there are ]{.class_3527}[twelve "charges" ]{.class_3526}[that are given to Timothy by the Apostle. A "charge," in this case, is a commandment to take care of something (i.e., to put someone "in charge," as we would use the term). The "charges" are twelve in number and are found in the following verses:]{.class_3527}

[1. 1 Timothy 1:3--20. This is a charge to remember the ]{.class_3527}[purpose of the law ]{.class_3526}[and those who pervert it. It is also a charge to remember the ]{.class_3527}[true nature of the gospel ]{.class_3526}[and the need for a MILITARY approach to maintaining its purity.]{.class_3527}

[2. 1 Timothy 2:1--8. This is a charge to continue in ]{.class_3527}[prayer ]{.class_3526}[and teach the church to pray.]{.class_3527}

[3. 1 Timothy 2:9--15. This is a charge to show the women in the church their proper place ]{.class_3527}[in the family ]{.class_3526}[and as a Christian witness.]{.class_3527}

[4. 1 Timothy 3:1--7. This is a charge to notify the prospective bishops (ordained elders) of their need to ]{.class_3527}[qualify ]{.class_3526}[for the office.]{.class_3527}

[5. 1 Timothy 3:8--13. This charge is the same type of thing applied to deacons (and "DEACONESSES," according to Dummelow, p. 998; the ]{.class_3527}[Amplified Version]{.class_3526}[ in Rom. 16:1; and the faculty members at Liberty University in Lynchburg: see comments by C. Sumner Wemp in ]{.class_3527}[The Liberty Bible Commentary,]{.class_3526}[ Vol. II, p. 634---heresy and apostasy are never the exclusive properties of unsaved liberals and modernists!).]{.class_3527}

[6. 1 Timothy 3:14--16. This charge is to be "aware" of the true nature of the ]{.class_3527}[body of Christ ]{.class_3526}[and of the salient truths for which it is to stand in the]{.class_3527}[ local churches.]{.class_3526}

[7. 1 Timothy 4:1--11. This charge is to beware of ]{.class_3527}[Roman Catholic teaching ]{.class_3526}[already creeping into the local assemblies in the first century and to concentrate on godliness.]{.class_3527}

[8. 1 Timothy 4:12--16. A charge to attend to ]{.class_3527}[personal holiness ]{.class_3526}[and intellectual and spiritual development in order to prosper as a minister.]{.class_3527}

9. 1 Timothy 5:1--16. A charge to treat older men and women properly; in particular, elderly widows with no support.

[10. 1 Timothy 5:17--25. A charge on how to deal with ]{.class_3527}[elders ]{.class_3526}[and a personal note on Timothy's own health.]{.class_3527}

[11. 1 Timothy 6:1--5. A charge showing the correct relationship between ]{.class_3527}[servants ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[masters]{.class_3526}[.]{.class_3527}

[12. 1 Timothy 6:6--21. A charge on dealing with ]{.class_3527}[rich people]{.class_3526}[ and money matters as they relate to eternal life and rewards at the judgment seat of Christ.]{.class_3527}

"[AN APOSTLE OF JESUS CHRIST BY THE COMMANDMENT OF GOD"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). The corrupt Alexandrian texts have "Christ Jesus" for ["Jesus Christ"]{.class_3529} and erase the ["Lord"]{.class_3529} from the second clause (["our Saviour, and LORD Jesus Christ"]{.class_3529}). Nestle's critical Greek text refuses to note the exchange and will not even admit the possibility for the [King James]{.class_3530} reading, although it is found in the Greek Receptus manuscripts. [The United Bible Societyies' Version]{.class_3530} (Aland and Metzger) is just as deceptive and unscholarly: it makes both changes in the text WITHOUT CITING THE MANUSCRIPT EVIDENCE FOR OR AGAINST THE CHANGE. (These Greek texts are the sources for the [ASV, NASV, NIV, RSV, NRSV, TEV, NWT,]{.class_3530} etc., so we can expect their "transcriptional errors" to be preserved; which they are.)

Paul's apostleship was brought about by three things:

[1. ]{.class_3527}["The will of God"]{.class_3528}[ (see 2 Tim. 1:1).]{.class_3527}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["The faith of God's elect"]{.class_3528}[ (Titus 1:1).]{.class_3527}

[3. The ]{.class_3527}[calling of God]{.class_3526}[ (Rom. 1:1).]{.class_3527}

[Here, however, he adds something: ]{.class_3527}[his apostleship was COMMANDED.]{.class_3526}[ The reason for inserting this new note is that he is about to give out some orders himself. The idea is that since he was ]{.class_3527}["not disobedient unto the heavenly vision"]{.class_3528}[ (Acts 26:19), then Timothy had better "follow suit."]{.class_3527}

"[Which is our hope"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1) is the ["hope"]{.class_3529} of Romans 8:24 and 1 John 3:1--3, which the reader will notice is never a "hope" like "perhaps we will be saved," as the Campbellites and Catholics teach it. In every case it is the ["hope"]{.class_3529} of Hebrews 6:19: a Person who has gone ahead and promised to return. This is the ["blessed hope"]{.class_3529} of Titus 2:13.

"[My own son in the faith"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). (See the Introduction for further comments.) The souls you lead to Christ are your spiritual children; the souls they lead to Christ are your spiritual [grandchildren, "und so weiter"]{.class_3530} (etc.). I have great, great grandchildren in the ministry winning souls to Christ; these souls will be MY great, great, great grandchildren. Hugh Pyle led me to Christ, I led Cecil Ford to Christ, Cecil Ford led Barney Iha to Christ, and Barney Iha drove buses for Hyles Anderson nearly ten years, winning souls to Christ. If those souls (they are grown now, being between twenty and thirty years of age) lead anyone to Christ, they will be Hugh Pyle's great, great, great grandchildren.

[Are YOU a soul winner? Do you produce "after YOUR kind" (Gen. 1:12, 21)? No child born into a Catholic home has a spiritual father, ]{.class_3527}[for no man leads him to Christ]{.class_3526}[. He is ]{.class_3527}[sprinkled ]{.class_3526}[(or has water poured on him) by a priest who never leads him to Christ. To call such a man "father" is a violation of the direct commandment of Jesus Christ (see Matt. 23:9).]{.class_3527}

[The critical texts have all reversed the third ]{.class_3527}["Jesus Christ" ]{.class_3528}[(vs. 2) to read "Christ Jesus." We call this corruption of the text "harmonizing tendencies" (see Burgon's work on ]{.class_3527}[The Causes of Corruption in the Traditional Text]{.class_3526}[), and it comes from the fact that the sloppy, clumsy, careless scribes of the ]{.class_3527}[ASV, NASV, NIV,]{.class_3526}[ and ]{.class_3527}[RSV ]{.class_3526}[took the sloppy, clumsy, careless work of the Alexandrian scribe to be right because the term "Christ Jesus" appears in chapter one at verses 12, 14, and 15. "Reason," therefore, would dictate (see ]{.class_3527}[The Christian's Handbook of Science and Philosophy,]{.class_3526}[ p. 132), that Paul could not have written ]{.class_3527}["Jesus Christ"]{.class_3528}[ in verses 1 and 2. Since we do not believe in demented reasoning like this or lunatic fringe nuts who think like this, we accept the ]{.class_3527}[King James]{.class_3526}[ text and throw Nestle, Aland, Metzger, and their Greek texts into the dumpster.]{.class_3527}

"[That they teach NO OTHER DOCTRINE" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 3). No other doctrine than that given in Titus 1:9---["SOUND DOCTRINE."]{.class_3529} (Notice the emphasis on DOCTRINE in the Pastoral Epistles: 1 Tim. 1:10, 4:1, 6, 13, 16, 5:17, 6:1, 3; and 2 Tim. 3:10, 16, 4:3, plus Titus 1:9; 2:1, 7, 10). Since the first purpose for the writing of the Scriptures (2 Tim. 3:16) was NOT to give "a history of redemption" and NOT to show "man's search for God" and NOT to "reveal God's love to man" and NOT to give the "basic concepts and principles of redemption" nor to bring "peace of earth," we cannot expect any Charismatic or Catholic in this age to be "Gung Ho" (World War II expression) about STUDYING [BIBLE DOCTRINES]{.class_3530}. [Doctrine ]{.class_3530}is the [FIRST thing]{.class_3530} God had in mind when He told anyone to pen anything later called ["scripture" ]{.class_3529}(2 Tim. 3:16).

[Specifically, Paul is talking about the doctrines that deal with the purpose of the law as contrasted with the RESULTS of GRACE. Note verses 7--10 show what happens when the law is used as a means of salvation: it does not produce the RESULTS (see vs. 5) that the Grace of God does (see vss. 12--16). A servant, serving because of fear, cannot produce the required ]{.class_3527}["fruit of the Spirit"]{.class_3528}[ (see Gal. 5:22 and comments in that commentary) that can be produced by a SON who obeys through love (see Gen. 22:2--8 and comments in that commentary).]{.class_3527}

"[Neither give heed to FABLES and endless genealogies, which minister questions" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 4). The ["fables"]{.class_3529} are like the[ "old wives' fables"]{.class_3529} found in 4:7 and 2 Timothy 4:4. They are called ["Jewish fables" ]{.class_3529}in Titus 1:14, since such Roman Catholic doctrines as "PURGATORY" and "SALVATION BY WORKS" were taught by some unsaved Jews (see the writings in the [Talmud, Mishna, Gemara, Kaballa]{.class_3530}, and the works of Edersheim). "Holy water," legitimate in Leviticus 14, is a FABLE after the resurrection. So are "Holy priests" and a "Holy priesthood" outside of all believers (1 Pet. 2:4--8), "Holy Grails," "Holy Mother Church," "Holy Mary," and "Holy Cats." These constitute FABLES, although they were truths in the Old Testament Jewish Theocracy. (See ["holy ark," "holy vessels," "holy garments," "holy place," "holy men," "holy water,"]{.class_3529} etc., in Num. 5:17; Exod. 28:2; Lev. 23:2; 2 Chron. 5:5; 2 Chron. 35:3.) This bag and baggage is trustled OVER THE BLOOD of Christ at Calvary by the Catholic priests and loaded down on the backs of the New Testament Christians who were [relieved ]{.class_3530}of this load (Col. 2:16, 18--19) at Calvary (Rom. 6:6).

[This results in an aura of magical mysticism which pervades the mysterious operations of the Vatican hierarchy as it sallies forth to conquer the world in the name of "redeemed humanity," "oppressed minorities," "the evils of apartheid," and similar socialistic booby traps for boobies. In the New Testament, there are only three "HOLY THINGS." One is the ]{.class_3527}[Lord Jesus Christ Himself, ]{.class_3526}[who is not now a "thing" (wafer), but a PERSON; the second is the ]{.class_3527}[Holy Ghost]{.class_3526}[ in the body of the believer; and the third is ]{.class_3527}["THE HOLY SCRIPTURES"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Tim. 3:15). There is no holy water, holy candles, holy rosary, holy father, holy mother, holy church, or "holy hollyhocks." These are FABLES.]{.class_3527}

[The fables that ]{.class_3527}["minister questions"]{.class_3528}[ are the fables of TULIP (John Calvin's system of absolute predestination); the fables of the "Dry Cleaners" (Stam, Baker, O'Hare, Bullinger, etc., who taught water baptism was not for "this age"); the fables of the faculty members of Bob Jones University, Tennessee Temple University, Dallas Theological Seminary, etc. (that the "plenary, verbally inspired, original autographs are the final authority" for a twentieth-century Christian); and the fables of Greek scholarship, which were invented from sheer fantasy by Westcott, Lightfoot, Ellicot, Hort, Nestle, Aland, Metzger, Robertson, Wuest, Semler, Kenyon, and others (see 2 Tim. 2:14).]{.class_3527}

"[Endless genealogies"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4). There are four practical applications of the warning. In Timothy's day, one obvious one was the Rabbis' endless discussions of their own people and ancestries; in particular, all of the brilliant sayings of other Rabbis. These are called the ["commandments of men"]{.class_3529} in Titus 1:14.

[Another application would be to the genealogies of the "gods" taught by gnostics and by Greek philosophers.]{.class_3527}[ Bullfinch's Mythology]{.class_3526}[ will give the reader some idea of what is involved in the family of the "gods," and any standard encyclopedia on witchcraft and demonology will give the reader some idea of what is involved in the gnostic's teaching about the "gods." (For further details on gnosticism, see ]{.class_3527}[The History of the New Testament Church,]{.class_3526}[ Vol. I, 1983, pp. 81--94.)]{.class_3527}

[The idea is well expressed in the ]{.class_3527}[International Standard Bible Encyclopedia]{.class_3526}[ (p. 1244), which says, "The fulness of deity could flow out in no other way than in emanations or aeons or angels, all of which are necessarily imperfect, the highest of these emanations or aeons or angels, being more spiritual than the grade immediately below it. Of these aeons, there is a gradation so numerous that at length the lowest of them is almost wholly corporeal\...in this way the gulf is bridged between God and the world of mankind." Now, names were given to these "aeons" or angels, and you will find to this day that ]{.class_3527}["Raphael"]{.class_3526}[ (and some others) remains in Catholic literature, although you couldn't find "Raphael" in the Bible with a laser beam and a microscope. In a real encyclopedia of witchcraft and occult knowledge, you will find literally scores of names applied to "higher beings" (]{.class_3527}["principalities and powers"]{.class_3528}[ in Eph. 3:10; 6:12) arranged in levels or planes of a hierarchy.]{.class_3527}

[This brings us to two excellent, modern applications: the omnibus of names of "unbaptized sinners" in the vaults of the Salt Lake City Tabernacle (]{.class_3527}[Mormons]{.class_3526}[) and the ]{.class_3527}[list of "popes"]{.class_3526}[ published regularly in various periodicals. Neither list will do anything but cause one to strive without profit to the ]{.class_3527}["subverting of the hearers"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Tim. 2:14). The list of popes for 200 years (A.D. 70 to 270) is pure fiction in a class with ]{.class_3527}[Grimm's Fairy Tales]{.class_3526}[ and ]{.class_3527}[Alice in Wonderland,]{.class_3526}[ and the list of unbaptized dead in Salt Lake City is no more going to affect their present condition (in heaven or hell) than holding a "mass" for them.]{.class_3527}

"[Godly edifying which is in faith"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4). Godly edifying is produced by FAITH, not by a study of genealogies or fables. Obviously this is the faith in what God SAID (compare Rom. 10:17 with Gen. 3:1), and since it is, we can expect a monumental defection from the [Authorized ]{.class_3530}text at this point by the Alexandrian Cult; we will not be disappointed in our expectation. This is the crew that fell overboard at Colossians 2:8 (see comments on that verse in that commentary) and had such a terrible time with Ephesians 5:13 and Ephesians 6:17 (see comments in that commentary). Wherever [belief in the words of God ]{.class_3530}show up (Gen. 3:1), there is a mad scurrying and scampering for the nearest fire escape.

[Upon seeing ]{.class_3527}["GODLY EDIFYING WHICH IS IN FAITH,"]{.class_3528}[ the false pens of the false scribes at the "World's Most Unusual University" go to work and produce "THE DISPENSATION OF GOD," giving us the monstrous sentence: "the dispensation of God which is in the faith," which means nothing. Sensing that it means nothing, yet unwilling to believe what God said (]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[1611), the ]{.class_3527}[New International Version]{.class_3526}[ flips out to "God's work," which no Greek text says. The ]{.class_3527}[RSV ]{.class_3526}[goes to "divine training," which no Greek text says. Taylor (]{.class_3527}[The Living Bible]{.class_3526}[) conjectures "God's plan of faith," which isn't found in any Greek text known to God or man. The ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[(1901) says "dispensation," and the ]{.class_3527}[NASV ]{.class_3526}[alters this to "ADMINISTRATION," which means NOTHING.]{.class_3527}

[Having obliterated the truth of God and having produced a mish-mash of nonsense, the faculty at Liberty University (Lynchburg) is left powerless and intimidated. Desiring to alter the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[text to match these silly, superstitious, bungling scribes (and we say that, knowing they are all "good, godly, saved, scholarly believers in the plenary, verbal inspiration of Aunt Sally's housecat"), and yet not having the nerve to do it, Jerry Falwell recommends one text---]{.class_3527}[The "New" King James Bible]{.class_3526}[---and publishes another (the real ]{.class_3527}[King James Bible]{.class_3526}[) in his commentary; then his commentator, ]{.class_3527}[Sumner Wemp,]{.class_3526}[ SEWS HIS UPPER LIP TO HIS LOWER LIP AND FAILS TO COMMENT ON EITHER TEXT. In the ]{.class_3527}[Liberty Bible Commentary,]{.class_3526}[ Vol. II, Wemp simply stares at the last clause in verse 4 with horror, and then goes bravely on to verse 5!]{.class_3527}

[Typical.]{.class_3526}[ Typical of the "greatest Bible correspondence course" available today in America, etc.]{.class_3527}

[Faith MINISTERS]{.class_3527}[ "godly edifying." ]{.class_3528}[Fables ]{.class_3527}["minister questions."]{.class_3528}[ Could anything be clearer?]{.class_3527}

[Faith dispenses light (]{.class_3527}["edifying"]{.class_3528}[), and does it in a ]{.class_3527}["godly"]{.class_3528}[ manner (see the text). A man who is "strong in the faith" ministers and enlightens (Rom. 12:1--8). The problem in the passage for the apostates was "faith in WHAT?" Since they could find no antecedent, some made "faith" the ]{.class_3527}[container ]{.class_3526}[of godly edification; ]{.class_3527}[it was the SOURCE.]{.class_3526}[ This faith is called ]{.class_3527}["faith unfeigned"]{.class_3528}[ in the next verse, meaning unpretended, genuine faith; faith from God.]{.class_3527}

1:5 Now the end of the commandment is charity out of a pure heart, and of a good conscience, and of faith unfeigned:

6 From which some having swerved have turned aside unto vain jangling;

7 Desiring to be teachers of the law; understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirm.

8 But we know that the law is good, if a man use it lawfully;

9 Knowing this, that the law is not made for a righteous man, but for the lawless and disobedient, for the ungodly and for sinners, for unholy and profane, for murderers of fathers and murderers of mothers, for manslayers,

10 For whoremongers, for them that defile themselves with mankind, for menstealers, for liars, for perjured persons, and if there be any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine;

11 According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.

The passage in the Laodicean age (present) is aimed primarily at the Seventh-day Adventists who use the law as an "aid" or "appendage" to salvation.

"[Now the end of the COMMANDMENT"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5).[ "THE COMMANDMENT"]{.class_3529} is stated in 1 John 3:23, and ["the end"]{.class_3529} in both cases is the same. Observe here how the Holy Spirit in the [Authorized Version]{.class_3530} overruled all of the Greek lexicons and scholars who had been deceived into thinking that "the commandment" was just the charge Paul was giving Timothy. (This was done by pointing out that the Greek word here \[παραγγελιας, paraggelias\] is not the same Greek word \[εντολη, entole\] of 1 John. Thus, we find no ["commandment"]{.class_3529} mentioned anywhere in the [RSV, Living Bible, ASV,]{.class_3530} or the [NASV]{.class_3530}. Again, Falwell's staff at Lynchburg gets the jitters, and desiring to respect and honor the apostates of the [ASV ]{.class_3530}and the [NASV ]{.class_3530}as much as possible, Sumner Wemp writes, "the end or purpose of HIS CHARGE" for[ "the commandment" ]{.class_3529}([Liberty Commentary]{.class_3530}, p. 629). Naturally, we shall dispense with Wemp, Plummer, Wiesinger, Alford, Trench, Vincent, Thayer, DeWitte, Kuenen, and Koelling with one swat. "Ha ha! Ho ho! Ten at one blow," etc.

"[Charity out of a pure heart" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 5), which was purified BY faith (see Acts 15:9), shows the exact meaning of verse 4 in the [AV ]{.class_3530}text, contrary to every modern translation on the market.

"[And of a good conscience"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5) that answered God (1 Pet. 3:21) by following His Son in water baptism. It had been a defiled conscience (Titus 1:15), or even a ["conscience seared with a hot iron"]{.class_3529} (1 Tim. 4:1--2), but it now is a ["conscience also bearing\...witness in the Holy Ghost" ]{.class_3529}(Rom. 9:1).

"[From which some having swerved"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). They turned aside from [three ]{.class_3530}things: a pure heart, a good conscience, and genuine faith. The heart was polluted (Matt. 12:35); the conscience was seared (1 Tim. 4:1--2); and the faith gave out, so they made it up and [pretended ]{.class_3530}they had what they [did not ]{.class_3530}have (for examples, see the "700 Club," "PTL Ministries," the "Full Gospel Businessmen's Association," etc.).

"[Unto vain jangling"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). A beautiful English expression which describes it exactly. For the right sound effect, get both fists full of chain links about two feet long, rattle them together, and jerk them up and down; you will get a perfect "audio" reproduction of your nearest Seventh-day Adventist minister. The apostate [ASV ]{.class_3530}cools it to "vain talking," which is a real watered-down job of presenting the truth. The still more apostate [NASV]{.class_3530}---coming at a later time---says "fruitless discussions," which is about as good as any unsaved liberal could translate the passage. The [NIV ]{.class_3530}says "meaningless talk," and so forth. Anyway, to "smooth down the text polite and snuggly, keep damnation out of sight." (We wonder what happened to all of that talk about "DYNAMIC EQUIVALENCE" that the modern translators gave us when talking about a "good translation." "I got spurs that JINGLE, JANGLE, JINGLE" was not written in 1600, 1700, 1800, or even 1900. [It was written in 1940.]{.class_3530}) Something "jangling" someone's nerves is just as common in 1990 as it was in 1611. The new translators simply don't know what is going on.

"[Desiring to be teachers of the law"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). These God-called and God-ordained teachers were LEVITES (see Ezra 7:1--6), and their job was to teach the law (see Mal. 2:5--8; 2 Chron. 31:4; Ezra 7:6--8). But with the believer now ["justified from all things, from which ye could not be justified by the law of Moses"]{.class_3529} (Acts 13:38--39), and with the believer under grace instead of the law (Rom. 6 and 7), and the tablets of stone done away with as a means of salvation (2 Cor. 3), the Seventh-day Adventists are left as [false teachers,]{.class_3530} ["understanding neither what they say, nor whereof they affirm"]{.class_3529} (vs. 7). A saved, Christian teacher of the LAW would teach what Romans 13:9 and Hebrews 8:13 teaches, for ["THE LAW IS NOT MADE FOR A RIGHTEOUS MAN" ]{.class_3529}(vs. 9).

[The ]{.class_3527}[intent ]{.class_3526}[of the law was to reveal sin in an ungodly man (Rom. 7:7--9) and lead him to Christ (Gal. 3:24). The law is ]{.class_3527}["holy, and just, and good"]{.class_3528}[ (Rom. 7:12), but it is ]{.class_3527}[not the means of salvation ]{.class_3526}[(Gal. 2:16). Its righteousness is fulfilled IN the Christian, not BY the Christian (see Rom. 8:1--4). ]{.class_3527}["The law is good, if a man use it lawfully"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 8)---as a means of showing a sinner his need for salvation, as a moral guideline for absolutes regarding personal conduct toward a fellow man, as a warning to the Christian that God will judge sin, and that a man will reap from bad conduct even after he is saved---but Timothy's troublemakers were using it ]{.class_3527}[unlawfully,]{.class_3526}[ as the Seventh-day Adventists do in 1989.]{.class_3527}

"[For the lawless"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). Those outlaws living outside the law who pretend that it doesn't exist.

"[And disobedient"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). For those who read it and then violate it, like the Jews in the book of Romans.

"[For the ungodly"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). Those whose conduct is not in the least like God (see Jude 15, 18).

"[And for sinners"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). In the Jewish sense of Luke 7:39 and John 9:25 and 31: grossly immoral.

"[For unholy and profane" ]{.class_3528}[(vs. 9). Those that show no interest in God, the Bible, the church, or anything spiritual.]{.class_3527}

"[For murderers" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 9). Three kinds are given: those who kill their fathers, those who kill their mothers, and those who kill anyone they don't like (["manslayers," ]{.class_3529}vs. 9). All three are rampant in America in the twentieth century.

"[For whoremongers"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). "Whore-hopper" in the "living speech" of the "living English" that is a "living language," and therefore must be revised every two years according to Bible scholars (over ninety translations since 1880!). The reference is to a man like the prodigal (Luke 15) who keeps company regularly with "hustlers" (i.e., whores).

"[For them that defile themselves with mankind"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). Lesbians, fruits, queers, homos; you name it---anyone who professes to be "GAY" while perverting sex, human nature, and natural laws.

"[For menstealers"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). See Exodus 21:16, where stealing and selling is forbidden, but not BUYING (Exod. 21:2). A slave already bought is told to [stay in harness]{.class_3530} (1 Cor. 7:21--24) and [SERVE his master ]{.class_3530}(1 Tim. 6:1--5).

"[For liars"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). Any modern Bible reviser, any journalist working for the Gannett string of newspapers, Hearst, etc. Any CBS, NBC, or ABC telecaster, any Greek teacher in a large college or university, any Jehovah's Witness, Communist, Roman Catholic pope, or Roman Catholic cardinal. (See chapter seven in [The Christian's Handbook of Biblical Scholarship,]{.class_3530} 1988.)

"[For perjured persons"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). A liar under oath, like any Roman Catholic, following the orders of the hierarchy as given in Ligouri's [Moral Theology]{.class_3530} (Vol. I, pp. 364--366, Vesuntione, 1828).

"[Any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 10). Note that in this case, ["sound doctrine"]{.class_3529} is not merely a reference to [doctrinal correctness ]{.class_3530}in regard to beliefs, creeds, and dogmas, but to [conduct ]{.class_3530}(see also 1 Tim. 6:3).

"[According to the glorious gospel"]{.class_3528} (vs. 11), which Paul is about to tell you the meaning, is connected with ["any other thing that is contrary to sound doctrine"]{.class_3529} (vs. 10). Sound doctrine, ["according to the glorious gospel,"]{.class_3529} is to LIVE RIGHT (see Titus 2:1--7). Christians should not be liars, queers, murderers, perjurers, profane, ungodly, unholy, or lawless. These sins are to be confessed, judged, repented of, and turned from if they are found in our lives.

[The]{.class_3527}[ "New" King James]{.class_3526}[ has gotten rid of the]{.class_3527}[ "jangling" ]{.class_3528}[in verse 6 and made it "idle talk." It has also altered ]{.class_3527}["minister questions"]{.class_3528}[ to "cause disputes" (vs. 4). The ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[(1901) has converted the ]{.class_3527}["disobedient"]{.class_3528}[ to "unruly," and dear old Kenneth Taylor (]{.class_3527}[The Living Bible]{.class_3526}[) has invented a whole little sermon of his own for verse 5 that isn't connected with anything in either Testament known to God or man. Huther, Von Soden, DeWette, and Ewald prefer "THAT law" to ]{.class_3527}["THE law"]{.class_3528}[ in verse 8, while Koelling and Souter prefer "A law." (]{.class_3527}[I ]{.class_3526}[prefer strawberry shortcake.) ]{.class_3527}["The glorious gospel"]{.class_3528}[ has been perverted into "the gospel of the glory" by Alford, Plummer, Vincent, Kuenen, and Souter, so the ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[wrote it that way, and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown buy it. We don't.]{.class_3527}

[Since the ]{.class_3527}["glorious gospel"]{.class_3528}[ is not the "gospel of the GLORY of God," Paul goes on immediately to explain why it is a ]{.class_3527}["glorious gospel,"]{.class_3528}[ even as he described it in verse 11.]{.class_3527}

1:12 And I thank Christ Jesus our Lord, who hath enabled me, for that he counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry;

13 Who was before a blasphemer, and a persecutor, and injurious: but I obtained mercy, because I did it ignorantly in unbelief.

14 And the grace of our Lord was exceeding abundant with faith and love which is in Christ Jesus.

15 This is a faithful saying, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners; of whom I am chief.

16 Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy, that in me first Jesus Christ might shew forth all longsuffering, for a pattern to them which should hereafter believe on him to life everlasting.

17 Now unto the King eternal, immortal, invisible, the only wise God, be honour and glory for ever and ever. Amen.

[This is the]{.class_3527}[ "glorious gospel"]{.class_3528}[ mentioned in verse 11. It was ]{.class_3527}["committed" ]{.class_3528}[to Paul as a trust, and he instructs Timothy to commit it to others as a trust (2 Tim. 2:2). Every Christian is a steward who has been entrusted with the glorious gospel (Acts 20:20), and its ]{.class_3527}[seven mysteries ]{.class_3526}[(1 Cor. 4:1--2). The "mystery of the Mass" is NOT one of these, nor the "mystery" of the "sacraments," nor any other magical, mysterious, esoteric, African piece of religious hocus-pocus drummed up by a fake "Christian."]{.class_3527}

"[Who hath enabled me\...counted me faithful, putting me into the ministry"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). Note that the "enabling" is not always synonymous with the [calling]{.class_3530}. Paul is not "in the ministry" until he returns from forty days and nights on the backside of a desert (see Gal. 1:17 and comments in that commentary). Even then, he is not [accepted ]{.class_3530}until Acts 9:27.

[This is the place where many a young man has trouble. He confuses the ]{.class_3527}[calling ]{.class_3526}[with the "enabling." Paul's religious background before he was saved was so thorough that it didn't take long for God to "enable" him to be a minister. ]{.class_3527}[For Timothy, it took nearly ten years]{.class_3526}[ (see introduction). It is possible for God to call a boy to preach before he is ten-years-old. In Bob Jones Sr.'s case, he was "enabled" before he was fifteen years old, but this is the exception. A "calling" usually precedes an "enabling" by five to fifteen years, sometimes longer.]{.class_3527}

[Putting someone ]{.class_3527}["into the ministry"]{.class_3528}[ means more than calling someone to witness, or even teach the Bible or "preach." To be put ]{.class_3527}["into the ministry"]{.class_3528}[ is to be placed completely at the disposal of God for nothing but the teaching and preaching of the word of God. Although Paul worked "part-time" (Acts 18:3), he did this for a testimony's sake (1 Cor. 9:14--18) and made it clear (1 Cor. 9:14) that this was not the "standard procedure" (1 Cor. 9:15--20). All Christians are called to ]{.class_3527}[witness ]{.class_3526}[(Acts 1:8), and all Christians are called to ]{.class_3527}[preach ]{.class_3526}[(Acts 8:1, 4), but not all Christians are PUT ]{.class_3527}["INTO THE MINISTRY."]{.class_3528}[ Paul was appointed, not only as a witness and soulwinner, but also as ]{.class_3527}["a preacher, and an apostle, and a teacher"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Tim. 1:11).]{.class_3527}

"[Who was before"]{.class_3528} (vs. 13). Each word is to be taken at face value. He [did ]{.class_3530}blaspheme (Acts 26:11); he [did ]{.class_3530}persecute (Acts 26:11); he [was ]{.class_3530}injurious (Acts 26:10). He was a first-century, Catholic Torquemada or "Saint" Ignatius Loyola. Paul was as ignorant as the men who killed Christ, and hence, he received the mercy they received (Luke 23:34). This in itself is a testimony to the fact that 99 percent of the commentators and teachers misread verse 16. Paul was certainly NOT the first man to demonstrate God's longsuffering in that matter. He wasn't even the 50,000th. (When we get to that verse, we will correct all of the commentators, all of the teachers, and all of the expositors with, of course, the infallible text of the Holy Bible. There were thousands of men who were blasphemers, injurious, and persecutors, who were not in the least bit "ignorant." God had mercy on many of them as he did on Paul, who WAS ignorant.)

"[This is a faithful saying"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15). It is true. It can be said to anyone and can be safely believed by anyone. (There are three more of these "faithful sayings" in the Pastoral Epistles: one in 1 Tim. 4:9, one in 2 Tim. 2:11, and one in Titus 3:8. One has to do with exercising oneself in godliness; one has to do with the resurrection; one has to do with eternal justification and eternal glory.)

[The purpose of Christ's coming was ]{.class_3527}["to save sinners"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 15). This was His first and main objective. Founding a church, "sharing God's love," showing "the way to God," giving us an "example," reconciling institutions, revealing God's nature, etc., are all secondary matters, or tertiary matters. He came ]{.class_3527}["to save sinners."]{.class_3528}[ He came to call sinners (Matt. 9:13). He came ]{.class_3527}["to seek and to save" ]{.class_3528}[sinners (Luke 19:10). He came ]{.class_3527}["to seek and to save that which was lost"]{.class_3528}[ (Luke 19:10). This gives us our primary commission (see John 20:21 and Acts 1:8--9), and it shows that]{.class_3527}[ no pope ]{.class_3526}[who ever lived was ever even slightly concerned with ]{.class_3527}[obeying God ]{.class_3526}[or Jesus Christ. In the twentieth century, popes have addressed (over radio-linked satellite communications) more than 2,000,000,000 sinners ]{.class_3527}[without even attempting to tell one sinner how to get saved]{.class_3526}[. Popes are unfaithful stewards, religious charlatans, blind leaders of the blind.]{.class_3527}

"[Of whom I am chief" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 15). Paul certainly was this in his own sight, as every truly converted sinner sees himself in the light of the Book. "Chief of sinners" could be claimed by at least 4,000,000 saved men in the last hundred years. Nothing is more debasing, degrading, or disillusioning than for a man to see himself as [God ]{.class_3530}sees him (Isa. 6:1--5; Job 42:5--7; Luke 5:8). "Positive thinkers" are people who simply have never seen a true picture of themselves or their fellow man. The[ "grace"]{.class_3529} and[ "love"]{.class_3529} (vs. 14) are in ONE MAN, and it is no man living on earth today in the flesh.

"[Howbeit for this cause I obtained mercy"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16). What all of the commentators and revisers have done is assume that the ["THIS CAUSE"]{.class_3529} was a reference to being "chiefest of sinners." Having done this with one consent, Dummelow, Matthew Henry, Ellicott, John Peter Lange, Adam Clarke, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Williams ([The Student's Commentary]{.class_3530}), A.M. Stibbs ([The New Bible Commentary]{.class_3530}), Wilbur Wallis ([The Wycliffe Bible Commentary]{.class_3530}), and the rest---including Sumner Wemp (commenting for [The Liberty Bible Commentary]{.class_3530}, Lynchburg, Virginia)---decided that the "PATTERN of longsuffering" was the [Lord's pattern,]{.class_3530} and that the UNSAVED WERE TO TRUST THAT PATTERN SO AS TO BE ASSURED THAT GOD WOULD SAVE THEM TOO.

This is the consensus of conservative, evangelical, fundamentalist, and Catholic scholars---living or dead. We shall dispense with it at once.

[1. ]{.class_3527}[Paul was NOT the FIRST to show that God was "longsuffering" in dealing with bad sinners.]{.class_3526}[ Manasseh would go circles around Paul (2 Kings 21:1--9), and Ahab would "beat him to a frazzle" (see 1 Kings 18:18 and 1 Kings 21:25).]{.class_3527}

[2. The verse said ]{.class_3527}["THAT IN ME FIRST,"]{.class_3528}[ not "in God's dealing ]{.class_3527}[with ]{.class_3526}[me." The pattern was to be found IN PAUL, and he is careful to tell you that his pattern is YOUR example (2 Thess. 3:7--13), ]{.class_3527}[AFTER you are saved.]{.class_3526}

[3. The peculiar obsession to force the verse to say something it does not say has lead several of the versions to make the "which should hereafter believe on him" refer to THE UNSAVED WHO WERE GETTING READY TO BELIEVE ON HIM. This sudden defection of 500,000 students, professors, teachers, revisers, commentators, and scholars from the truth should be carefully noted, for the following verses are the Holy Spirit's amplification of the verse as we have given it here:

"[That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, being made conformable unto his death"]{.class_3528} (Phil. 3:10).

"[Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body's sake, which is the church"]{.class_3528} (Col. 1:24).

THERE IS NO "HIS" IN ANY SET OF GREEK MANUSCRIPTS FROM ANY FAMILY, IN ANY EDITION, OF ANY GREEK TESTAMENT, PRINTED OR UNPRINTED, ON THIS EARTH.]{.class_3527}

[These are the lying hypocrites who said that the "article" shouldn't be in John 4:24. Remember? [This, then, is the RESULT of "readable" translations that "clear up the archaic" English; they either ]{.class_3527}[pervert ]{.class_3526}[the truth or ]{.class_3527}[obscure ]{.class_3526}[the truth or ]{.class_3527}[eliminate ]{.class_3526}[the truth. 1:18 This charge I commit unto thee, son Timothy, according to the prophecies which went before on thee, that thou by them mightest war a good warfare;

19 Holding faith, and a good conscience; which some having put away concerning faith have made shipwreck:

20 Of whom is Hymenaeus and Alexander; whom I have delivered unto Satan, that they may learn not to blaspheme.

[If there were any doubt about the meaning of verse 16, observe that it was said to be ]{.class_3527}["THIS CHARGE I COMMIT UNTO THEE"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 18). Can anyone on this earth believe that Paul charged the young pastor to remind all of his audience that]{.class_3527}[ if they wanted to get mercy from God]{.class_3526}[ (vs. 16),]{.class_3527}[ they should "carry on" like HE DID?]{.class_3526}[ What a blasphemous false teaching! The CHARGE was to tell the converts that]{.class_3527}[ they should get ready to suffer,]{.class_3526}[ since a fruit of the Spirit (Gal. 5:22) was ]{.class_3527}["LONGSUFFERING."]{.class_3528}[ Included in the charge were:]{.class_3527}

1. Teaching correct doctrine (vss. 3--4).

2. Reminding the converts that there was a purpose in salvation by grace (vss. 5--6).

3. Warning the converts that false teachers would pervert the law (vss. 7--11).

4. Reminding the converts that salvation was wholly by grace and undeserved (vss. 12--15).

[5. ]{.class_3527}[That Paul's calling to longsuffering and patience was to be their EXAMPLE or PATTERN]{.class_3526}[ (vs. 16).]{.class_3527}

6. And that glory and honor belonged to God alone (vs. 17).

[THAT is the charge. ]{.class_3527}[Not one reference was made anywhere in the chapter about unsaved people who should remember those things so they could get saved.]{.class_3526}[ One hundred percent of the commentators and revisers abandoned the context.]{.class_3527}

"[According to the prophecies which went before on thee"]{.class_3528} (vs. 18). This was at his ordination and laying on of hands (2 Tim. 1:6). At that time, a preacher with the gift of prophecy (see 1 Cor. 12:10) foretold Timothy's future in regards to the ministry.

"[By them mightest war a good warfare"]{.class_3528} (vs. 18). This is the military figure we commented on in Ephesians. (See the lengthy comments under Eph. 6:10 in that commentary.) It is found again in 2 Timothy 2:3--4 and other places (Heb. 2:10 and 1 Cor. 9:7). The warfare is [spiritual]{.class_3530}, and the main weapons are the word of God and prayer (2 Cor. 10:4--5).

"[Holding faith, and a good conscience"]{.class_3528} (vs. 19). As in verses 4 and 5, which see.

"[Concerning faith have made shipwreck"]{.class_3528} (vs. 19). Two of the apostate fundamentalists are named (one of them is amillennial; see comments under 2 Tim. 2:17--18), and the idea is that they have "shipwrecked" the faith of some other Christians by teaching that the first resurrection was [spiritual ]{.class_3530}(as you FIND IT GIVEN IN OUR SUNDAY VISITOR, Sept. 20, 1985, BY THE ROMAN CATHOLIC "FATHER"). Alexander might be the Alexander of 2 Timothy 4:14, but probably not.

"[Whom I have delivered unto Satan" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 20). This is the practice he recommended for a man fornicating with his father's wife (1 Cor. 5:1--7). Obviously, this "delivering" was done through [prayer]{.class_3530}, for that is the only way it could be done. Although "churching" the member (see 1 Cor. 5:13) might have [accomplished ]{.class_3530}this, being kicked out of a church would certainly not deliver a Christian to Satan---notwithstanding the official Roman Catholic teaching backed up by the [WYCLIFFE (!) BIBLE COMMENTARY]{.class_3530} (p. 1372).

Here the commentator (Wilbur B. Wallis) tells us that since the local church is THE BODY OF CHRIST (!), you either belong to it or you are "UNDER THE DOMINION OF SATAN." (To which may be replied, "Ahh, your father's moustache!") The excommunicated person was to be brought to "repentance and SALVATION," according to Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, which assumes every person kicked out of the church is LOST. This is a Roman Catholic myth from start to finish.

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks, be made for all men;

2 For kings, and for all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty.

3 For this is good and acceptable in the sight of God our Saviour;

4 Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth.

5 For there is one God, and one mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus;

6 Who gave himself a ransom for all, to be testified in due time.

7 Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an apostle, (I speak the truth in Christ, and lie not;) a teacher of the Gentiles in faith and verity.

Paul begins talking about prayer, but quickly digresses into matters on the Mediator who will handle the prayers (vss. 3--5). He returns to his subject at verse 8 after magnifying his office. (See Rom. 11:13, and compare it with 1 Tim. 2:7.) This is to reinforce his requests made in verses 8--15.

"[Giving of thanks, be made for all men"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). ["All"]{.class_3529} means [all]{.class_3530}. We are to pray for all the lost people we know, even the masses we don't know. We are to give thanks while praying, and thank God for things that our brethren may forget to thank him for. ["Supplications"]{.class_3529} are prayers for a supply of something that you or someone else needs.[ "Prayers"]{.class_3529} are simply lists of things ASKED for: prayer is ASKING (see Luke 11:9--13). ["Intercessions" ]{.class_3529}are prayers that are said on behalf of someone who is unable to get the prayer answered, or who doesn't see the need of prayer. You can make intercession for, or AGAINST, someone (see Rom. 11:2--3). ["Giving of thanks"]{.class_3529} is to accompany all three types of requests: supplications, prayers, and intercessions.

"[For kings, and for all that are in authority"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). The reason is given immediately; it is not to "save society" nor to bring "peace on earth" nor to end the "nuclear threat"; and it is not to guarantee "civil justice for oppressed minorities," etc. (and all of that Catholic-socialistic garbage). It is so the Christian can live in peace and practice godliness in an honest fashion.

"[That we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). This is the prayer, and not that "world peace" may come. The prayer is for the individual Christian, never for the world. Christ prayed ["NOT FOR THE WORLD"]{.class_3529} (John 17:9); that is what Catholic popes do to impress people. It makes "good press."

[Notice how accurately Jeremiah explains this text in Jer. 29:7. ]{.class_3527}["Pray for the peace of Jerusalem: they shall prosper that love thee"]{.class_3528}[ (Psa. 122:6). World peace is a joke. There are three World Wars coming up (Rev. 6, 19, 20).]{.class_3527}

"[Who will have all men to be saved, and to come unto the knowledge of the truth"]{.class_3528} (1 Tim. 2:4). The believer should notice the numerous apologies that are about to be made on behalf of John Calvin and Arthur W. Pink. They cannot be allowed to lose face before the [King James ]{.class_3530}text. Since it is perfectly apparent to any five-point Calvinist that God wouldn't "will" anything that He couldn't bring to pass, the text of the [Authorized Version]{.class_3530} HAS to be in error. Consequently, we find all the Roman Catholic Bibles, [recommended by Bob Jones University, Tennessee Temple University, Arlington, Springfield, and Liberty University]{.class_3530} ([ASV, NASV]{.class_3530}, and [NIV]{.class_3530}), getting rid of God's ["will" ]{.class_3529}just as quickly as possible.

[The passage, along with 2 Peter 3:9, is intolerable to people like Pink, Shelton, Shelton Jr., Gill, Ethelbert Bullinger, and the ]{.class_3527}[Sovereign Book Club]{.class_3526}[, the ]{.class_3527}[Old Puritan Press, The Baptist Examiner, The Christian Baptist,]{.class_3526}[ etc.; for it violates the first philosophical premise of TULIP speculation: i.e., if God really WANTED everyone to be saved, they would get saved; otherwise God's "sovereignty" ]{.class_3527}[would ]{.class_3526}[suffer damage. This is the root foundation behind the twisted structure of all five-point Calvinism. It really means that nothing can go on of which God does not APPROVE. We all know the various dodges that take place when Calvinists are pressed on this point. They see the mess into which their philosophical theories have gotten them when confronted with such verses as Matthew 23:37 and John 5:40, so they will grant, at least, that God allows ]{.class_3527}[some things ]{.class_3526}[of which He doesn't approve, but when it comes to salvation, no such allowance will be made. The TULIP sniffers hang up on John 12:40, 6:44, and 10:16, and then interpret the Pauline Epistles in the light of these verses after stuffing them into Ephesians 1:4.]{.class_3527}

[Since we have thoroughly covered these matters in our ]{.class_3527}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Galatians-Ephesians-Philippians-Colossians]{.class_3526}[ (and the booklet ]{.class_3527}[Hyper-Calvinism]{.class_3526}[), we will not waste the reader's time going back over the ground. Here, notice that the ]{.class_3527}["all men"]{.class_3528}[ of verse 4 is the ]{.class_3527}["all men" ]{.class_3528}[of verse 1, and has nothing to do with the "elect" or anyone "chosen in Christ." Further note that the ]{.class_3527}["all"]{.class_3528}[ of verse 6 defined the]{.class_3527}[ "many"]{.class_3528}[ of Matthew 20:28. Have you ever noticed how all "Hardshell Baptists" quote Matthew 20:28 over and over as a foundation truth, without once going to 1 Timothy 2:4 to tell you to what the ]{.class_3527}["many"]{.class_3528}[ of Matthew 20 is a reference? [Not all ]{.class_3527}["kings"]{.class_3528}[ are "elect" (vss. 1--3). All of those]{.class_3527}[ "in authority"]{.class_3528}[ certainly are not (Nero and Satan, for example), and Christ the Mediator is certainly not just placed between God and the "elect." The]{.class_3527}[ "men" ]{.class_3528}[of verse 8 are men who are already saved, but that is in another section, you will notice, separated from the context by a statemen on Paul's calling (vs. 7).]{.class_3527}

"[Men"]{.class_3528} and ["women"]{.class_3529} in verses 8 and 9 are saved individuals in the local church as the[ "man"]{.class_3529} and ["woman"]{.class_3529} of verse 12, but the ["men"]{.class_3529} and[ "man"]{.class_3529} of verses 2, 4, and 5 NOT only include the unsaved, but they are also people who are not in [any ]{.class_3530}local church. [Believing the text of the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version ]{.class_3526}[will always yield more Biblical truth than ten years spent studying the religious speculations of Gill, Gilpin, Ross, Calvin, Dabney, Kuyper, Hodge, Berkhof, Pink, Shelton Jr., and the "hypers."]{.class_3527}

The Roman Catholic "Mary" (]{.class_3527}[to whom Pope John Paul II graciously gave the whole world:]{.class_3526}[ AP, 1985) is a vicious fraud: a pagan invention of depraved emotional ties with fallen men and their Catholic mothers.]{.class_3527}

[The modern Catholic translators bend over backwards to honor this demoniac spook, but the text is so plain (notice the "ones") that all they can do is give us: "One also is the mediator" (the Roman Catholic ]{.class_3527}[New American Bible]{.class_3526}[), thus altering the reader's attention from His SINGULAR OFFICE, which has no competitors, to His NATURE, which has nothing to do with His OFFICE in the context. The Roman Catholic ]{.class_3527}[NASV]{.class_3526}[, recommended by Bob Jones University, "durst not" go that far, but they will insert the "also" into the text from Rome: ]{.class_3527}[they do.]{.class_3526}[ Vincent (]{.class_3527}[Word Studies]{.class_3526}[)---the man who never believed that Paul wrote one line of 1 Timothy---expounds the text this way: "As the One God, so the one mediator implies the EXTENSION OF THE SAVING PURPOSE TO ALL" (p. 219), which is cowardly claptrap. The text ]{.class_3527}[implies ]{.class_3526}[nothing. It states that the one mediator is the only way you can get to the one God. "Saving purposes" and "extensions" are about as relevant to the text as "lifestyles" and "meaningful areas."]{.class_3527}

[Taylor does the best job, however, of covering up for the pope. Having converted Jesus Christ to Simon Peter (]{.class_3527}[The Living Bible,]{.class_3526}[ Matt. 16:18), and confirming the ancient lie that Peter was in Rome (1 Pet. 5:13), and lying about the people to whom James wrote (James 1:1 in ]{.class_3527}[The Living Bible]{.class_3526}[), Taylor says there is NOT ONE MEDIATOR; there is simply Christ Jesus, whose "job" is to bring mankind (people) to God. ]{.class_3527}[There is no "ONE GOD" in Taylor's text.]{.class_3526}[ He has "one side" and "the other side," but no ]{.class_3527}["ONE GOD"]{.class_3528}[ and no]{.class_3527}[ "ONE MEDIATOR."]{.class_3528}[ ]{.class_3527}[The Living Bible]{.class_3526}[ is a textbook for dead sinners, but its "READABILITY" is excellent. Most lies are.]{.class_3527}

"[A ransom for ALL"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). ["All"]{.class_3529} here includes false prophets and false teachers who went to hell (see 2 Pet. 2:1--2), as well as Christ-rejecting Jews who tread underfoot the blood of Christ (see Heb. 10:29). This is perfectly clear from the Scriptures, although Barnard, Shelton, Hodge, Gilpin, Ross, Gill, Kuyper, Dabney, Hodge, and Calvin couldn't find either verse due to a philosophical hemorrhage.

"[Limited Atonement" (particularly, "Hardshell Baptists" and "Primitive Baptists") was a false description of what was actually "limited APPLICATION." Theological speculators could never find the difference. Since the blood was ]{.class_3527}[applied ]{.class_3530}only to the sinners who accepted Jesus Christ, the Calvinist took for granted---in outrageous violation of the text before us---that there was no more blood [available ]{.class_3530}than the amount that was [applied]{.class_3530}. This is an indirect denial of the Deity of the Lord Jesus Christ, which piously passes off as "magnifying the sovereign grace of God," etc. There never was any real problem.

A Yankee from New York was about to confiscate the property of fifty home owners in Fort Myers who could not pay their bills. All of them lived in Fort Myers. A stranger came to town, went to the Florida National Bank, and deposited enough money to pay the Yankee off on all fifty of the homes. The only catch was that every home owner had to come down to the bank and write out a check to himself and draw the money out of the bank to give to the Yankee. Most of them came; some did not. After all the sane ones had paid off their debts (about \$800,000), there was left in excess of the owed amount something over \$4,000,000,000,000,000,000. THE BLOOD IS ETERNAL BLOOD (Acts 20:28).

[The Calvinists claim that this "excess" was ]{.class_3527}[wasted ]{.class_3526}[because it represented a ]{.class_3527}[futile attempt ]{.class_3526}[of the generous donor if ALL of it was not used up. Cuckoo! Cuckoos gone bananas. Somebody is "sleigh riding" on happy dust.]{.class_3527}

"[To be testified in due time"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). Note, this is NOT the testimony of Matthew 20:28 before Calvary, cited by all Calvinists. Matthew is NOT the "good news" (1 Cor. 15:1--5) given to Paul AFTER Calvary about which he is now testifying (1 Tim. 1:15). The ransom NOW is for ["ALL"]{.class_3529} (see above), and he never ["testified" ]{.class_3529}once that Christ came to die only for the elect. All Calvinists make the surreptitious blunder of believing that because 90 percent of Paul's writings are aimed at people who have already been saved (1 Cor. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; Gal. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6; Col. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5; Rom. 5, 6, 7, 8, 9; etc.), that whatever he says about Christ's death applies only to THEM.

"[Whereunto I am ordained a preacher, and an apostle\...a teacher"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). He preaches [the gospel ]{.class_3530}to the Gentiles---not ["limited atonement."]{.class_3530} He demonstrates the signs and wonders of a Jewish apostle (2 Cor. 12:12) so that they will be certain of the Jewish origins of the gospel (see John 4:22 and Acts 8:14--17), and he teaches the Gentile converts the Old Testament while he is writing the New Testament Epistles, ["in faith and verity"]{.class_3529} (vs. 7).

2:8 I will therefore that men pray every where, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting.

9 In like manner also, that women adorn themselves in modest apparel, with shamefacedness and sobriety; not with broided hair, or gold, or pearls, or costly array;

10 But (which becometh women professing godliness) with good works.

11 Let the woman learn in silence with all subjection.

12 But I suffer not a woman to teach, nor to usurp authority over the man, but to be in silence.

13 For Adam was first formed, then Eve.

14 And Adam was not deceived, but the woman being deceived was in the transgression.

15 Notwithstanding she shall be saved in childbearing, if they continue in faith and charity and holiness with sobriety.

[Here Paul returns to the theme of verses 1 and 2. The ]{.class_3527}[Mediator insertion ]{.class_3526}[was necessary because his subject had been PRAYER, and whoever prays is undertaking to "mediate" for sinners. The point Paul injected into the prayer meeting was that any prayer made to Mary, Joseph, or "Blessed John the Baptist" would fizzle out before you lit the fuse. Testimonies as to these kinds of prayers being answered (St. Jude, St. Thomas, Fatty Fatima, et al.) are CLASSIFIED IN THE WORD OF GOD AS DEMONIAC PERMISSION TO DESTROY THE PETITIONER (see Deut. 13:1--5 and Ezek. 14:1--16 for exact analysis).]{.class_3527}

"[That men pray every where, lifting up holy hands, without wrath and doubting"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). It is apparent that a saved man can pray anywhere: in a jail (Acts 16:25), in the belly of a whale (Jonah 2:1), on a beach (Acts 21:5), on a mountain (Matt. 14:23), during a conversation (Neh. 2:4), sitting on a well (Gen. 24:11--14), running for his life (Gen. 19:18--20), on a ship (Jonah 1:14), or even on a cross (Luke 23:42).

"[Lifting up holy hands"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). This is the Old Testament manner (see 1 Kings 8:22 and Psa. 88:9). The position of the dying thief is the position of prayer, as was Moses' posture during the battle with the Amalekites (Exod. 17:11--12). Jeremiah 7:16 and 11:14 show that the original gesture accompanied the lifting up of the prayer itself. (Hebrews 12:12, then, becomes an exhortation for the backslidden Christian to renew his prayer life.)

"[Without wrath and doubting"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). According to the finest twentieth-century brains in the country, stewed down to a cold, grey mush, the word ["doubting"]{.class_3529} should not be in the verse. So in loyal obedience to the Roman Catholic hierarchy ([New American Bible]{.class_3530}), the darling of Bob Jones University ([New American Standard Version]{.class_3530}) has changed it to "dissension" to match Rome, and the [New International Version ]{.class_3530}leans far enough toward Rome to make it "disputing." The [Revised Standard Version]{.class_3530} says "quarreling," and Taylor wraps up the whole godless, despraved, hellish mess with "resentment" ([The Living Bible]{.class_3530}).

Naturally, a Bible believer will reject the whole "caboodle" at sight. These are simply attacks on our "faith" (ACLU).

[We have here the old case of "he couldn't have used this word because it doesn't line up with the thought that we think he is trying to get across" (see 1:16 and comments). The phenomenon is not "phenomenal"; it is SOP with all twentieth-century destructive critics. ]{.class_3527}["WRATH"]{.class_3528}[ was a reference to the ]{.class_3527}[petitioner's attitude toward his fellow men,]{.class_3526}[ and ]{.class_3527}["DOUBTING"]{.class_3528}[ was his attitude ]{.class_3527}[toward the God to Whom he was praying. ]{.class_3526}[But the great, new, "twentieth-century" mind with "access to older and better manuscripts" and a "wealth of material due to archaeological light"---blah!, blah!, blah!---decided that both words had to refer to HUMANISM: the petitioner's attitude towards humans (hence the change).]{.class_3527}

"[In like manner"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). Like what? Like verse 8: they are to adorn themselves as the petitioner was adorned in verse 8.

"[HOLY hands"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). With the right heart attitude.

"[MODEST apparel"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). This defines once and forever all matters of "dress" for the Christian woman. ["MODEST"]{.class_3529} is that which does not attract attention because the greatest temptation any woman ever had on this earth was to attract attention. Modern dress is for that purpose. Any fashion show or "Miss America Contest" will reveal at once what is going on: the women are splitting their corsets to try to get a man to look at them. No woman was ever too poor as to not own a mirror.

[This means that you cannot legislate ]{.class_3527}[spirituality]{.class_3526}[, although you can enforce "standards," ]{.class_3527}[if you can get free of federal control.]{.class_3526}[ (When there is ]{.class_3527}[federal control]{.class_3526}[, you can't have any moral standards.) But if you make the ladies all wear dresses so as not to violate Deuteronomy 22:5, you will discover that they will wear skirts with the hemline somewhere between their thigh and their waist. Having regulated this to "two inches below the knee," the skirts now will be so thin a mosquitoe could fly through without breaking a wing. Having legislated thick material (so parents will think your school is spiritual and send you tuition!), the skirt now sticks as close to her thighs as a scuba diver's outfit. And when you reach the ultimate---knee-length, thick, loose skirts and neck-collar blouses with no makeup, three-quarter-length sleeves, with no braided hair---we find all the dear ladies wearing RED SHOES with four inch heels and sporting a FLOWER as big as a grapefruit.]{.class_3527}

Don't kid me! I've preached in "youth camps" for thirty-eight years.

[I've seen the youth director insist on no "pedal pushers," no "matador britches," no "blue jeans," etc., only to see his wife turn over in a canoe with her dress flying over ]{.class_3527}[her head ]{.class_3526}[while all of the teenagers were laughing ]{.class_3527}[theirs ]{.class_3526}[off. Nothing on earth could be more ]{.class_3527}[IMMODEST ]{.class_3526}[than teenage girls playing softball or volleyball in LOOSE, knee-length dresses. ]{.class_3527}[You cannot legislate spirituality. ]{.class_3526}[Young people are spiritual or they are not. Some of the coldest, deadest, driest meetings I have ever had in my life have been with Christian school groups in their "teens." They sat there like rebellious, condemned prisoners and never gave out with a holy grunt for forty-five minutes. I have seen more spiritual responses from a chain gang or a road gang. The "teens" were DRESSED right, and their hair was cut right; they were just DEMON POSSESSED. They had no more use for Bible preaching than for a dead skunk.]{.class_3527}

"[Shamefacedness and sobriety"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). These are obviously "old English" or "archaic, Elizabethan expressions," to cite the modern, apostate fundamentalists. [Vincent (who doesn't accept one word of 1 Tim. as coming from Paul) assures us that ]{.class_3527}["shamefacedness"]{.class_3528}[ is a CORRUPTION of the Old English "shamefastness." (He cites Chaucer and then Shakespeare: ]{.class_3527}[Richard III,]{.class_3526}[ 1.4.) According to him, it was a word like "steadfast," "soothfast," "rootfast," "handfast," "bedfast."]{.class_3527}[ "Shamefaced"]{.class_3526}[ changes and DESTROYS the the original force of the word "which was bound or made fast by an honorable shame" (p. 223).]{.class_3527}

My, how charitable!

Shall we consult someone who has more sense than Vincent? Let's!

[It was "Lye" who SUPPOSED ]{.class_3527}["shamefacedness"]{.class_3528}[ was a corruption of "shamefastness" (Vol. 2, p. 70, Webster's original ]{.class_3527}[American Dictionary of the English Language,]{.class_3526}[ 1828). Shakespeare knew exactly what it meant when he said "Conscience is a blushing, SHAMEFACED spirit." The word meant "bashful," with extreme modesty. ]{.class_3527}[Dryden ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[Woolton ]{.class_3526}[both used it in this sense. It means a face that is not boldly and aggressively seeking to attract the attention of the opposite sex (as all faces in all modern women's and teenager's magazines are presented). It means a face that shows the owner is aware of woman's place and station in life (see vss. 11--15), and that she has nothing to be proud of in the flesh (see 1 Cor. 4:7).]{.class_3527}

[Every form of the root Greek word (σωφρων]{.class_3527}, [sophron) is connected with something that means "a sound mind," "a humble or modest mind," "temperate, staid, and chaste," "soundness and saneness of mind," etc. ]{.class_3527}["THE FEAR OF THE LORD IS THE BEGINNING OF WISDOM"]{.class_3528}[ (Prov. 9:10). God has given us ]{.class_3527}["a sound mind"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Tim. 1:7). The word "FACE" (]{.class_3527}["shameFACEedness"]{.class_3528}[) is correct, and "shame-FASTness" is incorrect.]{.class_3527}[ "Sobriety"]{.class_3528}[ is apparent. "Dressing up" before God is not a matter of [outward appearance ]{.class_3530}(see 1 Sam. 16:7); it is a matter of INNER CONDITION (see 1 Pet. 3:4) and outward conduct (1 Tim. 2:10).

We are not advocating "Bluebeard" tyrannizing the wife and beating up the children; we are not so "chauvinistic" (news media cliché in the 1970s) that we believe the wife is to suffer lockjaw in silence and never put her "two cent's worth" in. Opinions and suggestions are needed from both sides, but DECISIONS are male, and the male is responsible for the decision (1 Cor. 11:3, 7--8). Adam was responsible for the decision he made. When it was made, the Lord told the woman that from then on he would ]{.class_3527}["RULE"]{.class_3528}[ over her (Gen. 3:16).
