CHAPTER FOUR

4:1 Now the Spirit speaketh expressly, that in the latter times some shall depart from the faith, giving heed to seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils;

2 Speaking lies in hypocrisy; having their conscience seared with a hot iron;

3 Forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats, which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving of them which believe and know the truth.

4 For every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused, if it be received with thanksgiving:

5 For it is sanctified by the word of God and prayer.

6 If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things, thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ, nourished up in the words of faith and of good doctrine, whereunto thou hast attained.

[The passage is "hate literature" from the first word to the last. It is aimed directly at the ]{.class_3527}[Roman Catholic hierarchy ]{.class_3526}[and those gullible enough to follow it. The reference to celibacy (vs. 3) and fish eating (vs. 3) are unmistakeable, and for those with more spiritual discernment, the references to the "seared conscience" (bingo, beer, and wine with no Bible studies in Protestant churches) and the ]{.class_3527}["doctrines of devils"]{.class_3528}[ (vss. 1--2) are unmistakeable earmarks. No one but a devil would teach that a Jewish woman was omnipresent and sinless. No one but a devil would teach that she was a mediator between God and man. No one but a devil would allow people in the twentieth century to call him "Holy Father," and no one but a devil would teach that Christ's blood was not a complete, finished payment for sin the day it was shed, or that Christians had to burn awhile in "purgatory" before getting clean. This is the work of ]{.class_3527}["seducing spirits"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 1), exactly as the text states.]{.class_3527}

"[Now the Spirit speaketh expressly"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). That is, in plain words that anyone can understand, in contrast to the scholarly fog of 1 Corinthians 2:4 and the flattering opportunism of Romans 16:17--19.

"[That in the LATTER TIMES"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). You are in them. These ARE the ["last days"]{.class_3529} of 2 Timothy 3:1--7. Paul did not use the expression ["last DAYS"]{.class_3529} here (as in 2 Tim. 3) because the things he is about to mention [do not wait until the Laodicean period of church history. ]{.class_3530}The[ "latter times,"]{.class_3529} here, is [anytime ]{.class_3530}AFTER A.D.100., [anytime ]{.class_3530}after the New Testament is completed (A.D. 90).

"[Depart from the faith"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). This is the faith as a body of revealed truth, as the word is used in Titus 1:1, 4; 3:15; Colossians 1:23; and Philippians 1:27.

"[Seducing spirits, and doctrines of devils"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). The seduction is 4,000 years old. It is carried out by ["beguiling"]{.class_3529} (see 2 Cor. 11:1--3) ["unstable souls"]{.class_3529} (2 Pet. 2:14). In Romanism, it simply means convincing a sinner that he needs the CHURCH to be saved, and as long as he obeys THE CHURCH (see comments on "THE Church" under 1 Tim. 3:15), he is safe. This indirectly substitutes "the church" for the One to be obeyed: [God. ]{.class_3530}The Catholic literature (official literature bearing the imprimatur of the hierarchy) states that obedience to the hierarchy is the same or SUPERIOR (!) to obeying God (Leo XIII, [Great Encyclical Letters, ]{.class_3530}Benzinger Brothers, New York, p. 193). This makes salvation conditional on a [religious whore,]{.class_3530} in the case of the Catholic hierarchy. Luther understood this perfectly and stated it clearly. ["Doctrines of devils"]{.class_3529} are as follows:

[1. That Mary, ]{.class_3527}[as Christ,]{.class_3526}[ was born sinless.]{.class_3527}

[2. That Mary, ]{.class_3527}[as Christ,]{.class_3526}[ lived sinless.]{.class_3527}

[3. That Mary, ]{.class_3527}[as Christ,]{.class_3526}[ has a part in the believer's salvation.]{.class_3527}

[4. That Mary, ]{.class_3527}[as Christ,]{.class_3526}[ beat the resurrection in getting up to heaven.]{.class_3527}

[5. That Mary, ]{.class_3527}[as Christ,]{.class_3526}[ can be prayed to.]{.class_3527}

[6. That Mary, ]{.class_3527}[as Christ,]{.class_3526}[ is an intermediator between God and man.]{.class_3527}

7. That no man can be a priest if he is married.

8. That babies are regenerated by pouring water on them, etc., etc., etc.

One devil a minute.

"[Speaking lies in hypocrisy" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 2). "We tolerate all religions." "The Pope is the Vicar of Christ." "We are for human rights." "We are anti-Communist." "We fought Hitler." "We were really for the Jews." "We have been against slavery from the beginning." "The mass is not a repeated sacrifice, but a continued celebration," etc., etc., etc.

"[HAVING THEIR CONSCIENCE SEARED WITH A HOT IRON"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). The nerve endings are dead. They have no conscience. Like their forefathers who murdered the prophets (Matt. 23:29--35), they only have a conscience about entering the wrong buildings (John 18:28) while engaged in MURDER (Acts 7:52). Their conscience is dead; it was delivered to a priest. [The "Church" is their conscience:]{.class_3530} a bloody whore on seven hills. (See exact documented proof for the choice of EACH word just written by reading [The History of the New Testament Church]{.class_3530}, Vol. II, pp. 220--233).

[Don't you know that Laodicean Fundamentalists are going to be upset with those two verses? Do you think that Kuenen, Wiesinger, Plummer, Koelling, Trench, Alford, Vincent, Wemp, the ]{.class_3527}[ASV, NASV]{.class_3526}[, and the ]{.class_3527}[NIV]{.class_3526}[, along with the Catholic hierarchy, are going to take THAT lying down? Don't you know!]{.class_3527}

[Vincent: It shouldn't be ]{.class_3527}["doctrines of devils,"]{.class_3528}[ but "teaching of demons" (p. 244); ]{.class_3527}["speaking lies in hypocrisy"]{.class_3528}[ is WRONG, and ]{.class_3527}["with a hot iron"]{.class_3528}[ is "superfluous" (p. 245). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown: It shouldn't be ]{.class_3527}["doctrines of devils,"]{.class_3528}[ but "teaching suggested by demons." We should "rather translate through the hypocrisy of the lying speaker" (p. 1363). YOU MEAN LIKE THE LAST GANG OF "FUNDAMENTALISTS" WE STUDIED IN 1 TIMOTHY 3:16? It should be "having their OWN conscience seared." It should be "who have branded with consciousness of guilt" (Alford, DeWitte, Weiss, and Huther). It should be "deceiving spirits" (not ]{.class_3527}["seducing spirits"]{.class_3528}[), according to the ]{.class_3527}[New "King James" Bible]{.class_3526}[. It should be "through plausible liars" (vs. 2), according to the Catholic ]{.class_3527}[New American Bible;]{.class_3526}[ and the ]{.class_3527}[NASV, ASV,]{.class_3526}[ and the ]{.class_3527}[RSV ]{.class_3526}[(twin sisters or triplets) who all get rid of the ]{.class_3527}["devils" ]{.class_3528}[(vs. 1) and refuse to translate the Greek (any Greek, in ]{.class_3527}[any ]{.class_3526}[edition).]{.class_3527}

Having a little problem there, are we, kiddies?

[No Bible believer in 370 years had any trouble with the text as it stood in ANY edition of the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version.]{.class_3526}[ From where did all of these problems suddenly come? How did they arise out of nowhere? Easy: THE NEED OF MONEY IS THE RESULT OF ]{.class_3527}["THE ROOT OF ALL EVIL"]{.class_3528}[ (see 1 Tim. 6:10 and coments).]{.class_3527}

"[Forbidding to marry, and commanding to abstain from meats"]{.class_3528} (vs. 3). All of the commentators, except Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, hasten to assure us that the warning should be put on the shelf back in the A.D. 65 cupboard as applying to "ascetic gnostics" of the first and second centuries. Williams sneaks out the fire escape with: "IN ALL AGES men have sought a spurious spirituality" (p. 956). Sumner Wemp (Lynchburg, Falwell, etc.) allows that "forced celibacy is not scriptural" (p. 636), but is extremely careful not to say WHO recommends this, practices it, allows it, or commands it. And in case you were tempted to think it was your nearest Catholic parish priest, Wemp prefaces it with "The Essenes OF THE FIRST CENTURY felt marriage was only necessary for\...."

Isn't it amazing? Isn't it beautiful? Did you ever in your life see such careful, delicate, subtle (Gen. 3:1), crafty, cautious PLANNING in the outlay of words? Only a pope could compete with Liberty University.

Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown almost run their machine out on to the interstate. They say, "As these Judaizing gnostics combined the harlot or apostate Old Testament church\...so ROME'S JUDAIZING ELEMENTS shall ultimately be combined with the open, worldly-wise anti-Christianity of the false prophet or beast" (op cit, p. 1363). We shall be more specific:

1. Catfish Friday and Tennessee Saturday Night (plus "Sunday, Monday, or Always," etc.) are violations not only of 1 Timothy 4:1--3, but also Colossians 2:16--17 and Romans 14:1--12.

2. Adolph Hitler, as a good Catholic, not only abstained from meats on Friday, but from meats on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Donnerstag, Samstag, and Sonntag as well. He was a vegetarian, like Jean Dixon.

[3. ]{.class_3527}["Marriage is honourable in all"]{.class_3528}[ (Heb. 13:4), and especially so among pastors and bishops (see 1 Tim. 3:2 and comments). Forbidding anyone to marry on any grounds but those given in 2 Corinthians 6:14--16 is demoniac. "Under age" would come under Romans 13:1--5, and "annulments" would be a matter of personal choice and preference or parental responsibility; but all Catholics are told that JOSEPH WAS\
A PERPETUAL VIRGIN, EVEN THOUGH MARRIED, AND MARY WAS A PERPETUAL VIRGIN, THOUGH MARRIED, and that therefore any priest serving MARY should be a perpetual virgin---]{.class_3527}[CLERICAL CELIBACY.]{.class_3526}[ The complete history, with all of the details documented, is the work by Henry C. Lea, published in 1932, titled ]{.class_3527}[The History of Sacerdotal Celibacy in the Christian Church]{.class_3526}[ (Watts and Co., London). The bachelor "virgins" are the fish eaters. We know where they get their "doctrines," and they are certainly not from the "early Christians" (A.D. 33--90); they are from the "late apostates" (A.D. 120--500).]{.class_3527}

"[Which God hath created to be received with thanksgiving\...every creature of God is good, and nothing to be refused"]{.class_3528} (vss. 3--4). My dear friend and fellow Bible believer, [Lester Roloff]{.class_3530} (1914--1982), always had a terrible time with the passage. (But then, who [doesn't ]{.class_3530}have a terrible time with some passages? Boy, have I sweated out 1 Pet. 3:8 and Eph. 5:4!) Roloff figured there had to be a good reason behind the dietary laws of Leviticus 11, so pork chops, barbecued ribs, bacon, and sausage disappeared from his menu. He also took Leviticus 11:9--11 to heart; and there went the shrimp, lobster, clams, oysters, scallops, and crabs. Since "bodily damage" to the "temple of the Holy Ghost" was the point at issue, the caffeine and alcohol had to go, and with them went "sody pop," soft drinks, fizz water, iced tea, milk, and coffee. I made the mistake one time of showing up in the cafeteria at the Bethesda Home for Girls early in the morning (I was holding a Bible conference there) and asking for an "eye opener" (a cup of "Java"). Mrs. Barnwell smiled, and said, "Oh, Brother Ruckman, we never have coffee around here!" I had known it; getting up early I had forgotten it.

[Now, I will not throw a stone at Brother Roloff. Diet, for him, kept him in remarkably good health. He learned from experience what he could eat and what he couldn't eat. So have I. I have learned what Peter learned in Acts 10:5--15, and when God sanctifies my food through ]{.class_3527}["the word of God and prayer"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 5), I can eat it. I used to eat out with Brother Roloff when we shared the same platform in a meeting, and I would usually order a barbequed pork sandwich and load it down with onions, garlic, pickle, and Tabasco sauce just to see what he would say while he was eating his salad without salt, pepper, or dressing. But he was a good scout; he never said anything.]{.class_3527}

[(Perhaps one of these days my guts will fall out, and I will have to go to Pablum or something else; I don't kid myself about such matters. But I honestly do not see how anyone named ]{.class_3527}[Ruckman,]{.class_3526}[ whose grandmother's name was ]{.class_3527}[Fullenweiter]{.class_3526}[, could get by without KNACKWURST, BRATWURST, WEISSWURST, and POLISH SAUSAGE. Sausage, cheese, and black bread are the staff of life!)]{.class_3527}

[I have learned a few things, however, about ]{.class_3527}["nothing to be refused."]{.class_3528}[ I have to refuse WHITE PORK ROAST. I cannot take flexible bacon that you can wrap around your finger, and too much fish without crackers, grits, rice, or bread will cause some trouble. I cannot drink tea after 4:00 in the afternoon, and two cups of coffee before 10 a.m. is all I can handle for twenty-four hours. Hot rice and cabbage will knock me out like a light: I will drop in my tracks just like someone hit me with a club. Otherwise, there is no problem. God marvelously blessed me with something like a cast-iron gut which can take vinegar, garlic, onions, Tabasco sauce, jalapeno peppers, and hot chili at the same sitting and never burp. I have bowed my head over 10,000 empty plates from Austria to Japan and from Canada to the Philippines and have seen God fill it up with something good to eat (Acts 14:17).]{.class_3527}

[Thanksgiving and prayer "sanctify" the fare according to 1 Timothy 4:4--6. I will never forget the "grace" (down South, they call it "askin' the blessin"') my Episcopal father used to intone before each meal: "Lord, help us to be truly thankful for what we are about to receive." Somehow or another that has always seemed to me to be one of the most OMINOUS REQUESTS a man ever made. Who can tell what a day may ]{.class_3527}["bring forth"]{.class_3528}[ (Prov. 27:1; Eccl. 11:2)?]{.class_3527}

"[If thou put the brethren in remembrance of these things"]{.class_3528} (v. 6). So I just did. ["Thou shalt be a good minister of Jesus Christ"]{.class_3529} (vs. 6). The things discussed in regards to eating were said to be ["the words of faith"]{.class_3529} and ["good doctrine," ]{.class_3529}so I never dictate to anyone what to eat or drink. If you can ask God to bless it before you put it in your mouth (try that on the next cigarette you light up!), you can put it in. Personally, I can eat about anything as long as it doesn't move before I stick it. I drink about two Cokes a year, about ten Sprites a year, and about five Root Beers a year. The rest of the time it is iced tea, milk, white grape juice, red grape juice, cranberry juice, apple juice, and unsweetened grapefruit juice.

My favorite meal is Polish sausage with Mexican rice and guacamole nachos. My second favorite is German sausage on black bread with Swiss cheese melted on the bread, and my third is fresh-fried spots, mullet, or trout and fried okra with pepper sauce and yellow rice seasoned with soy sauce and Tabasco sauce! Ah, beloved! Let us get on with the exposition before I have to wipe my mouth with my sleeve.

4:7 But refuse profane and old wives' fables, and exercise thyself rather unto godliness.

8 For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.

9 This is a faithful saying and worthy of all acceptation.

The rest of the chapter consists of practical tips and suggestions for a successful ministry. Among them is to be found advice given in regards to exercise (vs. 8), godliness (vs. 8), exercising authority though still young (vs. 12), how to spend your time (vs. 13), developing gifts that you already have (vs. 14), consecration and devotion to the ministry (vs. 15), and steadfastness and perseverance (vs. 16) in the will of God.

"[But refuse profane and old wives' fables"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). Notable among them are the "oracles" of [Fatima, Lourdes, ]{.class_3530}or some other demoniac freak, the fables of "old wife" Annie Besant ([Theosophy]{.class_3530}), old wife Mary Baker Patterson Eddy ([Christian Science]{.class_3530}), old wife Ellen White ([Seventh-day Adventism]{.class_3530}), old wife Emily Cady ([Unity]{.class_3530}), old wife Madam Blavatsky ([Theosophy]{.class_3530}), and old wife Jeanne Dixon ([Roman Catholicism]{.class_3530}).

"[For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto ALL things"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). Observe that bodily exercise is not being [prohibited;]{.class_3530} it is being recommended, but only as something that profits ["little."]{.class_3529} The [real ]{.class_3530}profit, the big profit, is in [godliness,]{.class_3530} for bodily exercise profits only in THIS LIFE. Godliness applies to that life ["which is to come"]{.class_3529} (vs. 8), as well as in this temporal life, here. ["Godliness"]{.class_3529} can keep you out of trouble; physical prowess can often get you into it. ["Godliness"]{.class_3529} causes you to sow to the spirit; physical exercise can cause you to sow to the flesh. The Lord ["taketh not pleasure in the legs of a man"]{.class_3529} (Psa. 147:10), so the strong man is not to glory in his strength (Jer. 9:23).

However, physical training and physical fitness are required of a Christian (1 Cor. 9:26--27) if he is able to take it. Paul was sick all of his life, but his activities (see 2 Cor. 11) do not show an infirm recluse reclining on a hospital bed. Paul is "up and at 'em" all of his life. With whatever infirmities he had (they required a practising physician with him most of his life: see Col. 4:14 and 2 Tim. 4:11), he carried on like a TRAINED ATHLETE (1 Cor. 9:24--27) and professed to be engaged in that kind of activity (see Heb. 12:1--3).

Preachers are notorious for eating too much food and not getting enough exercise. Heart attacks before fifty (and often before forty) are quite common among Bible-believing ministers; ulcers and colon trouble are also quite common. Dallas Billington suffered three heart attacks before one finished him off. Lester Roloff was falling apart before he was thirty and had to learn about diet and exercise to survive. Many of my ministerial friends have had heart surgery before they were sixty, and several have had real nervous breakdowns.

Among the most tragic cases was the case of Don Mangus, a dedicated, soul-winning, Bible-believing pastor in Louisville, Kentucky, who was taken with a disease which crippled his lips and tongue so that for awhile he could not preach. He had already been in the ministry nearly twenty-five years and had built one of the finest soulwinning works in the United States.

[Many times such troubles are unavoidable, but where they ]{.class_3527}[can ]{.class_3526}[be avoided the fault is usually at the dinner table or on the Spa floor. I do not know of any "cure-alls" for sicknesses that God sends, and I recognize absolutely, without any mental reservations, that good health is a gift from God which can be taken by God at any moment \[Job 1--2\]. Knowing this, I have tried to stay in some kind of "shape" through the years. At present \[sixty-seven years old\], I am still able to jog three miles, knock off 2000 "jumps" with a jump rope, swim half a mile, and enjoy playing racquetball, volleyball, softball, soccer, "blood" ball, ice hockey, and field hockey. My weight lifting is mighty puny, and I have long ago gotten out of a league with the twenty and thirty-year-olds when it comes to sit ups and push-ups.]{.class_3527}

My rules for health are simple, and they probably don't work for anyone but me. I eat two meals a day: breakfast and supper. On Sunday, I eat one meal at noon, and a snack at bedtime. No white sugar: I use saccarine or honey. No salt, except on salads or baked potatoes, and occasionally on green beans and spinach. Little or no soft drinks (Pepsi, Sprite, Dr. Pepper, Coca Cola, Seven Up, etc.). No cigarettes, no liquor, no dope, no aspirin, and no antibiotics, except after getting stitches for a chain saw cut, stingray puncture, nail in foot, foot torn open on cement, racquet cut over eye, hockey puck into eye, finger ripped open, etc. What good health I have, I owe to the grace of God.

Verse 9 is self-explanatory.

4:10 For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.

11 These things command and teach.

12 Let no man despise thy youth; but be thou an example of the believers, in word, in conversation, in charity, in spirit, in faith, in purity.

13 Till I come, give attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine.

14 Neglect not the gift that is in thee, which was given thee by prophecy, with the laying on of the hands of the presbytery.

15 Meditate upon these things; give thyself wholly to them; that thy profiting may appear to all.

16 Take heed unto thyself, and unto the doctrine; continue in them: for in doing this thou shalt both save thyself, and them that hear thee."

[These verses constitute practical instructions to a young pastor. Vincent's continual "word used only here," "never used by Paul," and "not found in the Pauline Epistles" (]{.class_3527}[Vincent's Word Studies]{.class_3526}[) begin to grate on our nerves. He repeats these expressions interminably as though they constituted some kind of proof that Paul could have not added some words to his vocabulary in twenty to thirty years. Such scholarly "high jinks" are not to be countenanced by a sober-minded junior high school student.]{.class_3527}

"[Because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of ALL MEN, specially of those that believe"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). The verse knocks Calvin's limited atonement and unconditional election into a cocked hat. Christ is not just the SAVIOUR of the "elect" or "His people" (see any "Hard-Shell Brazil Nut" Predestinarian on Matt. 1:21). The "TULIP" sniffers have all assumed that since 80 percent of what Paul writes to Christians speaks of Christ's death for THEM, that the matter [began ]{.class_3530}and [ended ]{.class_3530}there. This is nonsense. He is ["the Saviour of ALL MEN"]{.class_3529} (vs. 10), and the ["ALL"]{.class_3529} here (as the ["ALL"]{.class_3529} in 1 Tim. 2:4) is no more the "elect" than Orphan Annie's eye pupils. The "elect" are ["SPECIALLY OF THOSE THAT BELIEVE"]{.class_3529} (vs. 10).

[Here then, we have ATONEMENT AND APPLICATION clearly defined as two separate items. The atonement for ALL men is for ALL men, but it is ]{.class_3527}[applied ]{.class_3526}[to those who "believe." Calvin was wrong. Gill was wrong. Dabney was wrong. A.W. Pink was wrong. So were L. R. Shelton and his son and John Gilpin and Bob Ross and all of the "hardshells" in Texas, Arkansas, Tennessee, South and North Carolina, Kentucky, Alabama, Mississippi, and Georgia. Christ is the Mediator for]{.class_3527}[ "ALL" ]{.class_3528}[men (1 Tim. 2:5), He is the Saviour of ]{.class_3527}["ALL"]{.class_3528}[ men (text), and He is the Blood Atonement for ]{.class_3527}["ALL"]{.class_3528}[ men (John 1:9, and 29). ]{.class_3527}[Limited atonement ]{.class_3526}[is a philosophical myth. It is theological moonshine.]{.class_3527}

"[We both labour and suffer reproach, BECAUSE" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 10). The verse demonstrates that the world system has always been anti-God and pro-Satan (see 2 Cor. 4:4 and Gal. 1:4). Simply TRUSTING GOD will bring down the wrath of the heathen Roman populace who are trusting the pope, Caesar, the sacraments, Mary, water baptism, good works, the golden rule, humanism, their parents, the government, or their own righteousness. "Trusting God" means abandoning trust in self (Psa. 118:8) and taking God's righteousness (Isa. 64:6; Rom. 10:1--3) as a [replacement ]{.class_3530}for one's own righteousness (Phil. 3:3--7). This is the "offense of the cross" found in Galatians 6:12--16 which all "do gooders" avoid like the plague, and it explains why all "worldly," earthly, man-made systems of "salvation" are WORK SYSTEMS. That way, there is no "reproach" or "offense," for all self-righteous people will agree that a man has to be a "GOOD MAN" to get to heaven. The hallucination is worldwide and perennial in any civilization on any continent for 6,000 years.

"[Let no man despise thy youth"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). It is extremely difficult for a young man of say twenty to twenty-five to command the respect of a congregation of people in their forties, fifties, and sixties who have already "trodden the path" and have been through a dozen experiences that the "pastor" knows nothing about (see 2 Cor. 1:1--6, for example).

"[But be thou an example of the believers"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). This is like 1 Peter 5:3 commands the pastor to be. An ["example"]{.class_3529} is a "sample" of the real thing; hence, the word ["ENSAMPLE"]{.class_3529} in 1 Thessalonians 1:7, 2 Thessalonians 3:9, 1 Peter 5:3, and other places.

[1. ]{.class_3527}["IN WORD"]{.class_3528}[: speech, and especially when speaking of the Bible and Bible doctrines.]{.class_3527}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["IN CONVERSATION"]{.class_3528}[: ]{.class_3527}[manner of life,]{.class_3526}[ as in 1 Peter 3:2--4 and Galatians 1:13. The Old English is appropriate, for it emphasizes the doctrinal truth that ]{.class_3527}["OUT OF THE ABUNDANCE OF THE HEART THE MOUTH SPEAKETH"]{.class_3528}[ (Matt. 12:34), and CONDUCT is conditioned on what comes OUT of the mouth (Matt. 15:17--18): hence, ]{.class_3527}["conversation."]{.class_3528}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["IN CHARITY"]{.class_3528}[: willingness to forgive personal wrongs and insults, willingness to give an erring brother a second chance, willingness to go out of the way to help someone in need, compassion upon the unsaved, and above all, love for God and God's words.]{.class_3527}

[4. ]{.class_3527}["IN SPIRIT"]{.class_3528}[: in the attitude taken toward the ministry, the flock, persecution, trouble, success, and victory.]{.class_3527}

[5. ]{.class_3527}["IN FAITH"]{.class_3528}[: shown in waiting on God for answers to prayer, claiming in prayer what the Bible says is the "lot" or "inheritance" of the believer, claiming the promises found in the Bible, and trusting God in difficult situations.]{.class_3527}

[6. ]{.class_3527}["IN PURITY"]{.class_3528}[: hence, ]{.class_3527}["keep thyself pure"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Tim. 5:22). Avoid filth---moral, religious, and social---and never be as stupid as the faculty members of Columbia, Harvard, Yale, Priceton, Dartmouth, Chicago, and Berkeley who cannot see the difference between FILTHINESS (2 Cor. 7:1; 2 Pet. 2:7; Col. 3:8; Psa. 14:3; Rev. 22:11) and CLEANLINESS (James 4:8; 2 Cor. 7:1; Psa. 19:12). Those who cannot tell the difference between GOOD and EVIL (Isa. 5:20)---any pragmatist, logical positivist, psychiatrist, existentialist, phenomenologist, etc.---have to stay FILTHY, for they recognize "filth" only as manure, garbage, dung, offal, disease, infection, maggots, etc. Filthy ]{.class_3527}[TALK ]{.class_3526}[and filthy ]{.class_3527}[PICTURES ]{.class_3526}[are not in their vocabularies; they are simply in their "life-styles." If you don't know what "FILTH" is, walk by a newsstand. If you don't know what FILTH is, live in San Francisco for two years. If you don't know what FILTH is (in contrast to purity), take a trip to ROME or CAIRO. If you don't know what FILTH is, watch "Dallas," "Dynasty," "Hotel," or "Soap" for a month.]{.class_3527}

"[Give attendance to reading, to exhortation, to doctrine"]{.class_3528} (vs. 13). These are three essentials for the young minister. He is to spend time reading THE BOOK (Psa. 119; Deut. 11:18--21). In his "extra-biblical" reading, he would do well to read things like the [Sword of the Lord, Pulpit Helps, The Flaming Torch, Great Pulpit Masters;]{.class_3530} Jack Hyles' books on soul winning and church building; the works of David Otis Fuller and Edward Hills, Dean Burgon and Scrivener; the biographies and autobiographies of Wesley, Luther, Cartwright, Norris, Patton, Carey, Judson, Goforth, Livingston; and church histories by Moyer, Armitage, Robinson, Mosheim, Neander, Schaff, Kurtz, and others. For [dispensationalism:]{.class_3530} Stam, Bullinger, Baker, and O'Hare. For [prophecy:]{.class_3530} Clarence Larkin, Pember, Bullinger, and David L. Cooper. For [soul winning:]{.class_3530} Carl Hatch, Dr. Walter Wilson, Jack Hyles, and Hugh Pyle. For [fidelity to the Book:]{.class_3530} Cimino, Musselmann, Fuller, Hills, Ruckman, and Clarke. For [fidelity to the Receptus Greek text:]{.class_3530} Waite, Hodges, Miller, Burgon, Scrivener, and Pickering. For [prayer:]{.class_3530} R. A. Torrey, E. M. Bounds, E. L. Maxwell, and John R. Rice. For [personal holiness:]{.class_3530} Watchman Nee, Ruth Paxton, Andrew Murray, E. L. Maxwell, and others.

"[To exhortation"]{.class_3528} (vs. 13). Learn how to "call mourners." Learn how to press for a decision. Don't be content with a dry lecture and a prayer. STIR 'EM UP! [Upset 'em!]{.class_3530} "Afflict the comfortable, comfort the afflicted!" Peel the bark, shell the corn; dynamite, root, blast, stump, plow, and till. NEVER FOLLOW THE LAODICEAN EXAMPLES OF "CHUCK" SWINDOLL, RICHARD DeHANN, JOHN MacARTHUR, J. VERNON McGEE, BILL GOTHARD, ORAL ROBERTS, REX HUMBARD, AND "REV. GOODSKY." Those poor little amateur psychologists couldn't bust a butterfly's wing with a ten-pound sledge hammer. Go at it, like Maze Jackson and Carl Lackey. Put it on them like Oliver Green and Bobby Ware. [Learn how to PREACH, young man!]{.class_3530} Get out on the street and yell at them. "Harangue" them like your forefathers did (see [The History of the New Testament Church]{.class_3530}, Vol. I). ["LIFT UP THY VOICE LIKE A TRUMPET" (Isa. 58:1)! "CRY ALOUD, SPARE NOT" ]{.class_3529}(Isa. 58:1)!

"[To doctrine"]{.class_3528} (vs. 13). Get the Book "rightly divided"---even though that commandment has been [removed ]{.class_3530}from 2 Timothy 2:15 by Truman Dollar, A.V. Henderson, Criswell, and a dozen other apostate blanks (the [New "King James" Bible]{.class_3530} of 1984).

Learn the difference between the Kingdom of God and the Kingdom of Heaven; C. I. Scofield missed it.

Know the difference between church age salvation and millennial salvation; none of the faculty members at Tennessee Temple have any idea what it is. Know the difference between Old Testament salvation under the law and New Testament salvation under grace; none of the faculty members at Liberty University have any idea what it is. (Their teachers never "attended" to doctrine for a minute. All they taught was "historic positions" of "Fundamentalists.")

Know why Lincoln's "Emancipation Proclamation" was an ANTI-CHRISTIAN document from start to finish (1 Tim. 6:1--3)! No one in Washington D.C. has any idea why it was or why it wasn't.

Understand the spiritual circumcision of the soul from the body, inside the body. There is not one graduate of Dallas Theological Seminary or Denver Theological Seminary who has any more knowledge about it than a lost liberal in the Glide Memorial Church in San Francisco.

"[Neglect not the gift"]{.class_3528} (vs. 14). We are not told what this gift was, but it certainly had to do with the [pastorate]{.class_3530}. Some elder prophesied something about Timothy at his ordination.

"[The laying on of the hands of the presbytery"]{.class_3528} (vs. 14). This is the laying on of the elder's hands at ordination. The procedure will be found in Acts 14:23 and follows the ancient custom of "passing on authority" in transfer by laying hands on the heads of the "successor" (see the exact case in Deut. 34:9). Guilt was also "transferred" in this way (see Lev. 1:4, 4:15, et al.). There is a warning not to make this "transfer" too quickly; the man being ordained should first be "proved" (see 1 Tim. 3:10 and comments).

"[Meditate upon these things"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15): the GIFT, READING, EXHORTATION, and DOCTRINE. ["Give thyself wholly to them"]{.class_3529} (vs. 15). Be "ADDICTED" to them (cf. 1 Cor. 16:15): be a Christian "addict." After giving yourself wholly to God (see Rom. 12:1--4), give yourself wholly to God's work. That is the only order for any real pastor so that ["we will give ourselves continually to prayer, and to the ministry of the word"]{.class_3529} (Acts 6:4).

"[That thy profiting may appear to all"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15). Results and success are never to be the [aims ]{.class_3530}of the ministry, for all ministers will be tried for the QUALITY of their work (1 Cor. 3:13, ["of what SORT it is"]{.class_3529}). Paul's ["profiting"]{.class_3529} that "appears to all" at the end of his life, is a nameless grave, without leaving behind any school buses, any school, any sons or daughters, any real estate, any record Sunday School attendance, or any stocks and bonds. The ["profiting"]{.class_3529} here is in souls saved, lives changed, communities upset, wicked people rebuked, prayers answered, needs met, and converts witnessing. Paul does not have in mind a large Sunday School plant and several acres of parking lot, although these things [might ]{.class_3530}attend a man in the USA who gives himself ["wholly"]{.class_3529} to the ministry.

"[Take heed unto thyself"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16), and then ["unto the doctrine."]{.class_3529} The man [teaching the doctrine]{.class_3530} needs more attention than the [doctrine]{.class_3530}. The supercilious comment of Vincent on the commandment is a classic. He says that the important thing is taking care of your PERSONALITY (p. 254): "Personality goes before teaching." That's how Swindoll, MacArthur, Roberts, Humbard, Bob Jones III, et al., all look at it. The "personality-plus" kid. Smile, big boy! Shake hands. Measure your words. Vary the pitch and volume so they will think you mean what you say. Be helpful; "share." Share your shares as a sharer who shares his share with other sharers. Flash 'em a smile; you're on camera! Look pleasant. Don't "hurt the Christian testimony." Manifest the "sweet spirit of Christ" as you "share a verse of scripture" on your sharing time that you share with DUPES. Be a "caring person." Convince the dupes you aren't self-centered.

No, "personality" is the last thing on earth the Holy Spirit has in mind.

He has in mind:

a) Look out for bad women (Prov. 1, 5--7).

b) Look out for bad men (Prov. 1--3).

c) Pay attention to your inner thought life (Psa. 19:14).

d) Keeps your sins judged and confessed (1 Cor. 10--11).

e) Watch your temper (James 1:19).

f) Forgive quickly and don't harbor grudges (James 5:9; Eph. 4:32).

g) Make sure you spend your time in the Book and in prayer (Psa. 1; Jer. 17:5--8).

[h) ]{.class_3527}["Abstain from all APPEARANCE]{.class_3528}[ \[not found in ANY English Bible but the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version ]{.class_3526}[of 1611\] ]{.class_3527}[OF EVIL" ]{.class_3528}[(1 Thess. 5:22).]{.class_3527}

[That is what is meant by ]{.class_3527}["take heed unto THYSELF."]{.class_3528}[ Timothy is told that he will SAVE himself (vs. 16) by doing this. Observe that the word ]{.class_3527}["SAVE"]{.class_3528}[ here has nothing to do with getting out of hell and going to heaven anymore than the word ]{.class_3527}["SAVED"]{.class_3528}[ did in 2:15, which see. If Timothy continues in ]{.class_3527}["THEM"]{.class_3528}[ (i.e., taking care to stay right himself and taking care to keep his beliefs right), he will save ]{.class_3527}["thyself, and THEM THAT HEAR THEE"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 16).]{.class_3527}

[This admonition is to be taken with 1 Corinthians 9:26--27 and Romans 12:7 and like passages (1 Cor. 10:33, for example) to mean that there is such a thing as the saving of a minister's ministry (Col. 3:17) and the salvation of a congregation's spiritual health. SICK CONGREGATIONS are the hallmark of Laodicean Christianity. They are sick from head to foot (Isa. 1:6). The ]{.class_3527}[sermons ]{.class_3526}[are sick, the ]{.class_3527}[singing ]{.class_3526}[is sick, the ]{.class_3527}[announcements ]{.class_3526}[are sick, the ]{.class_3527}[preacher ]{.class_3526}[is sick, the ]{.class_3527}[congregation ]{.class_3526}[is sick, man, sick. "God bless!" "Something GOOOOD is going to happen to you today!" "God love ya!" "The man upstairs." "The sky pilot." "Blessalleuia!" "Halleluia to God!" "Baptized IN the Spirit." "Hastalashantieuntieabowtie," etc.]{.class_3527}

[The modern song writers are so infected with African evolutionism they cannot end a song because they have no ]{.class_3527}[goals ]{.class_3526}[or ]{.class_3527}[aims]{.class_3526}[. (Evolution teaches origins are ]{.class_3527}[accidental]{.class_3526}[, so their goals have to be accidental.) There are very few "codas" on the end of modern songs. The Christian codas that show up are rising, screaming, ear-busting, super-decibel roars that leave you applauding without knowing for what you are clapping, except the racket. Most of the secular tunes just "fade out." The recording studio "fades down the pot" so the tune doesn't end. The "contemporary" Christian music is hard acid rock or "cocktail lounge music" (for lounge lizards).]{.class_3527}

Polluted with African corruption, the writers have revolutionary new thoughts like: "I will praise the Lord because He saved me, I will praise the Lord because He saved me, I will praise the Lord because He saved me, I will praise the Lord\...." You will have to admit that this is a long way from: "Why should I wander an alien from Thee, and cry in the desert for bread? Thy foes will rejoice when my sorrows they see, and smile at the tears I have shed!" It's a long way from "Alleluia, Alleluia, Alleluia, Alleluia," etc. (repeat interminably) to "I need Thee every hour, Most Gracious Lord, No tender voice like Thine, Can peace afford!"

There is the old math and the new math.

And here we conclude 1 Timothy 4. The Greek and Hebrew scholars, as usual, have much to offer: egg shells, dead shrimp, and coffee grounds. Their tireless efforts to restore for us the "originals" by "older and better manuscripts" has contributed much to 1 Timothy 4: mainly cockle burrs and punctured balloons.

[Williams ]{.class_3526}[(p. 956) must have never walked a half a mile in his life, for he states that the ]{.class_3527}["BODILY EXERCISE"]{.class_3528}[ of verse 8 is a reference to not being married (!) and being a vegetarian (!). Exactly how one arrives at such a profound insight into the "spiritual depths" of the word of God is a mystery greater than Ephesians 5:32. He undoubtedly used a more "readable" translation than the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version ]{.class_3526}[of 1611.]{.class_3527}

[The Wycliffe Bible Commentatry]{.class_3526}[ (p. 1377) had the same trouble with "FILTH" that we mentioned in our comments on verse 12. The audacious and undaunted "soldier of the faith" tells us that ]{.class_3527}["PURITY"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 12) doesn't mean "PURITY"; it means "careful observance of religious duties." That is the same CONSTRUCTION put upon the word BY THE FILTHIEST MURDERERS WHO HAVE EVER LIVED (see Mark 3:6; Matt. 23:1--25; and Acts 7:54--59 for exact documentation). Those killers thought that ]{.class_3527}[religious observance ]{.class_3526}[(Mark 2:24) was ]{.class_3527}[PURITY]{.class_3526}[.]{.class_3527}

(What would we do without all of these "good, godly, dedicated" conservative scholars to help us out?)

[Following ]{.class_3527}[Vincent's]{.class_3526}[ unfaithful and unbelieving scholarship on verse 8, we are to read "for a little" as meaning "for a little time" (Vincent, p. 249), which turned out to be only a suggestion. Vincent had to settle for the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version ]{.class_3526}[reading of ]{.class_3527}["little,"]{.class_3528}[ meaning the exercise extends only "to a few things." Nonetheless, the ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[of 1901 inserts the conjecture. The grossly corrupt ]{.class_3527}[NASV ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[NIV ]{.class_3526}[say "of some value," which, of course, is a PARAPHRASE. The word "some" is not in any Greek text. All of the Greek texts read "ολιγον" (oligon), which any student of Greek knows means "little," "small in number," "short," or "briefly." The Greek sentence said, "For bodily gymnastics toward a little, it is profitable." But this is one of those cases of "DYNAMIC EQUIVALENCE!" (Fanfare! Star clusters! Thirty-one gun salute!) instead of LITERAL EQUIVALENCE. Since it is, we shall discard the ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[and the ]{.class_3527}[NASV ]{.class_3526}[with the ]{.class_3527}[NIV ]{.class_3526}[and the "Dead Dodo" ("Bodily exercise is all right") and take the text: ]{.class_3527}["BODILY EXERCISE PROFITETH LITTLE"]{.class_3528}[ (]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version]{.class_3526}[).]{.class_3527}

"[Reading"]{.class_3528} has "got to go," according to the best pea brains of the twentieth century. You see, "the Greek" (Ah yes, buster, have we ever busted your balloon before!) says "αvαγνωσει" (anagnosei), and this HAS TO BE "the public reading of the scriptures" (Vincent, p. 251; Wycliffe, p. 1377; [The Expositor's Greek Testament,]{.class_3530} p. 126: the [NIV, RSV,]{.class_3530} and [NASV]{.class_3530}). Since the word "public" is not in any Greek text, the [NASV]{.class_3530}, [NIV]{.class_3530}, and [RSV ]{.class_3530}do not hesitate to stick in BOTH words after complaining about the two thieves who ["CAST THE SAME IN HIS TEETH" ]{.class_3529}(Matt. 27:44) in the [Authorized Version]{.class_3530}. But as we have said on at least a dozen other occasions, hypocrites these days are born as well as made. [Sumner Wemp ]{.class_3530}(Liberty University) goes with the apostates ([Liberty Bible Commentary,]{.class_3530} p. 636). We would expect him to; that is how to qualify as a "recognized scholar."

Finally, nearly all of the apostate Fundamentalists link the last verse in the chapter with the first two verses in the chapter and decide that to "SAVE oneself" means simply to save oneself from false doctrines taught by devils and seducing spirits. We assume that any man who would take that position (see Sumner Wemp, p. 637) must not have a ministry worth saving. That would constitute about one-tenth of "saving" a minister or a congregation in the sense of the text.

Sermon material is abundant.

A. THE MARKS OF ROMANISM

1\. Clerical celibacy (vs. 3).

2\. Regulated diet (vs. 3).

3\. Doctrines of devils (vs. 1).

4\. Seducing spirits (vs. 1).

5\. Pathological lying (vs. 2).

6\. Dead conscience (vs. 2).

7\. Total inability to grasp the truth (vs. 3).

B. ACCEPTABLE FOOD AND CONDUCT

1. Anything you can pray about before doing it (vs. 5).

2. Anything you can thank God for before doing it (vs. 4).

3. Anything that lines up with the word of God (vs. 5).

4. Anything God will clean (vs. 5).

C. TWO TYPES OF EXERCISE

1\. Bodily exercise:

a. Keeps the major organs functioning properly.

b. Rests the mind and relaxes the nerves.

c. Adds years to the physical life.

d. Aids digestion and elimination.

e. Tends to stop depression and discouragement.

2\. Spiritual exercise:

a. Keeps the individual from sin.

b. Strengthens the "inner man" in the faith.

c. Enables prayers to be answered.

d. Keeps the individual in fellowship with God.

e. Lays up treasure in heaven.

f. Guarantees a millennial inheritance.

D. THE SAVIOUR OF ALL MEN

1. He is the Saviour of all men in that He was a man Himself.

2. He is the Saviour of all men in that He lived, suffered, and died as all men do.

3. He is the Saviour of all men in that He died for all men and bore their sins.

4. He is the Saviour of all men in that He is willing to save ANY of them.

E. TAKE HEED UNTO THYSELF

1\. To thy friends.

2\. To thy family.

3\. To thy ministry.

4\. To thy thoughts.

5\. To thy works.

F. TAKE HEED UNTO THE DOCTRINE

1. The doctrine of Salvation.

2. The doctrine of Eternal Security.

3. The doctrine of Premillennialism.

4. The doctrine of Good Works.

5. The doctrine of Heaven.

6. The doctrine of Hell.

G. THREE VALUABLE OCCUPATIONS

1\. Reading:

a. The right kind of books.

b\. With the right motive.

c\. With the right purpose.

2\. Exhortation:

a\. To the lost.

b\. To the saved.

c\. To oneself.

d\. To backsliders.

3\. Doctrine:

a. Those things that are similar.

b. Those things that differ.

c. Those things that are certain.

d. Those things that are conjectural.

[]{#sYORfekySpmggT0K0tcNCA822.xhtml}

CHAPTER FIVE

5:1 Rebuke not an elder, but intreat him as a father; and the younger men as brethren;

2 The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, with all purity.

3 Honour widows that are widows indeed.

4 But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents: for that is good and acceptable before God.

5 Now she that is a widow indeed, and desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications and prayers night and day.

6 But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth.

7 And these things give in charge, that they may be blameless.

The instructions apply to those in the local church. Here, specifically, the proper manner of treating elderly men and women---especially WIDOWS---and younger men and women.

"[Rebuke not an elder, but intreat him as a father"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). This is a New Testament corollary to Leviticus 19:32 in the Old Testament and simply states that when dealing with an older man who disagrees with you, or who "crosses" your way of thinking or of doing things, that you are not to rebuke him as you would someone your own age or younger. He is to be "intreated," which is a supplication or a request for reconciliation, not a demand. The word is used in this fashion in 1 Corinthians 4:13.

"[And the younger men as brethren"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1), since they are "brothers in Christ" (Heb. 2:13) and brothers TO Christ (see Heb. 2:12 and Luke 8:21).

"[The elder women as mothers; the younger as sisters, with all purity"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). The idea is that since incest is sinful (see Amnon's case in 2 Sam. 13), the younger women should be treated as sisters; meaning there will be no danger of fornication, "sexual harrasment," or "suchlike" going on. One of the greatest solutions for the sex problems between older men (ages 35--65) and younger women (ages 15--30) is for the man to look at the woman as his DAUGHTER and figure how he would like an older man to treat her if she were his daughter. In the text, the men are to treat the younger women as[ "sisters"]{.class_3529} and remind themselves that anything they would not do to their own sister should not be practiced with the ["younger"]{.class_3529} women.

"[Honour widows that are widows INDEED"]{.class_3528} (vs. 3). The[ "indeed" ]{.class_3529}emphasizes the fact that there are some[ "widows"]{.class_3529} who are not real widows (see Rom. 7:1--4 for an illustration). A widow ["indeed"]{.class_3529} is about to be defined.

"[But if any widow have children or nephews, let them learn first to shew piety at home, and to requite their parents" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 4). Paul is about to instruct the local church in undertaking the support of widows, but this support is not due to a widow unless she is absolutely alone. If she has relatives who can take care of her (especially CHILDREN; see the text), their job is to take care of her and not dump her off on the church payroll.[ "Nephews"]{.class_3529} is supposed to be an obsolete term derived from the Latin "neros." A "nephew," in the Bible, can be a [grandchild]{.class_3530}, for that is the English word's root. There is another reason for it, and that is that the UNCLE was required to raise up children to the ["widow"]{.class_3529} if her husband died (Deut. 25:6--9). Many times, the SONS of an UNCLE are the nephews of the widow. (Observe how the English gives "light on the text" not found in the Greek.)

"[And to requite their parents"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4). Pay them back for the care they lavished on them when they were helpless. Any strong young man or woman should remember there was a time when they couldn't dress or feed themselves and had to have slobbered food wiped off their mouths, and other things, too!

"[Desolate, trusteth in God, and continueth in supplications"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5). The illustration the Bible gives is [Anna ]{.class_3530}(Luke 2:36). She trusts God because there is no "bread winner" around to trust. In her single condition, she is ["happier"]{.class_3529} (according to 1 Cor. 7:40) than she would be in taking on another husband, but this is certainly PERMISSIBLE (note 1 Cor. 7:7--39).

[Once the "holiness" factor appears in regard to the widows, the pro-Catholic commentators reach out and grasp the straw in the wind and add to the deaconesses THE ORDAINED "ELDERESSES." This would make an ordained elderly woman ]{.class_3527}[a candidate for a bishop's office.]{.class_3526}[ No wonder Rome had a female POPE (see ]{.class_3527}[The History of the New Testament Church]{.class_3526}[, Ruckman, Vol. 1, p. 295)! "These seem to have been WOMEN ELDERS rather than WOMEN DEACONS or DEACONESSES\...this would involve house to house visitation\...such conditions seem clearly those for MINISTRY rather than maintenance" (]{.class_3527}[Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_3526}[, p. 1174).]{.class_3527}

Apostasy is no respecter of persons.

"[But she that liveth in pleasure is dead while she liveth" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 6). For example: Grace Kelly, Rita Hayworth, Brooke Shields, Dolly Parton, Farah Fawcett, Priscilla Presley, Jean Harlow, Jackie Kennedy, Miss Universe, et al. Like Liz Taylor, she is "outwardly splendid as of old, inwardly lifeless, dead and cold; her power and warmth all dead and gone, like the dead moon she still shines on!" The graceful movements in the gorgeous gown are the dance of death. The gilded body is a casket for a putrifying heart. The hairdo, the polished nails, the frantic laughter, and the nervous smiling are the spasms of a headless chicken: [she's dead.]{.class_3530} Poor Marilyn Monroe, with the world at her feet and Hollywood at her doorstep, could not find a reason to stay alive; neither could Judy Garland. Rock Hudson---"a man's man"---turned out to be a "man's man"; he just didn't look like Liberace and Bill Tilden. Lauded by the world, bemoaned by the press, deified by the news media, and worshipped by fornicators and perverts, he died miserably, alone in the world, without hope, and without God. But that was nothing new in 1611: HE WAS [DEAD ]{.class_3530}(Eph. 2:1--5) ALL THE TIME HE LIVED.

The immediate reference here (in the text) is to the "Merry Widow," the widow who takes advantage of her "newly found liberation," gathering up the insurance money while having a "ball" with as many men as possible in the shortest time.

"[And these things give in charge, that they may be blameless"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). The ["THEY"]{.class_3529} would be a reference to the Christian families of verse 4 who were charged with caring for their widows. This is apparent in what follows.

5:8 But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel.

9 Let not a widow be taken into the number under threescore years old, having been the wife of one man,

10 Well reported of for good works; if she have brought up children, if she have lodged strangers, if she have washed the saints' feet, if she have relieved the afflicted, if she have diligently followed every good work.

11 But the younger widows refuse: for when they have begun to wax wanton against Christ, they will marry;

12 Having damnation, because they have cast off their first faith.

13 And withal they learn to be idle, wandering about from house to house; and not only idle, but tattlers also and busybodies, speaking things which they ought not.

14 I will therefore that the younger women marry, bear children, guide the house, give none occasion to the adversary to speak reproachfully.

15 For some are already turned aside after Satan.

16 If any man or woman that believeth have widows, let them relieve them, and let not the church be charged; that it may relieve them that are widows indeed.

[There are ]{.class_3527}[seven ]{.class_3526}[qualifications given to apply to a widow before the church undertakes to support her financially.]{.class_3527}

1. She is to be at least sixty years of age (vs. 9).

2. She is to have been faithful to her husband (vs. 9).

3. She is to have a good reputation (vs. 10).

[4. She is to ]{.class_3527}["have brought up children"]{.class_3528}[ (her own or others) well (vs. 10).]{.class_3527}

5. She is to have shown hospitality to strangers (vs. 10).

[6. She is to ]{.class_3527}["have washed the saints' feet"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 10).]{.class_3527}

7. She is to have been fruitful in various good works (vs. 10).

[This is the reference to ]{.class_3527}["WIDOWS INDEED"]{.class_3528}[ of verse 3.]{.class_3527}

"[But if any provide not for his own\...he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). He is not an infidel; he is not lost. He is ["worse"]{.class_3529} in the sense that he is worse in matters of [responsibility ]{.class_3530}and [common decency;]{.class_3530} most unsaved men will make an effort to provide for their families. ["HIS OWN"]{.class_3529} in this clause are his own widows. A nephew, son, or grandson should take care of his ["own"]{.class_3529} widows in his own family. ["Those of his own house"]{.class_3529} (vs. 8) would be a direct relative, such as a widowed mother or a widowed daughter.

"[Let not a widow be taken into the number"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). Into the number of widows that the church is going to support (see Acts 6:1--4 for an illustration). We have listed the items in verse 10 above. The foot washing here is obviously not a church ordinance and has nothing to do with the local church assemblies; it has to do with [personal ministries ]{.class_3530}to travelers who stop by for a meal (see Gen. 19:2 for an illustration). It is apparent that walking barefoot or with sandals will produce a pair of dirty, dusty feet in a few hours.

"[But the younger widows refuse\...have begun to wax wanton against Christ\...Having damnation, because they have cast off their first faith"]{.class_3528} (vss. 11--12). Observe again (for the third time!) how such words as "saved," "faith," and ["damnation"]{.class_3529} are not always highway markers to salvation from death and hell. The word ["damnation"]{.class_3529} has [two ]{.class_3530}applications in the Bible. One is a [temporary condemnation]{.class_3530} (see Rom. 13:2 and 1 Cor. 11:29, for example), and the other is [eternal condemnation]{.class_3530} (see John 5:24 and Mark 16:16, for example). After bellyaching for 200 years about the word ["damnation"]{.class_3529} being wrong here---Vincent, for example: ["'Damnation']{.class_3529} is an unfortunate rendering (Ah yes, you dirty wretch, haven't we tracked you down before!) in the light of the present common understanding of the word" (p. 263). (Yes, you miserable cuss, don't you know that YOU are an authority on "present, common understanding" of words!)---the twentieth-century apostates could not understand the word translated EITHER WAY (condemnation or damnation) in Romans 8:1, SO THEY TOOK HALF OF THE VERSE OUT ON THE GROUNDS THAT IT MEANT ETERNAL DAMNATION, WHEN IT DIDN'T.

[This is the "scholarship" behind the ]{.class_3527}[ASV, NASV, NIV, RSV, NRSV, Living Bible, TEV, NWT,]{.class_3526}[ and the ]{.class_3527}[NEB]{.class_3526}[.]{.class_3527}

[It would make a rattlesnake look as straight as a yard stick (see ]{.class_3527}[The Christian's Handbook of Biblical Scholarship,]{.class_3526}[ Bible Baptist Bookstore, 1999).]{.class_3527}

["their first faith"]{.class_3529} (vs. 12) is the faith they first had after their husbands died, that they would follow the instructions of 1 Cor. 7:39--40 and not remarry. 5:17 Let the elders that rule well be counted worthy of double honour, especially they who labour in the word and doctrine.

18 For the scripture saith, Thou shalt not muzzle the ox that treadeth out the corn. And, The labourer is worthy of his reward.

19 Against an elder receive not an accusation, but before two or three witnesses.

20 Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear.

21 I charge thee before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, and the elect angels, that thou observe these things without preferring one before another, doing nothing by partiality.

[Five things are now said about the ordained "elder" (not just an ]{.class_3527}[older man ]{.class_3526}[as in vs. 1) who functions in the church as a leader or overseer.]{.class_3527}

1. If he rules WELL, he deserves twice the honor a man should ordinarily get.

2. This is especially true in a case where his labor is in THE WORD and doctrine.

3. You are to pay him for his work and labor (see 1 Cor. 9:1--12).

4. You are to rebuke him (or his adversaries) OPENLY when sin is apparent.

5. You are not to believe any rumors about him unless two or more witnesses testify, and they have to confront him when they testify (see Matt. 18:15--17 for an illustration).

"[Let the elders that rule WELL\...labour in THE WORD and doctrine"]{.class_3528} (vs. 17). [2. His primary job is to FEED THE SHEEP (John 21:15--17; 1 Pet. 5:1--4). "[For the scripture saith"]{.class_3528} (vs. 18). The Scripture here is Deuteronomy 25:4, and Paul mentions these matters back in 1 Corinthians 9:14. "[Against an elder receive not an accusation"]{.class_3528} (vs. 19). We have mentioned this under verse 1.

"[Them that sin rebuke before all, that others also may fear"]{.class_3528} (vs. 20). In the Laodicean news media set-up, this is impossible, for it involves the unpardonable sin of the Gannett string, Luce magazines, and Hearst publications: it involves being "JUDGMENTAL." If any preacher dared obey this command, he would be "discriminating" and showing a "bigoted" and "prejudiced" attitude that did not manifest the "sweet spirit of Christ" coming from the "warm heart of God" to "SHARE the blankety, blank, blank of the blippety, blip, blip, etc." You know the cuisine: stewed prunes.

The young pastor is to rebuke an elder if the two or three witnesses confront him in the pastor's presence and he confesses guilt. Further, the pastor must rebuke the witnesses if they turn out to be false. "OPENLY" means publicly, to the congregation. The best way to handle such things is to call a special private meeting so that 100 percent of the members aren't shocked out of their socks. In the old days it was done without warning. 5:22 Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men's sins: keep thyself pure.

23 Drink no longer water, but use a little wine for thy stomach's sake and thine often infirmities.

24 Some men's sins are open beforehand, going before to judgment; and some men they follow after.

25 Likewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise cannot be hid.

"[Lay hands suddenly on no man, neither be partaker of other men's sins"]{.class_3528} (vs. 22). The two things come together by association, for if you ordain a novice hastily (see 3:6 and remarks), you are indirectly responsible for his sins when he "blows it."

"[Keep thyself pure"]{.class_3528} (vs. 22). Do this by refusing to partake with sinners (see the detailed explanation given in Prov. 1:10 and 2 Cor. 6:14--18 independent of any Greek scholar's works, ideas, thoughts, writings, exegesis, or opinions) and by refusing to be responsible for those who do sin. No man in the twentieth century in America, at least, can keep himself ["pure"]{.class_3529} if he spends much time in any daily newspaper or any magazine that has a large circulation. No man can keep himself pure if he watches TV an hour a day. No man can keep himself pure if he listens to "contemporary Christian music" or "country and western music" more than one hour a week.

America, today, is a mass of filthy talk, filthy pictures, filthy ideas, filthy sounds, and filthy people. No documentation is necessary; the police records, jail populations, dope cultures, suicides, hospital wards, and insane asylums give you all the documentation you need. "PLURALISTIC SOCIETIES" create mongrel inhabitants. Moral filth in America is as "American" as Howard Johnson's and "apple pie."

"[Drink no longer water, but use a little wine"]{.class_3528} (vs. 23). Every "sneaky Pete" from here to Fairbanks, Alaska knows the verse. 1\. Stomach trouble.

[2. A continually weakened condition that came upon Timothy at times (]{.class_3527}["and thine often infirmities"]{.class_3528}[). You never met a liquor-headed Catholic priest in your life that took a "LITTLE" wine or did it only when he was sick.]{.class_3527}

[Wine drinkers, like Westcott and Hort (and other half-Roman sacramentalists), decide the verse needs a little "labor in doctrine," so Hort tells us that it really was just to undo what he had just said about keeping ]{.class_3527}["pure."]{.class_3528}[ Paul was afraid that when Timothy read ]{.class_3527}["KEEP THYSELF PURE"]{.class_3528}[ he would go off on an Origenistic ascetic "kick" that would damage him spiritually, so he said "don't get me wrong, its alright to inbibe once in awhile. As a matter of fact, I think you should take up WINE DRINKING instead of water drinking so you don't get too fanatical."]{.class_3527}

We cite Hort: "THIS IS NOT MERELY A SANITARY BUT QUITE AS MUCH A MORAL PRECEPT."

That is, the inventors of the Westcott and Hort text of 1881 said if you are moral you should drink wine.

"[I do not mean that you practise a rigid asceticism; on the contrary I think that you are likely to INJURE YOUR HEALTH BY YOUR COMPLETE ABSTINENCE FROM WINE (!); so be no longer a water drinker\..." (]{.class_3527}[Expositor's Greek Testament]{.class_3530}, p. 138).

"[Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging"]{.class_3528} (Prov. 20:1). ["Look not thou upon the wine when it is red, when it giveth his colour in the cup, when it moveth itself aright"]{.class_3529} (Prov. 23:31). (See Prov. 23:31 and comments in that commentary.)

[The ]{.class_3527}["double honour" ]{.class_3528}[elders who ]{.class_3527}["labour in the word"]{.class_3528}[ have a time of it, don't they? [Ellicott and Wiesinger are much closer to the truth this time, for they grasp the kindergarten lesson that every Bible-believer saw immediately: "no longer drink ONLY water, but put a little wine in it for medicine." (Medicine often has some alcoholic content.) Paul was talking about medicine ]{.class_3527}[when sick,]{.class_3526}[ not a moral precept for a man in danger of becoming an ascetic. "ASCETICS" ALL DRINK WINE. If you don't believe it, ask them at "Christian" Brothers Distillery. (Compare Isa. 1:22.)]{.class_3527}

"[Some men's sins are open beforehand, going before to judgment; and some men they follow after"]{.class_3528} (vs. 24). There are two applications. The first one is that some men get caught in their sins openly here and now and have to be judged and dealt with here and now. In particular, the text is dealing with the sins of the elders or the accusers just mentioned (vs. 20). Other men's sins do not catch up with them until later. The sins trail and track them and finally catch up with them. Although this usually happens in this life, it does not ALWAYS happen. There has been many a "perfect crime" committed down here where the culprit was not detected nor caught nor arrested nor convicted down here. However, "UP THERE" (Eccl. 12:14; Rom. 2:16) is "somethin' else," as they say "down home." And this means that the Judgment Seat of Christ will deal with sins (["GOOD OR BAD,"]{.class_3529} 2 Cor. 5:10), even though the sins were dealt with judicially at Calvary (Col. 1:14; Rom. 5:1, 9). If you don't reap down here, you WILL reap up there.

"[Likewise also the good works of some are manifest beforehand; and they that are otherwise]{.class_3528} (i.e., "not manifest")[ cannot be hid"]{.class_3529} (vs. 25). Down here, the good works of men are usually manifest immediately for the simple reason that bad works are so abundant (this is no pessimistic overstatement: see Gal. 1:4) that truly good works stick out like a new Mercedes Benz in a junkyard. However, if the good works are not manifest (["otherwise"]{.class_3529}), at least they cannot be hidden. Your good works will find you out as well as your bad ones (Gal. 5:19--21), either down here or up there or both. When a man engages to carry out the admonitions of 2 Corinthians 9:6--11, his good works cannot be HID because the payment for them comes in before he dies (Matt. 19:29). If they should be "hidden" here, they will certainly never remain hidden (1 Cor. 3:10--15) at the Judgment Seat of Christ. Right will come out as well as wrong.

6:1 Let as many servants as are under the yoke count their own masters worthy of all honour, that the name of God and his doctrine be not blasphemed.

GODLINESS IS GAIN, and ["godliness with CONTENTMENT is GREAT gain"]{.class_3529} (vs. 6). Happy is the man who is content in any state (Phil. 4:11) and still in fellowship with God and enjoying God's fellowship! Worldly gain will have no influence on him, and what the twentieth-century Christians consider to be "gain" will be irrelevant to him, one way or another. He can get by on a wall-to-wall floor without any wall-to-wall carpet. ["Food and raiment"]{.class_3529} (vs. 8) are the vital essentials, and as we have said before, there are no ten Christians in the USA who ever obeyed THAT command. You are to be content with ["food and raiment"]{.class_3529} (see Matt. 6:31--33), and the greatest gain on God's earth is to be content with that meager fare and still be able to live right and please God. As the ninety-year-old saint who was dying in the garret said, "Imagine all of this, and Jesus into the bargain!"

6:9 But they that will be rich fall into temptation and a snare, and into many foolish and hurtful lusts, which drown men in destruction and perdition.

10 For the love of money is the root of all evil: which while some coveted after, they have erred from the faith, and pierced themselves through with many sorrows.

11 But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.

12 Fight the good fight of faith, lay hold on eternal life, whereunto thou art also called, and hast professed a good profession before many witnesses."

When a millionaire died, some newspaper reporter asked, "How much did he leave behind?" A Christian relative (whose answer was never printed in ANY paper) replied, "HE LEFT EVERYTHING." [Money won't buy your way out of hell. Money can't buy you the love of a clean woman. Money can't buy your own baby. Money can't buy peace of mind or forgiveness of sins. You cannot PURCHASE the gift of God (Acts 5:1--10), for it is a GIFT; it is not an earned wage like DEATH (Rom. 6:23).]{.class_3527}

Years ago on the shores of Lake Michigan, Bob Jones Sr. (NEVER to be confused with Jr., III, or IV) was eating at the home of a millionaire. The man's estate, counting the yacht and the yacht basin, was worth about \$4,000,000 (back in the thirties). The mansion was filled with original oil paintings, statues from Greece, carpets from Persia, rugs from the Orient, and hand-carved objects from Bavaria, plus custom-made furniture, cut-glass chandeliers, and cedar paneling. Bob Jones Sr. sat down at a long table that had the man at one end and his wife at the other. Across from the preacher was an empty high chair. After several moments of small talk, Jones said, "Do you have a child?" The man coughed and wiped his mouth, and the woman dropped her head. The man said, "Well, several years ago, God gave us a little baby boy, but he died before he was three years old." He hesitated and, then facing up to it, said, "We leave that chair there. We have tried to move it several times, but we just can't seem to be able to do it."

[Now, suppose that man had been ]{.class_3527}[lost]{.class_3526}[. Can't you see him? Going through that million-dollar mausoleum and stopping before his Greek statues and screaming, "Speak to me! God has taken my baby boy!]{.class_3527}[ Oh, comfort me!]{.class_3526}[ You cost me so much money; help me now! ]{.class_3527}[Do something for me!"]{.class_3526}[ He falls down on the \$8,000 bearskin rug, pounds it with his fists, and cries, "Comfort me! My son is gone! Say something! You cost me so much MONEY! You must be worth something to me now!"]{.class_3527}

It ain't worth a dime, man. It ain't worth a \-\-\-- dime.

[If the commandment is kept ]{.class_3527}["without spot,"]{.class_3528}[ it will be "spotless": free of any "smudge" or defilement of any kind. If it is kept ]{.class_3527}["unrebukeable,"]{.class_3528}[ it will be one of those "blameless" things that appears at the Judgement Seat of Christ (see Phil. 1:10) and earns a reward (1 Cor. 3:10--15). One of the strongest arguments on earth that the Body of Christ will not go through Daniel's Seventieth Week has nothing to do with quoting a "proof text" somewhere or altering 2 Thessalonians 2:7 to make it teach that. The strongest proof is that the Body of Christ is a ]{.class_3527}[wife,]{.class_3526}[ and the wife is a "bride" who must be present at a marriage BEFORE THE ADVENT (see Rev. 19:7--8, Ps. 45:1--10, and Song of Sol. 6:8--10).]{.class_3527}

Furthermore, the Judgment Seat of Christ will have to take place BEFORE this marriage (1 Cor. 3:10--15; 2 Cor. 5:10; Rom. 14:10). How does this Body get "caught up" at the END of Daniel's Seventieth Week, get judged, married, mount their horses, then attack and hit the ground in thirty minutes (or even a day or a week)? Christ dies in the MIDDLE of a week and does not come up for 3½ days. Christ's ministry is 3½ years long. If Daniel's Seventieth Week is a "week" of seven years, what would a rapture of the BRIDE be doing at the tip end of it? She would certainly be caught up at the beginning of the period or, at the latest, in the MIDDLE of the period. Enoch settles the matter (Gen. 5:24), for he is the only man in the entire Bible that is a picture of a living Christian caught up before death. No other man can qualify for the office. And Enoch was caught out BEFORE ONE DROP OF RAIN FELL DURING NOAH'S FLOOD. Noah's flood, according to Jesus Christ, is a picture of the Great Tribulation (Matt. 24:36--40).

"[Which in his times"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15). Notice the plural, as ["in the days of Noah,"]{.class_3529} not the singular as in ["THE DAY THAT LOT WENT OUT OF SODOM"]{.class_3529} (Luke 17:29).
