[1. Coming down into the atmosphere to meet ]{.class_3527}["Rebekah"]{.class_3528}[ (Gen. 24; 1 Thess. 4:16--18).]{.class_3527}

2\. Returning to glory with a purchased Bride who was rescued from "kidnappers" (Eph. 5:24--25).

[3. Judging the bride and presenting her SPOTLESS (see comments above) and ]{.class_3527}["without blemish"]{.class_3528}[ (compare Esther 2 and Eph. 5:27).]{.class_3527}

4\. Marrying her at a gala celebration (Esther 1) that will make the greatest Hollywood party ever thrown down here look like a backyard barbeque (Matt. 22; Luke 14:14--23; Song of Sol. 6).

5. Returning with her to reign on earth for a thousand years (Rev. 19--20).

6. Sitting down on David's throne, judging the Gentiles, and setting up a millennial kingdom with the capital of the world at Jerusalem (Luke 1:30--33; Matt. 25:30--40; Psa. 110).

"[His TIMES"]{.class_3528} stretch from his appearing to His Bride to the White Throne Judgment: 1007-plus years.

"[POTENTATE, the King of kings, and Lord of lords"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15). This is exactly as is found in Revelation 19:16 and many times in the Psalms (2:6, 10:16, 29:10, 44:4, 95:3, 98:6, etc.). A ["Potentate"]{.class_3529} is an Oriental despot, King of the Mountain, and ruler of the roost, the word being from "POTENT" (powerful). A similar phrase in the Old Testament would be "THE ALL POWERFUL ONE," and hence, the term ["THE ALMIGHTY"]{.class_3529} (Gen. 17:1, 28:3; Exod. 6:3; Num. 24:4; Ruth 1:20; Job 5:17, et al.). It is the great title for God that appears in the Book of [Job ]{.class_3530}more than any other book in the Bible (over thirty times), clearly telling the reader that the ["King of kings"]{.class_3529} and ["Lord of lords"]{.class_3529} does not land on earth until Job's forty-two chapters (forty-two months, making 3½ years) and his seven days and nights on the ground (Job 2:13, making seven years in Daniel's Seventieth Week) are up. (See Job 42 and comments in that commentary.)

"[Who only hath immortality\...the light which no man can approach unto\...whom no man hath seen, nor can see"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16). The verse is a foundational proof text for the Jehovah's Witnesses, who find two things in the verse they can use to prove that Jesus was not ["GOD\...MANIFEST IN THE FLESH."]{.class_3529}

[1. People ]{.class_3527}[have seen ]{.class_3526}[Jesus Christ (1 John 1:1--2); therefore, He is not Jehovah, but only a "lesser god" (see the ]{.class_3527}[NASV ]{.class_3526}[on John 1:18, which is a perfect Jehovah's Witness' rendering).]{.class_3527}

[2. Since God ]{.class_3527}["ONLY hath immortality,"]{.class_3528}[ then every sinner is ANNIHILATED at the White Throne Judgment who doesn't have eternal life. That is, their souls are not IMMORTAL, for "only God is immortal." (See how it's done? Spiffy, ain't it?)]{.class_3527}

[This is the logical consequence of equating the mortal body with the immortal ]{.class_3527}[soul ]{.class_3526}[(see Col. 2:11--13 and comments in that commentary). If the physical body is corruptible (Rom. 1:23) and subject to decay (1 Cor. 15:53)---and it is (Acts 2:31)---then the "JW" surmises that the soul is subject to the same; hence, "the soul goes to the grave with the body." This has further complications, for now one must equate the GRAVE with HELL. So if "only God" has immortality and can "live forever," no man will "live forever." If he is not a Jehovah's Witness, his soul and body will be annihilated (not "destroyed") in the Lake of Fire, and he will blank out as a conscious entity altogether.]{.class_3527}

[This lie carries even more serious overtones, for it means that no sinner should really ]{.class_3527}[fear immortality ]{.class_3526}[or ]{.class_3527}[wickedness in any form, ]{.class_3526}[as his end will be unconscious annihilation ending not only life, but ]{.class_3527}[suffering and punishment.]{.class_3526}[ Such a doctrine is the greatest encouragement in the world to sin, for when "counting the cost," any depraved sinner would come to the conclusion that annihilation after sixty to seventy years of doing "as you well pleased" would be more enjoyable than denying yourself everything your flesh wanted for sixty to seventy years and then "going to heaven." The real name of Russell and Rutherford's cult should be "The Jehovah's False Witnesses." The ARMSTRONGS (THE WORLDWIDE CHURCH OF GOD) both followed this cult.]{.class_3527}

[Note: After the death of Armstrong in 1986, the leadership of the World-Wide Church of God made the transition from the cultic theology of Herbert W. to the standard, fundamental doctrines of Biblical Christianity (]{.class_3527}[The Kingdom of the Cults,]{.class_3526}[ 1997, pp. 471­--473)]{.class_3527}

"[IMMORTALITY"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16) here means "not subject to decay; imperishable; enduring; undying; not subject to the laws of entropy." Christ's [body ]{.class_3530}is the outstanding example. His [flesh ]{.class_3530}did not decay and was not subject to decay (Acts 2:27--31). His deathless, immortal [soul ]{.class_3530}was in a deathless, immortal [body]{.class_3530}, and He laid down His life voluntarily, or as sure as you live and breathe, HE WOULD BE ALIVE TODAY ON THIS EARTH AS A 33½-YEAR OLD MALE WITH NOT ONE GREY HAIR ON HIS HEAD (see Gen. 2:7 and comments in that commentary). Since God is self-sustaining, self-existing (Exod. 3:14), and self-maintaining, He is not subject to His own laws: the laws of deterioration, decay, degeneration, and disintegration, which are perfectly obvious to the telescope, microscope, test tube, laboratory, and "geiger counter" (see [The Christian's Handbook of Science and Philosophy,]{.class_3530} 2000).

[Therefore, He can sustain physical life (see Enoch and Moses) after death or before death, and He sustains spiritual life (Eph. 2:1--8; 2 Cor. 5:1--10) before and ]{.class_3527}[after ]{.class_3526}[death. The statement is not that God has confined immortality ]{.class_3527}[to Himself;]{.class_3526}[ it is that HE is THE POSSESSOR OF IT (]{.class_3527}["who only HATH immortality"]{.class_3528}[). He can distribute it where He will, and He does (John 10:28). When the ]{.class_3527}["mortal shall have put on immortality"]{.class_3528}[ (see 1 Cor. 15:50--56), it will be dealing with PHYSICAL LIFE given to GLORIFIED PHYSICAL BODIES (1 Cor. 15:51): specifically, bodies of FLESH AND BONES (Luke 24:39).]{.class_3527}

[The ]{.class_3527}[soul's life]{.class_3526}[ was never a point at issue.]{.class_3527}

"[IMMORTALITY"]{.class_3528} in 1 Timothy 6:16 is a reference to God's DEATHLESSNESS. His flesh never comes to a physical "end," not even on the cross or in Joseph's tomb. ["Christ died for our sins according to the scriptures,"]{.class_3529} but ["according to the scriptures,"]{.class_3529} ["NEITHER DID HIS FLESH SEE CORRUPTION"]{.class_3529} (Acts 2:27, 31). He was immortal. As ["GOD\...MANIFEST IN THE FLESH,"]{.class_3529} He not only could[ "shew, who is the blessed and only Potentate" ]{.class_3529}(vs. 15), but the titles of that Potentate are HIS TITLES (compare vs. 15 with Rev. 19:16); hence, we are not surprised to find Jesus Christ claiming to be THE JEHOVAH OF JEHOVAHS (Rev. 19:16), ["the FIRST and the LAST"]{.class_3529} (Rev. 22:13), and the Great[ "I AM"]{.class_3529} Himself (John 8:58). So all of the Pharisees and scribes understood the matter (John 10:33). John 5:26 states it well. God can dispense immortality.

[Since it was given to ME (1 John 5:8--13), some day I will ]{.class_3527}["put on immortality"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Cor. 15:50--55) and cover myself ]{.class_3527}["with light as with a garment"]{.class_3528}[ (Psa. 104:2), for my covering is ]{.class_3527}["THE TRUE LIGHT,]{.class_3528}[ ]{.class_3527}[WHICH LIGHTETH EVERY MAN THAT COMETH INTO THE WORLD"]{.class_3528}[ (John 1:1--9).]{.class_3527}

[When the "J.W.'s" wind up with a ]{.class_3527}[lost soul ]{.class_3526}[(Matt. 10:28) and a ]{.class_3527}[destroyed soul]{.class_3526}[ (Matt. 5:29), they will learn to their terror and frustration (]{.class_3527}["gnashing of teeth,"]{.class_3528}[ Matt. 22:13) that the word "DESTROYED" never meant "ANNIHILATION" one time that the word was used in over 400 passages of Scripture (see Jer. 4:20, 17:18; 1 Cor. 5:5; Jer. 48:4; Josh. 10:35, 37, 39; Lev. 26:30; Isa. 34:2; Gen. 18:23; Ezra 6:12; 2 Thess. 1:9, etc.). "Annihilation" is quite similar to such terms as "EVOLUTION" and "WORLD PEACE." No such things exist ]{.class_3527}[anywhere ]{.class_3526}[outside of the depraved imagination of a lost man who has abandoned his senses through violating his conscience (Rom. 1:18--28).]{.class_3527}

"[Whom no man hath seen, nor can see"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16). See the detailed doctrinal analysis of this in [The Bible Believer's Commentary on Exodus,]{.class_3530} 1993 (Exod. 32--34) where Moses SEES God and the elders "see" Him, without really "seeing" Him.

6:17 Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;

18 That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate;

19 Laying up in store for themselves a good foundation against the time to come, that they may lay hold on eternal life.

20 O Timothy, keep that which is committed to thy trust, avoiding profane and vain babblings, and oppositions of science falsely so called:

21 Which some professing have erred concerning the faith. Grace be with thee. Amen.

[Paul now returns to his theme of verses 5--11. The interlude was to remind the pious faculty members at Bob Jones, Maranatha, Pillsbury, King's College, Santa Rosa Schools, Liberty University, Dallas Theological Seminary, Denver, and the Laodicean bigshot Christian "celebrities" that ]{.class_3527}["POWER BELONGETH UNTO GOD"]{.class_3528}[ ALONE (see Ps. 62:11); hence, the reference to ]{.class_3527}["POTENTATE"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 15). God is in charge, no matter how many times you mistake your ]{.class_3527}["GAIN"]{.class_3528}[ for godliness, and if you are ungodly (as we have proved the correctors of the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[text to be on about fifty occasions), your]{.class_3527}[ "gain"]{.class_3528}[ is only proof that you are kin to Jacob; you are a crafty, smooth, slick, "Christian" opportunist.]{.class_3527}

"[Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches"]{.class_3528} (vs. 17). That is about as tough a charge as was ever laid on a young minister in America. The commentators piously mouth all kinds of devotional generalities about the matter, but this is because they never obeyed the charge a day in their own lives. Imagine these modern book writers and Bible expositors who are always "COPING" and "SHARING," standing in a pulpit on Sunday morning in a town of 40,000 people and charging the "money-men" in their own congregations (who support the building program, the bus repairs, the Daily Vacation Bible School, and pay the Pastor's salary) to be not ["HIGHMINDED"]{.class_3529}! The scene is fantastic. Imagine pastoring 200 people in a town of 30,000 and facing the same flock every Sunday for at least forty-five Sundays a year and warning the rich people there not to trust in their money and not to be stuck on themselves!

[Why, these hot-house Bible-revisors, translators, and "Bible teachers" know less about that kind of obedience than a Montana cowbow knows about "floundering." If MacArthur, Swindoll, Gothard, and Jerry Falwell were to bring a series of three messages on ]{.class_3527}["The Conceit of 'Money-men',"]{.class_3526}[ they would go bankrupt in a month. "Money-men" like to control TV broadcasts, radio broadcasts, seminaries, colleges, and local churches. It makes them feel "godly." They assume that ]{.class_3527}["GAIN IS GODLINESS"]{.class_3528}[ (see 6:5 and comments). ]{.class_3527}[They buy preachers with favors. ]{.class_3526}[They often do it as a substitute for answering the call to the ministry themselves. This way they can run churches by "proxy." (When did you hear Dobson or Gothard talking about "HOW TO COPE" with THAT?)]{.class_3527}

[1. ]{.class_3527}["Not highminded"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 17). Don't get the big head (Rom. 12:16). You will leave it all with the undertaker, the lawyers, and your kinfolk. You will wind up in a hospital with that silly little linen jacket on while the kids vie with each other to see who can be the nicest to you so they can get the biggest share of what's left. (When was the last time you heard "Chuck" Swindoll preach about THAT?) "And to my nephew Wilbur\...'Hello, Wilbur!"']{.class_3527}

"[Not highminded,"]{.class_3528} God can take your mind (Job 12:20).[ "Not highminded,"]{.class_3529} God is no respector of persons (James 2:1--5). ["Not highminded,"]{.class_3529} your brains won't fill a good-sized garbage can, even if it's "gold lined." Some janitor threw Talleyrand's brains out of the window into a sewer opening after a hasty embalming job. He didn't know what they were; they just looked like a mess on the table. [Talleyrand ]{.class_3530}was the crafty, sharp, smooth, Catholic ambassador who duped Napoleon and the Kings of France, Prussia, and Spain. His was one of the most brilliant "minds" that ever operated out of a brain. He was ["highminded"]{.class_3529} all of his life. His "mind" wound up below the gutter level; the sewers in France run underground for miles.

[2. ]{.class_3527}["Nor trust in uncertain riches"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 17). Riches are ]{.class_3527}["uncertain"]{.class_3528}[ (Prov. 23:4--6), for they come and go with ]{.class_3527}["time and chance"]{.class_3528}[ (Eccl. 9:11). You cannot depend upon the value of anything but gold and silver, and they will "fluctuate" from \$100 an ounce to \$800 an ounce in less than twenty years. Both are corruptible (James 5:1--5), so that is why ]{.class_3527}["riches"]{.class_3528}[ appear after an interlude of remarks on the IMMORTAL, INCORRUPTIBLE GOD (vs. 16).]{.class_3527}

[Rich men tend to trust their riches ]{.class_3527}[instead ]{.class_3526}[of God (see the Holy Spirit's exact comment in Luke 18:24). They find you can BUY people with money; not just literal slaves, but preachers, judges, lawyers, doctors, ambassadors, and newspaper reporters. "MONEY IS POWER" in the world. (]{.class_3527}[Murphy's Golden Rule]{.class_3526}[---he who has the gold makes the rules.)]{.class_3527}

[But it is apparent from the list we gave under verse 10 that ]{.class_3527}["riches"]{.class_3528}[ are nothing to trust. They are something to USE for God's glory (]{.class_3527}["That they do good, that they be rich in good works, ready to distribute, willing to communicate,"]{.class_3528}[ vs. 18). Outside of supporting God's ministers, financing missionaries, publishing Bibles and tracts, helping out poor Christians, propagating the Gospel, and disseminating Bible-honoring literature, you can't do much with money but feed your face and clothe your body.]{.class_3527}

I have stood by many a swimming pool in a rich man's back yard that cost him over \$50,000 to build, and he wasn't there to swim in it. Some of them died of heart attacks within a year after the pool was completed. I have stood many a time in the vestibule of a five bedroom, three bathroom house, split level, with a three car garage on two acres of landscaped yard, and waited for a sad-faced, broken down, discouraged rich man (or woman) to invite me in to talk about the death of a husband or wife or child or grandchild. I never talked to one of them who was interested in anything in the living room, dining room, bedrooms, or kitchen. They didn't mention the shrubbery or the rose bushes or the magnolia trees or the stone pathways. Once or twice they showed me little pictures of the dead child or a relative.

[I've strolled across King Ludwig's ]{.class_3527}[Chiemsee ]{.class_3526}[estate in Germany and shuffled through his castle at ]{.class_3527}[Neuschwanstein]{.class_3526}[. He wasn't there to comment. Shortly after Ludwig II finished his wonderful palace at Füssen (it would make the work at Disneyworld look like the work of amateurs), ]{.class_3527}[he drowned in one of his ponds.]{.class_3526}[ Ludwig's three estates are worth about \$500,000,000 apiece in anyone's cash, if one includes the artwork and landscaping done on them. If he trusted in ONE FOOT OF THAT GROUND OR ONE ROOM IN THOSE CASTLES, he was a fool. ]{.class_3527}["Nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the LIVING GOD"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 17).]{.class_3527}

[The objects are dead; they are inanimate. They cannot give LIFE (Jer. 10:11--14), in spite of the 450,000,000 miles of baloney sausage that comes out of colleges and universities in America and Europe every year (see ]{.class_3527}[The Christian's Handbook of Science and Philosophy,]{.class_3526}[ 2000). [The verse is handmade for libertines (see Jude 10--16) in that if the ]{.class_3527}["all things to enjoy"]{.class_3528}[ is applied to "acid rock"---which it is by at least ten rock bands composed of "Christians"---then you are to enjoy African sex music. It is the "flying nun" playing the guitar for the "glory of God." It is "Father Murphy" drinking beer at the pub to reach the "down-and-outers." It is bingo on Saturday night (ashtrays will be supplied at each table) for "the glory of God." In the dark ages, it was the torturing and murdering of thousands of innocent men and women (and children) for "the glory of God" after "blessing" the garrote, the rack, the thumb screw, the burning coals, and the "iron maiden." When a pope yells, "IT IS THE WILL OF GOD!" (see ]{.class_3527}[The History of the New Testament Church]{.class_3526}[, Vol. I), it often means "KILL 'EM."]{.class_3527}

[The text is talking about 1 Corinthians 3:22--23, which see. To ]{.class_3527}["enjoy"]{.class_3528}[ the meals you eat, the stars, sun, and moon that you look at, the beaches on which you walk, the mountains that you climb, the liquids that you drink, the children that you raise, the house that you live in, the church that you attend, the garden that you till, the game you take in hunting and fishing, the summer nights and fall evenings, the sports you play or look at, the music that you listen to, and the jobs at which you work, you must have a right relationship with GOD (see Matt. 6:33 and Prov. 21:4). We are told NOT to love the things in the world (1 John 2:15) and, at the same time, are told to enjoy them (compare 1 Cor. 7:31). REAL ENJOYMENT, then (]{.class_3527}["the desires of thine heart,"]{.class_3528}[ Psa. 37:4), is related to GOD. JOY is a GIFT of God (Eccl. 2:24--26), and it is manifest in the natural creation (see ]{.class_3527}[The Christian's Handbook of Science and Philosophy,]{.class_3526}[ 2000). Among the obvious pleasures of life, there are these for the Christian:]{.class_3527}

1. Joy in the complete destruction of the apostate Catholic Church and the Vatican hierarchy (Rev. 19:1--6).

2. Joy in the restoration of the Jew to Jerusalem and the restoration of Palestine (Psa. 122:6--7).

3. Joy in the conversion of souls (Luke 15:10; Psa. 126:6; 1 Thess. 2:19--20).

4. Joy in the fact that God has been pleased to TAKE HIGHLY-EDUCATED PEOPLE, ALONG WITH BRILLIANT INTELLECTUALS, AND BLIND THEM SO THAT ORDINARY, COMMON, STUPID PEOPLE CAN GET REVELATIONS FROM GOD THAT THEY CANNOT (Luke 10:20--21).

(Hey, buster! When was the last time you heard "John MacArthur" preach on THAT one?)

[The world belongs to me ("It is my idea," to quote Schopenhauer). God gave it to ]{.class_3527}[me ]{.class_3526}[when He gave me His Son (1 Cor. 3:22--23), because His Son made it (Col. 1:15--18; John 1:1--8).]{.class_3527}[ I own it.]{.class_3526}[ The oceans are mine. The forests, rivers, hills, and trees are mine; the fruits, flowers, beaches, and lakes are mine. I did nothing to earn them;]{.class_3527}[ they were given to me by the Owner]{.class_3526}[ (1 Cor. 3:21--22). [To back up Mounce's slaughter of 1 Timothy 6:19, Donald Guthrie says that "The Greek (της οντως ζωης) MUST BE RENDERED 'life which is life indeed'" (p. 118). (Note, "MUST BE." He gets THIS: "bringing out vividly its contrast with life propped up by so uncertain a support as riches." ]{.class_3527}[Eternal life dropped out of the discussion.]{.class_3526}[ The comment was worded so that two kinds of ]{.class_3527}[human life]{.class_3526}[ were contrasted: a life "propped up by riches" and a life where riches were "distributed" for ]{.class_3527}["good works"]{.class_3528}[ (see vs. 18). ETERNAL LIFE, which was the subject of the admonition (]{.class_3527}["TIME TO COME," "WHICH IN HIS TIMES," "UNTIL THE APPEARING,"]{.class_3528}[ etc.), dropped "plumb out of sight." The flesh took over and prevailed. Typical. Par for the course.]{.class_3527}

At the end of the six weeks, he reviewed his notes. He had over 300 pages of INSIGNIFICANT NONSENSE. Not one item in six weeks was a revelation of anything "new." 2\. He knows nothing.

3. He is hung up on questions and strifes.

4. He is motivated by envy.

5. He has a corrupt mind.

[6. He is ]{.class_3527}["destitute of the truth."]{.class_3528}

7. He mistakes GAIN for godliness.

1. Its root and source is godliness, not the love of money.

2. Its requirements are food, clothing, and the will of God.

3. It is gained by following after righteousness.

4. It is enriched by getting rid of riches.

D. THE RESULTS OF KEEPING UP WITH THE JONESES:

1. You will have temptations that otherwise you would not have.

2. You will fall into traps that were set for that kind of person.

3. You will pander to lusts that are stupid and foolish.

4. This pandering will hurt your body, mind, family, and soul.

5. You will err concerning the faith.

6. You will bring many sorrows on yourself.

1. He can quicken anything.

2. He will appear publicly, soon.

3. He is "blessed forevermore."

4. He alone can stop death and decay.

2. A trust in God.

3. An ability to give liberally.

4. A willingness to give liberally.

5. A solid foundation for a Christian life.

6. Rewards in "time to come" at the judgment.

2\. Most scientific achievements lead to national profanity.

SECOND TIMOTHY

INTRODUCTION

The Epistle of 2 Timothy has 4 chapters, 83 verses, and 1703 words. It was written somewhere around A.D. 68 and is Paul's last will and testament. It is his "swan song," in which he gives his final advice, comfort, exhortations, encouragement, and warnings to a young pastor who is "fighting the good fight" and "running the race with patience."

[Paul is in the "slammer" while he writes this, although this is not generally included in what we call the ]{.class_3527}["Prison Epistles."]{.class_3526}[ Obviously, he is in jail (1:8), and chained at that (1:16), so it is NOT the first imprisonment mentioned in Acts 28. He is in a Roman jail (1:17), as Peter was (Acts 12), James was (Acts 12), and John the Baptist was (Mark 6:17). ]{.class_3527}[ROME JAILS CHRISTIANS.]{.class_3526}[ (Learn that one lesson, and you will know more than any senator or congressman or Supreme Court judge in Washington D.C. ]{.class_3527}[ROME JAILS CHRISTIANS.]{.class_3526}[ In Paul's case, John's case, and James' case, they KILL them.)]{.class_3527}

The contrasts between Paul's incarceration in Acts 28 and his last "hitch" in the slammer is apparent to anyone. In the first imprisonment, he was permitted to live outside the "clink" (Acts 28:30) and was surrounded with friends (Acts 28:17--31; Col. 4:10--14; Phil. 1:13--14); anyone could get in to see him (Acts 28:30), and he expected to be "out of hock" soon (Phil. 1:25--26, 2:24). In his last stint of "doing time," he does not expect to get out; he expects to "depart and be with the Lord" (2 Tim. 4); and his friends have left him (2 Tim. 4): he is in close confinement.

These things should be noted by the Bible believer, and then he should go back and review the "Introduction to First Timothy." There, you remember, the Alexandrian Cult and the Scholar's Union were hollering about "no time lapse between" Acts 28 and 2 Timothy 4, and therefore, "there could have been only ONE imprisonment." If there were only one, it lasted from A.D. 62 to 68, and Paul's expectation of getting out (Phil. 1:25--26, 2:24) was vain presumption. We presume he spoke the truth.

Before his arrest, Paul had left some things at Troas (2 Tim. 4:13). It is assumed that Nero was arresting Christians right and left from A.D. 64 to 68 and blaming the Great Roman Fire on them. (Some writers give A.D. 66 as the date of Paul's death, others A.D. 67, and others A.D. 68. Almost any date before the destruction of Jerusalem \[A.D. 70\] and AFTER A.D. 64 will do.) Nero was not just lopping off heads, he was torturing Christians to death and lighting his palace gardens with them. We assume that Paul was beheaded simply because he had an advantage that many other Christians did not: he was a Roman citizen (Acts 22:29), and he had friends in Caesar's household (see Phil. 1:13).

[The two great New Testament passages that show most clearly that Simon Peter never got within 300 miles of Rome are Romans 16 and 2 Timothy 4. To think that Peter was the BISHOP of Rome before Paul's first imprisonment (A.D. 62) is to think like Edward Lear or Johnny Carson, for when Paul writes to the Church at Rome (Rom. 16), he lists a long list of church members to greet, ]{.class_3527}[and forgets the "Bishop"!]{.class_3526}[ Imagine writing a letter to thirty Christians by name (plus their families) in Rome and forgetting to say "hello" to their Pastor who was "The Prince of the Apostles" and the "Vicar of Christ," in charge of "feeding the flock"! My, what unforgivable rudeness! Sounds like Martin Luther, doesn't it? Why, when Paul came to Rome (Acts 24, 25, 26, and 27), he came into virgin territory (Rom. 15:22) where Christ "had not been named" (Rom. 15:20), as far as a nationwide evangelistic effort was concerned. If Peter had been "Vicaring" for Christ or "Holy Fathering" for Christ (or any other demoniac blasphemous Catholic operation), why had he not "NAMED CHRIST" (Rom. 15:20)?]{.class_3527}

[There is even less chance that Peter could have been in Rome AFTER Paul got there, for what was he doing? Playing scrabble with Nero's guards while Paul was getting his head cut off? If ]{.class_3527}["ONLY LUKE IS WITH ME"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Tim. 4:11), what did Peter do? Tuck his tail between his legs and run off to the Bingo hall? Well, Peter "was crucified upside down before Paul was executed" (Dionysius, Bishop of Corinth, cited in Eusebius' ]{.class_3527}[Ecclesiastical History]{.class_3526}[, 2.25), but it was "ABOUT THE SAME TIME" (ibid.). Well, "heavens to Betsy" and "Beka's kiddy school"! Why didn't Paul tell Timothy what a great suffering martyr his fellow prisoner Peter was? Rather neglectful, don't you think?]{.class_3527}

[No, there is not the chance of a snowball in hell that Peter ever got within 300 miles of Rome, north, south, east, or west. All of the Roman Catholic fairy stories based on myth and fantasy in the "church fathers" break down when faced with the clear, plain statements of inspired Scripture. The Scriptures completely overthrow all of Rome's pagan nonsense, and all of the eternal efforts of her priests and writers for ]{.class_3527}[Our Sunday Visitor ]{.class_3526}[(to try to unravel the balled-up mess that the Church has made of her "teachings") amount to little more than the spasmodic gyrations of psychotic sophists fighting for their lives. Peter was never in Rome, Mary was never sinless, no "Mass" could save a mission bum, purgatory is as real as green cheese on the moon, nuns and monks are as Scriptural as Easter bunnies, baptismal regeneration is as effective as a seance, Catholic tradition is as sound as a Ouija board, Mary is waiting for the resurrection, Acts 2:38 couldn't save a dead horse, popes and nuncios are as honest as the Mafia and the Cosa Nostra, papal encyclicals are about as Scriptural as the ]{.class_3527}[Book of Mormon]{.class_3526}[, and a pope's word is about as reliable as Ted Kennedy's word or Richard Nixon's. If a pope tells you the sun is coming up tomorrow, you had better get your house in order and make out your last will and testament. Peter no more got to Rome than to Las Vegas or Oklahoma City.]{.class_3527}

So here we begin the final words that Paul had to say before laying down his life (see Rom. 16) on the chopping block; a thing which he was more than willing to do (Acts 21:13), and for which he had actually been hoping (Phil. 1:23) for many years (2 Cor. 5:1--9). Other famous "last speeches" are found in Acts 20 (Paul to the Ephesian elders), John 13--17 (Christ to His disciples), Genesis 49 (Jacob to his sons), Deuteronomy 32--33 (Moses to Israel), Joshua 24 (Joshua to Israel), 2 Samuel 23:1--7 (David to the world), and Stephen (Acts 7) to the Sanhedrin.

[]{#sYORfekySpmggT0K0tcNCA1328.xhtml}

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, according to the promise of life which is in Christ Jesus,

2 To Timothy, my dearly beloved son: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.

3 I thank God, whom I serve from my forefathers with pure conscience, that without ceasing I have remembrance of thee in my prayers night and day;

4 Greatly desiring to see thee, being mindful of thy tears, that I may be filled with joy;

5 When I call to remembrance the unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice; and I am persuaded that in thee also.

"[Paul, an apostle\...by the will of God"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). Compare this with all of the gas that you got from the scholars about the "authorship" of the Epistles. If they were forged, the writer LIED like a dog, and just like the modern popes and Charismatics, he claimed "apostolic authority" when he had no more of it than any pope or Charismatic does in 2000.

[The opening here matches Colossians 1:1 (see ]{.class_3527}[The Bible Believer's Commentary on Galatians, Ephesians, Philippians, and Colossians,]{.class_3526}[ 1997, comments under Col. 1:1).]{.class_3527}

"[According to the promise of life"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1), called the ["hope of eternal life"]{.class_3529} in Titus 1:2, which see. The promise was made first to the Son by the Father before Genesis 1:1 and had to do with everlasting life as a deathless existence [in a BODY]{.class_3530} (see Titus 1:1--2, and comments). Jesus Christ IS eternal life (1 John 5:11, 20), and the one therefore who "has" Him has eternal life (1 John 5:10--13). He is the Gift (Rom. 6:23) and the Giver (John 10:11), so the promise was given to Him (Titus 1:2) and is IN HIM (2 Tim. 1:1).

"[I thank God, whom I serve from my forefathers"]{.class_3528} (vs. 3). The reference is to his Jewish ancestry, and those members of it who did serve God. Paul did not have a ["pure conscience"]{.class_3529} after the stoning of Stephen (see Acts 9 and comments in that commentary), but later he got "back on the track" (Acts 23:1 and 24:16). Paul endeavored all of his life to keep his conscience pure (Rom. 9:1 and 1 Tim. 1:19), and he didn't slip often. ["I thank God"]{.class_3529} means first of all that he is thanking God for being able to remember Timothy daily in prayer. The commentators seem to have a little trouble finding the cause for this thanks, although the English construction is perfectly apparent. Paul has two remembrances: 1. Timothy's ministry and 2. Timothy's faith (vs. 5). Hiebert says he is simply recording a "feeling of gratitude" (not "thanks") and, therefore, has no [object ]{.class_3530}of thanks.

[Donald Guthrie tells us that "the Greek is better rendered as 'without ceasing I remember you,'" rather than ]{.class_3527}["THAT without ceasing"]{.class_3528}[ (p. 122). ]{.class_3527}[Anything to get rid of the Authorized text.]{.class_3526}[ "The meaning is that 'as often as,'" according to Lock. Neither change adds anything to the meaning. "As without ceasing" does nothing to explain ]{.class_3527}["that without ceasing."]{.class_3528}[ When Paul told the Christians to ]{.class_3527}["pray without ceasing"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Thess. 5:17), anyone could see that it was not a sixty minute per hour, twenty-four hour per day; obviously, it meant "don't quit praying."]{.class_3527}

"[Greatly desiring to see thee"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4). Paul had a special attachment to Timothy, because he had trained him as a ["son" ]{.class_3529}in the ministry. It is always a great blessing and a delight to be entrusted with the lives of young men (ages 18--30) and to be given the responsibility for taking them through "boot camp" and into the "field" for maneuvers with "live ammo." Moses left all in faith when he was one third as old as his final age, which would be, by ratio, twenty-three years old for a normal "threescore and ten." [Joseph]{.class_3530}, as a young man (about seventeen), endured all in hope. [Daniel ]{.class_3530}triumphed over all through prayer, [Rebekah ]{.class_3530}left all in love, [Esther ]{.class_3530}risked everything for others, [Jonathan ]{.class_3530}surrendered all to David in devotion, and [David ]{.class_3530}"sallied forth" against the enemies of God when he was not twenty years old. ["Let no man despise thy youth"]{.class_3529} (1 Tim. 4:12).

"[The unfeigned faith that is in thee, which dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and thy mother Eunice"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5). This is the kind of faith that dwelt in Enoch, Noah, Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and others (Heb. 11:1--15). It is "saving faith" only in the sense that it responds to the truth when the truth shows up. Notice the exact illustrations given by the Holy Spirit in Acts 16 and John 3 apart from the Greek scholars and commentators. Timothy's parents (at least on his mother's side) were "godly" in the sense of Romans 2:13--16 and Galatians 4:4 and 3:23. Both of them got saved when they heard the gospel. We presume that Timothy's father rejected it.

[Vincent says that the words ]{.class_3527}["greatly desiring"]{.class_3528}[ would be "better" if it were translated as "longing." Guthrie and the ]{.class_3527}[RSV ]{.class_3526}[think that ]{.class_3527}["being mindful of thy tears"]{.class_3528}[ is "better rendered as 'I remember your tears'." This is because "the oldest manuscripts" (ah, yes! Casey at the bat again! They never give up, do they? They missed the ball so many times that if they threw their bat at the ground they would miss that, too) read, "when I called to remembrance." What he means is that the majority of Greek manuscripts have always read with the ]{.class_3527}[King James]{.class_3526}[ reading ("call" in Greek: λαμβανον,]{.class_3527} [lambanon), while ten manuscripts out of 800 read with Nestle, Brown, Hort, Jamiesson, Fausset, etc. (p. 1374). Alford alters ]{.class_3527}["faith that is in thee" ]{.class_3528}[to "faith that WAS in thee" to line it up with Eunice and Lois. Thanks for nothing. God doesn't need any help from helpless critics who cannot read their own language.]{.class_3527}

1:6 Wherefore I put thee in remembrance that thou stir up the gift of God, which is in thee by the putting on of my hands.

7 For God hath not given us the spirit of fear; but of power, and of love, and of a sound mind.

8 Be not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;

9 Who hath saved us, and called us with an holy calling, not according to our works, but according to his own purpose and grace, which was given us in Christ Jesus before the world began,

10 But now is made manifest by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ, who hath abolished death, and hath brought life and immortality to light through the gospel:

11 Whereunto I am appointed a preacher, and an apostle, and a teacher of the Gentiles.

12 For the which cause I also suffer these things: nevertheless I am not ashamed: for I know whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is able to keep that which I have committed unto him against that day.'

Timothy is now reminded again of his ministry which must proceed fearlessly (vss. 6, 8, and 12); this is because salvation is a completed transaction (vs. 10) that was given by grace (vs. 9).

"[Stir up the gift of God"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). In this case, a prophetic gift which Timothy was given at his ordination (see 1 Tim. 1:18). Since the Greek word here contained the idea of "fire" in it (αναζωπυρειν, anazopurein), the translators are dead-set on "kindle" ([NASV]{.class_3530}), "fan into the flame" ([NIV]{.class_3530}), "rekindle" ([RSV]{.class_3530}), or "stir into flame" ([Living Bible]{.class_3530}). These schismatic, church-splitting versions, recommended by Bob Jones III, Custer, Panosian, Wisdom, and others are so divided among themselves, one would think they were ["cloven tongues like as of fire"]{.class_3529} (Acts 2:1--30).

[Stir WHAT into flame? "Rekindle"? Had the fire gone out? Not according to Herbert (p. 35). Mounce has Timothy "fanning into a flame" a fire he did not light (p. 105); and Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, in an effort to impress us with their knowledge of "the Greek," have Timothy "reviving the spark of faith" (p. 1374). But it was the laying on of Paul's hands that "kindled the spark" (]{.class_3527}["which is in thee BY THE PUTTING ON OF MY HANDS,"]{.class_3528}[ vs. 6). Paul did not lay hands on Timothy's mother or his grandmother; therefore, the ]{.class_3527}["gift" ]{.class_3528}[here is not the ]{.class_3527}["unfeigned faith" ]{.class_3528}[of verse 5 that needed to be "stirred up."]{.class_3527}

[This gift is called ]{.class_3527}["THAT GOOD THING"]{.class_3528}[ in verse 14, so it cannot be broken down into the three items found in verse 7. (Guthrie does this and, thereby, limits three of the most precious promises in the entire Bible for the Christian to some MINISTERIAL gifts for a pastor \[pp. 126--127\]). When Sumner Wemp hits the ]{.class_3527}["GOOD THING"]{.class_3528}[ of verse 14, he cannot find anything to match it, so he runs it back to verse 13 and says it is the PATTERN (not ]{.class_3527}["form,"]{.class_3528}[ as the text says) ]{.class_3527}["of sound words."]{.class_3528}[ But Timothy's gift was committed to him as a gift of God. No Christian on this earth needs a "gift" to ]{.class_3527}["hold fast the form of sound words."]{.class_3528}[ All that takes is honesty and faithfulness (see comments under verse 13).]{.class_3527}

[Now, this is why the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[translators did not mess around with flames, fires, and "kindling" when they hit verse 6. Instead of taking the "Greek word" and digging vertically until they hit mud, they let the matter stand as ]{.class_3527}["STIR UP,"]{.class_3528}[ so a number of applications could be made. Vertical grave digging is always the case where an apostate faculty member at any major Christian school has messed with the Book. ]{.class_3527}[They dig vertically]{.class_3526}[ (downward) ]{.class_3527}[to LIMIT APPLICATION. ]{.class_3526}[This confines the Holy Spirit's teaching to their own knowledge and reduces their students to the level of their own stupidity (see 300-plus illustrations documented in the commentaries on ]{.class_3527}[Job, Exodus, Genesis, Matthew, Acts,]{.class_3526}[ and ]{.class_3527}[Hebrews]{.class_3526}[).]{.class_3527}

[The ministerial gift that Timothy was given could have been anything listed in 1 Corinthians 12 (apart from sign gifts for Israel) or Romans 12:5--8. He is told to stir this gift up. If the word "flame" or "fire" had been inserted according to the dictates of the Greek grammars and lexicons, it would have completely ]{.class_3527}[misled ]{.class_3526}[the reader, for it would have intimated that Timothy was cold and backslidden or, at least, cool and unconcerned. No such thing is being discussed. Since the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[translators were much wiser than the translators who used "the oldest and best manuscripts," and since they knew the Greek meaning of αναζωπυρειν]{.class_3527} [as well as any apostate in the nineteenth and twentieth century, they translated the word so it could be understood to relate to TIMOTHY'S MINISTERIAL GIFT. "Stir it up." Get it goin'. Use it! This was the Holy Spirit's intent and meaning in the passage, and the "original Greek" this time did nothing but confound the commentators, confuse their readers, contaminate the truth, and calumniate the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[translators.]{.class_3527}

"[But of power, and of love, and of a SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). Since sin is insanity (see Exod. 16:3 and comments), and all modern revisers, translators, Greek scholars, and commentators lose their minds in places like Job 37:1--4; Job 40:15; Matthew 25:1; Joel 2:4; Ephesians 3:18; Proverbs 11:30; Proverbs 18:1--2; and Numbers 33:52 (see [The Unknown Bible]{.class_3530} for documentation), we would expect them to flinch like a cat about to be kicked when they get to the ["SOUND MIND"]{.class_3529} of verse 7. Our realizations will live up to our expectations. With one accord, they go bonkers and decide that no Christian is given the spirit of a ["SOUND MIND."]{.class_3529} Instead, we find "self-discipline" ([NIV]{.class_3530}), "discipline" ([NASV]{.class_3530}), "self-control" ([RSV]{.class_3530}), "discipline" ([ASV]{.class_3530}), "wise" ([New American]{.class_3530}), "personal restraint" (Mounce), "sobriety" ([Jesuit Rheims,]{.class_3530} and "discipline of self and then of others" ([The Expositor's Greek Testament,]{.class_3530} p. 155, according to Blass, p. 61).

It's really remarkable when one considers it. Here are "Bible-believing" Conservatives (none of them profess to have read ANY Bible and to have "believed" it) who are going to help us find "the original intent of the author" by going to the "oldest and best manuscripts," and they are so "dedicated" and consecrated that they say, "None can be too careful in stating the truths of scriptures IN THE LANGUAGE OF THE INSPIRED WRITERS. When men cannot convey their thoughts in the words of THE SCRIPTURES, it is generally because they do not hold sound doctrine" (Libscom, cited by Hiebert, p. 45). That is, they have stated publicly that if you cannot expound the Bible IN GREEK from GREEK manuscripts you cannot believe in SOUND DOCTRINE.

[After this egomaniacal demonstration of psychotic megalomania, we find that these same gentlemen have taken your ]{.class_3527}["SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528}[ from you and have forbidden for you to be "ACCEPTED" in Jesus Christ. (The phrase ]{.class_3527}["ACCEPTED IN THE BELOVED"]{.class_3528}[ is missing in Eph. 1:6 from the ]{.class_3527}[ASV, NASV, NIV, RSV, NRSV, NEB, NWT]{.class_3526}[, and the "Dead Dodo" \[]{.class_3527}[Living Bible]{.class_3526}[\].) My, what a debt of gratitude we owe these "godly, qualified authorities" who "labored in the originals" to bring in plainer language "the true meaning of the original Greek" that it may no longer "be obscured by the archaic language of the blah, blah, blah, blah!"]{.class_3527}

[Let us recompense them with a just and due reward! Five hair pins, sixteen buttons, three empty match cartons, four "Indian head" pennies, two ball-point pens from K-Mart, a used copy of ]{.class_3527}[Mad Magazine]{.class_3526}[, and a bag of jelly beans from Delchamps. (Be generous! ]{.class_3527}["Give, and it shall be given unto you,"]{.class_3528}[ Luke 6:38!)]{.class_3527}

[We have the spirit of POWER, for ]{.class_3527}["the righteous are as bold as a lion"]{.class_3528}[ (Prov. 28:1). Where we lack it, we are to ask for prayer, as Paul did when he lacked it (Eph. 6:18--19). Though ]{.class_3527}["ignorant and unlearned"]{.class_3528}[ (and we never professed much more in the Scholar's Union), we have ]{.class_3527}["been with Jesus"]{.class_3528}[ (Acts 4:13) and don't have to back down before any apostates like Robert Sumner, Donald Waite, Ed Hindson, Harold Willmington, or their friends. "The spirit of power" is spiritual power (Zech. 4:6), not papal power, which is always political and religious. We have power to overcome sin (Rom. 6--7), get prayers answered (1 John 3:22), witness (2 Tim. 4:2--4), rebuke sinners (Titus 1:13) and sins (Eph. 5:11--13), endure affliction and suffering (2 Cor. 7:4--7), and overcome the world (1 John 5:1--5).]{.class_3527}

[We have the spirit of LOVE. We can love ]{.class_3527}[God,]{.class_3526}[ even when He treats us like we think we don't deserve (Rom. 8:28; 2 Cor. 11:25--30, 12:8); we can love ]{.class_3527}[God's]{.class_3526}[ ]{.class_3527}[word]{.class_3526}[ and ]{.class_3527}[His words]{.class_3526}[ (John 14:23), even when our brethren alter them, attack them, hate them, despise them, misuse them, and ignore them. We can love ]{.class_3527}[God's people]{.class_3526}[ (1 John 5:1), even though they disagree with us (Acts 15:39) and are "hard to get along with" (1 Cor. 11:18--19) and sometimes attack us (3 John 9). We can love]{.class_3527}[ lost souls]{.class_3526}[ (Psa. 126:5--6) because we know their condition (Eph. 2:1--5), and we can love ]{.class_3527}[heavenly things]{.class_3526}[ (Col. 3:1--3) by setting our affections]{.class_3527}[ "on things above."]{.class_3528}

[We have the spirit ]{.class_3527}["of a SOUND MIND."]{.class_3528}[ No silly little Greek scholar with forty years of education is going to "buffalo" us long enough to keep us awake. The Greek word σωφρονισμος]{.class_3527} [is plainly "to restore to a right mind," "calm vigor of mind," "in the manner of a person in his right mind," and "soundness of mind," "a sane mind." φρην]{.class_3527} [is the "mind" and the σος]{.class_3527} [is the "sound"---]{.class_3527}["SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528}[ (]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[1611). Well, where did all of this "discipline" stuff come from? I mean after all of this blather about "Greek roots" and the "original Greek," how did these scatter brains suddenly come to ignore BOTH ROOTS of a Greek word? Why, even Vincent (]{.class_3527}[Word Studies,]{.class_3526}[ p. 290) says it is NOT "self control" (see Appendix No. XIII).]{.class_3527}

[Well, there is no light. Every apostate Fundamentalist who used the term did so on sheer conjecture, or to be more honest, on the grounds that he despised the Holy Text of the Holy Bible. The only straw in the wind was the possibility that the word meant "to render anyone a sound mind" or to "restore to someone else a sound mind." But ]{.class_3527}["SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528}[ was still the meaning---not self-control. (See Appendix No. VIII.)]{.class_3527}

[Let's face it. Someone has a vacant attic upstairs, and they want yours to be just as empty. Someone is not playing the game with a full deck, and they want to shorthand your deck also. But we stick with the text. When Timothy and Paul and Peter Ruckman (or any other 40,000,000 sinners) got saved, the Lord gave them a ]{.class_3527}["SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528}[: a mind that can see through sham, hypocrisy, prevarication, strut, show, falsehood, treachery, and deceit; a mind that can find the truth (John 17:17) and rightly divide it (2 Tim. 2:15), separating the genuine (1 Cor. 2:13) from the plastic (Rom. 16:18).]{.class_3527}

[We are not about to trade our "sound minds" in for a degree in Greek grammar or a Greek lexicon compiled by a lost Greek grammarian. Nor are we about to surrender it in order to learn to think like Hiebert, Plummer, Mounce, Guthrie, Dummelow, Williams, Wiesinger, Kuenen, and Alford think. We are neither neurotic nor psychotic. We have no illusions of grandeur. We do not fancy ourselves to be "accredited recognized scholars." We have no persecution complexes. God has been more than lenient and gracious to us. We have no misconceptions about our abilities or power, and we don't believe half of what anyone says about us, GOOD or BAD. ]{.class_3527}["A SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528}[ knows what to take and what to leave, what to accept and what to reject, and what is good and what is not (see Appendix XXII). The mind that is ]{.class_3527}["sound"]{.class_3528}[ is stayed on God (Isa. 26:3) and is kept in peace (Phil. 4:7), because it is the ]{.class_3527}["MIND OF CHRIST"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Cor. 2:16). This mind is not ]{.class_3527}["soon shaken"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Thess. 2:2) and is not]{.class_3527}[ "puffed up"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Cor. 8:1). It has wisdom (Col. 2:2, 3) and is not defiled (Titus 1:15). It is a ]{.class_3527}["SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528}[ as the Holy Bible (]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[1611) says.]{.class_3527}

[The new, "reliable translations" are the products of people whose "sound minds" abandoned them before they sat down to translate. They were given a ]{.class_3527}["sound mind"]{.class_3528}[ at conversion, but it was CORRUPTED (see 2 Cor. 11:1--3) and became CARNAL (Rom. 8:6). ]{.class_3527}[All carnal, puffed-up, corrupted scholars demonstrate their carnality when they attack the Holy Bible.]{.class_3526}[ If God forgot to give the Bible revisers a ]{.class_3527}["SOUND MIND,"]{.class_3528}[ that is their problem, not ours, nor is it a problem with any SANE child of God in the Body of Christ. Barring nerve damage, brain tumors, pathological defects, and birth defects, every sinner is given a ]{.class_3527}["SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528}[ when he is converted. BEFORE THAT, HE IS ]{.class_3527}[MAD ]{.class_3526}[(Eccl. 9:3). At least that is what the Book says (Rom. 1:22).]{.class_3527}

"[Be not thou therefore ashamed" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 8). In view of the fact that you are sane (even though people think you are crazy), don't worry about being put to shame if you stand by the apostle; most people were ashamed of him (2 Tim. 4:16) and forsook him (2 Tim. 4:16).[ "The testimony of our Lord"]{.class_3529} (vs. 8) was that He, as Paul (["nor of me his prisoner"]{.class_3529}), was arrested and imprisoned (see Isa. 53:8). Note that Paul doesn't consider himself to be Nero's prisoner anymore than Christ considered Himself to be Pilate's prisoner (John 19:11). Both Saviour and servant acknowledge that God Almighty decides who gets arrested and who doesn't (Rom. 8:28), and if you find yourself in jail (Phil. 1:12--14), it is because [God allowed it]{.class_3530} (1 Pet. 3:14), not because the sheriff was particularly smart.

"[Who hath saved us, and called us"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). Observe that the CALLING (as in 1 Cor. 1:26) is a reference to the present condition which follows the "saving." Calvin reversed it. When the Tulip-sniffer insists that the "calling" is the "effectual calling" of the "elect," point out to him how ridiculous this is in view of Matthew 20:16, for here you would have to read that ["MANY]{.class_3529} (the elect) [be called, but FEW]{.class_3529} (the ELECT)[ CHOSEN."]{.class_3529} You see, ["many" ]{.class_3529}is the word that all Calvinists lean on to prove that Christ didn't die for everybody. Hence, ["if MANY be called, but FEW chosen,"]{.class_3529} you have only "many of the elect" being called, but only a few of them "chosen." To save face, the Calvinists invented a philosophical concept called "general calling" and "effectual calling," which means nothing because the effectual calling is never a reference to any LOST MAN; see the order here for example.

"[Not according to our works"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). Neither the saving nor the calling was based on our works. The saving was based on the blood atonement of the Lord Jesus Christ, and the calling was based on the fact that God placed us into Christ (Eph. 2:10) when we received Him (John 1:12--13). ["But according to his own purpose and grace" ]{.class_3529}(vs. 9), exactly as it is found in Ephesians 1:4. His purpose was to adopt some individuals (Eph. 1:5) as sons which He had NOT done for 4,000 years (Gen. 1--Acts 2), and His grace was to give the undeserving sinner a chance to get to Heaven by accepting a free gift (Rom. 6:23).

"[Given us in Christ Jesus BEFORE the world began"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). This, with Ephesians 1:4, is the great stumbling-block that eventually forced Calvin to have a brother in Christ (Servetus) burned at the stake and caused the Puritan Theocracy in New England to hang brothers and sisters in Christ at the stake. You see, the Calvinists never could rightly divide ["the word of truth"]{.class_3529} (2 Tim. 2:15). (They certainly would have sided with Truman Dollar, Robert Sumner, Harold Willmington, Curtis Hutson, and the Roman Catholics in getting rid of that verse \[2 Tim. 2:15\].)

[By making salvation the SAME in both Testaments, Calvin had everyone ]{.class_3527}["IN CHRIST"]{.class_3528}[ before Genesis 1; at least all of the "sovereignly elected-chosen" ones, which included HIMSELF. This gave Calvin the right to practise Old Testament passages on New Testament Christians (specifically Exod. 22:18; Deut. 21:18, 25:3)---which he did. And this is why no right-thinking Baptist would ever call himself a "Calvinist" or a "Lutheran." My "dog tag" says "Bible-believing Baptist" and will never say anything else.]{.class_3527}

[No, Calvin's problem came about because of his inability to read. When he read this text (2 Tim. 1:9) and Ephesians 1:4, he insisted (against all Greek texts, all French texts, all German texts, all English texts, and all manuscript evidence behind all translations) that the saved Christians in his day were ]{.class_3527}["IN CHRIST"]{.class_3528}[ before Genesis 1:1. That is, ACTUALLY ]{.class_3527}["in Christ"]{.class_3528}[ because the text before us is written to people who were ACTUALLY]{.class_3527}[ "in Christ"]{.class_3528}[ as those of Ephesians 2:10 and Colossians 2:5--6. "[BUT IS NOW MADE MANIFEST by the appearing of our Saviour Jesus Christ" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 10). Now the "critical texts" receive their "due reward." The ["Saviour" ]{.class_3529}is either connected with[ "the LORD"]{.class_3529} (see Luke 2:11) or JESUS CHRIST (see Acts 13:23; Phil. 3:20; Titus 1:4). He is called ["JESUS" ]{.class_3529}(not "Christ") because He will ["SAVE his people from their sins"]{.class_3529} (Matt. 1:21). "CHRIST" is a [title]{.class_3530}. "Jesus" is a [name]{.class_3530}. The Saviour's NAME is ["Jesus"]{.class_3529} (Acts 4:12). Hence, ["OUR SAVIOUR JESUS CHRIST"]{.class_3529} ([AV ]{.class_3530}text opposed to any forty- five others).

"[Abolished death\...brought immortality to light"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). God's purpose was not "manifest" until Jesus Christ showed up, for no one was "IN CHRIST" [before ]{.class_3530}Jesus showed up. The Calvinists have confused God's purposes in eternity with His operations in TIME. ["Abolished death"]{.class_3529} (vs. 10) in the sense of Hebrews 2:14--15 and John 16:11. Death is not done away with until at least 1,000 years from now (Rev. 20:14). But it is ["abolished"]{.class_3529} in the sense that it no longer has power to intimidate the believer and no longer has power to keep him down if he is buried (see 1 Cor. 15:49--55). ["OH DEATH, WHERE IS THY STING?"]{.class_3529}

[Life and immortality are "brought to light" in that both desirable items were obscure until Matthew 28, Luke 24, Mark 16, and John 20. Everyone wants to stay alive; everyone wants to live forever. Even if conditions of physical life are intolerable and suicide is desirable at times (see Job 3:20--21 and Rev. 9:6), man's nature still drives him to survive if there is a spark of hope anywhere. ]{.class_3527}["LIFE" is the desired commodity.]{.class_3526}[ Men don't want to die. LIVING may be intolerable (as when under torture, for example), but LIFE is desired and has always been desired.]{.class_3527}

When the poor Russian peasants (in twice as much enslavement now as they were under the Czars) throw a sprig of a fir tree into the grave of a dead loved one, they weep. Communism ends at the grave---right where Marxism, Democracy, Catholicism, Buddhism, Taoism, Atheism, Pantheism, Mohammedanism, Hinduism, Fascism, Nazism, and Humanism end. YOU CAN'T TAKE YOUR OWN RIGHTEOUSNESS BEYOND THE GRAVE if you are lost. You leave everything---religion first.

[The desire for immortality is what led the Egyptians to leave supplies for dead loved ones by the mummy. It caused others to leave flowers to smell or food to eat or weapons with which to hunt, by the grave. Science, philosophy, and psychiatry have nothing to offer to satisfy it, absolutely nothing, nor does education or religion. ]{.class_3527}["HE THAT HATH THE SON HATH LIFE; AND HE THAT HATH NOT THE SON OF GOD HATH NOT LIFE" ]{.class_3528}[(1 John 5:12), and that is all there is to it.]{.class_3527}

"[Immortality"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). Observe how the word is used to match 1 Timothy 6:16. It is the ability to resist decay and deterioration and live forever in an incorruptible (1 Cor. 15:53--54) BODY (1 Cor. 15:35--36) with ["flesh and bones"]{.class_3529} (Luke 24:39).

"[This verse and the next are considered by some scholars to be cited from a CHRISTIAN HYMN" (Guthrie, p. 128) "\...it would be necessary to SUPPOSE that the apostle had himself adopted his own teaching to a suitable LITURGICAL FORM" (see 1 Tim. 1:17 for more of the same "scholarly" horseplay). "[A preacher\...an apostle\...a teacher"]{.class_3528} (vs. 11). This is "the ministry" which Paul mentioned in 1 Timothy 1:12.

"[A preacher"]{.class_3528} (vs. 11). Like Jesus Christ, Paul was primarily a PREACHER (see 1 Tim. 2:7 and comments). [Preachers ]{.class_3530}are what the world cannot stand. They can "put up" with TEACHERS; as a matter of fact, they will "heap teachers to themselves" in the Laodecian age, but [PREACHERS ]{.class_3530}are neither wanted nor needed where sin is relished or cherished. A PREACHER "preaches at you." He doesn't "SHARE" anything. "Sharing ministries" come today from people who have never been called to preach. A preacher will CRY with a loud voice (Isa. 58:1).[ He will yell at you.]{.class_3530} "Nice" Christians and people with "refined sensibilities" simply will not take that; the Pharisees and scribes didn't.

[You see, the real thing they had against Jesus Christ was his ]{.class_3527}[PREACHING]{.class_3526}[. If all He had done was ]{.class_3527}[teach ]{.class_3526}[something with which they disagreed (see Matt. 12:2, 19:3, for example, or John 7:27, 8:5, for example), He might have "passed," but the Lord Jesus Christ never confined His teaching to "sharing" ideas or "sharing Scriptures" or "sharing thoughts" or "sharing God's love." In His PREACHING (see Matt. 23:33, 21:43, 46; Mark 7:6; Luke 11:52; John 8:44, 2:16), He ripped, blasted, scalded, steamed, "tore hide," "peeled the bark," "shelled the corn," and came out with the most VICIOUS (Matt. 23:17), SCATHING (Matt. 12:34), SARCASTIC (Matt. 15:14), RUDE (Matt. 8:22), and OFFENSIVE (Matt. 16:3) diatribes known to man. Any man who follows his "example" (1 Pet. 2:21) and walks "in his steps" (Sheldon, etc.) is obligated by integrity and honesty to OFFEND several hundred people so badly (John 6:60--66) that they DESERT HIM. "[An apostle"]{.class_3528} (vs. 11). A "sent one" (αποστελλω). Not one of ["the twelve apostles of the Lamb" ]{.class_3529}(see Rev. 21:14), but one of the "eight" who was "born out of due time" (see Apollos, Silas, Timothy, Barnabas, et al.).

"[A teacher"]{.class_3528} (vs. 11). See comments under 1 Timothy 2:7.

"[I also suffer\...I am not ashamed\...I know whom I have believed, and I am persuaded"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). This sums up Paul's feelings about his calling and ministry. He is suffering for it as all will who ["live]{.class_3529} [godly in Christ Jesus"]{.class_3529} (2 Tim. 3:12). He is not ashamed to suffer for the LORD'S SAKE (see Acts 21:13). He has assurance that he has believed on One who is worthy of his own sacrifice (Rom. 12:1--3), and he is fully persuaded that he is saved and saved eternally, being in the hands of One who keeps what is entrusted to Him, and places it (Eph. 4:30) where Satan cannot get it (Rom. 8:29--39). This is "Paul's persuasion" (which was the old word for "denomination"). Paul believed in receiving mud instead of medals, and he counts the brickbats thrown at him instead of the bouquets (2 Cor. 11:23--29). His glory is in his wounds, stripes (2 Cor. 11:23), and infirmities (2 Cor. 12:10), not his "write-ups" in the [Sword ]{.class_3530}and his niche in the "Hall of Fame" or his "widely read articles in the [Fundamentalist Journal."]{.class_3530}

"[Against that day"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). The day of Christ (2 Thess. 2:1--2), which for the believer begins with the Judgment Seat of Christ (1 Cor. 3:10--16; 2 Cor. 5:10; Rom. 14:10), where the PURCHASED POSSESSION (Eph. 1:13--14) is permanently redeemed (Rom. 8:29) and confirmed sinless forever (Phil. 3:20--21; 1 John 3:1--3).

"[HOLD FAST the form of sound words"]{.class_3528} (vs. 13). ["Hold fast that which is good"]{.class_3529} (1 Thess. 5:21). ["Hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing" ]{.class_3529}(Heb. 3:6). ["Hold fast the profession of our faith"]{.class_3529} (Heb. 10:23).[ "Holding fast the faithful word"]{.class_3529} (Titus 1:9). ["Take fast hold of instruction"]{.class_3529} (Prov. 4:13). You see, the instructions were not limited just to the GREEK words that Paul spoke in GREEK to Timothy (["which thou hast heard of me"]{.class_3529}), although that is the primary application. The words come over into ["THE LAST DAYS"]{.class_3529} of the same epistle (2 Tim. 3:1) and warn the child of God that the ["FORM OF SOUND WORDS"]{.class_3529} should be retained and maintained. These WORDS were mentioned by the Lord Jesus Christ in John 8:47 and John 14:23, and the FORM of those words is to be "held fast."

1:15 This thou knowest, that all they which are in Asia be turned away from me; of whom are Phygellus and Hermogenes.

16 The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus; for he oft refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain:

17 But, when he was in Rome, he sought me out very diligently, and found me.

18 The Lord grant unto him that he may find mercy of the Lord in that day: and in how many things he ministered unto me at Ephesus, thou knowest very well.

[Before launching into the minsterial charge (2:1--26), Paul brings the young pastor up-to-date on his present affairs: ]{.class_3527}["all they which are in Asia be turned away from me"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 15). Although the majority of commentators sense that the "turning away" was from Paul because of his bad testimony with the Roman officials (]{.class_3527}["from ME"]{.class_3528}[), Sumner Wemp of Liberty University insists that the "turning away" was "positional from the truth" (p. 644). He makes Phygellus and Hermogenes "apostates," although there is no point in doing so, for "apostasy," according to all apostate Fundamentalists (including Wemp and the man for whom he works and the entire staff with which he works), is confined to UNSAVED LIBERALS. No, Paul is talking about how he himself was treated upon his arrest. His Asiatic buddies, like Demas, "ditched him" (2 Tim. 4:10): like some of Roloff's friends ditched him, and like nearly ALL of Haralan Popov's friends ditched him. ]{.class_3527}["Asia," ]{.class_3528}[here of course, is not Asia Major (China, India, Japan, etc.) but Asia Minor (Turkey). Paul merely adds some names to a list about which Timothy knows (]{.class_3527}["this thou knowest"]{.class_3528}[ vs. 15). ]{.class_3527}["ALL"]{.class_3528}[ is used here in the sense of Ephesians 6:21, Romans 1:8, and other places where all "without distinction" is meant---not without exception.]{.class_3527}

"[The Lord give mercy unto the house of]{.class_3528} [Onesiphorus"]{.class_3529} (vs. 16). Alford, the great Greek scholar (they all think their own kind are "great"), claims this as a prayer for a dead man (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, p. 1376). Bernard and Easton also adopt this Roman Catholic private interpretation to support the Catholic blasphemy about praying for the dead (Guthrie, p. 135). ["The Lord give mercy unto the house of Onesiphorus\...The Lord grant unto HIM that he may find mercy of the Lord in that day"]{.class_3529} (vss. 16, 18). Paul singles out the one friend that stood by him while he was being arrested and held before being shipped off to Rome. He prays for mercy on him as an individual and on his family because of him. Onesiphorus lives in Ephesus (see 4:19), so this probably is where Paul was arrested. Onesiphorus not only ministered to Paul before his arrest (see 1:18), but after he had been carted off by the IRS and the Soviet Catholic government in Washington, D.C. (Just kiddin', folks, just kiddin'!) He followed after him and went through Rome with a "fine-toothed comb" until he found his prison. He had to seek for Paul ["diligently"]{.class_3529} (vs. 17) in that teeming metropolis in order to find him.

[The scholars take the expression ]{.class_3527}["IN THAT DAY"]{.class_3528}[ to be a reference to the ]{.class_3527}[White Throne Judgment]{.class_3526}[ of Revelation 20, which of course it is NOT. No saved child of God needs a prayer for mercy in THAT day, for he has already been exactly like Jesus Christ for a thousand years ]{.class_3527}[before ]{.class_3526}["THAT day"]{.class_3528}[ shows up. But since not one of these Bible-correcting, Bible-rejecting, Bible-perverting dunces can rightly divide ]{.class_3527}["the word of truth"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Tim. 2:15---]{.class_3527}[And NOTICE what they do with THAT text when they get to it!]{.class_3526}[), they cannot handle the matter. Sumner Wemp, of course, knows the truth, but he slyly pleads the fifth amendment and says merely: "What reward there will be IN THAT DAY for those who minister to the ministers!" (p. 645).]{.class_3527}

[D. Edmond Hiebert sheds some typical Alexandrian light on verse 18 by telling us that "the insertion of the word ]{.class_3527}['UNTO ME']{.class_3528}[ in the ]{.class_3527}[King James Version]{.class_3526}[ rests on insufficient manuscript evidence" (op cit, p. 50). So do Brother Hiebert's comments. "Insufficient manuscript evidence" was never any problem with the ]{.class_3527}[NASV ]{.class_3526}[translators when giving a proof text for Arianism in John 1:18. They did as good a job as Judge Rutherford and Pastor Russell could have done with an "original autograph." Nestle kept Mark 16:7--20 clear out of his text for ninety years when the "sufficient evidence" he had for doing it was TWO uncial manuscripts and less than five other manuscripts. Don't waste our time talking about "insufficient manuscript evidence" just because you don't like the ]{.class_3527}[King James's]{.class_3526}[ text.]{.class_3527}

"[For he oft refreshed me, and was not ashamed of my chain"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16). When he found Paul, he ran errands for him. He brought him cool water and maybe some fruit juices to drink, and if Paul looked a little "run-down," Onesiphorus took Dr. Luke's prescriptions (see 4:11) to the drugstore and got the right stuff. It didn't bother him to be associated with a criminal in the slammer. He knew what God thought of the "criminal," and he probably knew something about Matthew 11:2 and Matthew 25:36.

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 Thou therefore, my son, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.

2 And the things that thou hast heard of me among many witnesses, the same commit thou to faithful men, who shall be able to teach others also.

3 Thou therefore endure hardness, as a good soldier of Jesus Christ.

4 No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of this life; that he may please him who hath chosen him to be a soldier.

5 And if a man also strive for masteries, yet is he not crowned, except he strive lawfully.

6 The husbandman that laboureth must be first partaker of the fruits.

7 Consider what I say; and the Lord give thee understanding in all things.

[Now the ]{.class_3527}[charges ]{.class_3526}[start in earnest. The first four verses liken the ministry to ARMED WARFARE (see 1 Tim. 1:18). Bible-believing teachers are to teach Bible-believing teachers to teach other saved teachers.

[Soldiers have to be ]{.class_3527}[hard]{.class_3526}[. Infantry men are called "doughboys" or "dog-faces" or "grunts." They have to be tough. The soldiers of WWII were twice as tough as those who are now in the army, and the soldiers of WWI were tougher than their sons who fought in WWII. The "hard face" (see Prov. 21:29 and comment) or "lion's mouth" (1Chron. 12:8) is the mark of the soldier who has ]{.class_3527}[endured hardships.]{.class_3526}[ Being in hot water all the time makes you hard-boiled. We are not speaking here of hardening the heart (Heb. 3:8) against God, when applying Paul's admonition to the young ministers (plural: not Timothy only!). We are not talking about hardening yourself against people as such. We are talking about a Damascus-Steel, Bessemer-Iron, Granite-Rock reaction to persecution, slander, lack of funds, false brethren, inconsistent Christians, scheming deacons, blabber-mouthed females in the local assembly, destructive critics of the Holy Bible (]{.class_3527}[say, anyone named in this commentary]{.class_3526}[), sleepless days and nights, lack of food or clothing, and oppositions of the world, science, flesh, philosophy, and the devil.]{.class_3527}

[Our examples in these matters are Irwin Rommel, Sepp Dietrich, General Patton, Capt. Whittlesey, Audie Murphy, Col. McAuliffe, Sergeant York, General MacArthur, Chesty Puller, and a list of over five hundred Congressional Medal winners published in ]{.class_3527}[America's Medal of Honor Recipients,]{.class_3526}[ Highland Publishers, Golden Valley, Minnesota, 1980.]{.class_3527}

Nearly any unsaved man in combat goes through more for his own government than an American Christian (1900--2000) goes through for the TRUTH OF GOD. You must be able to sleep in the rain. You may have to sleep with your "coveralls" stuck to the ground by the ice and frost. The K-rations may not come up every time, and you may have to fight after having had nothing to eat for forty-eight hours. You will see men go crazy around you ("jungle jolly," "combat fatigue," "shell shock").

[You will see your best friends and sometimes relatives killed. You may find one of your buddies strung up on a jungle trail with certain parts of his body cut off and stuck in his mouth. You may lie in a rice paddy waiting for gangrene to rot off your leg after getting a wound that you can't dress and the medic can't reach. You may lose a leg going through a mine field; you may have a grenade go off between your legs. You may be caught below "C-deck" when the whole ship is a raging caldron of burning oil and gasoline. You may drown or be eaten by sharks as you swim for your life by night to get away from a sinking ship. They may capture you and put a cage on your head with live rats in it. They may nail you in a four-by-four-by-four box studded with nails to get you to give more than your "name, rank, and serial number," and you may pull off your foot when you pull off your boot, as your leg from the ankle down may have been ]{.class_3527}[frozen solid ]{.class_3526}[for four hours.]{.class_3527}

[That is what is in the text. The "original Greek" could no more bring it out than flush a Kodiak bear, and the shallow, simple-minded, stupid revisors, commentators, and Greek teachers could no more expound the text than they could play ping-pong on the floor of the Atlantic Ocean. Christian]{.class_3527}[ "HARDNESS"]{.class_3528}[ is something that will not be found on any board of ]{.class_3527}[Bible translators ]{.class_3526}[or ]{.class_3527}[publishers ]{.class_3526}[since 1800. observe: ]{.class_3527}["AS A GOOD SOLDIER OF JESUS CHRIST"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 3).]{.class_3527}

The soldier referred to was an infantryman, if you are to believe the Bible (Eph. 6:10--14). He was the "dog face," grunt, etc., referred to above. Paratroopers are airborne infantry, and the Marines (up to 1946 at least) were waterborne infantry. A good soldier, according to the Holy Bible (see the TO and TE in 1 Chronicles 12:8, 30, 32, 36, 37), must:

1. Be able to keep rank and not fall back so that the unit next to him is flanked.

2. Be able to fire "expert" with more than one weapon and know how to clean, fire, and fieldstrip the enemy's weapons.

3. Use wisdom in planning tactics according to "the situation and the terrain."

4. Be brave and attack when he is scared, and fight when he doesn't feel like fighting.

5. Be able to cross natural obstacles: he must know how to run, climb, swim, hide, march, and jump (sometimes carrying 70--150 pounds with him).

6. Be able to use EITHER hand in handling weapons in case he loses an eye or a hand.

7. Be able to identify enemy planes, tanks, troops, ensignia, ships, shells, and troop movements.

[In addition to these things, a truly thoroughly trained soldier must not over or underestimate his enemy's strength; he must obey orders under ALL circumstances (]{.class_3527}[Befehl ist Befehl]{.class_3526}[), and he must trust his commanders and superiors in the most trying situations.]{.class_3527}

"[ENDURE HARDNESS, AS A GOOD SOLDIER OF JESUS CHRIST"]{.class_3528} (vs. 3). "The original Greek" yields nothing. The "oldest and best manuscripts" yield [nothing]{.class_3530}. "[No man that warreth entangleth himself with the affairs of THIS LIFE"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4). If you are too busy to pray, you are too busy. If you are too busy to witness, you are too busy. If you are too busy to read the Book, you are too busy. You have your "forepaws" caught in the underbrush. ("Paw, you acomin' in ta supper?" "Naw, son, not right now." "Well, Maw says hits been on the table since sundown. When is you a'comin?" "Later. Cain't right now." "Well why not, Paw?" "Cause mah foot's caught in a bar trap.") That's the problem a lot of "soldiers" have these days (see 1 Tim. 6:9--10 and remarks).

[A soldier is supposed to receive ]{.class_3527}["food and raiment"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Tim. 6:8) at government expense. In my day (1938, 1939) it was: "A dollar a day an' the work ain't hard. Never hurry, never worry, never volunteer. Stay away from the Orderly Room; keep your mouth shut. If it's movin', salute it; if it's lying down, pick it up; and if you can't pick it up, PAINT IT." An infantry lieutenant in my day (1942--1944) got \$120 a month. With that came free food, free uniforms, and free lodging, and the usual \$10,000 to the "nearest" if things didn't "work out too well." You wore your UNIFORM into town (not your "civies"), and the uniform had a necktie.]{.class_3527}[ You were conspicuous by your non-civilian appearance.]{.class_3526}[ One might say you were a ]{.class_3527}["PECULIAR PEOPLE." ]{.class_3528}[You were ]{.class_3527}[separated]{.class_3526}[. You were apart from the "affairs of this life" because you were a G.I., which means "Government Issue." You belonged to "Uncle Sam" (see Rom. 13:1--2).]{.class_3527}

"[That he may please HIM who hath chosen him to be a SOLDIER"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4). [Lt. Max Schoening]{.class_3526}[ falls off a 300-foot ledge in the Aleutians and drops ten feet, staggers ten feet, bounces ten feet, rolls fifty feet, drops off twenty feet, rolls sixty feet, drops off thirty feet, etc., and after six months in the hospital with a broken leg, broken wrist, three broken ribs, a broken jaw and nose, cuts and bruises, is back up in the snow-covered mountains, on skis, in twenty-degrees-below-zero weather.]{.class_3527}

[Paul Baumer's company commander]{.class_3526}[ (1918) crawls out of a shell hole cover to fire on a flame thrower about to "lightup" his men, and lying in the prone position, he gets hit in the shoulder. How marvelously this contrasts with the "total sports" picture in America where every other coach and every other athlete before the TV camera is babbling like a mockingbird. ''Winning is everything!" "The real thing that counts is winning!" "We're Number One! We want some winners!" I have seen an American team of Negros disqualified in a relay race when they were fifty feet ahead of the nearest competitor (Russian) and had outraced every opponent on the track. They blew the hand-off on the last stretch.

In swimming, if you get out of your lane, you are out of the race. Every game on earth has rules that tell you when a ball is a strike or a ball, whether it is foul or fair, whether it is in or out of bounds, whether it was a complete or incomplete pass, whether it was fumbled or kept, and whether it was caught or dropped. You cannot "ice" the puck in hockey or have a teammate across the blue line before the puck carrier. To get rid of sticky fingers in volleyball, they finally made all of the teams hit it with one hand locked in the other one.

The acid test is never WINNING. ]{.class_3527}[The acid test is LOSING ]{.class_3526}[(see Matt. 10:39, Luke 22:42--45, Phil. 3:7--10). A famous, soulwinning preacher in America wrote a number of books about raising children and the home. Naturally, he couldn't apply what he wrote. He had told his sons that they always could be "winners." They didn't ever have to "lose."]{.class_3527}

[That was a very foolish thing to tell any man, let alone a young man. ]{.class_3527}[YOU WILL LOSE somewhere.]{.class_3526}[ If the devil doesn't get your wife, he'll get your kids. If he doesn't get your family, you will go bankrupt. If you make the payments, you will lose your health. No, Virginia, there is no Santa Claus.
