"[The husbandman that LABOURETH"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). Observe: the first phase of the ministry is pain, suffering, hardship, self-denial, discipline, obedience, toughness, and bravery. The second phase of the ministry is self-discipline, training, honesty, exertion, concentration, coordination, motivation, self denial, and alertness. The third phase of the ministry is sweat, hard work, attention to the business at hand, carefulness, concern, prayer, and anticipation.

[The farmer or vinedresser has to stay by his crops and work the ground (Prov. 27:23--27). With the soldier it was "no fighting, no victory." With the athlete it was "no striving, no mastery," and with the farmer it is "no work, no crop." Ground must be plowed (see comments under 4:2). The wall has to be built to keep the foxes out (Song of Sol. 2:15; Lam. 5:18; Isa. 5:1--7). A tower must be put up for a caretaker (Matt. 21:33). The laborer is entitled to eat from the vine he tends (1 Cor. 9:7), and when he enters the work ("hires out"), he is given a taste of some of his crop before he is really entitled to it (]{.class_3527}["must be first partaker of the fruits"]{.class_3528}[).]{.class_3527}

[The clause above has the primary meaning (obviously) that the first man to partake of the fruit when it is produced is the man who worked on it (1 Cor. 9:7). There is another meaning hidden in the English which is ]{.class_3527}[not ]{.class_3526}[discernable in any "Greek originals" (or the "original autographs" or the "oldest and best Greek manuscripts" or the men that idolized them or professed to have known what was in them). This meaning would be discernable only to a soulwinner who "began from scratch" as a street preacher and later was entrusted with a large work. Since NONE OF THE COMMENTATORS, REVISERS, TRANSLATORS, OR "RECOGNIZED" TEACHERS SINCE 1800 come under this heading, none of ]{.class_3527}[them ]{.class_3526}[can find the "nugget."]{.class_3527}

[The nugget is that when you hire out to work for God for ]{.class_3527}["whatsoever is right"]{.class_3528}[ (Matt. 20:4), the Lord gives you a foretaste (some ]{.class_3527}["handfuls of purpose,"]{.class_3528}[ Ruth 2:16) of the good things to come. Even before you are equipped to be a soulwinner, while you are yet a "babe in Christ" and so green that if someone stuck you in the ground you would "root," God, in His mercy, allows you to PARTAKE OF THE FRUITS (Gal. 5:22) that He is going to give you for working in the vineyard. ]{.class_3527}["MUST BE FIRST partaker of the fruits."]{.class_3528}[ ]{.class_3527}[You have to have a sample before you go to work! ]{.class_3526}[And God doesn't fail to give it, as anyone with any experience knows.]{.class_3527}

But since Mounce, Guthrie, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Dummelow, Stibbs, Vincent, Rendall, White, Alford, and Wuest know NOTHING about these matters, they must confine themselves to the "grammatical construction" where the "emphatic expression" of the "Greek article" dictates that we translate "FLIBBERTY JIBBET" instead of "FLIPPITY FLOP."

"[Consider what I say"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). We will. ["And the Lord give thee understanding in all things"]{.class_3529} (vs. 7). We trust that He will if we believe what He said, as He said it, where He said it.

"[No man that warreth"]{.class_3528} is BETTER RENDERED as "no soldier on service" (Guthrie, p. 140). Got out of [combat]{.class_3530}, didn't you, you old yellow belly? 2:8 Remember that Jesus Christ of the seed of David was raised from the dead according to my gospel:

9 Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds; but the word of God is not bound.

10 Therefore I endure all things for the elect's sake, that they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.

11 It is a faithful saying: For if we be dead with him, we shall also live with him:

12 If we suffer, we shall also reign with him: if we deny him, he also will deny us:

13 If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.

14 Of these things put them in remembrance, charging them before the Lord that they strive not about words to no profit, but to the subverting of the hearers.

The young preacher is first told to remember something (vs. 8), and then he is told to put his congregation in remembrance of something (vs. 14). This starts and completes the section.

"[According to MY GOSPEL"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). This is the full revelation of the Gospel (1 Cor. 15:1--6) with all that God showed Paul along with it (see Rom. 2:16). It includes not merely the bare facts of the "Gospel" (the death, burial, and resurrection of Christ), but the revelations given to Paul that [accompany ]{.class_3530}that Gospel, hence he sometimes calls it ["MY GOSPEL." ]{.class_3529}He has a right to do this because of Galatians 1:6--12, which see.

"[Wherein I suffer trouble, as an evil doer, even unto bonds"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9). The first part of the verse is self-explanatory. Such obvious truths are voiced in 2 Corinthians 4:7; 1 Corinthians 4:10--13; and Philippians 1:27--29, as well as 1 Peter 3:14--15. The world looks at a Bible-believing preacher as a threat to humanity and an enemy of ["the people" ]{.class_3529}(see Acts 21:28 and comments in that commentary).

"[The word of God is not BOUND"]{.class_3528} (vs. 9).[ Look out!]{.class_3530} Once ["the word of God"]{.class_3529} shows up (see 2 Tim. 2:15 and 2 Tim. 3:16 and comments), there is going to be a panic in the faculty lounge, where the "reverent Biblicists" and "visiting" professors and pastor "emerituses" are sitting around on their fannies ridiculing the [Authorized Version.]{.class_3530}

[1. ]{.class_3527}["The word of God is not BOUND"]{.class_3528}[ by anything, which includes the COVERS of a Book.]{.class_3527}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["The word of God"]{.class_3528}[ cannot be bound with anything because it is ALIVE and MOVING.]{.class_3527}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["The word of God"]{.class_3528}[ today (never mind trying to limit it to one message about one thing done by one Man Who died nineteen hundred years ago, you dead orthodox lounge lizards) is not bound by age limits, sex distinction, stations in life, or race. It appeals to all, rich and poor, young and old, Caucasian, Negroid, or Mongoloid; and it cannot be bound or limited to preachers, Christians, teachers, or Westerners. [4. ]{.class_3527}["The word of God"]{.class_3528}[ cannot be bound by time, circumstances, critics, skeptics, or infidels. You can shut the preacher up, but you can't shut up the Book from which he preaches. IT GETS OUTSIDE THE COVERS.]{.class_3527}

"[THE WORD OF GOD IS NOT BOUND."]{.class_3528}

[The commentators, for the umpteenth time, don't have a "tad" of an idea about what they are talking. They were simply running for cover when the old, roaring ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[Lion of 1611 bounced out of the bushes at them.]{.class_3527}

"[Therefore I endure all things for the elect's sake"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10), which, you must admit, would be pretty stupid if you were a Five Point Calvinist, for according to "TULIP," ALL OF THE ELECT WOULD GET SAVED WHETHER ANYONE ENDURED ANYTHING OR NOT. (Note the same problem the hardshells have with 2 Peter 3:9, when God's ["longsuffering"]{.class_3529} is to no purpose if the ["any"]{.class_3529} and ["all"]{.class_3529} are the "elect." In God's divine, eternal decrees, NONE OF THESE WOULD PERISH no matter who suffered or [didn't]{.class_3530} suffer, or how long they did or didn't. TULIP sniffers have a time of it, don't they?)

"[That they may also obtain the salvation which is in Christ Jesus WITH ETERNAL GLORY"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). Observe that all Hyper-Calvinists leave out the last three words. They read the passage as "I endure all things so the elect may get saved," which, as we have said, is nonsense. In Calvin's system, the "elect" are predestinated to be saved before ADAM. If Paul went to sleep under a shade tree and then got up and became a tentmaker the rest of his life, it would not effect the salvation of one "elect," one hundred years [before or after ]{.class_3530}his death; that is, if Calvin were right. But Calvin was wrong. The verse was talking about the elect obtaining something above SALVATION. Not ["with eternal glory"]{.class_3529} (vs. 10). Paul's suffering was for converts AFTER they were saved (see Gal. 4:19; Phil. 1:7--10; and 2 Cor. 6:11--13). It was in order to get something more for them than a reservation in New Jerusalem. Observe that the suffering here, as in 1:12, 2:3, 2:9, 2:12, 3:10, and 4:14, has to do with the complete ministry: winning men to Christ and then rooting and grounding them in the faith (see Phil. 1:27 and Col. 1:22--23 and comments in that commentary).

"[ETERNAL GLORY"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10) includes a Millennial reign right in the context (see vs. 12) and rewards (vss. 20--21). Observe that REIGNING (vs. 12) [is conditioned upon SUFFERING]{.class_3530} (vs. 12), and in Colossians 3:24, it [is conditional upon SERVING]{.class_3530}. The reign in glory is an earned reward and has nothing to do with "getting the elect saved." Mark how clearly the Holy Spirit brings this out in the [English text]{.class_3530}, independently of any Greek scholar's comments or opinions, for in verses 12 and 13, He gives two conflicting statements if they are applied to the "salvation of the elect." (Wait till you see the Alexandrian Cult hit those two verses!) Verse 12 says He will DENY you; verse 13 says He CANNOT. Obviously, they are talking about two different things.

[Since Falwell at Lynchburg (Liberty University) hasn't the "foggiest" idea about what ]{.class_3527}[either verse]{.class_3526}[ is talking, his staff helped produce a "bible" that said the ]{.class_3527}["BELIEVE NOT"]{.class_3528}[ in verse 13 was not ]{.class_3527}["believe NOT"]{.class_3528}[ but was "faithless," and the ]{.class_3527}["SUFFER"]{.class_3528}[ in verse 12 was not "suffer" but "endure." When their commentator (Sumner Wemp) faced this mishmash, all of his sensibilities left him, and he declared that you can deny Christ and not be denied if your inward condition is not like those of 1 John 2:19.]{.class_3527}

Nothing could be further from the truth.

[To reinforce this Biblical interpretation of the Bible passages by the Author Himself, we find coming up immediately the warning that ALL of the Fundamentalists, Conservatives, Catholics, Liberals, Modernists, Humanists, Atheists, and "godly Evangelicals" IGNORED: ]{.class_3527}["STUDY\...RIGHTLY DIVIDING THE]{.class_3528}[ ]{.class_3527}[WORD OF TRUTH."]{.class_3528}

"[OF THESE THINGS PUT THEM IN REMEMBERANCE"]{.class_3528} (vs. 14). We just did, and THEY didn't because they never had the passage divided correctly in order to "remind" anyone of it.

"[Strive not about words to no profit"]{.class_3528} (vs. 14); say, for example, the fifteen thousand words they strove about in Vincent's [Word Studies,]{.class_3530} Trench's [Synonyms,]{.class_3530} Zodhiates' [Bible Study Helps,]{.class_3530} Rendall's [The Expositors Greek Testament,]{.class_3530} and Bullinger's [The Companion Bible.]{.class_3530}

"[But to the SUBVERTING OF THE HEARERS"]{.class_3528} (vs. 14). Observe the [real ]{.class_3530}purpose of Greek scholars and higher Christian education in this century. The word "SUBVERT" here is the one found in Acts 15:24 and Titus 1:11. [It means to bring you under the authority and control of someone other than God.]{.class_3530} If you want to see it demonstrated, read the ads in the [Sword of the Lord]{.class_3530} on "Study Bibles" or read Truman Dollar's sermon to his congregation at Temple Baptist Church, printed in the [Bible Believers' Bulletin.]{.class_3530} That is "subverting." Now, let us go back to the "scene of the crime" and survey the massive pile of debris heaped on the corpse. [Laying aside all of the "more accurate versions" which "better bring out the meaning of the original," depositing all "readable translations" that "preserve the essential fundamental doctrines of the faith," and dumping all "qualified, recognized, Fundamentalist scholars," let us look at the rarest thing on earth today: ]{.class_3527}[THE HOLY BIBLE.]{.class_3526}

"[Study"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15). You are to [read ]{.class_3530}the Scriptures (Deut. 17:19); you are to [believe ]{.class_3530}them (Psa. 119:160); you are to [meditate ]{.class_3530}on them (Psa. 119:15); you are to [pray ]{.class_3530}for God to reveal their meanings to you (Psa. 119:18, 34); you are to [claim ]{.class_3530}His promise that He will (Prov. 22:17--21); you are to [pour over them]{.class_3530} night and day (Psa. 1:2); and you are to [study ]{.class_3530}their similitudes (Hos. 12:10) and contrasts (Amos 3:3) to ascertain their doctrinal meanings (2 Tim. 3:16). "[Study to shew thyself approved unto God"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15)---not approved by Thomas Nelson publishers, not the Trinitarian Bible Society, not the British Foreign Missionary Society, not the United Bible Societies, not the World Congress of Fundamentalism, not the International Board of Jewish Missions, not the "Back to the Bible" broadcast, not the examining board for the doctorate, and not the faculty and staff at ANY university or college in the world: ["APPROVED UNTO GOD"]{.class_3529} (vs. 15). ["For not he that commendeth himself is approved, but whom the Lord commendeth"]{.class_3529} (2 Cor. 10:18). No Scholars' Union composed of boot-licking, back-slapping, self-congratulating fools---who recognize those who recognize those who recognize those who recognize [each other]{.class_3530}---is referred to within thirty thousand verses of 2 Timothy 2:15 in any direction. The pastor is to study so that he will please God, and God will back him up.

"[RIGHTLY DIVIDING the word of truth"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15). The expression is not found in one "reliable translation" on the market, nor has it been since 1885. "DIVISION" (as Segregation and Separation) is the cuss word of the twentieth century, and not even the "double-separated" brethren can tolerate ["DIVIDING THE WORD OF TRUTH."]{.class_3529} They want only the "fundamentals," which can be used as a rallying point for financial support. [There are ]{.class_3527}[divisions]{.class_3526}[. Things that are different are not equal.]{.class_3527}

1. No Old Testament saint was "born again"; none were "chosen in Christ" before or after salvation. None of them were spiritually circumcised, and none of them were "adopted" as literal SONS.

2. Faith and WORKS are part of Tribulation salvation and Millennial salvation (see the comments on Heb. 3:6, 14; 6:4--8; and 10:26--31; and the comments on Rev. 12:17; 14:12; and 22:14 in those commentaries).

5. Eternal salvation by grace through faith, APART FROM GOOD WORKS OF ANY KIND, was the official declaration of the "Prince of the Apostles," the Council of Jerusalem, and the Holy Ghost (Acts 15:23, 26, 28), fifty years before the word "Catholic" was applied to any Christian anywhere in the world.

"[And their word will eat as doth a canker: of]{.class_3528} [whom is Hymenaeus and Philetus" ]{.class_3529}(vs. 17). The statement was ]{.class_3527}[they had taught the resurrection was PAST.]{.class_3526}[ We see now why the scholars above were so anxious to drag the "Gnostics" in on it. The people who were spiritualizing ]{.class_3527}["the FIRST RESURRECTION"]{.class_3528}[ (Rev. 20:4--5) were Greek scholars in the congregation, who were saved, AMILLENNIAL teachers; and saved, POSTMILLENNIAL teachers. [Hiebert ]{.class_3526}[immediately applies the teaching to unsaved rationalists and unsaved liberals (p. 71) so as to get his twentieth-century fellow Christians "out of hock." We leave them on the pawn shop shelf.]{.class_3527}

"[The FIRST RESURRECTION"]{.class_3528} is a physical resurrection (Rev. 20:2--5), and it is [future]{.class_3530}, and it is not the SECOND RESURRECTION (WHICH IS [ALSO ]{.class_3530}FUTURE; Rev. 20:11--15). In fact, there are seven Resurrections in the Bible (Matt. 27:52; Rom. 1:4; Eph. 2:1--6; 1 Thess. 4:16--17; Matt. 24:31; Ezek. 37; Rev. 20:12), exactly as there are seven Baptisms (Matt. 3:6; Acts 2:38; 1 Cor. 12:13; 1 Cor. 10:2; Matt. 3:12; Matt. 28:19--20; Matt. 20:22) and seven Judgments (2 Cor. 5:21; 1 Cor. 11:31; 1 Cor. 3:10--16; Matt. 25:32; Isa. 4:4; Rev. 20:12--15; 1 Cor. 6:2--4). But those can only be found by STUDYING and ["RIGHTLY DIVIDING the word of truth."]{.class_3529}

"[Nevertheless the foundation of God standeth sure, having this seal"]{.class_3528} (v. 19).

"[The foundation of God"]{.class_3528} turns out to be Simon Peter's confession (Mounce, p. 128) or the "True Church of God" (Hiebert, p. 71) or "the church" (Vincent, p. 304) or Christ Himself (Wemp, p. 647); there are Scripture references for all four positions. Comparing Scripture with Scripture (Eph. 2:20; 1 Cor. 3:11), the foundation would be limited to Christ and the apostles. (The word in 1 Tim. 3:15 was not ["foundation"]{.class_3529} at all; it was ["pillar and ground."]{.class_3529}) However the context of 2 Timothy 2:19 is much better than putting "seals" on Jesus Christ or stamping them on churches. The context is someone's FAITH being overthrown, and this overthrown FAITH is compared with a FAITH that [cannot ]{.class_3530}be overthrown (cf. vs. 18 with vs. 19). [One foundation did not stand sure, and the other one did]{.class_3530}.

[This English reading is much preferable to all of the suggestions made by the Greek: ]{.class_3527}[Expositor's Testament, Word Studies, Synonyms, "Riches from the Greek New Testament,"]{.class_3526}[ and similar worthless publications. ]{.class_3527}["The foundation of God"]{.class_3528}[ is the faith that God laid and refers to the same use of the word that we find in 1 Timothy 1:2, 4, 19; 2 Timothy 3:8, 4:7; and Titus 1:1, 4. This faith has two parts to it, likened to a seal that closes up a letter with information in it. The wax is poured, and the King's seal (see Dan. 6:8 and Esther 3:12) is stamped onto the letter. The seal says two thing:]{.class_3527}

"[The Lord knoweth them that are His"]{.class_3528} (vs. 19). When "push comes to shove," only THREE PERSONS in the universe know for certain whether you are saved or lost: 1. You, 2. God, 3. the Devil. The rest of us take your word for it, one way or another. The Lord knows His sheep (John 10:14), even though they were UNKNOWN to Him as individuals before they trusted Him (see Gal. 4:9 and comments, for this piece of anti-Calvinism).

"[And, Let every one that nameth the name of Christ depart from iniquity"]{.class_3528} (vs. 19). 2:20 But in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and of silver, but also of wood and of earth; and some to honour, and some to dishonour.

21 If a man therefore purge himself from these, he shall be a vessel unto honor, sanctified, and meet for the master's use, and prepared unto every good work.

22 Flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith, charity, peace, with them that call on the Lord out of a pure heart.

23 But foolish and unlearned questions avoid, knowing that they do gender strifes.

24 And the servant of the Lord must not strive; but be gentle unto all men, apt to teach, patient,

25 In meekness instructing those that oppose themselves; if God peradventure will give them repentance to the acknowledging of the truth;

26 And that they may recover themselves out of the snare of the devil, who are taken captive by him at his will.

[Now an application is made, but the application certainly doesn't indicate that Hymenaeus was ]{.class_3527}[lost]{.class_3526}[, any more than Demas, Diotrephes, Ananias, Philetus, or "Alexander." [What follows is IN GOD'S ]{.class_3527}["HOUSE"]{.class_3528}[ (see vs. 20), and what is cautioned against is the really dedicated Christian fellowshipping with ]{.class_3527}[other ]{.class_3526}[Christians LIKE Hymenaeus and Philetus. (See the same thing in 1 Cor. 5:11 and Rom. 16:17.) "[Vessels of gold and of silver\...wood and of earth\...some to honour and some to dishonour"]{.class_3528} (vs. 20).

"[Vessels"]{.class_3528} in this verse refers to people, as in Romans 9:21. Note, however, that in Romans 9 the reference is to "believers and unbelievers," for it refers to God shaping any kind of clay into any kind of vessel. Here, [to the contrary,]{.class_3530} the reference was to the household of God, where God is desiring to USE the vessels in the ministry or in some type of service. The illustration is graphic, and the best exposition of the passage is a sermon by James Modlish (pastor of Faith Baptist Church in Chehalis, WA). He points out that every home had vessels for carrying water; these were not the vessels used to carry out urine. They had vessels to carry food in; these were not the vessels used to carry dirt after sweeping the house. God has all kinds of jobs.[ "A vessel unto honour\...meet for the master's use"]{.class_3529} (vs. 21) is a vessel that is ["prepared unto every good work"]{.class_3529} (vs. 21), showing that the ["vessel"]{.class_3529} had to READ THE SCRIPTURES, for ["THROUGHLY FURNISHED UNTO ALL GOOD WORKS" ]{.class_3529}(2 Tim. 3:17) comes from knowing the Scriptures---NOT THE "ORIGINAL AUTOGRAPHS" (see 2 Tim. 3:16 and comments). This vessel, in the context, must ["depart from iniquity"]{.class_3529} (vs. 19), and further instructions are given in the next few verses: ["flee also youthful lusts: but follow righteousness, faith\...."]{.class_3529}

[Since the scholars are still suffering from their engagement with verses 11--13, they trip over the ]{.class_3527}["vessels"]{.class_3528}[ in this passage. "[Meet for the master's use"]{.class_3528} (vs. 21) is the old English for a "match-mate," as it is found in Genesis 2:18 and Luke 15:32. Obviously it means "fitting"---something that comes up to meet something else. Observe how bravely and boldly all of the new, "updated" translations (written by men who profess to be "clearing up the archaic words of the [Authorized Bible"]{.class_3530}) never miss a chance to change these words, although they do "NOT EFFECT ONE HISTORIC, FUNDAMENTAL DOCTRINE OF THE FAITH." "[Flee also youthful lusts"]{.class_3528} (vs. 22). You see, what these modern, apostate, Fundamentalists have done is, they have talked themselves into believing they are "GODLY" on the grounds of two false presumptions:

[The ]{.class_3527}[NASV ]{.class_3526}[refuses to accept all of Vincent's private interpretation, but since it can't stand the thought of the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[text any more than he can, it erases ]{.class_3527}["captive by him AT his will"]{.class_3528}[ and conjectures "held captive by him to DO his will." CHAPTER THREE

3:1 This know also, that in the last days perilous times shall come.

2 For men shall be lovers of their own selves, covetous, boasters, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy,

3 Without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent, fierce, despisers of those that are good,

4 Traitors, heady, highminded, lovers of pleasure more than lovers of God;

5 Having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof: from such turn away.

6 For of this sort are they which creep into houses, and lead captive silly women laden with sins, led away with divers lusts,

7 Ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth.

8 Now as Jannes and Jambres withstood Moses, so do these also resist the truth: men of corrupt minds, reprobate concerning the faith.

9 But they shall proceed no further: for their folly shall be made manifest unto all men, as theirs also was.

[The passage applies directly to 1901 to 2001. ]{.class_3527}["The last days"]{.class_3528}[ in the passage is naturally mistaken for the days that ]{.class_3527}[begin ]{.class_3526}[with the Book of Acts (see comments in the ]{.class_3527}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Hebrews]{.class_3526}[, Heb. 1:2) by many of the scholars, although Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown get it right this time.]{.class_3527}

[Having erased the direct application to their own times (Hiebert, Mounce, Sumner, Guthrie, Dummelow, Wemp, Afman, Price, Martin, Willmington, Dobson, Hindson, Swindoll, MacArthur, Schaff, Robertson, et al.) by pretending that Paul couldn't have given Timothy anything that would apply LATER (look at Matt. 24:15 and Matt. 25:31!), Hiebert feels he must say ]{.class_3527}[something ]{.class_3526}[to show that he hasn't completely gone off his rocker, so he says: "Every informed believer is painfully aware of the fearful apostasy that is sweeping over Christendom in our times" (ibid). But we know ]{.class_3527}[how ]{.class_3526}[it started, ]{.class_3527}[who ]{.class_3526}[espoused it, what they ]{.class_3527}[did ]{.class_3526}[with the material they had, ]{.class_3527}[where ]{.class_3526}[they got that material, who ]{.class_3527}[believed them]{.class_3526}[ and followed them, and the ]{.class_3527}[results ]{.class_3526}[of their work (]{.class_3527}[The History of the New Testament Church]{.class_3526}[, Vol. II, chap. 9).]{.class_3527}

[What we have here is a warning that the basic sins of the Adamic nature will increase as time goes by, and in the days immediately preceding the Rapture (1901--1990), they will be matured to the place where they will be the outstanding characteristics of the general populace. This will be brought about by the news media, the NEA, and mass communications, as all of these organizations are humanistic and secular from top to bottom (including the Vatican press, hierarchy, and priesthood) and are aimed at PUFFING mankind: deifying man as his own savior (i.e., ]{.class_3527}["Man is the measure of all things"]{.class_3526}[). Hence the tirade begins with ]{.class_3527}["LOVERS OF THEIR OWN SELVES."]{.class_3528}[ The outstanding mark of mankind in general, before the Advent, is given here, and again in Matthew 24:12. It is ]{.class_3527}[self-love.]{.class_3526}[ It is the belief that man is smart enough to correct God, smart enough to disobey God, smart enough to eliminate God, and smart enough to invent his own God, fashioned after his own depravity (see Rom. 1:22--23). [This last generation, therefore, will be marked by the psychiatric sign of "compensation." They will ]{.class_3527}[talk ]{.class_3526}[about nothing but LOVE. "[Covetous, boasters, proud"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). Can't get enough land, can't build enough buildings, can't lay back enough in savings, can't get too much money. Bragging about endowments, busses, holdings, property, Sunday School attendance (see Rev. 3:17), ability to correct the Bible, number of women raped, number of "scores" on Saturday night, number of children abused, number of murders. "[Blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, unholy"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). They damn the fireman, the brakeman, and the engineer. Their mouths are ["full of cursing and bitterness"]{.class_3529} (Rom. 3:14). They damn the nail and the hammer, the saw and the bulldozer, the highway and the trucks, the drivers and the road. (Every now and then God answers their prayers and sends a plane down in flames or rolls the truck over three times on the Interstate.) They want "children's rights." They will not take out the garbage. They will sue their parents if their parents whip them, etc., and don't tell us this WAS A WORLDWIDE CHARACTERISTIC OF TIMOTHY'S DAY! Who are you trying to kid? It wasn't a general thing even in 1920 or 1940. It is in ["THE LAST DAYS."]{.class_3529} Some children have always disobeyed their parents, but Paul is not talking about occasional sins by individuals; he is talking about ["MEN"]{.class_3529} (vs. 1) in the last days. Modern children murder their parents (see 1 Tim. 1:9) for not letting them look at a TV show (try THAT in Timothy's day!)

"[Unthankful, unholy"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). Bingo with liquor on the [church grounds. ]{.class_3530}Flipping the [rosary ]{.class_3530}and then killing Protestant missionaries in Colombia. Eating at a Smorgasbord without bowing the head over the plate to ask a blessing. Griping about taxes, depressions, "recessions," bills, and government when their bellies are full and they have food on the table and money in the bank.

"[Without natural affection, trucebreakers, false accusers, incontinent"]{.class_3528} (vs. 3). Sex perverts, baby killers, child molesters: without NATURAL affection. ["Trucebreakers"]{.class_3529}: not one treaty signed in the last five hundred years has been worth the paper it was written on. A "Nuclear Ban Treaty" in 1989 would be a bigger joke than the Pope claiming popes didn't support Jewish genocide for a thousand years. "Ceasefire" truces and peace treaties today are nothing but tragic farces. NOT ONE MAN AT THE TABLE HAS ANY HIGHER AUTHORITY FOR ETHICS OR MORALS THAN POLITICAL EXPEDIENCY. Why didn't someone who was ]{.class_3527}["ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth"]{.class_3528}[ deliver her to ]{.class_3527}["Satan for the destruction of the flesh"]{.class_3528}[? My, my! Timothy's grandmother couldn't have been a "true child of God" (see 2:17 and comments) if she was teaching such an "injurious doctrine" (ibid)!]{.class_3527}

[Timothy thought he was reading ]{.class_3527}["HOLY SCRIPTURES\...GIVEN BY INSPIRATION OF GOD"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 15--16), and the Holy Spirit who wrote those Scriptures told Paul to WRITE it in that fashion. ]{.class_3527}[That is how it is written.]{.class_3526}

[In regards to the "Every scripture inspired is" (]{.class_3527}[RV, ASV,]{.class_3526}[ and other abominable corruptions), "The placing of the verb 'IS' in the translation (]{.class_3527}[RV ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[ASV]{.class_3526}[)\...is a strange unaccountable and serious BLUNDER" (Miller, ]{.class_3527}[General Biblical Introduction,]{.class_3526}[ Word Bearer Press, Houghton N.T., 1937).]{.class_3527}

"[Unaccountable"? Not at all, brother Miller! You underestimate the intelligence of the "godly" translators. They saw the problem immediately. If they allowed "]{.class_3527}[ALL ]{.class_3530}scripture" to be "inspired," it would extend to the ones ON WHICH Timothy was raised, AND THESE WERE NOT THE "ORIGINAL AUTOGRAPHS." "[It cannot be defended\...it is a violation of the law of Greek syntax" (ibid, p. 32).]{.class_3527}

[What?! The "good, godly, qualified men on the ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[committee, recommended by Theodore Epp, R. A. Torrey, G. Christian Weiss, Bob Jones Jr., B. H. Carroll, and every major Christian school in America would "violate" the laws of "Greek syntax" ]{.class_3527}[after correcting the AV in 30,000 places with "the Greek"?]{.class_3526}[ What? How could you say such a thing, Brother Miller!?]{.class_3527}

"[There is no rule in the Greek language which would permit such a construction. Here is a subject and two predicate adjectives connected by a coordinate conjunction." (Gee, it must be great to be ]{.class_3527}["ever learning,"]{.class_3529} right?) "Both adjectives must be predicate if either is, and if neither is a predicate adjective, there is no complete sentence" (ibid, p. 32).

Oh, that's all right, Miller, ole' son; THEY DID THE SAME THING IN 1 TIMOTHY 3:16 WHEN DEALING WITH THE FIRST FUNDAMENTAL OF THE FAITH (see 1 Tim. 3:16 and comments on the sentence that had a subject and no predicate).

"[The copulative verb ]{.class_3527}εστι (esti) is omitted, as is very often the case when it can be readily supplied. The place for this verb is between the subject and the adjective (or between the subject and the first of two or more adjectives), and it CANNOT be taken from that place and placed between the two adjectives\...here are a few samples." (The author then lists Heb. 4:12--13; 1 Cor. 11:30; 1 Tim. 2:3; 2 Cor. 10:10; and 1 Tim. 1:15, 4:9.)

[Well, has the reader had enough? You know by now that the ]{.class_3527}[King James ]{.class_3526}[translators in 1611 knew more about GREEK SYNTAX than the American translators of the ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[(1901), which included Dr. Thayer (]{.class_3527}[who wrote a Greek lexicon!]{.class_3526}[) and Dr. Riddle, Dr. Washburn, Dr. Dwight, Dr. Kendrick, Dr. Abbot, Bishop Lee, President Chase, and Professor Short. When in doubt, always throw out 100 percent of the "godly, dedicated, qualified, recognized authorities." They are ]{.class_3527}["ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth." "THE TRUTH"]{.class_3528}[ is the reading of 2 Timothy 3:16 in an ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[(any edition), and the LIE is the reading of the ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[(1901) in any edition.]{.class_3527}

[Now, what about those]{.class_3527}[ "scriptures"]{.class_3528}[ and that ]{.class_3527}["scripture"]{.class_3528}[?]{.class_3527}

[Having proved that the ]{.class_3527}[motive ]{.class_3526}[behind attacking the Holy Bible is hatred and fear, and having proved that Timothy ]{.class_3527}[read ]{.class_3526}[Scriptures that were "God-breathed" (if one goes by the Greek instead of the English, which we don't), and having proved that the]{.class_3527}[ "scriptures"]{.class_3528}[ here are certainly not limited to "lost, original autographs" Timothy did ]{.class_3527}[not ]{.class_3526}[have, there remains only one thing to do: overthrow all "historic positions" of all "historic fundamentalists" who were stupid enough to respect (see 2 Tim. 2:15 and comments) the INFIDELITY of twentieth-century, "recognized" scolarship. We do the most damaging thing we could possibly do: we turn to the Holy Spirit to see how the words ]{.class_3527}["SCRIPTURE"]{.class_3528}[ and ]{.class_3527}["SCRIPTURES"]{.class_3528}[ are used in His own Book.]{.class_3527}

[1. ]{.class_3527}["Did ye never read in the scriptures"]{.class_3528}[? (Matt. 21:42). A useless question by Jesus Christ if the people to whom He was talking couldn't have read them anyway, and they could ]{.class_3527}[not ]{.class_3526}[have read them if they were a reference to "original autographs."]{.class_3527}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["Ye know not the scriptures"]{.class_3528}[ (Mark 12:24), which they couldn't know ]{.class_3527}[anyway ]{.class_3526}[if they were "original autographs."]{.class_3527}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["This day is THIS scripture"]{.class_3528}[ (Luke 4:21), which is nonsense if the ]{.class_3527}["scripture"]{.class_3528}[ was an original autograph. No synagogue in Luke 4 had an "original autograph" of Isaiah, and if they did, THE ETHIOPIAN EUNUCH STOLE IT, FOR HE HAD THE ]{.class_3527}["SCRIPTURE"]{.class_3528}[ OF ISAIAH ON HIM (see Acts 8:32, 35).]{.class_3527}

[4. ]{.class_3527}["Searched the scriptures daily"]{.class_3528}[ (Acts 17:11). They did nothing of the kind if "God-breathed Scripture" is a reference to "original, inerrant, infallible, inspired autographs."]{.class_3527}

[5. ]{.class_3527}["That we through patience and comfort of the scriptures"]{.class_3528}[ (Rom. 15:4). Impossible. No one can receive ]{.class_3527}["comfort"]{.class_3528}[ from some lost scraps of paper and vellum that never got put together in one book.]{.class_3527}

Has any reader gotten the message yet? Well, if not, then here it is---fifty-two on the table face up, ball over the plate waist-high, straight-shot, right out of the barrel, dead-center in the bull.

[1. The Holy Spirit and the Holy Bible ]{.class_3527}[never ]{.class_3526}[use the term ]{.class_3527}["SCRIPTURE"]{.class_3528}[ as it is used by modern Greek and Hebrew scholars. The cultic private interpretation of what they ]{.class_3527}[think ]{.class_3526}[the word ]{.class_3527}[means ]{.class_3526}[is ignored by the Holy Spirit in both Testaments. THEIR TEACHING IS THE TEACHING OF A NON-BIBLICAL CULT (see Appendix IV). Not one verse in either Testament even SUGGESTS that the ]{.class_3527}["scriptures"]{.class_3528}[ were unavailable manuscripts.]{.class_3527}

[2. No matter how "godly" and "dedicated" the Fundamentalist is who uses the Cult terminology, it is still anti-scriptural, anti-biblical, anti-Christian, and is found in the mouth of NO CHRISTIAN IN THE NEW TESTAMENT OR ANY SAINT IN THE OLD TESTAMENT. ]{.class_3527}[It is non-Christian language.]{.class_3526}[ It is the language of educated idiots in a seminary who have been educated beyond their intelligence (see Appendix IV).]{.class_3527}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["THE HOLY SCRIPTURES"]{.class_3528}[ are words that you ]{.class_3527}[read]{.class_3526}[. Timothy read them. Paul read them. Christ read them. If ]{.class_3527}[you ]{.class_3526}[can't read them, then the God who ]{.class_3527}[inspired ]{.class_3526}[them was not able to ]{.class_3527}[preserve ]{.class_3526}[them. No theory, no number of alibis, reasons, proofs, "facts," or opinions contribute anything to the problem. EVERY VERSE IN EITHER TESTAMENT BELIES THE CONFESSION THAT the "verbally inspired, original autographs" are the subject of 2 Timothy 3:15--16. There are no competitors, there are no "options" in the Scriptures themselves. ]{.class_3527}[They are absolutely silent on "inerrant, infallible, original autographs."]{.class_3526}[ THAT IS AN ALEXANDRIAN CULT CRUTCH (see Appendix XV).]{.class_3527}

[Now, behold! Here are two apostates who read and believed what you just read. Department at Bob Jones University (Tootsie Wootsie Custer). Both men saw the problem: i.e., ]{.class_3527}[the position they had been taught]{.class_3526}[ was NON-SCRIPTURAL. So both of them did a remarkable thing to make things "jive." Custer---you wouldn't believe this unless you saw the letter as we printed it in the ]{.class_3527}[Bible Believers' Bulletin]{.class_3526}[---claimed that he had a VERBALLY INSPIRED GREEK NEW TESTAMENT WHICH HE READ EVERY DAY (see Appendix XI). This was Nestle's German edition from Stuttgart, that later altered over seven hundred readings, comprising over nine hundred words, ]{.class_3527}[which they had omitted for ninety years in their edition!]{.class_3526}[ (See Appendix V.) Kenneth Taylor was much smarter. He simply changed the "copulative verb" (gee, isn't it great to be ]{.class_3527}["ever learning, and never able to come to the knowledge of the truth"]{.class_3528}[?) from ]{.class_3527}["IS"]{.class_3528}[ to "WAS." This was the ideal "cop out." This made all Scripture in the PAST where no one, including Timothy or Paul, could read it. For it only]{.class_3527}[ "WAS]{.class_3526}[ GIVEN BY INSPIRATION OF GOD"---i.e., at the time of the original writing. To this day we don't know why Fundamental scholars condemn the]{.class_3527}[ Living Bible,]{.class_3526}[ for it states their own doctrinal convictions on inspiration ]{.class_3527}[in the exact creedal form in which they teach them and have taught them for two hundred years.]{.class_3526}

"[DECEIVING, AND BEING DECEIVED."]{.class_3528}

Terrible Timothy terrorizes tyrants.

[When John R. Rice wrote ]{.class_3527}[Our God-Breathed Bible,]{.class_3526}[ he was not referring to any Bible at which any of his readers had ever looked, and if he was talking about the "originals," he ]{.class_3527}[lied]{.class_3526}[, for the originals were NEVER IN ANY BIBLE ON THIS EARTH! A Bible is a ]{.class_3527}[book]{.class_3526}[, and no book on this earth contained the "original autographs" of Moses, Isaiah, Peter, and John in one cover.]{.class_3527}

"[DECEIVING, AND BEING DECEIVED"]{.class_3528} (see Appendix XXIII).

[There are two kinds of Christians in America today: ]{.class_3527}[those who believe THE BOOK and those who don't.]{.class_3526}

I did not create the schism; it was the "unhappy work" of the Holy Spirit. Blame Him, not me.

"[Which are able to make thee wise"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15) and ["able to build you up"]{.class_3529} (Acts 20:32) and able to [regenerate ]{.class_3530}you (1 Pet. 1:23) and able to give you the [new]{.class_3530} [birth]{.class_3530} (James 1:18) and able to [strengthen ]{.class_3530}you (Psa. 119:28), and able to [warn ]{.class_3530}you (Psa. 19:11) and able to [comfort ]{.class_3530}you (Rom. 15:4) and [admonish ]{.class_3530}you (1 Cor. 10:11) and several other things. (See the difference between Biblical exposition and seminary claptrap?) The ["scriptures"]{.class_3529} can lead a man to salvation, for they testify of the Life-giver Who gives life to them (see John 5:39). Since God BREATHED into these writings, they are ALIVE (see Heb. 4:12--13). Any [AV ]{.class_3530}edition, even if 370 years out of date, has more "LIFE" in it than any edition of a [NASV ]{.class_3530}or [NIV ]{.class_3530}that will come out in the next twenty years.

"[GIVEN BY INSPIRATION OF GOD"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16). We have said a lot about "God-breathed," for that is the Greek (θεοπνευστος, theopneustos) to which all apostates run to justify their anti-scriptural, anti-Christian, anti-biblical CULT above (which we call "The Alexandrian Cult"); however, we "prefer" (you might have known this!) the "original English" over the "original Greek." We take ["GIVEN BY INSPIRATION" ]{.class_3529}to be the proper way to treat the Scriptures which TIMOTHY and Christ and Paul and the eunuch (Acts 8:32) read.

[We do not refer to the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[as the "verbally inspired, inerrant Word of God." We refer to the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[as the ]{.class_3527}[Holy Bible, ]{.class_3526}[inerrant and infallible, ]{.class_3527}[preserved ]{.class_3526}[by the grace of God in our language, with the ]{.class_3527}[words ]{.class_3526}[that God wants us to have. That is our position, that has been our position, and that will be our position until "Hell freezes over," as they say in the "Koine."]{.class_3527}

[The ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[was GIVEN to us by God in spite of (or because of, either way) its board, translators, revisers, kingly advice, time in history attending circumstances, and everything else. We never have to worry about what text it came from, who brought it there, why they brought it, what they believed, or any other ALEXANDRIAN RUSE called to our attention to get us to abandon it (see Appendix XVI). ]{.class_3527}[We have the word of God.]{.class_3526}[ We have the ]{.class_3527}[words ]{.class_3526}[of God. Like Timothy, we have the ]{.class_3527}["HOLY SCRIPTURES."]{.class_3528}[ I have a copy right here on my desk. It is not in Greek. Neither was the copy the Eunuch was reading. YOU CAN'T FIND AN OLD TESTAMENT IN GREEK ON THIS EARTH UNTIL FIFTY YEARS AFTER THE COMPLETION OF THE NEW TESTAMENT (see Appendix XIV).]{.class_3527}

[Now, we are not going to park all night by 2 Timothy 3:16, but the peddlers and corrupters of God's word (2 Cor. 2:17---removed from the ]{.class_3527}[NIV, NKJV, ASV, ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[NASV]{.class_3526}[) think they are ]{.class_3527}[superior ]{.class_3526}[to the product of the "learned men" of King James. They are going to devote their entire lifetimes to destroying faith in that Book while USING it to make a living (see Appendix II). This has been their consistent practice since 1885, and they are not about to repent now. So for the benefit of our reader, we will lay before his eyes the truths and problems that none of these men discuss, no matter what route they take in order to get rid of your Book. When we point out that word-for-word preservation is not necessary, they will cry "heresy!" and in the same breath admit that ]{.class_3527}[no translation ]{.class_3526}[can be "word-for-word." This brings with it the untenable conjecture that GOD CEASES BREATHING WHEN A TRANSLATOR SITS DOWN. If the reverse is taken, then these incorrigible fakirs will claim that He SAT DOWN WITH THEM as much as He did with anyone else! There is no cure for apostasy. Here is the typical "simplistic" mentality of these matters:]{.class_3527}

"[The present, ]{.class_3527}[imperfect ]{.class_3530}condition of the Biblical text, which has [suffered ]{.class_3530}in its transmission through fallible scribes, is no ground for concluding against the [original text ]{.class_3530}being inspired \[although no such matter is mentioned directly or indirectly in any present condition of 2 Tim. 3:15--16\]. It is rather a reason why all CRITICAL diligence should be applied to seek to restore THE ORIGINAL TEXT as nearly as possible." Which is a subject not mentioned directly or indirectly [in either Testament, ]{.class_3530}except in regard to CRITICAL DILIGENCE being Satanic.

"[The ]{.class_3527}[present condition ]{.class_3530}of the text is no ground for conluding against the [original text ]{.class_3530}being inspired. \[You talk about "following a man"! I'm quoting TWO different authorities!\] But is a reason why we should use all CRITICAL DILIGENCE \[amazing, and hair raising, ain't it?\] to restore the [original ]{.class_3530}inspired text."

[Now compare those two statements. That is what you call "Alexandrian Apostolic succession." One is by ]{.class_3527}[D. Edmon Hiebert]{.class_3526}[ (p. 101), and the other is ]{.class_3527}[Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown]{.class_3526}[ (p. 1381). Neither apostate professes to be quoting the other. ]{.class_3527}[They are both motivated by one spirit.]{.class_3526}[ Their position is a "historic position" that has been handed down from one DESTRUCTIVE CRITIC to another and placed into the faculty and staff lounges of EVERY MAJOR CHRISTIAN COLLEGE, UNIVERSITY, SEMINARY, AND BIBLE INSTITUTE IN AMERICA AND EUROPE.]{.class_3527}

It is accepted as a holy decree, a dogmatic fiat, a divinely-inspired "rule of faith" by which to test "heretics."

[We consider it to be the vulgar prank of a ]{.class_3527}[spoiled brat ]{.class_3526}[(see Appendices I and III).]{.class_3527}

[Nothing either man said was connected with anything in 2 Timothy 3:15--16, nor did anything either man said have anything to do with the way the word ]{.class_3527}["scripture"]{.class_3528}[ is used in the ]{.class_3527}["scripture"]{.class_3528}[ in either Testament.]{.class_3527}

[A lot of "historic, fundamentalist positions" are just like ]{.class_3527}[that]{.class_3526}[.]{.class_3527}

[Let's cut the kiddy-car stuff, all right? Instead of tucking our tails between our legs when the Liberals and Modernists show up---like B. B. Warfield, A. T. Robertson, Philip Schaff, Westcott and Hort, Thayer and Trench, Sumner, Vincent, and Machen, Wuest and Zodhiates, Anderson and Willmington did---let us see what lies beneath all of this propane gas about "plenary, verbal inspiration of the ]{.class_3527}[inerrant]{.class_3526}[, infallible, original autographs."]{.class_3527}

[1. Paul wrote letters that you couldn't prove were inspired or uninspired ]{.class_3527}[if you found them,]{.class_3526}[ and if you thought they were, you couldn't put them into the New Testament (Rev. 22:18; Prov. 30:6).]{.class_3527}

[2. To assume that Paul was "inspired" only thirteen times (Romans--Philemon) in twenty-five to forty years is a bad hypothesis. If ]{.class_3527}["Holy men of God spake as they were moved by the Holy Ghost"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Pet. 1:21), what are these amateurs doing telling us that the Book of Jonah is all that Jonah wrote under inspiration---]{.class_3527}[if he wrote it at all?]{.class_3526}

[3. Did God move Nahum during ten to twenty years of prophecy to ]{.class_3527}[SPEAK ]{.class_3526}[what you find in only three pages of scripture? It said ]{.class_3527}["SPAKE"]{.class_3528}[ (not wrote) ]{.class_3527}["as they were moved by the Holy Ghost"]{.class_3528}[ (2 Pet. 1:21).]{.class_3527}

[4. If you found ]{.class_3527}["the book of Jasher,"]{.class_3528}[ could you prove it wasn't ]{.class_3527}[inspired]{.class_3526}[? On ]{.class_3527}[what ]{.class_3526}[grounds? That God didn't choose to include it in the ]{.class_3527}[canon]{.class_3526}[? Is that ]{.class_3527}[proof ]{.class_3526}[it wasn't inspired? Can you prove that Peter's "original autographs" weren't inspired if three or four of his letters "didn't make the grade" into the canon? If there is inspired literature ]{.class_3527}[outside ]{.class_3526}[of the canon, should it be ]{.class_3527}[in ]{.class_3526}[the canon? ON WHAT GROUNDS? THERE ISN'T ONE VERSE IN THAT BOOK THAT SAYS EVERY SCRIPTURE THAT GOD "INSPIRED" HAD TO BE COLLECTED INTO SIXTY-SIX BOOKS OR A HUNDRED BOOKS OR FOUR HUNDRED BOOKS.]{.class_3527}

[5. Do you doubt ]{.class_3527}["the epistle from Laodicea" ]{.class_3528}[was inspired (Col. 4:16)? ]{.class_3527}[On what grounds?]{.class_3526}[ On the grounds that the Holy Spirit didn't lead the body of Christ to include it? Is that "scholarly"? Is that being "critically diligent"? ]{.class_3527}[According to what rules of textual criticism was THAT decision made?]{.class_3526}

Why, these silly old women (2 Tim. 3:6) don't know with what they are dealing.

THEY ARE DEALING WITH GOD ALMIGHTY, INSPIRING, SORTING, EDITING, COLLECTING, AND PRESERVING, WITHOUT THEIR KNOWLEDGE, UNDERSTANDING, PERMISSION, OR APPROVAL. They just want you to think that they "and their friends" had a part in it: HUMANISM.

[6. Peter, being an unlettered commercial fisherman, used ]{.class_3527}[perfect grammar ]{.class_3526}[in his "original autograph," according to all apostates. Do you know whether the letter ]{.class_3527}[you now have ]{.class_3526}[even CAME from the ]{.class_3527}[first ]{.class_3526}[copy he wrote? He couldn't have ]{.class_3527}[rewritten]{.class_3526}[ a copy? Jeremiah did (Jer. 36) ]{.class_3527}[and added words]{.class_3526}[ that weren't in the first copy. What if Peter's first "autograph" was ]{.class_3527}[not ]{.class_3526}[inspired? You say it ]{.class_3527}[had ]{.class_3526}[to be. But you just tried to tell us that all of the writings and sayings of the prophets that are not in the canon ]{.class_3527}[couldn't have been inspired; otherwise, they would be in the canon]{.class_3526}[ (points 4--5). Backing out?]{.class_3527}

Son, let me tell you something: this crew backed off Pike's Peak before World War I.

[7. If Moses and Pharaoh spoke ]{.class_3527}[Egyptian ]{.class_3526}[(which they did), yet the "original autograph" of Exodus was written in ]{.class_3527}[Hebrew ]{.class_3526}[(which it was), why did the Holy Spirit fail to "plenary" report "WORD FOR WORD" what took place? "You always lose something in a translation, and NO TRANSLATION IS INSPIRED" (Alexandrian Cult Creed).]{.class_3527}

Well, the Holy Spirit really "messed up," then, didn't He? He "lost something" coming from Egyptian to Hebrew in the "inerrant, infallible original" of Exodus and then compounded His confusion when He demanded that Matthew, Mark, Luke, John, and Paul TRANSLATE over forty Hebrew Old Testament texts into GREEK (see, for example, Matt. 1:23; 2:15; 3:3; etc.).

[These passages weren't ]{.class_3527}[inspired]{.class_3526}[, I suppose, since "NO TRANSLATION CAN BE INSPIRED"?]{.class_3527}

Why, these money-making, money-grabbing, money-loving (see 1 Tim. 6:10 and comments) hucksters are taking advantage of the fact that you are going to accept their heresies on the grounds of their reputations and their degrees and their ability to impress you with Greek grammar! 1. There is no priest class in the New Testament to determine inspiration or canon. There was in the Old Testament (see Mal. 2:6; Deut. 31:25--26; 2 Chron. 17:8--9; Ezra 7:12; Deut. 33:10).

5. Since that God who created the universe is able to preserve it, plus the life of man and beast (Isa. 57:16), He is able to create the "new man in Christ" (2 Cor. 5:17) and PRESERVE HIM (John 10:28; 2 Cor. 4:16; Phil. 1:6).

Every Mongolian, Indian, Malayan, Vietnamese, Australian, Persian, Norwegian, Eskimo, and Argentinian on this earth has to set his WATCH BY ENGLISH TIME. Furthermore, his location on this earth at any given moment, whether on land, in the sea, or in the air, is DETERMINED BY ENGLISH LATITUDE.]{.class_3527}

God has warned the world about two "absolutes," and the Christian scholars simply turned up their noses at the warning. As far as they were concerned, all absolutes can be EXPLAINED (humanism) by natural means (HUMANISM).

CHAPTER FOUR

4:1 I charge thee therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;

2 Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine.

3 For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears;

4 And they shall turn away their ears from the truth, and shall be turned unto fables.

5 But watch thou in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, make full proof of thy ministry.

[Here comes the last charge. This is Paul's "last will and testament." In the first five verses there are ]{.class_3527}[three sets]{.class_3526}[ of "threes" that describe some "charge." The first three charges are involved in the command to ]{.class_3527}["PREACH THE WORD."]{.class_3528}[ They are:]{.class_3527}

[1. ]{.class_3527}["Reprove,"]{.class_3528}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["Rebuke,"]{.class_3528}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["Exhort"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 2).]{.class_3527}

[1. They will ]{.class_3527}["heap to themselves teachers."]{.class_3528}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["They shall turn]{.class_3528}[ ]{.class_3527}[away\...from the truth"]{.class_3528}[ (note the difference between these and those of 3:7. These people here HAVE the truth and know the difference between sound doctrine and false doctrine.)]{.class_3527}

[3. They ]{.class_3527}["shall be turned unto fables."]{.class_3528}

[The third charge is ]{.class_3527}["WATCH THOU IN ALL THINGS"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 5), and this consists of three "watches."]{.class_3527}

[1. ]{.class_3527}["Endure afflictions."]{.class_3528}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["Do the work of an evangelist."]{.class_3528}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["Make full proof of thy ministry"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 5).]{.class_3527}

[The reader will observe the desperate and deceitful effort of the Scofield Board of Editors (p. 1304, footnote 1) to convince the last generation that APOSTASY can apply only to ]{.class_3527}[lost sinners. ]{.class_3526}[The Scofield note here is ]{.class_3527}[revealing]{.class_3526}[, to say the least. It shows perfectly the condition of Fundamentalism between 1950 and 1990.]{.class_3527}

[The note is specifically designed to ]{.class_3527}[protect ]{.class_3526}["godly" apostates who "mess with the Book." It says "'Apostasy, falling away,' is the act of PROFESSED Christians who deliberately reject REVEALED TRUTH as to\..." (p. 1304); there follow ]{.class_3527}[two ]{.class_3526}[items. With "revealed truth" running into at least seven fundamentals, twenty essentials, and hundred of important truths, the "apostate" is rejecting only TWO. "Apostasy, therefore, differs from error concerning truth which may be the result of ignorance\...THE APOSTATE IS PERFECTLY DESCRIBED IN 2 TIMOTHY 4:3--4."]{.class_3527}

[The ]{.class_3527}[two truths]{.class_3526}[ an "apostate" rejects (according to the Scofield Board of Editors---]{.class_3527}[without one shred of evidence in either Testament to suggest such a thing]{.class_3526}[) is the Deity of Christ---WHICH ALL UNSAVED CATHOLICS BELIEVE IN, and "His atoning and redeeming sacrifice"---WHICH ALL UNSAVED CATHOLICS BELIEVE IN.]{.class_3527}

[This time all of the saved Greek philosophers throw all of the Greek lexicons out the window. The word for ]{.class_3527}["apostasy" ]{.class_3526}[is the one found in 2 Thessalonians 2:3, and not even Vincent dares "study" it in his ]{.class_3527}[Word Studies]{.class_3526}[ (p. 63). With all of the Greek scholars and all of the Fundamentalist commentators running for their lives to avoid the blast of the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[text (see the same thing in 1 Tim. 6:5, 10, 20), ]{.class_3527}[they refuse to tell us what the word "APOSTASY" MEANS.]{.class_3526}[ It was from two Greek words which any sophomore Greek student knows: απο (apo) and ιστημι (histemi). What could be so frightening about a small, compound Greek word like that? Well, the terror lies in the fact that the word ιστημι (histemi) means: 1. To make STAND; 2. To set or appoint in place; 3. To set down or establish; 4. To confirm or to place in a standing position. απο (apo) obviously means "from," "forth from," or "away from."]{.class_3527}

[Then "apostasy" (]{.class_3527}[in any of the texts with which the scholars used to correct the AV more than 30,000 times]{.class_3526}[) was the one definition none of them could accept because it applied to THEM. ]{.class_3527}[So they made up definitions. ]{.class_3526}[The Scofield note is as "original" a note as you ever saw invented by any novelist on this earth. "APOSTASY" (2 Thess. 2:3) means "TO FALL AWAY FROM A STANDING POSITION." It never means anything else. If the man ]{.class_3527}[professed ]{.class_3526}[to be still "in position," he ]{.class_3527}[wasn't.]{.class_3526}[ The point is that he WAS STANDING IN THE POSITION once and fell away from it.]{.class_3527}

"[Professing Christians" were not connected directly, or indirectly, with anything in the word, in English or Greek.]{.class_3526}

[Scofield simply excused the Scholars' Union and the Alexandrian Cult from the charge of apostasy by ]{.class_3527}[inventing a definition]{.class_3526}[ that would help avoid their detection.
