Someone who "fell away from a STAND they took" (a "standing position") wanted you to ]{.class_3527}[think ]{.class_3526}[they were still standing; ]{.class_3527}[they weren't.]{.class_3526}[ They were flat on their faces before Dagon and Baal. They were saved Conservatives who "professed" to believe in ALL of the fundamentals of the faith, just like all Catholics profess them in the Apostles' Creed.]{.class_3527}

"[I charge thee therefore before God and the Lord Jesus Christ"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). This verse "slanders the Deity of Christ," if you are to believe the ridiculous charges brought against the [AV ]{.class_3530}text of Titus 2:13 by the Alexandrian Cult (in 1970--1980).

"[Who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). Notice the two judgments, separated by a thousand years. One is a resurrection of the "just" (Dan. 12:2--3) and the other the "unjust." One is when Christ appears in the clouds to take home His ["quickened"]{.class_3529} (Eph. 2:1--6) Bride, and the other will be at the [beginning ]{.class_3530}of His eternal reign (Luke 1:30--33; Isa. 9:7; Rev. 21), which is "HIS KINGDOM." These will be "THE DEAD," exactly as they are said to be in Revelation 20:11--15.

"[The quick"]{.class_3528} are obviously those of Ephesians 2:1--6, who were spiritually quickened when they received Christ (John 1:12--13). Note how carefully the Holy Spirit corrects all of the amillennial Campbellites, Catholics, and Presbyterians in these matters by pointing out to Martha that a believer in this age DOESN'T HAVE TO WAIT FOR THE RESURRECTION OF John 5:28--29. John 5:29 is the parking place for unsaved Catholic and Campbellites who are counting on their works to save them. John 11:25 shows that someone is coming up BEFORE the ["last day"]{.class_3529} (see John 11:26). The two resurrections are separated by one thousand years (Rev. 20:1--6).

"[Preach the word"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). Of course, if you don't [have ]{.class_3530}it to preach, you can't [preach ]{.class_3530}it. The Neo-Orthodox way to get around this---adopted by all of the faculty members at Atlantic Coast, Pacific Coast, Arlington, Springfield, Denver, Bob Jones, Lynchburg, Dallas, Piedmont, Pillsbury, Tennessee Temple, and other Alexandrian offshoots---is to pretend that the "word of God" is [not the words found written in the Scripture;]{.class_3530} it is just the "message" or the "gospel" or whatever.

[Paul commended his converts to ]{.class_3527}["THE WORD OF HIS GRACE"]{.class_3528}[ (Acts 20:32) and said the word of God was NOT the ]{.class_3527}["word of men" ]{.class_3528}[(1 Thess. 2:13). To all apostates, this means "MESSAGE." And then (in the same breath), these despicable cowards will tell us they believe "the Bible is the ]{.class_3527}[Word]{.class_3526}[ of God"! What does that mean? ]{.class_3527}[Message?]{.class_3526}[ The "Word" of God in the Bible (John 1:1--2) is Jesus Christ (1 John 1:1--2). The ]{.class_3527}[word ]{.class_3526}[(Psa. 119:89) is composed of ]{.class_3527}[words ]{.class_3526}[(John 17:17) that often have nothing to do with the Gospel at all (see Eccl. 12, for example) and are not part of a "message" which is to be preached to anyone (see 1 Chron. 1--6, for example).]{.class_3527}

"[Preach the word"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). That is the first, basic, fundamental imperative for a pastor or evangelist. Make sure you [have ]{.class_3530}the word, make sure you [believe ]{.class_3530}the word, and then PREACH IT. ["In season, out of season"]{.class_3529} (vs. 2): when you get visible results with it, when you don't; when you feel inspired, when you don't; when you are ablaze with a holy anointing that almost takes you out of the pulpit, and when you feel like a discarded dishrag that the cat dragged in.[ "In season, out of season"]{.class_3529} like you find Paul doing in 2 Corinthians 11:25--28. Sow when it rains, keep it up until you're white-headed (Eccl. 11:6): ["in season, out of season."]{.class_3529}

"[Reprove, rebuke, exhort"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). Observe how this contrasts with ["gentle patient, in meekness instructing those"]{.class_3529} (2 Tim. 2:24--25). Note also how the modern Charismatic humanists and ecumenical Catholics can get none of this placed right, any more than the unsaved Liberal can place ["peacemakers"]{.class_3529} right (Matt. 5:9). A "peacemaker" to a Catholic, Communist, atheist, or religious Liberal is a [political celebrity ]{.class_3530}who gets attention by demonstrating against armaments. Before World War II broke out, all of these people in America were advocating DISARMAMENT (1920--1936) while Hitler and Hirohito were preparing to invade Europe and Asia. They are the same people now (1989)\* who are leading in demonstrations to get rid of nuclear power and nuclear plants while Russia and China prepare for the same thing. "The only thing men learn from history is\...."

"[Reprove, rebuke, exhort"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). Here are three kinds of "preaching." ["REPROVE"]{.class_3529}: the word means to convince someone of something (see John 16:8). It amounts to correction where someone is wrong. ["Rebuke"]{.class_3529} is a much stronger term (see Titus 1:13; 2:15) and means in the modern vernacular to "bawl somebody out" or to give them a "non sequitor." A rebuke is "You blew it; don't do it again." "You had no right to do that; one more time, and you'll get it." "You're wrong."

"[Rebukes" in the Bible occur by handfuls and fistfuls: see Nathan talking to David (2 Sam. 12:7), Abraham talking to Abimelech (Gen. 21:25), David talking to Goliath (1 Sam. 17:45--47), Paul castigating Bar-Jesus (Acts 13:10--11), the people rebuking the King (Saul in 1 Sam. 14:45), Christ contradicting a mother (Matt. 12:48--49), Christ insulting a disciple (Matt. 16:23), Christ ignoring a request (Matt. 15:26), Elijah vilifying a king (1 Kings 21:21), and John telling a king where to head in (Mark 6:18). These negative passages amplify the degenerate character of modern, apostate Christianity. They show there has been a wholesale departure from "preaching the WORD" by the ministers. The ministry of the prophets is about seventy-five percent REBUKES (see Hos. 5:2 and Amos 5:10). A ]{.class_3527}["rebuke"]{.class_3529} is a weight laid on someone, so the word is often used to describe a painful burden that the sinner has to bear (see Heb. 12:5; Ezek. 25:15; Isa. 51:20; 60:15; etc.).

"[Exhort"]{.class_3528} is a stirring-up to action; the modern term is to "motivate." Literally, it refers to calling someone forward to take action and therefore resembles closely an "invitation" at the close of a service. Moses gives the real exhortation in Exodus 32:26, and there are many other samples in the Old Testament (see especially Ezra 10:10--11; Neh. 8:9--12; Job 38--39; 1 Chron. 29; and Jer. 43--44).

[Real preaching,]{.class_3526}[ then, is not confined to counselling, rapping, "coping," giving advice, making recommendations, or suggestions. ]{.class_3527}[Real preaching ]{.class_3526}[does not show up on the TV channels in America one hour out of forty-eight in a seven day week. Modern preaching (Fundamentalists and Conservatives included, plus Church of Christ, Mormons, Seventh-day Adventists, Catholics, and Charismatics) is a polished, smooth, cultured "pitch" designed to "win friends and influence people." It is not preaching at all. It is ]{.class_3527}[politicking]{.class_3526}[, pure and simple. It is "personality selling," using the Bible to put across the speaker, so the speaker will be established in the minds of the Bible rejector as a "helper," "comforter," "assistant," "concerned individual," or "patcher-up" of emotional, social, and family problems.]{.class_3527}

[One look at the list of "New Books" in ]{.class_3527}[Pulpit Helps]{.class_3526}[ (Spiros Zodhiates) will show the reader what is meant by this. The Christian bookstores are literally cluttered to the attic with this paper-backed rubbish that deals with nothing but how to "cope" with yourself or someone else. It is humanism gone to seed. Any twenty books published will sport seventeen on how ]{.class_3527}[you ]{.class_3526}[can adjust to ]{.class_3527}[yourself ]{.class_3526}[so that ]{.class_3527}[others ]{.class_3526}[can adjust to ]{.class_3527}[you ]{.class_3526}[or how you can adjust to ]{.class_3527}[them]{.class_3526}[. GOD, HEAVEN, HELL, THE BIBLE, AND SOULS ARE OUT OF THE QUESTION.]{.class_3527}

"[With all longsuffering and doctrine"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). It will not be easy to reprove, rebuke, and exhort a modern Laodicean congregation, especially where the "mothers and daddys" have sent their children away to a Christian school that wants to "lord it over" the local church and make the pastor support the school. Bob Jones, Springfield, and Tennessee Temple are noted for this: glamorizing the school and making [it ]{.class_3530}the real final authority in all matters of faith and practice for the local churches. Brother Rasmussen (Canuga Park, California) has a raft of correspondence on such matters that would settle the matter beyond any "shadow of a doubt." Christian schools desire to get control of the local churches (see the material available by Claude Bonam on the infamous "World Baptist Fellowship" of 1985). This is best done by brow-beating the pastor with the idea that the faculty is smarter and better than he is and that the young people on campus live more separated lives---because they have hall monitors and dorm supervisors behind barbed wire fences!

[The American preacher who obeys 2 Timothy 4:2 will be attacked by Christian schools, he will be harassed by his own carnal church members, he will be assaulted by the local politicians in the community, he will be slandered by the news media in his area, he will be threatened by fanatical Catholics in town who are controlled by the priests and bishops, and he will be ridiculed by worldly Christians who attend other churches in the community where the minister never raises his voice loud enough to be heard on the third row. ]{.class_3527}["Longsuffering"]{.class_3528}[ will be the needed ingredient.]{.class_3527}

"[And doctrine"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). This time the ["doctrine"]{.class_3529} is the substance of Biblical truth, and not just a series of instructions on good behavior, as it is found in Titus 2:1--6 and 1 Timothy 6:3. This time the reference is like 1 Timothy 4:13; 2 Timothy 3:10, 16; Revelation 2:14, 24; Isaiah 29:24; Romans 6:17; and similar passages. This doctrine is a reference to correct Biblical teaching on revealed truth. These are ["THE THINGS OF GOD"]{.class_3529} (1 Cor. 2:11) which are revealed by the Holy Spirit (1 Cor. 2:10). It is called ["THE TRUTH"]{.class_3529} right here in the context (vs. 4) in contradistinction to ["fables"]{.class_3529} (vs. 4). The Bible believer should note these matters carefully, for they have to do with whether or not the "apostate" (see Scofield, above) is an unsaved Liberal who makes a "profession" or a born-again believer who "FALLS AWAY FROM A STANDING POSITION" (see above).

"[For the time will come when they will not endure SOUND DOCTRINE\...after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears\...they shall turn away\...from the truth...turned unto fables"]{.class_3528} (vss. 3--4). This is the deadly passage. This is the place that the Scofield Board (1909) decided to land upon when they wanted a ["Summary"]{.class_3530} of "apostasy" (see Subject Index in any edition of the [Scofield Reference Bible]{.class_3530}).

[As we have noted, C. I. Scofield confines this apostasy to an ]{.class_3527}[unsaved man ]{.class_3526}[who made a profession of faith. "Apostasy is irremediable and awaits judgment," Scofield says (p. 1304).]{.class_3527}

[The Scofield editors have picked up ]{.class_3527}[one-half]{.class_3526}[ of Noah Webster's definition. The half they picked up said, "Renunciation of a religious faith." The part they carefully avoided said (]{.class_3527}[Webster's New Collegiate Dictionary,]{.class_3526}[ Merriam Co., Springfield, Mass., 1977. p. 54): "ABANDONMENT OF A PREVIOUS LOYALTY." ]{.class_3527}[Now you know why no Fundamentalist]{.class_3526}[ (1901--2001) ]{.class_3527}[took the second definition.]{.class_3526}[ "Abandonment of a previous loyalty" and "falling away from a standing position" are two things of which any born-again, saved child of God could be guilty, any time day or night, in any century of the world's history. Scofield was not interested in THE TRUTH at this point; he was interested in covering up for his "buddies," many of whom were pure-blood, whole-hearted, ALEXANDRIAN apostates. (See, for example, his notes on Genesis 6:2; Romans 8:1; and Daniel 7:10--11). ]{.class_3527}["All have sinned, and come short\...."]{.class_3528}

[Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown]{.class_3526}[ says the passage is only for PROFESSING Christians (p. 1381).]{.class_3527}

[Guthrie ]{.class_3526}[says the passage is referring only to opposition to the GOSPEL by the lost (p. 167).]{.class_3527}

[Williams ]{.class_3526}[says the passage is only for the PROFESSING Church (p. 964).]{.class_3527}

[Mounce ]{.class_3526}[says it is unsaved men rejecting the Gospel (p. 148).]{.class_3527}

[Hiebert ]{.class_3526}[says it is a reference to PROFESSING CHRISTENDOM (p. 106).]{.class_3527}

Other writers generalize the passage so it comes out "people" won't want the truth, or "mankind" will give up hope in the truth, or "many" will not believe the truths about Christ.

ALL "GODLY" WRITERS AND COMMENTATORS (1880--2001) RELIEVE THEMSELVES OF THE CHARGE.

Like all the prisoners on a chain gang, they are all innocent: not a guilty one in the lot.

Isn't it strange? We don't seem to be able to locate any "apostates"! Of course, these vicious, unsaved "Liberals" and lost "Modernists" in the WCC and NCC (1920--1980) are apostates, of course! Of course, Sockman, Oxnam, Inge, Tillich, Niebhur, Kagawa, Weigle, Weatherhead, Jones, Peale, et al., are just as guilty as Hell (if you are a "militant Fundamentalist"); and Fosdick, Blake, Pike, Langston Hughes, Rauschenbusch, Ward, Ely, Hromadka, Brooks, and Dahlberg are just as "apostate" as an "apostate" can get (if you are a "militant Fundamentalist"); but what has that got to do with "ABANDONMENT OF A PREVIOUS LOYALTY" or "FALLING AWAY FROM A STANDING POSITION"?

[Not one unsaved Liberal out of twenty in the NCC was ever "loyal" to the Bible or the "historic position" of fundamentalism. Not one unsaved Liberal out of fifty anywhere was ever "standing" to start with, so he had nothing from which to fall. A "previous loyalty" was not a reference to a "previous PROFESSION of faith"; it was a previous ]{.class_3527}[loyalty]{.class_3526}[---a devotion to a thing, person, or cause. Some folks have a time with that dictionary, don't they?]{.class_3527}

[To get our bearings and draw an azimuth on our location, let us ask ourselves one simple question: "Could a born again, premillennial, soulwinning Fundamentalist (1880--2001) be characterized by anything said ]{.class_3527}[in the passage?]{.class_3526}[ It's that simple. Let us list what the Holy Spirit gave ]{.class_3527}[in the passage.]{.class_3526}

[1. ]{.class_3527}[Inability to endure sound doctrine:]{.class_3526}[ that is, if the Scriptures clearly show a thing to be so (see ]{.class_3527}[The Unknown Bible,]{.class_3526}[ Bible Baptist Bookstore, 1996, for fifteen examples), the Scriptures at this point are rejected (see "The Big Flap," ]{.class_3527}[Bible Believers' Bulletin]{.class_3526}[ for fifteen more examples).]{.class_3527}

[2. ]{.class_3527}[A desire to get rid of preachers and fill their shoes with TEACHERS]{.class_3526}[ (vs. 3), because preaching the word (vs. 2) to them is irritating and nerve racking. What is needed is "Bible teachers" in piles and heaps (vs. 3) who will tickle their ears to doctrines that are NOT ]{.class_3527}["sound"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 3). For fifty examples, see the]{.class_3527}[ Bible Believer's Commentaries ]{.class_3526}[on Hebrews, Acts, Job, Genesis, Exodus, and the Minor Prophets (1959--2001). For fifty more examples, read the material in the ]{.class_3527}[Bible]{.class_3526}[ ]{.class_3527}[Believer's Commentaries ]{.class_3526}[on Proverbs, Revelation, Galatians, Ephesians, Philippians, and Colossians, and the present commentary.]{.class_3527}

[3. ]{.class_3527}[A refusal to LISTEN to any Biblical doctrine that is true]{.class_3526}[ (vs. 4) if it crosses what they have been taught to believe or "feel" is right (vs. 3).]{.class_3527}

[4. ]{.class_3527}[A deeply rooted, strongly reinforced belief in FICTION]{.class_3526}[ (]{.class_3527}["fables," ]{.class_3528}[vs. 4) such as:]{.class_3527}

a\. Westcott and Hort, Nestle, Aland, and Metzger were Bible scholars.

b\. There was a "Lucian recension" at Antioch.

c\. Christ was crucified on a dogwood tree.

d\. Men were saved exactly the same way under the Old Testament Law as under New Testament Grace.

e\. The Rosary will bring you good luck.

f\. The Bible is inspired only in the "original autographs."

g\. The Pope is a Christian.

h\. Bob Jones University believes "THE BIBLE is THE Word of God."

i\. No translation could be inspired.

j\. Mary can pray for you nineteen hundred years after she died.

k\. Peace is coming on earth before the Advent of Christ.

l\. Nuclear disarmament will guarantee peace.

m\. Greek scholars know the Bible better than Billy Sunday or J. Frank Norris.

n\. Secondary separation guarantees spirituality.

[o. ]{.class_3527}["Gain is godliness."]{.class_3528}

p\. The love of money is only "a" root of all kinds of evil.

q\. The ASV and NIV are "reliable translations" of the Scriptures.

r\. You can pray a soul out of purgatory.

s\. Rock Hudson was a martyr.

t\. CBS is an American organization.

u\. Roman Catholics are American Christians.

v\. The NASV is a "Bible."

w\. Robert Sumner is a Bible believer.

x\. The IRS is constitutional.

y\. Tennessee Temple teaches young men that the AV is the word of God.

z\. The ecumenical movement answers Christ's prayer in John 17.

"[TURNED UNTO FABLES"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4).

To whom is the Holy Spirit referring in verses 3--4?

[To any saved, soulwinning, premillennial, "militant Fundamentalist" on the face of this earth ]{.class_3527}[who is stupid enough to correct, criticize, or attack the Book by which he was saved ]{.class_3526}[(Jer. 23:36).]{.class_3527}

The passage is a reference to apostasy WITHIN the Body of Jesus Christ. It can be applied to A. T. Robertson, Kenneth Wuest, or Spiros Zodhiates just exactly as it can be applied to any unsaved Liberal in the NCCC (see Appendix XXII).

[Anyone dealing with any "Christian celebrities" in the 1970s or 1980s knows exactly what the score is. Their ]{.class_3527}[previous loyalty ]{.class_3526}[was to a BOOK, and the ]{.class_3527}[stand ]{.class_3526}[they took for that Book was that ]{.class_3527}[the Book]{.class_3526}[ was the Holy Bible, the word of God. Their ]{.class_3527}[present position]{.class_3526}[ is that that Book is to be USED without being believed, and that it is not the Holy Bible but only a "reliable translation" from an inferior set of "late manuscripts." If you doubt this for a minute, put your pastor or teacher "on the spot" and check him out. If he believed the garbage that they taught him at ANY major Christian school in America (college, seminary, or university), he is no more LOYAL to his "previous position" and is no more truly "standing" on that position than Eugene Carson Blake or Norman Vincent Peale.]{.class_3527}

[The modern apostasy is within the Body of Christ.]{.class_3526}[ The apostasy that is now PRESENT in America was begun in the 1920s and 1930s, while the apostasy then present was begun in the 1880s and 1890s. The apostasy in the 1880s and 1890's had its roots in the 1800s and 1820s, and so forth. Apostasy is not an "instant" product. It doesn't "pop up." It has grounds, roots, seeds, sprouts, shoots, branches, and trunks in every decade, and in every decade it begins exactly the same way---at least in the United States of America. ]{.class_3527}[It begins with correcting and altering the AV text with "Christian" scholarship.]{.class_3526}[ The definitive work on this is by]{.class_3527}[ Bruce Musselmann]{.class_3526}[ of the Cleveland Baptist Temple (Roy Thompson, pastor); it clearly shows what is wrong with correcting the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[in the twentieth century, and what is wrong with the men who practice this.]{.class_3527}

Apostasy is always perfectly predictable except to church historians and Greek and Hebrew scholars.

[1. It always ]{.class_3527}[begins ]{.class_3526}[in an institute of higher learning that professes to be Fundamentalist, Conservative, or Evangelical; or Catholic or "Christian."]{.class_3527}

[2. It always ]{.class_3527}[begins with altering a text]{.class_3526}[ the Holy Spirit's power and blessing have been upon ]{.class_3527}[OUTSIDE of the Catholic or Christian school:]{.class_3526}[ A.D. 100--500, the Old Latin, Old Syriac, and Gothic Versions; 500--1500, the Itala and Peshitta versions; 1500--1600, Diodati, Luther, Valera, Visoly, Michaelis, etc.; 1600--2000, the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version ]{.class_3526}[and translations from it.]{.class_3527}

[3. It then proceeds to question the HISTORICITY of passages that cannot be believed and winds up denying any Biblical doctrine that cannot be believed (see ]{.class_3527}[The Unknown Bible,]{.class_3526}[ 1996).]{.class_3527}

[4. This eventually leads to a denial of MIRACLES, and this, in turn, terminates in a denial of the ]{.class_3527}[Virgin Birth]{.class_3526}[ and the ]{.class_3527}[Resurrection of Christ.]{.class_3526}

[NOW ]{.class_3526}[WE HAVE THE "RELIGIOUS LIBERAL": the "professing Christian" apostate to whom Mounce, Guthrie, Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Scofield, and Williams referred.]{.class_3527}

[They pick up only the ]{.class_3527}[tail-end]{.class_3526}[ of the scorpion. They discern that the barn is empty ]{.class_3527}[after ]{.class_3526}[the horses are five miles down the road. By their brilliant and "godly," dedicated methods of "scientific scholarship," they discern that the food has rotted in the deep freeze ]{.class_3527}[after the power has been off for five weeks.]{.class_3526}[ They are so "godly" and "qualified" that they actually are able to come to the conclusion that the stalled cars on the highway beside them are out of gas: ]{.class_3527}[they ran out of gas six months ago.]{.class_3526}[ It is their "knowledge of the original languages" and their "recognized" scholarship that enables them to see that there is a dead man in the coffin. They know this because they haven't seen him move for ]{.class_3527}[two days.]{.class_3526}[ Remarkable!]{.class_3527}

There were 5,300 Catholic priests in Boston and Chicago who did not even know where they were going when they died (almanac, 1960). There were 505 churches in Cinncinnati with over a thousand "ministers" (1965), and less than thirty of them even professed to know whether or not a man can know where he is going when he dies. We are in the age of the "ear ticklers."

[Their ears "itch," and they want someone to scratch them. They itch ]{.class_3527}["to tell, or to hear some new thing"]{.class_3528}[ (see Acts 17:21 and comments in that commentary). And what is "NEW" in the old, old Book is rejected as "heresy" (see ]{.class_3527}[The Unknown Bible]{.class_3526}[ for fifteen documented examples). They want a new "fable" (vs. 4), like "The Dead Sea Scrolls show a wealth of material on a B.C. Septuagint," or "Recent archaeological discoveries shed new light on certain obscure New Testament passages," or "The latest Greek-Hebrew Study Bible will make the Bible come ALIVE for you as it takes you to THE SOURCE," etc., etc.]{.class_3527}

As one occupant of an elevator said to another one when they landed in the basement: "Well, whatdaya know 'bout that? Sometimes you think you're goin' up when you're really goin' DOWN." I saw an old man in the hospital corridors trying out a new wheelchair which he was going to have to occupy for a few months, and he said to his family and friends: "Well, looky here! You can go backerds in this thing easier than ya kin forerds!" Exactly.

[There is no direct reference to any unsaved, "professing Christian" in the passage, for the ones who turn away their ears turn them away from ]{.class_3527}["SOUND DOCTRINE"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 3). It is this that they cannot ]{.class_3527}["endure."]{.class_3528}[ No unsaved, "professing Christian" in this age would know what ]{.class_3527}["sound doctrine"]{.class_3528}[ was in distinction to "unsound doctrine," for the ]{.class_3527}["natural man receiveth not the things of the Spirit of God\...neither can he know them, for they are SPIRITUALLY discerned"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Cor. 2:14). The context of those remarks was the simple Gospel of Christ (1 Cor. 15:1--6). The people in 2 Timothy 4 were listening to ]{.class_3527}[the truth]{.class_3526}[ when they turned away (vs. 4). They were listening to ]{.class_3527}[sound doctrine]{.class_3526}[ when they found it unendurable (vs. 3), and they were receiving ]{.class_3527}[Bible doctrine ]{.class_3526}[from ]{.class_3527}["the word"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 2) when they went to their pile of ]{.class_3527}["teachers."]{.class_3528}[ Obviously they were not all "unsaved Liberals."]{.class_3527}

The Scofield note is an example of apostasy itself (see Appendix XVII).

"[The quick"]{.class_3528} is a reference to born-again believers in every commentary on the market written by a conservative or evangelical. They just eliminated this class from the antecedent in verse 3---["THEY."]{.class_3529}

There he stands in all of his glory! (Fred Afman, Faulkner, James Price, Harold Willmington, Dobson, Zodhiates, Olson, Melton, MacRae, Carson, Bob Jones III, Sumner, Wuest, Wemp, Bob Jones Jr., Farstad, Hindson, Rice, Hutson, et al.)

Don't be ridiculous; this is Laodicea.

The present apostasy is within the Body of Christ.

"[Watch thou in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, make full proof of thy ministry"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5).

[This is the third set of threes, being a charge to ]{.class_3527}["WATCH"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 5). "Watching" consists of "enduring afflictions." You are to watch for them, for they will come (see John 16:1--3). You are to expect them (1 Pet. 4:12), so you won't be shocked when they come (]{.class_3527}["longsuffering,"]{.class_3528}[ vs. 2). You are to harden yourself ]{.class_3527}[before ]{.class_3526}[they come (see 2:3 and comments). "Watching" consists of doing ]{.class_3527}["the work of an evangelist"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 5), which means soulwinning as well as ]{.class_3527}[sheep-tending;]{.class_3526}[ it means preaching the Gospel as well as "feeding the sheep" (1 Pet. 5:1--4). It means travelling and taking the good news with you (Rom. 15:20--28). "Watching" means that a man will make ]{.class_3527}["full proof"]{.class_3528}[ of his ministry (see 2 Cor. 10:13--18) and show that he has been divinely called, commissioned, equipped, sent, and supported by the Holy Spirit (see Col. 4:17 and comments in that commentary). ]{.class_3527}["Full proof"]{.class_3528}[ means what Paul mentioned in Acts 12:25; Acts 13:25; and 2 Timothy 4:7. It means that there should be fruit to prove that the preacher was called, and that he did not enter the ministry as a "profession."]{.class_3527}

"[Fruit" would be converted souls, new converts rooted in the faith (see 1 Thess. 1:3--9), converts who had grown to maturity (Eph. 4:14--17), answers to prayer, the truth disseminated through the minister, lives altered and changed by his messages, Christ and God magnified before a heathen populace, wounds and scars from preaching the truth, false accusations and slanders for taking Biblical stances, and a victorious testimony when the armor is taken off (1 Kings 20:11; 2 Tim. 4:7). After this, of course, would come buildings, property, busses, Sunday School attendance, and offerings.]{.class_3527}[ Major in the majors.]{.class_3530}

[Before going on in ]{.class_3527}[Terrible Timothy]{.class_3526}[, let us step off the "beaten path" for a moment and into the ditches, sloughs, slime pits, quicksands, and sewage ditches of Laodicean, "godly, qualified, recognized scholarship." Since apostasy between 1901 and 2001 is in ]{.class_3527}[the Body of Christ,]{.class_3526}[ we will cite nothing but SAVED CONSERVATIVES. They will foul up just as badly as any ]{.class_3527}[unsaved Liberal]{.class_3526}[ in the WCC or the NCC.]{.class_3527}

[Vincent (]{.class_3527}[Word Studies]{.class_3526}[): "The ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[overlooks the article, which is important" (p. 820). ]{.class_3527}["'Having itching ears']{.class_3528}[ should be 'being tickled in their hearing.' ]{.class_3527}['Shall be turned into fables']{.class_3528}[ is more correctly 'will turn aside'" (p. 321). ]{.class_3527}["'Make full proof of thy ministry']{.class_3528}[ is better as 'fulfill.'"]{.class_3527}

[Since Vincent couldn't tell anyone what the Greek word αποστασια meant (see his comments under 2 Thess. 2:3) and was never in the ministry himself and never did ]{.class_3527}["the work of an evangelist"]{.class_3528}[ a day in his life, you may safely reject all of his comments and suggestions on the entire passage.]{.class_3527}

[The Wycliffe Commentary]{.class_3526}[ informs us that ]{.class_3527}["rebuke"]{.class_3528}[ actually means "to lay a value or charge on" (p. 138), and ]{.class_3527}["exhort" ]{.class_3528}[is often translated "comfort or beseech"---which does nothing but confound both words and gives no light to anyone reading the passage. For a real "nugget," we should read the quote by Ellicott found in ]{.class_3527}[Hiebert ]{.class_3526}[(p. 105), on verse 2: "It appears to mark an attitude of prompt attention that may at any moment pass into action." Which means nothing, unless it means: "It appears to be a small blemish on the left cheek which might turn into a pimple or a mole." Hiebert also tells us that "the ]{.class_3527}[oldest ]{.class_3526}[manuscripts read 'and' rather than ]{.class_3527}['at']{.class_3528}[ in verse 1" ("and the dead AND his appearing AND his kingdom"). This would be an excellent reason for throwing the "oldest manuscripts" out the window.]{.class_3527}

[After identifying the teachers of verse 3 as tickling the ears of their students with "the latest fancies and speculations of ]{.class_3527}[scholarship]{.class_3526}[," Hiebert realizes he has overstepped his Alexandrian bounds and has unwittingly told ]{.class_3527}[the truth;]{.class_3526}[ he corrects himself as quickly as possible (p. 106) with the hasty addition that this applied only to "THE SPIRIT OF MODERNISM." (He exempted himself and all of his buddies, who WERE obsessed all their lives with the "fancies and speculations of CONSERVATIVE scholarship," see above.)]{.class_3527}

[Timothy doesn't have to prove anything (]{.class_3527}["make full proof,"]{.class_3528}[ vs. 5) according to the Dead Dodo (]{.class_3527}[TLB]{.class_3526}[), which says "Leave nothing undone that you ought to do." Or "be a good little boy and finish the job," or "Look out, honey, the kitchen floor hasn't been mopped yet," etc. The ]{.class_3527}[RV ]{.class_3526}[(1885) has "by" His appearing for ]{.class_3527}["at" ]{.class_3528}[His appearing, and ]{.class_3527}["in season, out of season"]{.class_3528}[ should be "take opportunity OR make it" according to Dummelow (p. 1004). Since it doesn't mean that one time in the Scriptures (Eccl. 3:1; Psa. 104:19; Jer. 33:20; Gal. 6:9), we shall put Dummelow in with the "apostates," even though he was a saved "Evangelical."]{.class_3527}

[Well, Timothy is to be "sober" instead of "watchful" (vs. 5), and he is to "fulfill" his ministry instead of making ]{.class_3527}["full proof"]{.class_3528}[ of it (]{.class_3527}[ASV]{.class_3526}[, 1901). He is just to teach and not worry about ]{.class_3527}["doctrine"]{.class_3528}[ in the ]{.class_3527}[NKJV ]{.class_3526}[(vs. 2), and the ]{.class_3527}[NKJV ]{.class_3526}[also erases the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[reading of verse 5 and substitutes for it the ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[of 1901. The ]{.class_3527}[New International Version]{.class_3526}[ has inserted "in view of" into verse 1, altered the ]{.class_3527}["heap"]{.class_3528}[ of verse 3 to "a great number," and changed the ]{.class_3527}["fables"]{.class_3528}[ of verse 4 to "myths." What this did to clarify any text or make the Elizabethan English "easier to understand" is a mystery that Charlie Chan and the Thin Man couldn't figure out if they had Sherlock Holmes and The Shadow to help them.]{.class_3527}

[Kenneth Taylor's Dead Dodo (]{.class_3527}[TLB]{.class_3526}[) gets rid of the first resurrection by making both resurrections take place at the Second Advent ("who will some day judge the living and the dead WHEN he appears to set up\..."). Since this is the UNSOUND doctrine that apostates will teach to APOSTATES who have ]{.class_3527}["itching ears"]{.class_3528}[ in the APOSTASY, we would have to assume that Taylor is a lost man, if we take C. I. Scofield's note on ]{.class_3527}["Apostasy: Summary"]{.class_3526}[ to heart. Fortunately, we have more sense. We know that Taylor is a saved man. ]{.class_3527}[He is a saved apostate.]{.class_3526}[ "APOSTASY," IN THIS AGE, IS IN THE BODY OF CHRIST.]{.class_3527}

[The Dead Dodo (]{.class_3527}[TLB]{.class_3526}[) tells us that it was only Timothy's congregation that needed the instructions of verse 2 ("your people"), but the people of verse 3 were ]{.class_3527}[someone else's ]{.class_3526}[people ("when people won't listen"). These "people" will "blithely follow their own misguided ideas" (vs. 4), so you don't have to worry about ]{.class_3527}["fables"]{.class_3528}[ or "myths." Neither word is in the Dead Dodo (]{.class_3527}[TLB]{.class_3526}[).]{.class_3527}

[And what shall we say further? Does the reader really think that any of these "godly" men have any contribution to make to spiritual things, let alone understanding the Bible? Shall we close accounts on the matter with a "bottom liner" from Lynchburg? Here it is from Sumner Wemp (]{.class_3527}[Liberty University,]{.class_3526}[ the trainer of "Champions for Christ," etc.).]{.class_3527}

"[(Gr. ]{.class_3527}αποστρεφω) is in the Greek [active ]{.class_3530}voice and has the idea of [deliberate action]{.class_3530} by themselves (vs. 4). Shall be turned (Gr. εκτρεφω) means they are acted upon; it is a [medical term]{.class_3530} meaning "to twist out of place" (p. 650).

Is that how you train a "champion for Christ"?

No light.

No edification.

No exegesis.

No application.

No illustration.

[One gigantic helping of cool, grey, formless mush. The "active voice" was already in English; ]{.class_3527}[no Greek was needed.]{.class_3526}[ The action was "deliberate" according to the English text of verse 3;]{.class_3527}[ no Greek was needed.]{.class_3526}[ The "medical term" was about as relevant to sound doctrine as a remark by Truman Dollar on the Ku Klux Klan (]{.class_3527}[Baptist Fundamentalism,]{.class_3526}[ 1984, Washington, D.C.).]{.class_3527}

4:6 For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.

7 I have fought a good fight, I have finished my course, I have kept the faith:

8 Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.

Paul is through "charging" Timothy. He now turns to personal matters. From here on, he talks about himself, his friends and his enemies, and God's mercy and providential care through-out his "last days."

"[For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). We see immediately that the closing lines of his epistle are going to deal with such dramatic (and spiritual) elements that the Scholars' Union is going to come apart at the seams when they hit them, due to the fact that they have no real acquaintence whatsoever with the themes and subjects being discussed. The greatest apostle who ever lived (and the greatest follower of Christ who ever lived) is about to be martyred. What Vincent, Guthrie, Thayer, Hort, Farstad, Hutson, Price, Panosian, Dummelow, Sumner, Martin, Wisdom, Hiebert, Willmington, Swindoll, McGee, Wemp, and Faulkner know about such matters could be put into a matchbox and mailed to Australia with a eighty cent stamp.

"[I am now ready to be offered"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). He had been "offered" a number of times before (Phil. 2:17), for his whole life was a [burnt offering ]{.class_3530}(1 Cor. 13:1--3) for the Lord. He burned out for God like an Old Testament "offering" (see Lev. 1--10) and told his followers to present their bodies as ["A LIVING SACRIFICE"]{.class_3529} (Rom. 12:1--3). The Gentiles he led to Christ were called "offerings" to God (Rom. 15:16). He is about to "lay down his neck" (Rom. 16:4) for the Lord and get decapitated, as the offering of Leviticus 1--10 were decapitated (see Lev. 3:13 and 4:11).

"[The time of my departure is at hand"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). He knew [when ]{.class_3530}he was going to die, as Peter knew it (2 Pet. 1:14) and Christ knew it (John 12:23). I hear the words in my sleep at night. "Flight 957, loading at Gate B-10, [ready for departure!"]{.class_3530} "Time of departure: 11:35." "Flight 831 is [ready for departure."]{.class_3530} I have heard that call continually for fifty-five years (1985, time of reprint). When I first heard it in Atlanta, that airport was a series of plywood passageways that went out to the gates, with sixty-watt bulbs screwed into the ceiling every fifty feet. When I first heard that call in Manila, there wasn't a jet or a turbo prop on the runway; they were rebuilt bombers and transports from World War II, and we called them "DC-1's." Riding the two-motor jobs on the Texas-Arkansas Airways or the Allegheny or Mohawk lines (1950--1955) was like riding a bucking bronco over a gravel pit.

"[All passengers proceed to gate 15, Flight 205 loading and READY FOR DEPARTURE." ]{.class_3527}["The time of my departure is at hand"]{.class_3529}: Paul is getting ready to sprout wings and "take off" (2 Cor. 5:1--10). How many times I have sat with the seatbelt fastened at the end of a runway, I know not. Twenty-five meetings a year for twenty years, fifteen to eighteen meetings a year for seventeen more years. My "parish" ran from Bad Ischl in Austria to Dryden, Ontario, from Nova Scotia to San Diego, from Key West to Seattle, and from Nüremberg to Honolulu. "Flight 615; all passengers should be aboard. [Flight 615 departing!"]{.class_3530} The engines roar; the plane strains on its landing gear. (You sometimes think the wings are flapping!) The runway is clear. The control tower signals. [Up, up, and away!]{.class_3530}

"[I have fought a good fight\...finished my course\...kept the faith"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). The [departure ]{.class_3530}includes another set of "threes." They are given as a FIGHT and a FAITH and a FINISH. The ["fight"]{.class_3529} is described in our comments under Ephesians 6:10--15 (which see) and in 2 Timothy 2:1--5 (which see). Field Marshall Ney, before the firing squad, told the men who tried to put a blindfold on him: "For twenty-seven years I have been accustomed to facing the enemy's shots at fifteen paces or less. FIRE AWAY!" Mrs. Rommel, when she looked at the face of her dead general husband in the coffin, said that she saw a look on his face she had never seen in his life: it was a sarcastic smirk. Hitler had forced him to take poison or else be executed as a common criminal, with his wife and children sent to the concentration camp.

[Caleb ]{.class_3526}[fights until he is eighty. ]{.class_3527}[Joshua ]{.class_3526}[wars until he is a hundred and twenty. Old "Dodo the Ahohite" has a boy whose hand is so riveted to the haft of his sword that they have to pry his fingers loose from the handle when he comes "off the line" (2 Sam. 23:9--10). His fingers had cramped shut around the weapon. He had such a hold on "the word" (don't forget Heb. 4:12--13 and Psa. 149:6!) that you couldn't tell him from the BOOK! If anyone attacked HIM, they had to attack the BOOK, and if anyone attacked the BOOK, they had to attack HIM. Of course, they lied their way out and said, "HE THINKS HE'S RIGHT AND EVERYONE ELSE IS WRONG." They mistook ]{.class_3527}[the BOOK]{.class_3526}[ for HIM. It was the BOOK that was right, and "everyone else" is wrong.]{.class_3527}

[Alexander the Great]{.class_3526}[ fights the "bad fight" and dies after a drunken fit, sword still in hand. ]{.class_3527}[General Patton]{.class_3526}[ fights the "bad fight" and is murdered (probably) by a first rate Mafia job that is as undetected this day as the murder of John Kennedy. ]{.class_3527}[General Von Kluge]{.class_3526}[ fought the bad fight for Hitler and ended it with a cyanide pill after the "army bomb plot." ]{.class_3527}[Field Marshall Von Witzleben]{.class_3526}[ fought the bad fight and wound up hanging off of a meat hook at the Plötenzee prison. ]{.class_3527}[General William Booth]{.class_3526}[ fought the good fight and died in bed crying for the poverty stricken people in downtown London.]{.class_3527}

[Frederick the Great]{.class_3526}[ fought the bad fight and died a professing atheist, after insulting ]{.class_3527}[General Von Seeland]{.class_3526}[ at an officers' banquet. Von Seeland rose to his feet after hearing Frederick tell several obscene jokes about Jesus Christ and how cowardly Christians were. The General, in his seventies, cried out: "Sire! You know that I have fought with you in twenty-three engagements and have never been frightened by any enemy. I salute you, Sire, but as an old man who is about to pass into the presence of One greater than you: the God and Father of the Lord Jesus Christ Who died to save me from my sins. You have blasphemed His name. I salute you, Sire, but as a man who loves His Savior, the One you have blasphemed!" He saluted and left the table. Frederick turned pale, arose, and cried out, "General Von Seeland! Sir! I beg your pardon! I beg your pardon! ]{.class_3527}[I beg your pardon!]{.class_3526}[" But the party was over. In fifteen minutes, the hall was empty. Von Seeland fought a better "fight" than Frederick the Great.]{.class_3527}

[(You won't find ]{.class_3527}[that ]{.class_3526}[in the "original Greek." No modern Bible teacher or commentator could handle 2 Tim. 4:7 any more than he could handle 1 Tim. 6:5, 10, or 20.)]{.class_3527}

"[I have finished my course"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). The marathon has been run. Glenn Davis, Peter Snell, Jesse Owens, and Emil Zatopek are back there somewhere. Robert Hayes (1962) ran one hundred yards in 7.8 seconds, which is about 26.22 miles per hour, but if he found a lake of fire at the end of the course, the record didn't mean anything. Jim Ryan, Jim Hines, Henry Tono, and Jose Hermens all had courses to run, but they didn't ["finish]{.class_3530} their course" until the undertaker got them. The best time over 26 miles and 385 yards is two hours and eight minutes, run by Derek Clayton of Australia (1969). The world's records for running are [Jose Hermens]{.class_3530} (Netherlands) for 13 miles and 24 yards, [James Alder]{.class_3530} for 30,000 meters, [William Rodgers]{.class_3530} for 25,000 meters, [Jose Hermens]{.class_3530} for 20,000 meters, [Henry Tono]{.class_3530} for 10,000 and 5,000 meters, and [John Walker]{.class_3530} for 2,000 meters. The world record for a lifetime of running is held by[ Kenneth Bailey]{.class_3530} (51 years old, England) who ran 128,470 miles in 43 years. [Johnny Salvo]{.class_3530} ran from the Atlantic to the Pacific in the USA in 79 days, 5 hours, and 57 minutes.

[Paul ran to win, to break the tape (1 Cor. 9:24). He had been "pressing forward" to ]{.class_3527}["the mark" ]{.class_3528}[(Phil. 3:14) all of his life, and now he was about to cross the finish line. He had not looked back one time (Phil. 3:13), and he had "striven lawfully" (2 Tim. 2:5), which is evident by the next verse; in that verse he wins a crown.]{.class_3527}

"[I have kept the faith"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7), like the popes and cardinals did[ NOT. ]{.class_3530}Paul preached salvation by faith and grace apart from works or baptism all of his life; the popes and Catholics did [not]{.class_3530}, nor do they. Paul stuck by what was ["written"]{.class_3529} (Acts 24:14) and taught nothing [contrary ]{.class_3530}to it no matter how "traditional" it was (Mark 7:7, 13), or how many "early Christians" observed it; the Catholics [did.]{.class_3530}

[If Paul were on "Telstar" before 900,000,000 people (as the Pope often is), he would not hesitate to preach the death, burial, resurrection, and second coming of Jesus Christ; and he would not hesitate to beseech his viewers to be "reconciled unto God" by the death of God's Son (2 Cor. 5:20--21; Rom. 5:1--10). NO POPE HAS DONE ANYTHING OF THE KIND IN ANY PUBLIC APPEARANCE SINCE 1500. Popes are not "keepers of the faith", they are not "good" shepherds, and they are not ]{.class_3527}["faithful"]{.class_3528}[ stewards (1 Cor. 4:1--2). They are ]{.class_3527}[destroyers ]{.class_3526}[of the faith, ]{.class_3527}[idol shepherds]{.class_3526}[ (Zech. 11:17), and ]{.class_3527}[unjust ]{.class_3526}[stewards (Luke 16:2--13). They are as unlike Paul as anyone could possibly imagine.]{.class_3527}

[In "keeping the faith," the apostle has guarded the deposit of truth given to him by the Old Testament Scriptures; he has faithfully interpreted this in the light of the revelations the Lord gave him (Acts 26:16); he has not failed to believe every word of them as given to him (Acts 24:14); he has spoken out boldly (Eph. 6:20) in any matter that God wanted made known (Acts 20:20, 27); and it has eventually cost him his life. He has ]{.class_3527}["KEPT THE FAITH."]{.class_3528}[ (This is no reference to dopeheaded, African whoremongers trying to overthrow the Constitution of the United States. "Keeping the faith" is not a Communist expression in the "original.")]{.class_3527}

"[Henceforth\...a crown of RIGHTEOUSNESS"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). This is one of the five "crowns" reserved as [rewards ]{.class_3530}for the Judgment Seat of Christ (1 Cor. 3:10--16; Rom. 14:10; 2 Cor. 5:10), a subject that Dr. A. T. Robertson, J. G. Machen, Benjamin Warfield, and Philip Schaff knew nothing about at all. Since it was also a complete mystery to Delitzsch, Gesenius, and Kautzsch, you need not expect any Hebrew scholar to know anything about it unless he read something by Clarence Larkin or C. I. Scofield, where they had quoted a [King James]{.class_3530} text. Since it was also completely unknown to Machen, Davis, Trench, Thayer, Lightfoot, Alford, and Nestle, you can't expect any Greek scholar to know about what Paul is talking unless he picked it up from the same source.

The other four crowns are in 1 Thessalonians 2:19; 1 Corinthians 9:25; 1 Peter 5:4; and James 1:12.

[A ]{.class_3527}["crown" ]{.class_3528}[implies a reign (see 2 Tim. 2:11--14 and comments), and the reader cannot fail to notice that this reign will be ]{.class_3527}["ON THE EARTH"]{.class_3528}[ (Rev. 5:10; 20:1--6) after the Advent; not now, during the "church age." The idol shepherds and unjust stewards of verse 7 (see comments) wear their crowns ]{.class_3527}[now ]{.class_3526}[and sit on their thrones ]{.class_3527}[now ]{.class_3526}[as "princes of the church." They are the same bloody killers who signed concordats with Mussolini, Franco, Hitler, Charlemagne, and Napoleon. ]{.class_3527}[They are the princes of Hell.]{.class_3526}

"[Shall give me at THAT day"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). The day referred to is called ["the day of Christ"]{.class_3529} in 2 Thessalonians 2:1--2 and ["the day of redemption"]{.class_3529} in Ephesians 4:30. It overlaps ["the day of the Lord,"]{.class_3529} but that expression is always a reference to judgment of the unsaved whether at the beginning of the Tribulation or the end of the Millenium (see 2 Pet. 3:10). ["The day of Jesus Christ"]{.class_3529} (Phil. 1:6, 10 and 2:16) is the beginning of ["the day of the Lord"]{.class_3529} from the standpoint of the saved in this age (notice 1 Cor. 1:8 and 5:5).

"[But unto all them also that love his appearing"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8). Notice that this is a [reward ]{.class_3530}that shows up when the "Chief Shepherd appears" to the Flock,[ not to the unsaved world]{.class_3530}---as in Revelation 1:7. Unsaved "Church of Christ" preachers and Catholic priests can never get this distinction: the difference between Christ appearing in the clouds (1 Thess. 4:13--17) to [believers only]{.class_3530}---where they are caught up to a Judgment Seat and a marriage (Psa. 45; Rev. 19:7)---and Christ appearing with the clouds in "great glory" (Rev. 14:14--16) coming to STAMP OUT THE ROMAN CATHOLIC CHURCH AND THE UNITED NATIONS (Zeph. 3; Rev. 19; Isa. 63; Rev. 14:20). There is a difference. When Christ "appears" in 1 John 3:1--3, HE is certainly not appearing to unsaved Catholics at the battle of Armageddon.

4:9 Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:

10 For Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world, and is departed unto Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia.

11 Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.

12 And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.

13 The cloke that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring with thee, and the books, but especially the parchments.

14 Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his works:

15 Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly withstood our words.

He now turns to information on nine individuals: Demas, Crescens, Titus, Carpus, Luke, Mark, Tychicus, Alexander, and Timothy himself. Verse 9 ties in with verse 21; four times he makes mention of Timothy coming to see him in prison.

"[Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). If we took this with 1 John 2:15 literally (as most Greek scholars would), we would have to conclude that Demas [lost his salvation,]{.class_3530} if he ever had it; thus it is taken by all Campbellites, J.W.'s, Mormons, and Catholics. (The word ["Demas"]{.class_3529} is supposed to be a contraction of Demetrius and is found in Col. 4:14 and Philemon 24). Hiebert, after wasting your time telling you that "the tense of the ORIGINAL indicates that he has fallen in love with the present age\..." (p. 116), simply refuses to connect the verse with 1 John 2:15. Hiebert insists that Demas was not "charged with forsaking Christ, and the words do not prove that Demas became an APOSTATE from Christianity." Mounce sacks Demas: "he had forgotten that...friendship with the world is HATRED toward God" (p.154), but still "There is nothing to suggest that Demas was an apostate" (Guthrie, p. 172).

[How 'bout this, girlies? ]{.class_3527}["IF ANY MAN LOVE THE WORLD, THE LOVE OF THE FATHER IS NOT IN HIM"]{.class_3528}[ (1 John 2:15).]{.class_3527}

Will that do for today?

[Of course, we are only kidding. We don't think Demas was lost, although he certainly was ]{.class_3527}[an "apostate."]{.class_3526}[ ("ABANDONMENT OF A PREVIOUS LOYALTY!" Remember?) If he didn't ]{.class_3527}[abandon ]{.class_3526}[Paul after having been "previously loyal" to him, what did he do; ]{.class_3527}[give him free rent for a year?]{.class_3526}[ We just mentioned 1 John 2:15 to show how unable these commentators are to find any Scripture that deals with any of the Scriptures they are trying to explain. "Scripture with Scripture" exposition with "godly" Fundmentalists many times is a foreign operation altogether. What man who knew the Bible would not think of 1 John 2:15 when he read 2 Timothy 4:10? Demas fell in love with the "gods" of the news media of his day. Perhaps it was the art in the Greek museums; perhaps it was the concerts and plays in the Greek theaters; perhaps it was the contemporary poetry or literary works of the day. Maybe he had a passion for architecture or "city planning." He fell in love with a system that was so bombed out by A.D. 500, people go there now to look at the ruins. He loved the ]{.class_3527}[Roman Empire]{.class_3526}[ instead of ]{.class_3527}[the Lord's coming.]{.class_3526}

[Observe above that Paul has not limited the Crown of Righteousness to himself but ]{.class_3527}["unto ALL them also that love his appearing"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 8). ]{.class_3527}[Here is a crown that any believer can earn.]{.class_3526}[ All he has to do is avoid churches, priests, and popes that never talk about the Second Coming of Jesus Christ. Steer clear of the whole, rotten mess and spend time with the churches, people, and periodicals that make much of the Second Advent. Demas gave up. The ]{.class_3527}["blessed hope" ]{.class_3528}[of Titus 2:13 petered out for him, and with it went his own purity, if we are to believe 1 John 3:1--3.]{.class_3527}

[In contrast to Demas, who said, ]{.class_3527}["I go sir: and went not"]{.class_3528}[ (Matt. 21:30), is Mark, who said ]{.class_3527}["I will not: but afterward repented and went"]{.class_3528}[ (Matt. 21:29). Mark has recovered from his former "softness" (Acts 13:13) and is ready to ]{.class_3527}["endure hardness as a good soldier of Jesus Christ."]{.class_3528}[ ]{.class_3527}["He is profitable to me for the ministry"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 11). Crescens and Titus have left him, and only ]{.class_3527}["the beloved physician"]{.class_3528}[ Luke (Col. 4:14) has stuck by him.]{.class_3527}

[Timothy's coming has another set of "threes" connected with it. He is to bring ]{.class_3527}["the cloke," "the books,"]{.class_3528}[ and]{.class_3527}[ "the parchments."]{.class_3528}[ While Demas is out revelling in the splendid processions, the beautiful and impressive temples, the beaches, the rock gardens, the Roman seminaries, and teeming marketplaces, Paul will be studying the Scriptures (and writing the Scriptures) in the "slammer."]{.class_3527}

[You serve the idea that dominates you. You will eventually fashion yourself after your idol. Somewhere down the line, some great truth (or some great lie) will bring you under its spell. From then on your decisions will be based on your loyalty to it. ]{.class_3527}["The supreme affection"]{.class_3526}[ eventually determines all decisions in life. Demas fell under the spell of first century Rome. Whether it was music, sports, art, literature, business, religion, philosophy, or politics that hypnotized him, we know not. We only know that when "push came to shove," he was not ready to die with Paul. He wanted "one more night with the frogs" (see Exod. 8:10 and comments in that commentary). Paul is alone, shivering in a cell, awaiting winter, and the best he can think of is ]{.class_3527}["food and raiment"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Tim. 6:8), hence ]{.class_3527}["The cloke\...bring with thee"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 13). Evidently, his idea of FOOD (John 4:32) was READING THE SCRIPTURES (Heb. 5:14; Luke 4:4; Psa. 119:103).]{.class_3527}

"[The books"]{.class_3528} were probably papyrus codices, as the Christians were using them long before the scribes at Alexandria decided to make vellum codices (see the same thing with Moody "colportage books," putting out paperbacks [nearly ninety years before the bookstores caught on]{.class_3530}). ["The parchments"]{.class_3529} would be cheap vellum, actually made from goat or sheepskin instead of the "A-1" antelope skins of Alexandria, and they probably were Old Testament vellum scrolls.

"[Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil"]{.class_3528} (vs. 14), and Paul doesn't attempt to "instruct him in meekness" (2:25). He just asks God to give him what he has coming to him: ["The Lord reward him ACCORDING TO HIS WORKS."]{.class_3529} Since this is obvious spite, the pious, "godly" Greek scholars rush to Alexander's aid to tell us that "MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITY \[Oh, don't you know, you old God-forsaken rascals, you!\] favors the [future indicative ]{.class_3530}reading, rather than the OPTATIVE of WISH" (Hiebert, p. 120). What they were trying to say was "the Alexandrian manuscripts that help Alexander out have the future indicative, while the vast majority of manuscripts ([including readings from the Western Family, Syrian Family, and Alexandrian Family]{.class_3530}) read the OPTATIVE." Still, "it does not reveal any desire for revenge on Paul's part" ---if you believe a nut like Robert Mounce (p. 156). We are to presume that when Paul yelled, ["GOD SHALL SMITE THEE, THOU WHITED WALL"]{.class_3529} (Acts 23:3), it was not an "optative wish" but a "future indicative." [Like your Aunt Harriet's microwave.]{.class_3530}

[Unfortunately, "Alexander" has trouble "smithing," for he is a "metal worker" in Mounce and a "blacksmith" in Moffat, but a "brass founder" in Conybeare. Undoubtedly, he is the coppersmith of Acts 19:33--34, and being an unsaved Jew who made a living "underselling" ]{.class_3527}[SILVERSMITHS ]{.class_3526}[(see Acts 19:24) in the idol business, he would certainly be one of Paul's most formidable enemies for life. You hit a man in the pocket-book (like we have hit Thomas Nelson and Son, Bob Jones Jr., Eerdmans Publishing Co., Robert Sumner, Baker Book House, Logos Publishing Co., and Zondervan), and you hurt him.]{.class_3527}[ "Of whom be thou ware also"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 15). Then Timothy is at Ephesus, exactly where Alexander was (Acts 19:33--34). Timothy was warned of the rascal because he was living in Timothy's home town.]{.class_3527}

4:16 At my first answer no man stood with me, but all men forsook me: I pray God that it may not be laid to their charge.

17 Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known, and that all the Gentiles might hear: and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.

18 And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve me unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.

"[It's all over but the rejoicing" (at the marriage of the Lamb). With the exception of mentioning nine more of his friends (Pudens, Linus, Erastus, Trophimus, Prisca, Aquila, Eubulus, Claudius, and Onesiphorus), he confines his remarks to his death.]{.class_3527}

"[At my first answer"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16). The standard procedure of the Roman tribunal after passing a sentence of condemnation on a Christian was to ask the culprit if he would reject Christ or deny Christ. He was asked this question [three ]{.class_3530}times, giving him time to change his mind. If the [third ]{.class_3530}answer was "No," then the sentence was carried out: burning at the stake, thrown to the lions, whipped to death, flayed (skinned alive), beheaded, etc. We do not know what Paul's[ "first answer"]{.class_3529} was, but knowing him (!), he certainly put some terrifying addendas and appendices on the ["first answer."]{.class_3529}

One can imagine: "Do you continue to avow that Jesus is the Lord and not Caesar?" "Bless God, brother! Jesus Christ is King of Kings and Lord of Lords, and some day He will wipe out this Vatican State like a man turns a dish upside down and wipes it dry! I am not careful to answer thee in this matter, your majesty! You could have no power at all over me unless it were given to you from the Caesar of Caesars, the King of Kings, the Lord of Hosts, Jesus Christ, my Friend, my Saviour, and my Lord!"

[Woosh! The courtroom emptied! (]{.class_3527}["No man stood with me, but all men forsook me,"]{.class_3528}[ vs. 16.) It looked like Stephen's buddies heading for the exits in the second balcony when he got to that part about ]{.class_3527}["Heaven is my throne and earth is my footstool"]{.class_3528}[ (see Acts 7:49 and comments in that commentary). Paul had no witnesses left to bear testimony for him, no advocates to intercede for him, and no "counsel for the defence" to protect him. He "blew it" on the first examination. To this day I believe he did it ]{.class_3527}[on purpose ]{.class_3526}[(see Acts 14:20 and comments in that commentary). I think he was tired of the whole mess (Col. 3:1--3) and wanted to get on home (Phil. 1:21--23) by the shortest route possible: ]{.class_3527}[he made it.]{.class_3526}

[Although all men forsook him, there was one Counsel for the defence Who stayed with him: ]{.class_3527}["THE LORD STOOD WITH ME"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 17). The One Who had said, ]{.class_3527}["I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee"]{.class_3528}[ stood true to His word and ]{.class_3527}["strengthened"]{.class_3528}[ Paul (vs. 17). So he prays for the weakness of those who deserted him and asks God not to lay it ]{.class_3527}["to their charge"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 16). "Don't charge them when you make up the bill" (see Stephen's similar prayer in Acts 7:60).]{.class_3527}

[The ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[translators added three words to verse 16, and the Greek scholars don't seem to be able to do anything with them. The words were added exactly as the word ]{.class_3527}["God"]{.class_3528}[ was added to Romans 3:6 and Romans 11:1. The request was a ]{.class_3527}[prayer request ]{.class_3526}[in both cases, using a passive optative (in this case λογισθειν]{.class_3527},[ logisthein, and in Romans γενοιτο]{.class_3527},[ genoito). This explains ]{.class_3527}["I PRAY GOD" ]{.class_3528}[(vs. 16).]{.class_3527}

[The new Bibles (contrary to all of their professions about speaking in "plain language" to the "twentieth-century man," who cannot understand the "archaic ]{.class_3527}[AV of 1611]{.class_3526}[") have left it as "May it not be charged against them" (]{.class_3527}[RSV]{.class_3526}[), and "May it not be held against them" (]{.class_3527}[NIV]{.class_3526}[). ]{.class_3527}[No one since 1918 has ever talked in that latter fashion anywhere west of the Atlantic.]{.class_3526}[ "MAY it not be counted against them"? When? By whom? Who does the counting? Who blames them if they were "blamed"? When will they be "charged" or could be charged? No light at all. The new translations (this time) abandon "dynamic equivalence" (a standard alibi for altering the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[text) and take up "literal correspondence" for the sole purpose of altering it again: anything but the text.]{.class_3527}

"[And that all the Gentiles might hear" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 17). "Rome" stood for the [Gentiles]{.class_3530}, according to Romans 1. The Roman Empire at this time stretched from Abyssinia to Scotland, from Spain to the Black Sea, from Carthage to Denmark, and from Portugal to Syria. The expression is comparable to Acts 19:10, where "all they that were in ASIA heard the word of God." What confounds the commentator (see below) is very simple to anyone who has had any [experience ]{.class_3530}in preaching. Paul's defense became a matter of public gossip all over town and all through "Caesar's household." Furthermore, the preaching that he had done, when in "open arrest" (Acts 28), had had time to go all over Rome, and all over Italy for that matter. If he had visited [Spain ]{.class_3530}in the interim (see Introduction and Preface), ["the preaching" ]{.class_3529}had gone into Portugal and France before his second arrest. If he also made a trip to [Britain ]{.class_3530}in the interval (see Preface), ["the preaching"]{.class_3529} had gone to Scotland, Ireland, and possibly North Germany before Nero executed him. ["That all the Gentiles might hear"]{.class_3529} (vs. 17).

[Church history corroborates this, for the "hot-beds" of New Testament, anti-Catholic, evangelical Christianity were in northern Spain, northern Italy, Scotland, and Ireland immediately following the Council of Arles (314). Later, missionaries from these places "infested" the Danube, South Germany, southeast France, northwest Italy, and Austria (see ]{.class_3527}[The History of the New Testament Church]{.class_3526}[, Vol. I, chaps. 10, 12). Rome had been the goal of Paul's ministry according to his original commission (Acts 9:15), for it was the hub of the Empire. He considers his ministry finished when he has accomplished his preaching in this place.]{.class_3527}

"[And I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion"]{.class_3528} (vs. 17) refers to two things: 1. He was going to be decapitated, which is a merciful death alongside being "et" by the lions in the ampitheater. 2. God overruled the ["roaring lion" ]{.class_3529}of 1 Peter 5:8 and did not allow him to "touch the carcass" (as it was recorded in 1 Kings 13:28 for the benefit of Greek scholars who didn't know their Greek text from a hole in the ground). God ["delivered" ]{.class_3529}Paul in the sense that he kept him alive, [in spite of the Devil,]{.class_3530} until he had "finished his course." These references Vincent calls "imaginary" references (p. 329), because Vincent can no more expound the Scriptures than whistle and swallow at the same time. Vincent says it was "a [figurative expression ]{.class_3530}for danger of death," which, in view of the historical context, is about as stupid a thing as one could possibly imagine. Paul at this writing was not [delivered ]{.class_3530}from the "danger of death" at all:[ he was executed.]{.class_3530}

Makes you wonder, don't it, man?

[The writer knows he is going to get ]{.class_3527}[killed ]{.class_3526}[(vs. 6); he writes his ]{.class_3527}[last will ]{.class_3526}[and testament (1 and 2 Timothy), commits his soul to Christ at the Judgment Seat (4:8), announces his ]{.class_3527}[departure ]{.class_3526}[(vs. 6), and then says that God "delivered him from the danger of DEATH"? ]{.class_3527}[When?]{.class_3526}[ If in the past, WHO ]{.class_3527}["HAD THE POWER OF DEATH"]{.class_3528}[ (Heb. 2:14) if it wasn't a LION (1 Pet. 5:8)? What Vincent calls a "metaphorical" interpretation (]{.class_3527}[Word Studies in the New Testament ]{.class_3526}[by Vincent, p. 177) turns out to be a ]{.class_3527}[Biblical ]{.class_3526}[doctrine: "sound doctrine." "[Do thy diligence to come before winter"]{.class_3528} (vs. 21), before the haze of Indian Summer fades, and November winds strip bare the trees and put them to sleep in blankets of ice and snow. Imagine him standing there in the snow with ["the cloke"]{.class_3529} and ["the books"]{.class_3529} and the ["parchments,"]{.class_3529} and no one to give them to!

[Paul's last earthly residence was the lower dungeon of the Mammertime prison, called the ]{.class_3527}["Tullianum."]{.class_3526}[ It was a dungeon about six and a half feet high and eight feet square, with the only air coming through the roof, and that "roof" was the floor of the upper dungeon. It was cold and damp most of the year. Out of there, one morning, the old soldier of the cross was hauled like John the Baptist had been (Mark 6:27--28) and marched off to the chopping block. Perhaps he muttered what an English saint muttered in 1550 on the way to the same place: "In fifteen minutes I'll have the answer! In ten minutes I'll know it all! In five minutes I'll have my answers! Bless God, in one minute I'll know!"]{.class_3527}

We hope Timothy got the books and parchments to him before the fatal and final scene came.

The text (vs. 21) reminds us of the fact that opportunity knocks once, and then it wears wings. You can no more call back a lost opportunity and bag it than you can gun down the sparrows that hopped in the field last year. There are some things we never get over.

1. Opportunities to witness to lost loved ones.

2. Opportunities to show kindness to the departed.

3. Opportunities to develop character.

4. Opportunities to receive the Lord Jesus Christ.

Sermon outlines:

A. PASSING IT ON:

[1. The ]{.class_3527}[things ]{.class_3526}[that deal with the birth, life, death, burial, and resurrection of Christ.]{.class_3527}

[2. The ]{.class_3527}[things ]{.class_3526}[that deal with "repentance toward God" and "faith towards Christ."]{.class_3527}

[3. The ]{.class_3527}[things ]{.class_3526}[that deal with sober and godly living in a wicked and adulterous generation.]{.class_3527}

[4. The ]{.class_3527}[things ]{.class_3526}[that deal with the future and the Second Advent.]{.class_3527}

[5. The ]{.class_3527}[things ]{.class_3526}[that deal with persecutions, trials, and suffering for Christ's sake.]{.class_3527}

[6. The ]{.class_3527}[things ]{.class_3526}[that deal with preaching the truth and standing for the truth.]{.class_3527}

B. CHRISTIAN WARFARE\
(see remarks under 2 Tim. 2:3--4).

C. THE CHRISTIAN OLYMPICS (2 Tim. 2:5).

1. The runner must not look down at the track.

2. The runner must not look at the audience in the grandstand.

3. The runner must stay in his own lane.

4. The runner must run to win, not to "place" or "show."

5. The runner must stay in physical condition by practicing.

6. The runner must keep his eye on the Finisher of his course.

D. THE FRUIT BEARER (2 Tim. 2:6).

1. He must protect the vine from foxes and robbers.

2. He must water and fertilize the vine and prune it.

3. He must put it in charge of others when leaving it (Matt. 21:33).

4. He is entitled to its fruit (1 Cor. 9:7).

E. THE UNBOUND WORD OF GOD

(see remarks under 2 Tim. 2:9).

F. THE THREE IF'S (2 Tim. 2:11--13).

[1. ]{.class_3527}["If we suffer"]{.class_3528}[ with Him, we shall ]{.class_3527}["reign with him."]{.class_3528}

[2. If we are dead with Him, we shall ]{.class_3527}["live with him."]{.class_3528}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["If we deny Him,"]{.class_3528}[ He ]{.class_3527}["will deny us"]{.class_3528}[ a reign.]{.class_3527}

[4. ]{.class_3527}["If we believe not,"]{.class_3528}[ He will still confess us before God, as we are part of His Body.]{.class_3527}

G. THE CHRISTIAN WORKMAN (2 Tim. 2:15).

[1. The workman must ]{.class_3527}[study]{.class_3526}[,]{.class_3527}

a. by comparing Scripture with Scripture.

b. by noticing similitudes and contrasts.

c\. by constant repetition.

[2. The workman must be ]{.class_3527}[approved ]{.class_3526}[by God.]{.class_3527}

a. not the local ministerial association.

b. not by his friends.

c. not by the standards of the world or modern Chris tian scholarship.

[3. The workman must be able to ]{.class_3527}[divide ]{.class_3526}[the word of truth.]{.class_3527}

a. noting which things are different.

b. noting which things have different applications.

c. noting to whom these applications apply and when.

H. THE FOUNDATION OF GOD (2 Tim. 2:19).

1. It has a seal (because it is an official document).

2. It is built on the premise of eternal security.

3. It is built on the premise that a holy life should follow a holy profession.

I. AMWAY AND TUPPERWARE (2 Tim. 2:20--21).

[1. There are vessels of ]{.class_3527}[gold ]{.class_3526}[for the choicest jobs that God wants done (Paton, Judson, Carey, Paul).]{.class_3527}

[2. There are vessels of ]{.class_3527}[silver ]{.class_3526}[for display and eating (Moody, Finney, Sunday, Norris, et al.).]{.class_3527}

[3. There are vessels of ]{.class_3527}[wood ]{.class_3526}[for rough jobs and hard tasks that involve dirt and trash (lumberjacks, rescue missions).]{.class_3527}

[4. There are vessels of ]{.class_3527}[earth ]{.class_3526}[(clay) for dirty jobs and filthy jobs (prison ministries by prisoners).]{.class_3527}

5. God can use ANY vessel and does. A golden pitcher (Eccl. 12:6) is more honorable than a bed pan (2 Tim. 4:10).

J. YOUTHFUL LUSTS (2 Tim. 2:22).

1. They are not confined to young men. Illustrate.

2. They reside in the Adamic nature of the believer (Gal. 5; Rom. 5). Illustrate.

3. They are to be ABANDONED with speed and not tolerated or "coddled" (Rom. 6).

K. THE MAN WHO FELL IN LOVE WITH\
    HIMSELF (2 Tim. 3:2).

1. He has no competitors.

2. He wouldn't do it over again if he had a chance.

3. He loved an unworthy object.

-or-

[1. Always winds up wanting things that don't belong to him (]{.class_3527}[covetous]{.class_3526}[).]{.class_3527}

[2. Always winds up bragging about what he has (]{.class_3527}[boasters]{.class_3526}[).]{.class_3527}

[3. Always winds up lying about his competitors (]{.class_3527}[false accusers, traitors]{.class_3526}[).]{.class_3527}

[4. Always winds up with a big head (]{.class_3527}[proud, heady, highminded]{.class_3526}[).]{.class_3527}

[5. Always winds up opposing the truth (]{.class_3527}[fierce, incontinent, despisers,]{.class_3526}[ et al.).]{.class_3527}

[6. Always winds up hating God and the Bible (]{.class_3527}[unholy, blasphemers, unthankful]{.class_3526}[).]{.class_3527}

[7. Eventually imitates Christianity (]{.class_3527}["form of godliness"]{.class_3528}[) while going from one pleasure to another (vs. 4).]{.class_3527}

L. THE EVER-LEARNING SCHOLARS: (2 Tim. 3:7).

[1. Their ]{.class_3527}[motive ]{.class_3526}[(Prov. 18:1--2): to find an alibi to sin.]{.class_3527}

[2. Their ]{.class_3527}[specialty ]{.class_3526}[(1 Cor. 2:4): the wisdom of this world.]{.class_3527}

[3. Their ]{.class_3527}[language ]{.class_3526}[(Rom. 16:18; 1 Cor. 2:1): to flatter and misguide the unwary.]{.class_3527}

[4. Their ]{.class_3527}[confidence ]{.class_3526}[(1 Cor. 3:20): their own I.Q.'s and intellectual degrees.]{.class_3527}

[5. Their ]{.class_3527}[stupidity ]{.class_3526}[(1 Cor. 3:19; Eph. 4:18).]{.class_3527}

[6. Their ]{.class_3527}[inability ]{.class_3526}[to learn the Truth (Isa. 28:9--13; Luke 10:21).]{.class_3527}

M. THE PATH OF THE MINISTRY

    [(2 Tim. 3:10--11). The verses form a natural outline of nine points.]{.class_3527}

N. WHY GOD WROTE WHAT HE WROTE

    [(2 Tim. 3:16).]{.class_3527}

1. To show mankind what is true and what is false (doctrine).

2. To show mankind where his conduct is wrong (reproof).

3. To show mankind what they should do when they are wrong (correction).

4. To show mankind how to live right after they have corrected their mistakes (righteousness).

5. A man who rejects the Bible loses all FOUR supernaturally given blessings.

O. SCRIPTURE AND THE MAN OF GOD

    [(2 Tim. 3:16--17).]{.class_3527}

[1. A "perfect" man of God has to have ]{.class_3527}[the Scriptures ]{.class_3526}[to study.]{.class_3527}

[2. A "perfect" man of God has to have Scriptures that were "GIVEN BY]{.class_3527}[ INSPIRATION OF GOD."]{.class_3526}

[3. A "perfect" man of God has to be ]{.class_3527}[equipped ]{.class_3526}[(furnished) completely to serve God.]{.class_3527}

[4. A "perfect" man of God can be perfectly equipped]{.class_3527}[ to every good work.]{.class_3526}

[5. A "perfect" man of God takes REPROOF, ]{.class_3527}[CORRECTION]{.class_3526}[, AND INSTRUCTION from the Scriptures.]{.class_3527}

P. REAL PREACHING VERSUS "COPING" AND "SHARING" (2 Tim. 4:2).

[1. ]{.class_3527}[Real preaching]{.class_3526}[ reproves sinners for their sins, names their sins, and tells of the consequences of those sins. "Coping" or "sharing" or "rapping" avoids anything "judgmental."]{.class_3527}

[2. ]{.class_3527}[Real preaching]{.class_3526}[ rebukes the sinner for his sins and puts the sinner down in plain language where he "gets the message." Modern preaching rebukes "class sins" or "social sins" or "integration"; there is no personal application.]{.class_3527}

[3. ]{.class_3527}[Real preaching]{.class_3526}[ exhorts the sinner to do something about his sins and his sinful condition.]{.class_3527}

Q. THE MODERN APOSTATE FUNDAMENTALIST (2 Tim. 4:3--4).

1. He cannot sit still long under straight, hard, pointed, personal preaching (vs. 3).

2. He desires to be taught instead of "preached at" (vs. 3).

3. He prefers the "historical positions" of fundamentalism or the "traditional beliefs" of his "faith" to the WORDS of the Holy Bible.

R. BON VOYAGE! OR A GOOD EXIT (2 Tim. 4:6).

1. A good departure from this life can only be made after a good fight has been fought.

2. A good departure from this life can only be made after the race course has been run to the end.

3. A good departure from this life can only be made after a trust has been guarded throughout.

S. THE DAY OF RIGHTEOUSNESS (2 Tim. 4:8).

1. A righteous judge will give rewards to the righteous.

[2. ]{.class_3527}["A crown of righteousness"]{.class_3528}[ will be given the righteous.]{.class_3527}

[3. Those will be counted righteous who ]{.class_3527}["love his appearing"]{.class_3528}[: the Judge ("Heah come de Judge!").]{.class_3527}

T. IN LOVE WITH THE WORLD (2 Tim. 4:10).

1. When Demas forsook Paul he forsook

a\. a saint.

b\. a soulwinner.

c\. a soldier.

d\. a seer.

2. When Demas loved this world, he fell in love with

a\. an evil system (Gal. 1:4).

b\. a temporary system (1 John 2:15--17).

c\. an anti-Christian system (John 17:9, 14, 16).

d\. a destructive system (Matt. 7:13).

U. THE EVER-PRESENT HELP (2 Tim. 4:17--18).

1\. Paul, near death, claims that God delivered him in the past (vs. 17).

2\. Paul, near death, claims that God is delivering him in the present (vs. 18).

3\. Paul, near death, claims that God will deliver him in the future (vs. 18).

4\. The three tenses are found exactly in this order in 2 Corinthians 1:10.

[V. ]{.class_3527}["COME BEFORE WINTER"]{.class_3528}

(the sermon outline can be gleaned from the notes under that verse: 2 Tim. 4:21).

[Now, these, of course, are merely suggestions. There are many other possibilities, especially if the sermons are given as topical messages and then other verses of Scripture are referred to, to expand the "topic." We have stuck here more to the expository type of message, sticking closely to the subject matter ]{.class_3527}[in the verses.]{.class_3526}[ We hope they may be of some help to the preacher who wishes to "preach through" the pastoral epistles.]{.class_3527}
