TITUS

INTRODUCTION

[Titus is mentioned several places in the Scriptures (2 Cor. 2:13; Gal. 2:1; 2 Tim. 4:10), although he is not mentioned by name in the Book of Acts. In this epistle, Titus is addressed as the pastor of the church in ]{.class_3527}[Crete ]{.class_3526}[(vs. 5) and is given a set of instructions somewhat like those found in 1 Timothy, and for the same reasons. Both young men are pastors and need to know how to handle their "flocks." This accounts for the many similarities in the two epistles, especially information regarding false teachers, false doctrine, useless arguments, holy living, qualifications for bishops, and the reasons for living ]{.class_3527}["soberly" ]{.class_3528}[and ]{.class_3527}["righteously."]{.class_3528}

The scholars come apart only in a few places while studying Titus, for he is not nearly as terrifying as "Terrible Timothy" who "creams them" almost every time a verse shows up (see 1 Tim. 1:6, 16; 2:5, 6, 15; 3:2, 7, 16; 4:1, 8, 16; 5:23; 6:5, 10, 20; 2 Tim. 1:7; 2:3, 6, 12, 13, 15, 18, 26; 3:7, 15, 16 and 4:8, 10, 17, and 20). In Titus the only real stumbling blocks are 1:2, 12; 2:13; and 3:5, 10.

Titus has three chapters, 46 verses, and 921 words. The date of writing is probably between Paul's first and second imprisonments, which would be anywhere between A.D. 62 and A.D. 67. Naturally, "there are those" (a Campbellite and scholars' cliché: 1900--1990) who reject the Pauline authorship, and the reader can easily guess what we will do with these gentlemen. (And we use the term "gentlemen" here quite liberally.) These apostates tell us that in a letter as "intimate" as "Titus," Paul could not have put the stress on his apostleship, which he does. To which may be said: "Celibacy is not hereditary." They then tell us that the writer puts an emphasis on organization and orthodoxy quite unlike his other epistles. To which may be replied: "The other line always moves faster." And finally, they say that there is a change in style from Paul's former language and expressions. To which may be replied: "In order to get a loan, you first have to prove you don't need it."

[All seriousness aside, what man with a ]{.class_3527}["SOUND MIND"]{.class_3528}[ (see Appendix VIII) would tolerate a fifteen second discussion of such pious nonsense? Titus and Timothy are written to ]{.class_3527}[individuals]{.class_3526}[, not churches; why would their style or content match Romans and Philippians? Titus and Timothy are two pastors dealing with ]{.class_3527}[organizations;]{.class_3526}[ why would their instructions match Ephesians and Colossians? Titus and Timothy were written after A.D. 64; why would their words and expressions match Thessalonians and Galatians, written before A.D. 52? And who couldn't figure out such simple problems but a Greek or Hebrew scholar at Liberty University or Tennessee Temple University?]{.class_3527}

[Titus ]{.class_3526}[is a pastor in Crete. ]{.class_3527}[Crete ]{.class_3526}[is a large island off the north coast of Africa. The Cretans come from Ham. In 1 Samuel 30:14; Ezekiel 25:16; and Zephaniah 2:5, they are referred to as ]{.class_3527}["Cherethites,"]{.class_3528}[ which is taken to mean "Cretans." The name is connected with CAPHTOR and CAPHTORIM (Deut. 2:23; Jer. 47:4 and Amos 9:7). This connection with the Hamites (Gen. 10:14) deals such a blow to the scholars at 1:12 in Titus that all of them not only refuse to apply the recorded racial slam but refuse even to RECOGNIZE the ]{.class_3527}[overtones ]{.class_3526}[of the verse.]{.class_3527}

[The verse is a "racial bigot" passage where the "white supremist" is guilty of "unchristian prejudice" in ascribing a racial characteristic to a race EN MASSE. The characteristics ascribed to them ]{.class_3527}[by the Holy Ghost]{.class_3526}[ is that they are LAZY and that they are ]{.class_3527}["LIARS"]{.class_3528}[ (see Titus 1:12 and comments). The matter goes by the faces of the Alexandrian Cult like a phantom UFO. Not one of them dares apply the text. Titus 1:12 is a genuine piece of "hate literature" aimed at the "oppressed minorities" in America who do not get any "social justice," and it was written by the Third Person of the Godhead. (We assume if you take a different position it is because you are anti-God as well as "anti-apartheid.")]{.class_3527}

[According to mythology, ]{.class_3527}[Mount Ida ]{.class_3526}[in Crete was the birthplace of Zeus, and ]{.class_3527}[King Minos ]{.class_3526}[was supposed to have been his son. The island was colonized by the ]{.class_3527}[Dorians,]{.class_3526}[ and after the Trojan wars, the cities on the island formed themselves into little "republics." The chief cities were Cnossus, Cydonia, Gortyna, and Lyctus. Crete was annexed to the Roman Empire in 67 B.C. The ]{.class_3527}[Saracens ]{.class_3526}[invaded Crete in A.D. 823, and from the thirteenth to the seventeenth century it was held by the "Venetian Republic." In Paul's day, the Cretans were present at Pentecost (Acts 2:11), and Paul, of course, passed by Crete on his voyage to Rome, stopping over at the "Fair Havens" (Acts 27:8--9).]{.class_3527}

While Paul spent some time in Crete (Acts 27:7--9), he won some people to Christ who later formed a local church. He returned to Crete after his first imprisonment (see Phil. 2:24 and Philemon 22), and then after leaving he wrote these instructions back to Titus; he probably wrote from Corinth, although the matter will never be completely settled.

CHAPTER ONE

1:1 Paul, a servant of God, and an apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God's elect, and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;

2 In hope of eternal life, which God, that cannot lie, promised before the world began;

3 But hath in due time manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;"

[The opening shows the reader that a transition is about to take place in the New Testament, for the next book that opens this way is James (James 1:1, ]{.class_3527}["a SERVANT of God"]{.class_3528}[). Up until here, the term ]{.class_3527}["servant of God"]{.class_3528}[ has been missing in the openings to the epistles, at least where the word ]{.class_3527}["God"]{.class_3528}[ was used. Note that Romans 1:1 begins the Church Age epistles with ]{.class_3527}["a servant of Jesus Christ," ]{.class_3528}[but Titus winds up the Church Age epistles with ]{.class_3527}["a servant of God,"]{.class_3528}[ whereupon James picks up the Tribulation epistles with ]{.class_3527}["A SERVANT OF GOD,"]{.class_3528}[ and John winds the matter up with ]{.class_3527}["See thou do it not: for I AM THY FELLOWSERVANT"]{.class_3528}[ (Rev. 22:9).]{.class_3527}

[The reader understands that we are not "Hypers" and are no kin to the ]{.class_3527}[Dry-Cleaning Clan]{.class_3526}[ of Stam, Baker, O'Hair, and Bullinger; we take ]{.class_3527}[every book]{.class_3526}[ in the Bible as applying to a child of God where REPROOF, CORRECTION, and INSTRUCTION are concerned (2 Tim. 3:16), and where ADMONITION (1 Cor. 10:11) and COMFORT (Rom. 15:4) are concerned. We point out the dispensational aspects of Paul to James only to show the reader the peculiar place that "HEBREWS" is going to occupy in this chain of succession (see The ]{.class_3527}[Bible Believer's Commentary on Hebrews,]{.class_3526}[ 1998.)]{.class_3527}

"[The faith of God's elect"]{.class_3528} (vs. 1). Note the ["elect"]{.class_3529} are already saved, as they are in 1 Peter 1:2 (conditioned on foreknowledge; not "unconditional") and 1 Thessalonians 1:4. ["According to the faith of God's elect,"]{.class_3529} Paul is an apostle. He may not have been an apostle to the Judaizers and false teachers in 2 Corinthians 11:5; 12:11, but to the Lord's people he was a servant of God and an apostle. The altering of this verse to "for the faith" ([NIV]{.class_3530}) and "to further the faith" ([RSV]{.class_3530}) and "sent to bring faith" ([Dead Dodo]{.class_3530}) and other cute little Kutilek tricks (see Appendix VIII) are just so much Alexandrian flap-doodle inserted because the "recognized scholar" couldn't make heads nor tails out of the English. Paul is a servant and apostle [according to those who acknowledge THE TRUTH]{.class_3530} (vs. 1); ["which is after godliness."]{.class_3529}

[There is also a double application, in that Paul claims to be an acknowledger of the truth himself, the truth that is connected with ]{.class_3527}["godliness"]{.class_3528}[---not "ungodliness."]{.class_3527}

"[In hope of eternal life\...promised before the world began" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 2). Panic.[ "Promised"]{.class_3529}? Promised to whom? ["Before the world began"]{.class_3529}? [Who ]{.class_3530}was around to make such a promise to? "A contracted expression for "PURPOSED before the world began and promised ACTUALLY IN TIME" (Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, p. 1385). Oh, I see; Paul [lied.]{.class_3530} He didn't really mean what he [said;]{.class_3530} he really [meant ]{.class_3530}what you [think ]{.class_3530}he meant.

"[God's plan of salvation was not an afterthought" (Sumner Wemp, Liberty University, p. 64). No "promise"? "Plan of salvation"? If it wasn't an "afterthought" but a ]{.class_3527}[forethought]{.class_3530}, where would a "promise" come in? [A promise to whom?]{.class_3530}

"[There would SEEM TO BE \[oh, yes, buster, haven't you busted YOUR balloon before!\] a reference here to the same truth that John expresses in HIS logos doctrine, the profound RECOGNITION that God's promises are GROUNDED in His eternal purposes" (Guthrie, p. 182). What? No "PROMISE"? ]{.class_3527}["Promises,"]{.class_3530} is it? "Grounded," not [promised?]{.class_3530} "The same truth that John expresses"? I don't rememeber reading about any "PROMISE" in John 1:1--3 or 1 John 1:1--2. Did I miss something?

"[From different points of view, one MAY say that eternal life was promised and GIVEN to man in Christ before times eternal" (]{.class_3527}[The Expositors Greek Testament,]{.class_3530} p. 186). Well, one may say that a cow has six legs. From another "point of view," one may say that only the "elect" were guaranteed eternal life, and from another point of view\..., etc.

[But here is one man who brushed by the truth. Unfortunately, he didn't know enough Bible to exposit the text, but he brushed the text: "which God promised TO OUR SAVIOUR JESUS CHRIST" (Wilbur Wallis, ]{.class_3527}[Wycliffe Commentary,]{.class_3526}[ p. 1393). Having said this, the commentator disappears into the forest. But who that believed the Book (instead of the "original autographs": see Custer, Appendix XI) could refrain himself at this point?]{.class_3527}

[1. ]{.class_3527}["Who in the days of his flesh\...strong crying and tears unto him that was ABLE TO SAVE HIM FROM DEATH, AND WAS HEARD IN THAT HE FEARED"]{.class_3528}[ (Heb. 5:7).]{.class_3527}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["He asked LIFE of thee, and thou gavest it him, even length of days FOR EVER AND FOR EVER" ]{.class_3528}[(Psa. 21:4).]{.class_3527}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["But God will redeem my soul from the power of the grave: for he shall receive me"]{.class_3528}[ (Psa. 49:15).]{.class_3527}

[5. ]{.class_3527}["The sorrows of death compassed me\...the sorrows of HELL compassed me\...HE SENT FROM ABOVE, HE TOOK ME, HE DREW ME OUT OF MANY WATERS"]{.class_3528}[ (Psa. 18:4, 5, 16. Moses as a type! The "great deeps"! See Genesis 1 and comments).]{.class_3527}

[The promise was made to the Lord Jesus Christ when He was faced with the proposition of DYING for sinners (see Rev. 13:8 ]{.class_3527}["from the foundation of the world"]{.class_3528}[). The "Fundamentalists" fell apart before one of the greatest verses in the entire Bible on eternal life and the One Who brought it to man. Par for the course.

"[Which God, that cannot lie"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2) is not worded right. It is a little too plain and crude for "refined sensibilities" of the Alexandrian Cult. Consequently, "God never lies" ([RSV]{.class_3530}), or "does not lie" ([NIV]{.class_3530}), or is "free from falsehood" (Guthrie), or whatever. Easton's theory is that Paul could not have said such a thing because Christians and Jews "already took such a thing for granted." That is about as intelligent as saying that the Jews who crucified Christ knew He wasn't lying when they crucified Him.

Christians and Jews have always doubted God when He said something they didn't comprehend. The Bible is replete with case after case after case where they didn't believe Him (see Heb. 3; Jer. 42--45; Exod. 16; Num. 14; John 12:37; Isa. 57:1--9). [Men need to be reminded that ]{.class_3527}[God cannot lie. God told Balak that to his face]{.class_3526}[ (Num. 23:19).]{.class_3527}

"[But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 3). The word here was his promise, and it was manifested at the resurrection of Christ (Acts 1:3; Rom. 1:1--4). It is the preaching of this ["word" ]{.class_3529}in which Paul is engaged (Acts 9; 13; 14; 16): ["which is committed unto ME according to the commandment of God our Saviour"]{.class_3529} (vs. 3). Observe that the verse identified the ["God"]{.class_3529} of Titus 2:13, exactly as He is identified again in 2:10. There has been no attack on the Deity of Christ by the [AV ]{.class_3530}in Titus 2:13, in spite of the insidious rumble from the Alexandrian Cult between 1970 and 2000 after they read [Satan's Masterpiece: The NASV,]{.class_3530} where verse after verse after verse was produced to show that the [NASV ]{.class_3530}was an Antichrist "bible."

1:4 To Titus, mine own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.

5 For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in order the things that are wanting, and ordain elders in every city, as I had appointed thee:

6 If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly.

7 For a bishop must be blameless, as the steward of God; not selfwilled, not soon angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;

8 But a lover of hospitality, a lover of good men, sober, just, holy, temperate;

9 Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to convince the gainsayers.

"[Mine own son after the common faith"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4). Called his ["son"]{.class_3529} because he led him to Christ, as he had led Timothy (see 1 Tim. 1:2, 18). ["The common faith"]{.class_3529} would be the faith shared by all believers at this time. In Catholic mythology, this is called the "Catholic faith"---borrowing the word from a spurious copy of [Ignatius ]{.class_3530}which was forged by a Roman Catholic in the second century (see the lengthy list of Catholic forgeries in [Catholicism Against Itself, ]{.class_3530}Vol. I, chapter 5, O. C. Lambert, 1956). ["The common faith"]{.class_3529} shows that Titus is (according to that body of revealed truth) in the right faith as a convert of Paul's.

Verse 5 is self-explanatory (see Acts 14:23 and Phil. 1:1 and comments in those commentaries).

"[Having faithful children not accused of riot or unruly"]{.class_3528} (vs. 6). The first half of the verse was covered under 1 Timothy 3:1--6, which see. The ["faithful children" ]{.class_3529}here have been forced into being "CONVERTED children" by the Alexandrian Cult. This peculiar perverseness that came from "sticking by the original Greek" at this point, instead of consulting the Author, leads to the remarkable teaching that before a man can qualify as a pastor or bishop all of his children must be "believers." [This guarantees baptismal regeneration by baby-sprinkling]{.class_3530} (see it in evidence under 3:5), for no pastor's children have "believed" on Christ when they were [born,]{.class_3530} and many of them do not until five to ten years after they are born. The [AV ]{.class_3530}translators here, even though they were Episcopalians, used much better judgment than the apostate Conservatives who translated the [NIV]{.class_3530}, or the apostate Fundamentalists who translated the [NASV]{.class_3530}.

Exhortation, plus convincing, would include methods of argumentation ([apriori, aposteriori, afortiori, ad hominem, ex concesso, reducto ad absurdum,]{.class_3530} etc.). All of the Biblical prophets (Isaiah, Jeremiah, Ezekiel, Hosea, Joel, Amos) as well as Paul, James, Peter, and John use these methods in their writings. The "Dilemma" and "Trilemma" and Syllogism are all used and are all "legit." A good preacher, like a good lawyer, should be able to:

1\. Represent his "Client."

2. State his Client's position and cause.

3. Prove that his Client is honest and right in His actions and speech.

4. Win the favor of the court for the cause of his Client.

1:10 For there are many unruly and vain talkers and deceivers, specially they of the circumcision:

11 Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthy lucre's sake.

12 One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians are alway liars, evil beasts, slow bellies.

13 This witness is true. Wherefore rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;

14 Not giving heed to Jewish fables, and commandments of men, that turn from the truth.

15 Unto the pure all things are pure: but unto them that are defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their mind and conscience is defiled.

16 They profess that they know God; but in works they deny him, being abominable, and disobedient, and unto every good work reprobate.

[Titus is going to get trouble from the ]{.class_3527}["Cretians."]{.class_3528}[ The description that follows constitutes one of the greatest negative, "judgmental" pieces of work ever produced by the Holy Spirit through a Christian who talked about ]{.class_3527}["sound speech that cannot be condemned"]{.class_3528}[ (Titus 2:8). "[Unruly"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). They refused to let the pastor "pastor" and counted the admonitions of Hebrews 13:17 to be senseless. "[Vain talkers"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). This is like the ["vain jangling"]{.class_3529} of 1 Timothy 1:6, which all of the "newer, reliable versions" got rid of. ["Vain talkers"]{.class_3529} recognize their kinfolk when they show up!

"[Whose mouths must be stopped"]{.class_3528} (vs. 11) because of three things:

[1. Their motive in teaching is money (vs. 11, cf. 1 Tim. 6:5, 10), so they will not tolerate 1 Timothy 6:10 as it stands in the Holy Bible (]{.class_3527}[AV]{.class_3526}[). [The raving, fanatical ambitions of men like Robert Sumner, Pope John Paul II, Doug Kutilek, Ed Dobson, Bob Jones III, and Donald Waite are so simple that a child could understand them. "[One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians are alway liars\...slow bellies"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). [The citation is from a Cretian himself---]{.class_3527}[Epimenides ]{.class_3526}[of the sixth century B.C. Plato calls him "an oraculor"---speaking as an oracle. (Epimenides is contemporary with ]{.class_3527}[Solon]{.class_3526}[, born around 659 B.C. He composed a "Theogony" and poems concerning religious mysteries.) Vincent supposes that it was Epimenides who told the Athenians to erect altars to "unknown Gods" (]{.class_3527}[Word Studies,]{.class_3526}[ p. 189). The first three words found here are also found in the ]{.class_3527}[Hymn to Zeus]{.class_3526}[ by Callimachus, found in Hesiod's ]{.class_3527}[Theogony ]{.class_3526}[(26). At any rate, the Cretians have such a reputation for gluttony and lying that the Holy Spirit has written ]{.class_3527}["THIS WITNESS IS TRUE"]{.class_3528}[ (vs. 13.)]{.class_3527}

"[Evil beasts"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). That is cruel and brutal. For a good picture of it, obtain any of the material published by the Church League of America on what actually happened when Idi Amin (Hamite) and the revolutionaries in South Africa (1975--1984) took over. Mutilating women, gang raping of women in their fifties, cannibalism, and beating children to death with shovels and clubs is standard operating procedure with "Cretians." The alibi naturally would be "apartheid." "We were enslaved for so long, we are entitled to a little torture, rape, murder, and cannibalism." But Idi Amin was BLACK. There was no apartheid in his government. ["EVIL BEASTS."]{.class_3529}

"[Slow bellies"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). A very graphic picture of an individual who is 100--200 pounds overweight. He cannot move fast because his belly gets in the way. Furthermore, [Dunlap's Disease ]{.class_3530}("mah belly done overlapped mah belt") has produced something that looks like a walking gourd. ("I think I'm losing weight, but each year my hammock sinks lower and lower.") I have seen these walking bellies so laden with lard that, honestly, the man could not even see his TOES when he looked straight down. ("I tried on my old army uniform last night, and the only thing that fit was the tie.") I remember one "youth director" who was a "coach" for a Christian ball team. He was six feet tall and weighed 420 pounds. He could not lower his elbows to his side when driving an automobile. He had so much fat on his ribs, flanks, and under his armpits that his arms had to be raised to shoulder level when steering; it looked like a crab hanging on to a frisbee. ("I bought my wife something that didn't fit her, so I had to return it." "What was it, a new dress?" "No, a Volkswagen.")

[If a man has ]{.class_3527}[gland trouble]{.class_3526}[, there is an alibi, but 90 percent of the "beer bellies" and "fatsos" you see in America are not suffering from gland trouble: they are suffering from lack of self control. ("Well, how much am I supposed to weigh, Doctor?" "Well, how much is a hippopotamus supposed to weigh?") "She went on a water diet and gained fifteen gallons." "She was the first four 'America's Ten Best-Dressed Women.'" "When she wears her bikini it looks like an elephant in a diaper." "What passes the lips goes on the hips." "Eat, drink, and be merry, and tomorrow you'll be a big, fat, grinning drunkard."]{.class_3527}

[The slowest belly of all time was ]{.class_3527}[Robert Earl Hughes]{.class_3526}[ of Monticello, Missouri. He weighed 203 pounds when he was six years old and weighed 693 pounds when he was eighteen. He finally tipped (and I mean TIPPED) the scales at 1,069 pounds at thiry-two years of age. His waist was 122 inches around. The heaviest twins were ]{.class_3527}[Benny and Billy McCrary]{.class_3526}[, who, together, weighed almost 1,500 pounds (Hendersonville, N.C.). The heaviest woman was ]{.class_3527}[Mrs. Percy Pearl Washington]{.class_3526}[ (born in Louisiana in 1926) who weighed in at Milwaukee hospital at 800 pounds. That was the limit of the scales. Estimates gave her an additional 80 pounds.]{.class_3527}

What could be any more revolting than a big-bellied, lying BEAST? "Mr. T"? "The Refrigerator"?

Observe that these belly worshippers are mentioned in Romans 16:18 and Philippians 3:19 (see comments in that commentary). In Romans, they are characterized as SMOOTH, SLICK, CULTURED SPEAKERS (Rom. 16:18). This is the veneer for the professional liar (see Appendix XXIII).

"[Wherefore rebuke them SHARPLY, that they may be sound in the faith"]{.class_3528} (vs. 13). Note that those referred to are [Christians]{.class_3530}. Paul did not tell Titus to rebuke them sharply so they could get saved or repent or "recover themselves from ['the snare of the devil']{.class_3529}" or get "converted." He said it was necessary to rebuke them so they would ["be sound IN THE FAITH."]{.class_3529} The implications are that they were NOT ["sound in the faith,"]{.class_3529} although ["in the faith."]{.class_3529} This, of course, is entirely unacceptable to any apostate Fundamentalist (Kutilek, Henderson, Faulkner, Afman, Wisdom, Price, Martin, Wemp, Hindson, Dobson, Sumner, et al.) who accepted the Scofield definition of "apostasy" (see 2 Tim. 4:2--4 and remarks). They tie the ["THEM"]{.class_3529} of verse 13 with the ["THEY"]{.class_3529} of verse 16, in spite of the fact that the ["THEY"]{.class_3529} of verse 16 is a reference to the ["defiled and unbelieving"]{.class_3529} (vs. 15).

"[Rebuke them SHARPLY"]{.class_3528} (vs. 13), not kindly, as in 2 Thessalonians 3:15 or in ["love,"]{.class_3529} as in 1 Thessalonians 4:9, or in ["meekness"]{.class_3529} as in Galatians 6:1--3, or any other desperate side-tracking by a twentieth century American droid who is afraid of losing his income, attendance, subscriptions, enrollment, or property. The word is ["SHARPLY" ]{.class_3529}(see 2 Cor. 13:10). A sharp rebuke is a rebuke that comes from a sharpened weapon (see Rev. 1:16, 2:12; Heb. 4:12; Isa. 49:2). It is designed to pierce and draw blood (Jer. 48:10) and DISEMBOWEL the ["gainsayer"]{.class_3529} (see the exact case in Judg. 3:20--23).

[No modern Christian celebrity could handle (or even tolerate) these instructions. They smack of military combat (see 2 Tim. 2:1--5 and Eph. 6:10--17), and the modern, "militant Fundamentalist" is so occupied with replacing the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version]{.class_3526}[ of the Bible, while building a monument, he cannot rebuke anyone ]{.class_3527}["sharply"]{.class_3528}[ but a Bible believer. When it comes to rebuking Christian scholars, rebuking apostate Fundamentalists, rebuking Roman Catholic popes and priests, rebuking Hollywood's dope peddlers, whoremongers, and perverts, he is at a loss. You can always tell the congregations that have been raised by these charismatic "Fundamentalists" by the fact that they always think in terms of "reaching the lost by love" or "sharing God's love through the gospel" or "speaking kindly of everyone" or "preaching a positive and non-judgmental witness," etc. They are the nerds of Laodicea. There is nothing "Pauline" about them.]{.class_3527}

"[Jewish fables\...commandments of men"]{.class_3528} (vs. 14). For samples, obtain any copy of the Jerusalem or the Babylonian Talmud, and especially the commentaries on the Kabbalah. ["Commandments of men"]{.class_3529} will be the standard set of "do-gooder" regulations found in Mark 7:1--13 and other places. What they amount to is sacramental rituals which are supposed to help purify a man and make him holy or fit for eternal life. Excellent modern examples are the rosary, the "Hail Marys" and "Our Fathers," the "sacraments" or the "Holy Eucharist," "confessional," "anti-nuke demonstrations," evolution, "Vicars of Christ," papal nuncios, Women's Lib, racial integration, and the whole philosophical bag of left-wing nonsense held sacred by the NAACP, INS, UP, AP, CBS, NBC, [Life, Time, Newsweek]{.class_3530}, the NEA, the Vatican State, and the AAAS and AMA. (See [The Christian's Handbook of Science and Philosophy,]{.class_3530} 2000).

"[Unto the pure all things are pure"]{.class_3528} (vs. 15). See Romans 14:14, the idea being that a good conscience about an action about to be undertaken [sanctifies ]{.class_3530}the action (see 1 Tim. 4:1--6 and comments). Paul says a great deal about these matters in 1 Corinthians 8:12 and 1 Corinthians 10:29 and is careful to tell the "purist" that just because YOU see nothing wrong in an action does not necessarily give you the right to partake of it (1 Cor. 6:12; 10:23; and especially Rom. 14:21). If the statement in Titus is taken, isolated from these passages, it would imply that tobacco, manure, pornography, liquor, bingo, dirty jokes, vomit, and flies were PURE if the practitioner had a DEAD enough conscience not to be upset by them! And this is the exact way such matters are handled by "modern man." TOLERANCE OF SIN IS THE FIRST SIGN OF "PROGRESS" TO MODERN SCIENTISTS AND EDUCATORS (see [The Christian's Handbook of Science and Philosophy,]{.class_3530} 2000, pp. 405--410).

[To the unsaved man (and here the context is the]{.class_3527}[ "UNBELIEVING,"]{.class_3528}[ vs. 15), nothing is pure because the unsaved man has a defiled conscience and a defiled mind. The defiled mind is blinded (2 Cor. 4:4) by ignorance (Eph. 4:18) and has been polluted by a filthy heart (Matt. 15:18--20), and since a man speaks ]{.class_3527}["out of the abundance of the heart"]{.class_3528}[ (Matt. 12:34, 39), the TONGUE becomes an instrument to defile the mind (James 3:2--6). To "defile" something is to get it dirty or make it become unclean (see Gen. 34; Num. 19; Ezek. 18; Ezek 23; Num. 5; and Lev. 18). Thus, the unsaved man has a conscience that is no longer reliable. Paul was the greatest "stickler" for conscience (see Acts 23:1, 24:16; Rom. 2:5, 9:1; 1 Cor. 8:7, 10, 12; 2 Cor. 1:12, 4:2; 1 Tim. 1:5, 19, 4:2; etc.), but even Paul found out on the road to Damascus that his conscience PRICKED him in regards to the death of Stephen (Acts 9:5). If he really ]{.class_3527}[followed ]{.class_3526}[his conscience (see Rom. 9:1), he would wind up with Stephen's religion and Stephen's Saviour. At the "moment of truth" (Acts 26:19), Paul obeyed.]{.class_3527}

[This "moment of truth" has occurred so many times in America, since 1700 (during Gospel invitations to receive Christ), that only God knows how the population of Hell has increased through its defilement. ]{.class_3527}[Conscience ]{.class_3526}[leads a man to Christ (Rom. 2:13--16; Acts 10:1--22). The ]{.class_3527}[law ]{.class_3526}[leads a man to Christ (Gal. 3:23--24). Where either are ]{.class_3527}[violated ]{.class_3526}[or disobeyed ]{.class_3527}[continually ]{.class_3526}[(as all college students are taught in all "New Age" curriculums in all state universities), the conscience and mind are ]{.class_3527}[defiled]{.class_3526}[ and then ]{.class_3527}[seared ]{.class_3526}[(see 1 Tim. 4:1--4), until they are finally DEAD (see Herod's case in Luke 23:7--11). Modern scientists, psychologists, educators, psychiatrists, and philosophers are MINDLESS (Rom. 1:22) and CONSCIENCELESS (Rom. 1:28).]{.class_3527}

They cannot be counted on for any spiritual advice regarding anything ETERNAL.

[Hugh Hefner ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[Rock Hudson]{.class_3526}[ are excellent illustrations of the text. One is a drunken fornicator, and the other was a drunken sex pervert. ]{.class_3527}[Both received "good press" all of their lives.]{.class_3526}[ One lives in the dark, coming out after sundown in black airplanes and black cadillacs, and one lied to the man with whom he was sleeping about his VD (called "AIDS" so you wouldn't classify it with syphilis and gonorrhea), after contracting it from a queer who kicked the bucket at twenty-nine years of age. Neither Hefner nor Hudson confessed to committing ONE WRONG act in their life in regards to their lasciviousness and licentiousness (see Eph. 5:4--12). ]{.class_3527}[They were mindless and conscienceless;]{.class_3526}[ ideal "gods" for the Press. ]{.class_3527}[People Magazine ]{.class_3526}[(Oct. 21, 1985) practically canonized Hudson after he was cremated following the Roman Catholic rites of a Catholic priest.]{.class_3527}

[Errol Flynn]{.class_3526}[ and ]{.class_3527}[Marylin Monroe]{.class_3526}[ are two more excellent examples. ]{.class_3527}[John Belushi ]{.class_3526}[and]{.class_3527}[ John Lennon]{.class_3526}[ are two more. Lennon made a "Spanish bastard" out of Jesus Christ and wrote "Happiness Is a Warm Gun." He got one. ]{.class_3527}[Elvis Presley]{.class_3526}[ and]{.class_3527}[ Jane Fonda]{.class_3526}[ could qualify, as could also ]{.class_3527}[Michael Jackson, Bruce Springsteen, Martin Luther King Jr.,]{.class_3526}[ and]{.class_3527}[ Jesse Jackson.]{.class_3526}[ The simple rule of thumb is this: if the news media makes a "god" out of someone and refuses to give them "bad press," it is because they have a DEFILED MIND AND A DEFILED CONSCIENCE (see Luke 16:15). The news media is 100 percent consistent in these matters.]{.class_3527}

"[They profess that they know God" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 16), as did Bloody Mary, Idi Amin, Adolph Hitler (he further professed to be doing God's will!), all Catholic popes, all Catholic priests, all Charismatics, and all "Fundamentalists."

"[But in works they deny him" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 16). Imitating apostolic gifts they do not have ([Charismatics]{.class_3530}), professing to be able to change pieces of starch into Christ's literal body ([Catholic]{.class_3530}), killing five million Christians in the Dark Ages ([Catholic]{.class_3530}), killing two hundred and fifty thousand more in the twentieth century ([Catholic]{.class_3530}), instigating riots and vandalism ([NAACP and "Southern Christian Leadership Conference"]{.class_3530}), destroying the faith of young preachers in the Bible ([the Fundamentalist Scholars' Union]{.class_3530}), gambling ([bingo: Roman Catholic]{.class_3530}) to raise church funds, conning suckers out of their money ([Holiness and Charismatics]{.class_3530}), and many times drinking beer and wine, smoking, and falsifying bank accounts and financial records.

"[Every good work reprobate"]{.class_3528} (vs. 16). Every time they are "put to the test" on a truly "good work" they fail the test. If forced to define what they mean by "The Bible is the Word of God," they will lie their way through with a mass of Jesuit casuistry that would make Ignatius Loyola blush for shame (see Appendix II). When exhorted to witness and win people to Christ, they spend their time trying to get them to talk in tongues or get baptized in water. When given a missionary challenge (and preaching one themselves), they invest their money in buildings, lawns, parking lots, classrooms, and "gyms" in the USA. (You see, what the modern Christian cannot stand in a commentary is a real spiritual [APPLICATION ]{.class_3530}OF THE TEXT. Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown, Dummelow, and Sumner Wemp simply refuse to make any application. That's how you get "recognized.")

[The text applies ]{.class_3527}[directly ]{.class_3526}[to the unsaved, since they have no spiritual life (Rom. 8:1--3), but its APPLICATION AND OUTWORKINGS will be found many times in the lives and ministries of scores of twentieth-century, apostate Fundamentalists who ARE saved.]{.class_3527}

CHAPTER TWO

2:1 But speak thou the things which become sound doctrine:

2 That the aged men be sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience.

3 The aged women likewise, that they be in behaviour as becometh holiness, not false accusers, not given to much wine, teachers of good things;

4 That they may teach the young women to be sober, to love their husbands, to love their children,

5 To be discreet, chaste, keepers at home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.

6 Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.

[The instructions are somewhat similar to those given in 1 Timothy 3, which see. First, ]{.class_3527}["AGED MEN"]{.class_3528}[ are mentioned, then ]{.class_3527}["AGED WOMEN,"]{.class_3528}[ and finally ]{.class_3527}["YOUNG MEN."]{.class_3528}[ All of the admonitions come under the heading of the things that ]{.class_3527}["become sound doctrine,"]{.class_3528}[ which shows that ]{.class_3527}["DOCTRINE," ]{.class_3528}[in the Bible, is not merely a study of abstract truths dealing with "fundamentals" or "prophecy." It is a study in ANY field to show the believer what is ]{.class_3527}[true ]{.class_3526}[and what is ]{.class_3527}[not true;]{.class_3526}[ in this case, right and wrong conduct (see also 1 Tim. 6:1, 3--6 and comments). Bob Jones Sr. (never to be confused with II, III, or IV) used to say, "The ]{.class_3527}[ethics ]{.class_3526}[of the Bible are just as inspired as its ]{.class_3527}[doctrines."]{.class_3526}[ This is a faithful saying, although the actual case is, "moral conduct and ethics are BIBLE DOCTRINES."]{.class_3527}

"[Sober, grave, temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). The first word meant what it said (["sober"]{.class_3529}). The aged men were not to get drunk; they were to lay off the bottle. But to allow them to get drunk occasionally, the ASV of 1901 has erased the word ["sober"]{.class_3529} altogether and later in the verse has given us "sober-minded." (This was Chrysostom's private interpretation: [The Expostor's Greek Testament,]{.class_3530} p. 191).

[The grossly corrupt ]{.class_3527}[NIV ]{.class_3526}[(that has such excellent "readability" in the Christian schools ]{.class_3527}[where it has been promoted for four years]{.class_3526}[) gets the]{.class_3527}[ "aged"]{.class_3528}[ man off the hook also: he doesn't have to stay SOBER as long as he is "self-controlled." The Communist ]{.class_3527}[RSV ]{.class_3526}[of the Federal Soviet (IRS, District Court Judges, etc.) does it up "brown" and says that, as long as ]{.class_3527}["the aged men"]{.class_3528}[ are "sensible," they can get drunk as many times as they want to. The ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[says ]{.class_3527}["SOBER."]{.class_3528}

(When in doubt, throw 100 percent of the new translations out, especially when recommended by "godly" people who are upset about "King James Onlyism.")

[Unfortunately, the word ]{.class_3527}["grave" ]{.class_3528}[(vs. 2) can also be connected with a dirt grave. The word has a reference to "commanding respect by being serious-minded" and having the mind and heart set on eternal matters instead of temporary news items. The word "dignity" comes with this, but as anyone knows, the most "dignified" people in the world are ]{.class_3527}[religious ]{.class_3526}[hypocrites. The Pharisees were the perfect, sober-minded, grave, serious, cultured sacramentalists in their day. They did everything with dignity to "command respect," and this is why they made secret pacts with "hit men" to kill those that hurt their "image" and held secret "kangaroo courts" (Matt. 26:55) so the public would not see them losing their tempers (Mark 14:63--65).]{.class_3527}

Paul is telling Titus' aged men to BE grave and dignified, not to ACT like it. Where the act is, the "GRAVE" is the shroud of a dead man. Sam Jones used to say, "I'll be plenty dignified enough for some of you one day. In my coffin there won't be a hair of my head or a shoelace out of place." Fundamentalism is filled with these "grave" and "sober" Pharisees who come to pieces like spoiled brats when no one is watching them (see Appendix I).

"[Temperate"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2) is the right word for self-control that causes nothing to be "overdone." It is not the same word as ["sober"]{.class_3529} in the same verse, but it is "kin" to it. A [temperate ]{.class_3530}man will not eat too much, sleep too much, work too much, pray too much (you have no business praying when you ought to be witnessing), under-sleep, under-eat, or get his ministry into the place where whenever he opens the Bible to expound it the only illustrations he can think of are his own work and ministry. There is a lot more to "temperate" than knocking off liquor. The most intemperate people on this earth are in the Vatican hierarchy. They actually think and [believe ]{.class_3530}that the Catholic church is the God-ordained mistress that has been commissioned to RUN THE WORLD BEFORE JESUS CHRIST COMES BACK, and that any means to this end are justified (see [Murder in the Vatican,]{.class_3530} A. Manhattan, 1985, and [In God's Name]{.class_3530}).

Popes are "temperate" only when giving political speeches on socialism and capitalism.

"[Faith," "charity,"]{.class_3528} and ["patience"]{.class_3529} (vs. 2) are self-explanatory.

"[As becometh holiness\...not given to much wine"]{.class_3528} (vs. 3). Holiness (see Psa. 29:2, 96:9) is a beautiful thing, according to the Psalmist. ["The beauty of holiness"]{.class_3529} (see Psa. 110:3) is that it [magnifies ]{.class_3530}God, acts as an [antidote ]{.class_3530}to the Adamic nature, [suppresses ]{.class_3530}sin and thereby saves the sinner from reaping a "bountiful crop," [attracts ]{.class_3530}sinners who are tired of sin and "want something better," and [reinforces ]{.class_3530}the promises of God, showing that such a thing is possible.

[The things that ]{.class_3527}["becometh holiness"]{.class_3528}[ (that suit it or match it or "go well with it") are listed in verses 3 and 4. ]{.class_3527}["The aged women"]{.class_3528}[ (Greek πρεσβυιτδας]{.class_3527},[ presbutidas) are called a "PRIESTERLICH" by Von Soden (Vincent, p. 341), and Vincent, anxious to run the nuns into the act (see 1 Tim. 3:11), says: "This is the more striking if, as there is REASON TO BELIEVE, the presbutides represented a SEMI-QUASI OFFICIAL position in the church" (p. 341). Vincent is working hard to get the "deaconesses" installed as ]{.class_3527}["the husband of one wife"]{.class_3528}[ (see 1 Tim. 3:11 and comments).]{.class_3527}

[Fortunately, the ]{.class_3527}[King James]{.class_3526}[ translators had better sense than to translate "the original Greek" as "ELDERESS," although it came from the same Greek word as "elder" in 1 Peter 5:1. (Being raised around 1580 to 1611 gave a translator many advantages which he would not have had, had he been born ]{.class_3527}[after ]{.class_3526}[1860.)]{.class_3527}

"[Not given to much wine"]{.class_3528} (vs. 3) clearly states ["the aged women"]{.class_3529} are not to be chronic alcoholics (lushes). (Note that this is not listed this time under requirements for a church office, but given to the women in the congregation.)

"[Teachers of good things" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 3) here means teaching younger women not to lie (["false accusers"]{.class_3529}), not to get drunk (["sober"]{.class_3529}), but to love their husbands and children (vs. 4), to be ["keepers at home"]{.class_3529} (vs. 5), to use wisdom (["be discreet"]{.class_3529}), not mess with TV or the movies or strange men (["chaste"]{.class_3529}), and to obey their husbands (vs. 5).

"[To love their husbands, to love their children"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4), which one would think would be unnecessary to say, and yet in the ["last days,"]{.class_3529} it is absolutely necessary to say, for ["the love of many shall wax cold"]{.class_3529} (Matt. 24:12). That is why this generation in America [talks ]{.class_3530}so much about LOVE: they don't love anyone but themselves (see 2 Tim. 3:1--2). Vincent has to go to a tombstone in Pergamum to get the meaning of the words (p. 341), but then again, when trying to explain ["BEHAVIOUR"]{.class_3529} (vs. 3), he had to quote an apocryphal book ([Maccabees]{.class_3530}), from an LXX written two hundred years after the Resurrection, that dealt with "the maddened state into which the WAR ELEPHANTS were excited" (p. 340). Such are the ways of bookworms whose isolated mentalities make them unfit to give advice to anyone.

"[Husbands"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4) will "wear you out" at times, whether you are saved or not, and there is many an American woman with a house full of children (three to seven) that is so distracted by the end of the day that she is ready to pop pills or pop her children into solitary confinement. Most of them, in such cases, pop pills.

"[Keepers at home"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5). Vincent tells us the Holy Spirit is "WRONG" (p. 342), and it should be "WORKERS AT HOME." We charitably (remember that "love" bit, just mentioned?) correct Vincent and say "WRONG"; it meant ["KEEPERS."]{.class_3529} A "housekeeper" keeps a house. That is a profound truth found in the "searchlights" of the original English that no student of "the" Greek has access to (see Appendix XII).

[A ]{.class_3527}["keeper of the prison"]{.class_3528}[ (Acts 16:27) does not spend all of his time in the prison; a ]{.class_3527}["keeper of the vineyards"]{.class_3528}[ (Song 1:6) does not spend all of his time in the vineyards; and the ]{.class_3527}["keeper of the wardrobe" ]{.class_3528}[(2 Chron. 34:22) certainly didn't sleep in the closet with the vestments. Vincent realizes this, so he hastens to tell us in the footnote that "the meaning is not STAYERS at home," but then, again, ]{.class_3527}[the AV text never said it was.]{.class_3526}[ Vincent's rearrangement of the word destroyed the meaning he knew about and gave you a reading that he didn't mean to give. For if it was "WORKERS at home," no woman could hold a job ]{.class_3527}[away from home]{.class_3526}[ even if she was a good "housekeeper."]{.class_3527}

[Such are the ways of sin, death, and madness, that guided the Lockman Foundation (]{.class_3527}[Amplified ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[NASV]{.class_3526}[) and the Radio Bible Class (]{.class_3527}[NKJV]{.class_3526}[) and the Old Fashioned Gospel Hour (]{.class_3527}[NKJV]{.class_3526}[) and the faculty and staff of Moody, Fuller, and Wheaton (]{.class_3527}[NIV]{.class_3526}[). The ]{.class_3527}[NKJV ]{.class_3526}[says "homemakers." The ]{.class_3527}[NIV ]{.class_3526}[says "to be busy at home," confining the woman to ]{.class_3527}[one location,]{.class_3526}[ when the reference was not to location but to a ]{.class_3527}[charge]{.class_3526}[, the ]{.class_3527}[RSV ]{.class_3526}[says "domestic," which means nothing, and the ]{.class_3527}[Dead Dodo]{.class_3526}[ (Kenneth Taylor) comes right out with it and says "SPENDING THEIR TIME IN THEIR OWN HOME" (location), which was exactly what the text did ]{.class_3527}[not ]{.class_3526}[mean. (You understand that all of this is done with the most pious talk about "readable translations" and "qualified authorities" and "recognized scholars" who "no longer veil the true meaning in the archaic language," etc. Oh, you bet your booties, baby!)]{.class_3527}

[1. A woman can ]{.class_3527}[make ]{.class_3526}[a home without ]{.class_3527}[keeping ]{.class_3526}[it.]{.class_3527}

[2. A woman can spend ]{.class_3527}[time ]{.class_3526}[in a "home" without ]{.class_3527}[keeping ]{.class_3526}[it.]{.class_3527}

[3. A woman can be a ]{.class_3527}[domestic ]{.class_3526}[without "]{.class_3527}[keeping ]{.class_3526}[a home."]{.class_3527}

[4. A woman can be "busy at home" and have it a ]{.class_3527}[wreck]{.class_3526}[.]{.class_3527}

[The ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[reading stands inerrant and infallible against ALL "better translations" from all "older manuscripts," and it preserves the truth of the matter in spite of the faculty members of forty Christian schools. A woman who KEEPS a good house tends to the overall matters that deal with housekeeping, and they ARE ALL LISTED IN THE INFALLIBLE, INERRANT, ENGLISH TEXT OF PROVERBS 31, WHICH NOT ONE "RECOGNIZED SCHOLAR" COULD FIND.]{.class_3527}

Buying, selling, travelling, and giving to the poor are just as much a part of "housekeeping" as cooking and sewing and are far more important than sitting all day in the home watching TV soap operas and talking over the phone.

The "reliable" versions and their translators simply don't know about what they are talking; but then again, they never did.

[The real question is how much time a woman will have to spend in a HOUSE in order to maintain a "home." (The translators confused "home" with "house," a common mistake in the twentieth century. A HOUSEHOLD is a "home" whether it has a "house" to live in or not.) What is required here is that the woman maintain a good household by caring for her husband and children and managing the affairs of the HOUSE in an acceptable manner so that it is "KEPT" in good shape. Whatever time ]{.class_3527}[this ]{.class_3526}[takes should be taken. This will certainly vary from house to house and from household to household. No woman was told to spend all of her time ]{.class_3527}[in ]{.class_3526}[a house, and no woman was told to stay all day ]{.class_3527}[in ]{.class_3526}[a house. To tell the truth, today, that would be a calamity of unprecedented proportions. In case of confinement for sickness or nursing small children, certainly most of the time would be "indoors," but where the woman has enough health to move around, she certainly should not be "KEPT IN THE HOUSE," which is what all of the modern translators have tried to do. If they ]{.class_3527}[succeed, ]{.class_3526}[you will find their wives "climbing the walls" when they get home. If you live in an igloo at the North Pole, you might take the new versions at their word, at least nine months in a year.]{.class_3527}

"[Obedient to their own husbands that the word of God be not blasphemed"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5). The expression is of great significance, for it is mentioned two other times in the Bible; in one case the reference was to the Jews who preached what they did not practice (Rom. 2:24), and in the other it was to servants who disobeyed their masters (1 Tim. 6:1).

[Now, anyone who knows Twinkle Toes, Charlie Brown, Georgie Porgy, and "Little Goody Two Shoes" (see Appendix XX) knows what the "more up-to-date versions in the language of modern man" are going to do to the verse. ]{.class_3527}[They are going to get rid of it.]{.class_3526}[ It is absolutely essential to destroy the ]{.class_3527}[cross-reference]{.class_3526}[ to Romans 2:24, so they will go about their negative, critical, destructive, Satanic work immediately.]{.class_3527}

[1. The ]{.class_3527}[NIV ]{.class_3526}[says "so no one will ]{.class_3527}[malign ]{.class_3526}[the word of God" (and then refuses to translate it as "malign" in Rom. 2:24).]{.class_3527}

[2. The ]{.class_3527}[RSV ]{.class_3526}[says "that the word of God be not ]{.class_3527}[discredited]{.class_3526}[" (which is as watered down a piece of trash as ever a Brownie handed to a Campfire Girl).]{.class_3527}

[3. The ]{.class_3527}[Dead Dodo]{.class_3526}[ (]{.class_3527}[TLB]{.class_3526}[) says "so the Christian FAITH cannot be ]{.class_3527}[spoken against]{.class_3526}[" (which is a watered down bowl of thin broth that would gag a maggot), completely destroying the cross-reference to Romans 2:24.]{.class_3527}

[4. The ]{.class_3527}[NASV ]{.class_3526}[says "that the word of God be not DISHONORED" (which is simply more of the same old contaminated, leavened, thinned down, decimated, diluted gruel served out as "the word of God").]{.class_3527}

[Cancel all four versions on the spot.]{.class_3526}[ Ignore any man who recommends even one of them.]{.class_3527}

"[THAT THE WORD OF GOD BE NOT BLASPHEMED"]{.class_3528} is too strong a diet for twentieth-century revisers and translators. It would hurt their ''image" and "sales" to speak the truth.

[So they ]{.class_3527}[lie ]{.class_3526}[(see Appendices IX and X).]{.class_3527}

[The WORD OF GOD IS ]{.class_3527}["BLASPHEMED"]{.class_3528}[ WHEN:]{.class_3527}

[1. A man ]{.class_3527}[preaches ]{.class_3526}[what he does not ]{.class_3527}[practice ]{.class_3526}[or teaches what he does not practice (Rom. 2:24).]{.class_3527}

2. A servant refuses to obey his master with the right spirit (1 Tim. 6:1--4).

3. A Christian woman argues and fights with her husband (Titus 2:5).

[Observe,]{.class_3527}[ in all three cases]{.class_3526}[, the application is to someone in a subservient position (1 Cor. 7:21--22; Gen. 3:16), who was told to OBEY. In the Jew's case (Rom. 2:24), it was ]{.class_3527}["the oracles of God"]{.class_3528}[ (Rom. 3:1--4) he was to obey. The defection of the ]{.class_3527}[ASV, NASV, NIV, RSV, NRSV, NET, NWT, TLB, TEV,]{.class_3526}[ and ALL of the others from the Biblical truth is a telltale mark of Laodicean rebellion: "the people's rights." The twentieth century translators themselves have caused "THE WORD OF GOD" (not the "Christian faith") to be BLASPHEMED. There has never been one question about it since 1880 (see ]{.class_3527}[The "Errors" in the King James Bible]{.class_3526}[, chapters 9 and 11, 1999). As "servants of God" they were disobedient, as preachers and teachers they were disobedient, and as members of Christ's body (His wife: Eph. 5) they were disobedient. THAT IS WHY ALL OF THEM ALTERED THE TEXT IN SOME WAY. Their ]{.class_3527}[motives ]{.class_3526}[for translating were ADAMIC and SATANIC, and their profession of trying to bring out "the true meaning of the original" was about as honest a profession as Vito Genovese or Al Capone professing to be assisting a District Attorney.]{.class_3527}

[Vincent is so shocked at his own blasphemy that he simply refuses to comment on the statement. After commenting on ]{.class_3527}["keepers at home," "good," "obedient," ]{.class_3528}[and ]{.class_3527}["in all things,"]{.class_3528}[ he pretends that ]{.class_3527}["THAT THE WORD OF GOD BE NOT BLASPHEMED"]{.class_3528}[ was not in ANY Greek text. ]{.class_3527}[The Expositor's Greek Testament ]{.class_3526}[(which had access to all of the Greek scholarship in the world from Thales and Socrates to Rendall and Nestle) says, "If the OBSERVED EFFECT OF THE GOSPEL were to make women worse wives, it would not COMMEND IT TO THE HEATHEN" (p. 12).]{.class_3527}

Sanctified rubbish. Pious poppycock.

[It said ]{.class_3527}["THE WORD OF GOD"]{.class_3528}[ would be ]{.class_3527}["BLASPHEMED,"]{.class_3528}[ exactly as it would be in 1 Timothy 6:1 and Romans 2:24. ("Commend itself to the heathen"! The very idea!)]{.class_3527}

[People will curse God and curse the words that He wrote that are found in a Book where the three samples of disobedience occur. They have, they do, and they will (see Rev. 13:6). Verse 6 is self-explanatory; this is the "sober-mindedness" that was substituted for ]{.class_3527}["sober"]{.class_3528}[ in verse 2. ]{.class_3527}["Soberminded"]{.class_3528}[ simply means that young men are to think seriously about the real issues of life (Prov. 4:23) and not be taken up with the news media fads of their age and time. "Fads" come and go, and whether they are "goldfish swallowing," "telephone booth stuffing," "panty raids," "hoola hoops," "skateboards," "dance marathons," "roller derbies," "Mah-Jongg," "bingo," "the Twist," "the Big Apple," "the Lambeth Walk," "the Jitterbug," "flagpole sitting," "barnstorming," "rock concerts," "software," "videogames," "signs on T-shirts," or "mini-skirts," it is the same old world system that "passeth away."]{.class_3527}

A sober-minded young man will have his mind on God, Heaven, Hell, the Bible, Jesus Christ, the will of God for his life, controlling the flesh, learning the Bible, fleeing youthful lusts, finding a Christian wife, providing for his family, winning souls to Christ, and keeping in good shape to serve God.

2:7 In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness, gravity, sincerity,

8 Sound speech, that cannot be condemned; that he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.

9 Exhort servants to be obedient unto their own masters, and to please them well in all things; not answering again;

10 Not purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.

Having told Titus how his church members should conduct themselves, Paul turns on the pastor himself and admonishes him not only to teach these things but practice them himself as a pattern or sample for the others to follow.

"[In all things shewing THYSELF A PATTERN of good works"]{.class_3528} (vs. 7). This pattern consists of the proper teaching of doctrine (["uncorruptness"]{.class_3529}) in contrast to the corrupters of 2 Corinthians 2:17 (who have been removed by the [ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NIV,]{.class_3530} and [NKJV ]{.class_3530}revisers), ["gravity,"]{.class_3529} as given under verse 7, and ["sincerity"]{.class_3529}; that is, he is to believe in what he is saying and willing to back it up with conduct and even lay down his life for it, if necessary. His preaching on soulwinning is not to be that of a fake motivator; if he is spending more money on classrooms and dormitories than on foreign missions, let him keep his mouth shut about "soulwinning." If he is bragging about his "bold, militant stand" for the faith, while using a Roman Catholic bible ([ASV, NASV, RSV, NRSV, NKJV, NIV]{.class_3530}) and laughing at the Bible of the Protestant Reformation (see Appendix XXI), let him shut his mouth and have someone else preach.[ "SINCERITY"]{.class_3529} means to say what you mean and mean what you say and not kid anyone about your real beliefs (2 Cor. 4:2).

"[Sound speech, that cannot be condemned"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8), even though someone will certainly condemn it (see what they thought of Paul's speech in 2 Cor. 10:10, 11:6). ["Sound speech"]{.class_3529} is speech in line with the Bible, where a man speaks ["as the oracles of God" ]{.class_3529}(1 Pet. 4:11). The reference is not to the smooth, slick, psychological "pitches" of people like Swindoll, Custer, Ed Dobson, MacArthur, and the faculty members of Pacific Coast Bible College.

"[That he that is of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you"]{.class_3528} (vs. 8), although they certainly will say evil of you (see John 16:1--3; Matt. 12:24; Luke 23:2; etc.), if they are ["of the contrary part."]{.class_3529} Paul means they won't be able to JUSTLY say evil of you, exactly as Simon Peter presents the matter in 1 Peter 3:14--16 and 1 Peter 4:13--16. ["Sound speech,"]{.class_3529} in the mind of the modern American apostate Christian, means ["good words and fair speeches"]{.class_3529} (Rom. 16:18) that flatter egos, soothe, encourage apostates, caress backsliders, and rationalize sins. He is conditioned by the news media. To the average, apostate Christian, ["sound speech"]{.class_3529} would be epitomized in the speeches of John Paul II, Ted Kennedy, Martin Luther King Jr., or Barbara Walters. The Bible has another word for this kind of speech (Prov. 23:7, 26:23--25, 21:6, 20:19). Observe that the author who wrote these words was "contradicted" and ["blasphemed"]{.class_3529} by those ["of the contrary part"]{.class_3529} in Acts 13:45. Where ENVY is involved (see Acts 13:45), no speech will be accepted as ["sound speech." ]{.class_3529}Pilate knew that the Jews had delivered Jesus Christ through ["ENVY"]{.class_3529} (Mark 15:10).

[Now, if the reader wants to see these matters demonstrated in 1979, let him read Resolution 12 (]{.class_3527}[The Biblical Manuscripts Controversy]{.class_3526}[) passed by the Sixth Annual Congress on Fundamentalism at an Alexandrian offshoot of Bob Jones University (]{.class_3527}[Tabernacle Baptist Church,]{.class_3526}[ Virginia Beach, Va., Oct.--Nov. 1979). [Whereas discussion and debate concerning the Biblical manuscripts has begun to attract national attention and in the process there has developed considerable confusion and misunderstanding as to the ACTUAL ISSUES in the controversy \[he sidesteps ]{.class_3527}[THE ]{.class_3526}[ISSUE immediately: absolute authority\]. Paul did!\] who resort to VIOLENT and unfounded accusations, ]{.class_3527}[vicious name calling,]{.class_3526}[ and destructive campaigns in order to propagate their position \[]{.class_3527}[What ]{.class_3526}[position? About what?

"[Sound speech"]{.class_3528} tells the truth, lines itself up with the Scriptures, avoids false reporting and false accusations, gives light on any subject it is discussing (Eph. 4:29), [edifies ]{.class_3530}the saint, [comforts ]{.class_3530}the afflicted, [afflicts ]{.class_3530}the comfortable, [exposes ]{.class_3530}sin (Eph. 5:13), and is ABSOLUTELY INTOLERABLE TO A TWO-FACED, VACILLATING CHRISTIAN CELEBRITY who has ["men's persons in admiration because of advantage"]{.class_3529} (Jude 16).

[Verse 9 has been commented on under 1 Timothy 6:1--5, which see. ]{.class_3527}["Purloining"]{.class_3528}[ is the old English for ]{.class_3527}[stealing]{.class_3526}[. In this case it is "requisitioning" (an army term) what you need and getting a "knock off" or "sidekick" on the "goods." It is a common practice on all government installations and is manifest by pots, pans, tools, towels, knives, forks, spoons, plates, and blankets with "U.S. Navy" or "U.S. Army" marked on them, showing up in homes. ("Some terrible thief broke into our house and stole not only our TV set and jewelry, but all our Holiday Inn towels." "Don't ever steal another apple, Tommy; they might be poisoned!")]{.class_3527}

[When I ran a popcorn machine in Gage Park (Topeka, Kansas) at the ripe old age of sixteen, I learned to pay a colored boy to follow people around and pick up their discarded popcorn bags after the people were through eating. We paid the boy one cent for each bag. Each bag could be "re-sacked" with popcorn and sold again for ]{.class_3527}[ten ]{.class_3526}[cents. Since Jim Torsney (the concessions man for the park) only gave you fifty bags at a time, until they were sold, you could sell sixty to seventy bags with a ]{.class_3527}[resale ]{.class_3526}[and pocket the difference (\$1.00 to \$2.00, which in those days---1936 and 1937---was pretty big money). That is]{.class_3527}[ "PURLOINING."]{.class_3528}

"[That they may adorn the DOCTRINE of God our Saviour"]{.class_3528} (vs. 10). The idea is that if a servant is faithful (as a faithful wife is to her husband: see vss. 4--5) that the doctrines that deal with the Gospel and the revelation of God in Christ are "adorned"---they are "dressed up" in a passable fashion, so they are not blasphemed. First Peter 3:3 applies this type of thing to the woman who obeys her husband, thus reminding us again of the threefold tie between Romans 2:24; Titus 2:5; and 1 Timothy 6:1, which was [destroyed ]{.class_3530}by "GOOD, GODLY FUNDAMENTALISTS WHO HOLD THE OTHER POSITION" (see Rodney Bell, above). The Biblical doctrines that deal with the Holiness of God and the Righteousness of His Son LOOK better if they show up connected with the life of an honest, faithful servant who obeys his master (see especially 1 Pet. 2:18).

2:11 For the grace of God that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,

12 Teaching us that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we should live soberly, righteously, and godly, in this present world;

13 Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;

14 Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity, and purify unto himself a peculiar people, zealous of good works.

15 These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise thee.

[Now the bottom will drop out. Some Fundamentalist who is not waiting for Christ wants you to wait for his "GLORY" instead of ]{.class_3527}[Him ]{.class_3526}[(see any "reliable translation" such as the grossly corrupt ]{.class_3527}[ASV, NIV, NASV, RSV,]{.class_3526}[ or ]{.class_3527}[NRSV]{.class_3526}[). In addition to this, they want to make you think that the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[has been guilty of attacking the Deity of Christ, so that their own foul revisions can "qualify" as Bibles, too. Verse 13 was used by every Alexandrian Cult member in the Cult from 1950 to 2000 to prove that the ]{.class_3527}[ASV, NASV,]{.class_3526}[ and ]{.class_3527}[NIV ]{.class_3526}[were more "Christ-honoring" than the ]{.class_3527}[King James]{.class_3526}[ text. "[For the grace of God that bringeth salvation"]{.class_3528} (vs. 11). The reader will observe that, contrary to five point TULIP Calvinism (see Appendix VII), this salvation is brought ["TO ALL MEN."]{.class_3529} It [appeared ]{.class_3530}for the purpose of bringing salvation to [all men,]{.class_3530} as in 1 Timothy 2:4, 6. The ELECT were not the sole object of this "appearance." Here, the ["GRACE of God"]{.class_3529} is personified, for "the appearance" is contrasted immediately with another [future appearance ]{.class_3530}in verse 13. ["The GRACE of God" ]{.class_3529}in this passage is a reference to the Lord Jesus Christ. Notice that 2 Timothy 1:10 with 2 Timothy 4:8 is the same kind of a thing. The "appearing" was to ["put away sin,"]{.class_3529} as in Hebrews 9:26. Vincent muffs it as usual---[since he is counting on his Greek studies through forty-five years in two hundred volumes to help him]{.class_3530}---and says that the grace here is just a neuter reference to the ultimate "grounds" of salvation. Wrong again.

"[Teaching us" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 12). Now the "elect" show up, since they are already IN Christ, having been elected when they received Him as Saviour. ["The Grace of God"]{.class_3529} might not teach an unsaved man anything, but once he is saved, then the Grace of God teaches him all kinds of things IN ADDITION to the things dealing with salvation.

1. He is to deny ungodliness.

2. He is to deny worldly lusts.

[3. He ]{.class_3527}["should live soberly."]{.class_3528}

4. He should live righteously.

5. He should live godly.

6. He should look for Christ to show up.

[No unsaved man can learn any of these "teachings" by the Grace of God in his ]{.class_3527}[natural condition]{.class_3526}[. They are characteristic of what the Grace of God teaches a ]{.class_3527}[converted sinner]{.class_3526}[ who has done something about the Grace of God: he has accepted it (John 1:12, 3:36, 5:24; Rom. 10:9--10).]{.class_3527}

"[Ungodliness"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). The word contains the idea of [acting without reference to God]{.class_3530}. It is living as though God were not to be respected or reverenced (cf. Heb. 12:28 and comments). It is the absence of the ["fear of the Lord."]{.class_3529} Ungodly men are mentioned many times in the Bible (Psa. 1:1, 4, 5, 6; 3:7; 73:12; 2 Sam. 22:5; Prov. 16:27; 19:28; etc.). An "ungodly man" is wicked, perverse, irreverent, and without value (["sons of Belial"]{.class_3529}). His outstanding characteristic is the lack of "God-reference" in his thoughts, conversation, and conduct. [He is a practical atheist.]{.class_3530}

"[Godly"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12), by contrast, is a man who has God in his thoughts (cf. Psa. 10:4) and his actions (James 1:22). If a man is ["godly,"]{.class_3529} he consults God before he does anything and tries to look at any person, problem, book, idea, action, decision, institution, religion, or sin as GOD looks at it. This involves a study of God's word that amounts to a daily poring over (and over) the Scriptures. No man can know anything about the mind of God without knowing [what God SAID]{.class_3530} about what is in His mind (Jer. 32:35; Ezek. 23:17--18; Rom. 8:27).

[It is of the utmost importance for the Bible believer to grasp this simple concept, for it does away with about 90 percent of the people that modern Bible correctors call "godly." That word is the Christian cliché (1900--2000) for apostasy in Laodicea, and whenever an American apostate like Curtis Hutson or Robert Sumner wishes to justify his sins, he will always appeal to some "godly" man who did the same thing. The term "godly" is used as much by American Fundamentalists---when ]{.class_3527}[referring to Bible critics and correctors]{.class_3526}[---as the word "sharing" is used by Catholics, Communists, and Charismatics.]{.class_3527}

[In modern usage (see any issue of ]{.class_3527}[Faith for the Family, The Fundamentalist Journal, Pulpit Helps,]{.class_3526}[ or ]{.class_3527}[Christianity Today]{.class_3526}[), the term means a man who doesn't drink or smoke and doesn't support Neo-Evangelicalism or Neo-Orthodoxy; usually he has been married once (that is, he had one MARRIAGE LICENSE) and was connected with a soulwinning ministry to get the "leftovers." "[This present world"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12) is the world that any reader of Titus 2:12 is looking at. It was never limited to the "world" in which Titus lived. ["This present world" ]{.class_3529}(vs. 12) is the[ ]{.class_3530}["present evil world"]{.class_3529}[ ]{.class_3530}of Galatians 1:4. It is the world that Demas went after---["having loved this present world"]{.class_3529} (2 Tim. 4:10). Jamieson, Fausset, and Brown (p. 1388) get a heart attack at the expression and drop it out of the text; they make no comment. Vincent (p. 344) has a massive coronary at the expression and drops it from his [Word Studies,]{.class_3530} and the[ New Bible Commentary]{.class_3530} (p. 1182) checks into the coronary unit with him. The [Wycliffe ]{.class_3530}commentator (p. 1395) recognizes something has to be done with the expression, so he says coyly, "These words show the basic ORIENTATION of Paul's thought---life consists of THIS WORLD as well as the world to come." [Which doesn't say anything.]{.class_3530}

But Liberty University does the best in blaspheming the truth. Sumner Wemp ducks all Biblical exposition and says: "The gospel is not pie in the sky, but it is for the here and now" (p. 657).

[But ]{.class_3527}["THIS PRESENT WORLD"]{.class_3528}[---]{.class_3527}[when all of these men read the expression]{.class_3526}[---was a reference to the world system in which they (and the revisers and commentators) found themselves. For Sumner Wemp (]{.class_3527}[Liberty University]{.class_3526}[), it was the NEWS MEDIA WORLD OF TV. The writers for the ]{.class_3527}[Wycliffe Commentary ]{.class_3526}[and the ]{.class_3527}[New Bible Commentary]{.class_3526}[ (as well as Williams, Guthrie, and others) all had a ]{.class_3527}["present world"]{.class_3528}[ with which to deal, and it contrasted with what the Grace of God "taught" (vs. 11). The commentators all underplayed the expression or left it out altogether.]{.class_3527}

[There is a very easy way to identify the "godly" Christian whom the Holy Spirit has taught ]{.class_3527}[nothing; ]{.class_3526}[he is occupied with ]{.class_3527}["this present world."]{.class_3528}[ NOW (1970--2000) that means TV and COMPUTERS. The ]{.class_3527}[language ]{.class_3526}[is unmistakable: "lifestyles," "social justice," "oppressed minorities," "sharing," "structures," "areas," "share," "meaningful relations," "discs," "exciting studies," "reaching out," "outreach," "indepth," "total thrust," "modules," "programmed," ''dynamic," "tensions," "touching," "liberation," "discrimination," "prejudice," "committal," "fulfilling experience," "fulfillment," "values," "coping," "meeting needs," "full-scale," "valuable insights," and "spiritual maturity." [These news media orgies originate in ]{.class_3527}["this present evil world,"]{.class_3528}[ and they are designed to captivate the minds and imaginations of Christians who have lost the absolute authority of the Holy Bible and have been set adrift in a sea of relative "opinions" and "preferences" or "authoritative sources." "[Looking for that blessed hope, and the glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ"]{.class_3528} (vs. 13).

(My, aren't we holy! Aren't we "valiant for the truth"! Oh, what would we poor, ignorant peons do without these "bold, faithful, godly men" who stand up to honor the "first fundamental of the faith" while CREATING TWO GODS IN THE TRINITY---see John 1:18 in the ]{.class_3527}[NASV]{.class_3526}[!)]{.class_3527}

Let us, as usual, keep our wits while all about us go into fanatical and hysterical panegyrics over the "holiness" of these "good, godly, qualified scholars" who fell in love with themselves the moment they saw their reflection in a Greek mud puddle.

[1. The figure of speech used here (]{.class_3527}[AV]{.class_3526}[) is so common in the Bible that only a fool would have thought that God and Christ were two ]{.class_3527}[different ]{.class_3526}[persons. The figure is called a ]{.class_3527}["hendiady"]{.class_3526}[ and is so common in the book of Proverbs that a list of the verses would take nearly a page to list. A New Testament example: ]{.class_3527}["THE HOLY ONE AND THE JUST"]{.class_3528}[ (Acts 3:14) is certainly not TWO persons by the wildest stretch of anyone's imagination. (See Num. 24:17.)]{.class_3527}

[2. All new translators maintained the figure (]{.class_3527}[hendiady]{.class_3526}[) in Ephesians 5:5, and none of them altered the verse to make you think "Christ was God." ]{.class_3527}["PURE RELIGION\...BEFORE GOD AND THE FATHER"]{.class_3528}[ (James 1:27) is certainly not a reference to some]{.class_3527}[ "Father"]{.class_3528}[ who is not God. In their effort to overthrow the Biblical figure of speech (which is constantly used in Hebrew ]{.class_3527}[and ]{.class_3526}[Greek), the modern translators have inserted "our" into James 1:27, which occurs in NO GREEK TEXT KNOWN TO GOD OR MAN.]{.class_3527}

[3. The figure (]{.class_3527}[hendiady]{.class_3526}[) means simply "one by means of two." The first word (]{.class_3527}["God"]{.class_3528}[) expresses the thing, and then the second one (in this case, ]{.class_3527}["our Saviour Jesus Christ"]{.class_3528}[) intensifies it by being changed into something else. The figure is ORIENTAL (Hebrew) and is used frequently in both Testaments since Paul is a Hebrew. Note: ]{.class_3527}["How goodly are THY TENTS, O JACOB, AND THY TABERNACLES, O ISRAEL"]{.class_3528}[ (Num. 24:5).]{.class_3527}

[4. The classic New Testament case is Matthew 21:5, citing Zechariah 9:9, where Christ is pictured as riding TWO animals instead of one. The ]{.class_3527}[NASV ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[NIV ]{.class_3526}[simply erased the conjunction (]{.class_3527}["and"]{.class_3528}[) from both the Hebrew and the Greek manuscripts while talking about "older and better manuscripts." "[Who gave himself for us, that he might redeem us from all iniquity" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 14). ["All iniquity"]{.class_3529} is a guarantee that eventually ["the purchased possession"]{.class_3529} (Eph. 1:14) that was "knocked down on the block" (1 Cor. 6:20) will be able to say ["Bless the Lord, O my soul\...who forgiveth ALL thine iniquities"]{.class_3529} (Psa. 103:2--3)---not only in the judicial sense, but in the SINLESS sense (see Rom. 8:28; 1 John 3:1--4; and Phil. 3:21).
