CHAPTER THREE

3:1 Put them in mind to be subject to principalities and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,

2 To speak evil of no man, to be no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all meekness unto all men.

3 For we ourselves also were sometimes foolish, disobedient, deceived, serving divers lusts and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.

4 But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,

5 Not by works of righteousness which we have done, but according to his mercy he saved us, by the washing of regeneration, and renewing of the Holy Ghost;

6 Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;

7 That being justified by his grace, we should be made heirs according to the hope of eternal life.

[Verse 1 is self-explanatory and is amplified in Romans 13:1--6 and 1 Peter 2:17--19. The ]{.class_3527}[principalities ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[powers ]{.class_3526}[here are not the ]{.class_3527}[principalities ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[powers ]{.class_3526}[of Ephesians 6:10--12, which is evident by the command to obey them. The principalities and powers in the ]{.class_3527}["high places"]{.class_3528}[ (Eph. 6:12) are to be fought against with prayer and resisted (Eph. 6:13--17). In the present anti-tax movement (the Tax Rebellion), it would mean that the Christian would pay taxes for conscience sake, and because he would get thrown in jail if he didn't (Rom. 13:1--7). We pay taxes for the same reason that a man threatened with a loaded gun hands over his billfold.]{.class_3527}

"[To speak evil of no man"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2), as John spoke of Diotrephes, and Paul spoke of Demas, and Christ spoke of the Pharisees (Matt. 23:15, 23), and Jeremiah spoke of the scribes (Jer. 20, 23), and Elijah spoke of Baal (1 Kings 18).

[Now, our purpose in listing these "discrepancies" is to reinforce (for the fiftieth time) the vast "credibility gap" that exists between the thinking of the modern American Christian and the mind of the Holy Spirit. "Speaking evil of a man," in the minds of Georgie Porgie, Tinkerbell, and Little Goody Two-Shoes (see comments under vs. 2:8) means calling a spade a "spade," identifying a liar, exposing a con-man engaged in deceiving the Body of Christ, telling the truth about the popes, telling the truth about the Catholic canon law, and telling the truth about Charismatics who are no more "gifted" than Dutch Schultz (syndicate gangster, 1930). In the Laodicean vocabulary, TRUTH is evil-speaking. All Laodicean apostates cancel out charity ]{.class_3527}["REJOICETH IN THE TRUTH"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Cor. 13:6) when dealing with these matters. They do not rejoice in THE truth about anything they "like" or "prefer."]{.class_3527}

"[Evil speaking" here is a reference to lying or slandering; for example, saying that "Ruckman believes the ]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3530}is the plenary, verbally inspired Word of God, and a word-for-word exact translation of the originals." That is a sample of what is meant by "EVIL SPEAKING."

[(We received a nasty letter from Bob Jones III in October of 1985 accusing us of broadcasting around the country that his grandfather, Bob Jones Sr., had been ]{.class_3527}[divorced ]{.class_3526}[and remarried. We have never even INTIMATED such a thing. Jones III got that one from one of his little Alexandrian punk friends who read our pamphlet on ]{.class_3527}[Marriage, Divorce, and Remarriage]{.class_3526}[ and purposely lied to Jones III about the material on page 21. He took for granted, as all Catholic historians do, that Jones III had never read the pamphlet because it was by "Ruckman." ]{.class_3527}[He guessed right.]{.class_3526}[ Jones III was in the dark. So he took the lying, little fool's word for it---but this is not "evil speaking"---and broadcast that "Ruckman" had said it. Evil speakers ]{.class_3527}[multiply;]{.class_3526}[ they produce their own kind.)]{.class_3527}

"[To be no brawlers, but gentle"]{.class_3528} (vs. 2). We have commented on this under 1 Timothy 3:2--6 and 2 Timothy 2:25. The references are to personal conduct when dealing with people face-to-face on controversial issues or personal grievances. ["Shewing all meekness unto all men"]{.class_3529} (vs. 2). This meekness is called ["the meekness\...of Christ"]{.class_3529} in 2 Corinthians 10:1. To show how "meek" Christ was, one should study his preaching SPEECH in Matthew 23:1--30; John 8:40--45; John 8:7; John 9:41; Matthew 23:33; and Luke 16:15. Again, we point out the "discrepancies" to drive home to the Bible believer the tremendous difference between the Holy Spirit's ["meekness"]{.class_3529} and the meekness of modern Catholic-Conservatives who blab: ["blessed are the peacemakers."]{.class_3529}

[No pope has "made peace" between any two countries since the term "pope" was invented. When ]{.class_3527}[Our Sunday Visitor]{.class_3526}[ (Nov. 3, 1985) tells us that the Mystery of the Catholic Church (p. 7) is how it can survive through two thousand years as "the lifeline to Christ," it is just too funny for words. There never has been any ]{.class_3527}[mystery ]{.class_3526}[as to why the Catholic Church "has managed to survive so long\...crises have been overcome and will continue to be overcome\...the church lives on because the PROMISE lives on" (Margaret Findlay, Ontario, Cal.).]{.class_3527}

Rubbish. Pure, fictional rubbish.

[The Catholic Church "lives on" because, as a monstrous, political, two-headed chameleon, it adapts itself to every political situation where it can survive as a CHURCH while playing politics between two political groups or two nations. There never has been any ]{.class_3527}[mystery ]{.class_3526}[or anything ]{.class_3527}[mysterious ]{.class_3526}[about Papal politics. Any pope will do ANYTHING to maintain the hierarchy that he heads. He will sacrifice his own people (see ]{.class_3527}[The History of the New Testament Church]{.class_3526}[, Vol. II, pp. 294--295); he will kill members of other churches (ibid, pp. 299--301); he will order armies to kill people whose land he wants (]{.class_3527}[The History of the New Testament Church]{.class_3526}[, Vol. I, pp. 366--367); his cardinals will even kill a pope that might "get in the way" (see ]{.class_3527}[Murder in the Vatican,]{.class_3526}[ Avro Manhattan, 1985, pp. 152--174); he will sign concordats with Fascists and Nazis, support terrorists, oppose his own church members one time (John Paul II against Mexican "Liberation Theology") and support them another time (Paul VI supporting "Liberation Theology" in Cuba and South America). In short, any pope will do anything to preserve his own hierarchy, so gullible suckers (like the writers for ]{.class_3527}[Our Sunday Visitor]{.class_3526}[ will think "THE PROMISE OF CHRIST" (Matt. 16:18) is "working."]{.class_3527}

[May we remind these ignorant sinners that CRIME, SIN, TORTURE, LYING, SWEARING, STEALING, DEATH, and GARBAGE were here long before any pope showed up, and they have survived six millennia nicely. Why? Because of God's "promise"? No. No, the reason why the Catholic Church survives emperors, kings, governments, and religions is that ]{.class_3527}[as a religious whore, she will sell herself to anyone for "favors," if they are willing to pay the price.]{.class_3526}[ We all know what the "oldest profession" in the world is. It is not called "Roman Catholicism"; it is called "prostitution." A ]{.class_3527}[whore ]{.class_3526}[can survive a gang war or several gang wars (see the history of the Black Hand, the Mafia, the Syndicate, La Cosa Nostra, etc., between 1900 and 1980). So can a ]{.class_3527}[double-agent ]{.class_3526}[(Sorge, for example) survive a World War. You simply use both sides against each other and stay "neutral." That is "Christ's promise," according to the deluded writer for ]{.class_3527}[Our Sunday Visitor.]{.class_3526}[ She knows about as much Church History as Ted Kennedy or Walter Mondale.]{.class_3527}

"[Meekness"]{.class_3528} is the attribute assumed by a WOLF before slaughtering sheep (Matt. 7:15). "She stoops to conquer."

There now follow eight attributes of the "old life" of the believer which marked him out before his conversion to Christ.

[1. ]{.class_3527}["Foolish."]{.class_3528}[ He thought learning the first ten "hit songs" on the Hit Parade or the Hill-Billy Hit Parade or the Rock Parade was important. He thought people like Springsteen, Jackson, Presley, Lennon, and Dolly Parton were "stars." He messed with heroin and cocaine, or at least marijuana and benzedrine. ]{.class_3527}[He didn't believe in "speaking against anyone's faith."]{.class_3526}[ He thought the Super Bowl and the World Series were important events. He believed what he read in the newspapers. He thought if he missed ]{.class_3527}[Jaws, The Towering Inferno, Star Wars, Rosemary's Baby, The Omen,]{.class_3526}[ or an Academy Award winner, that he had missed a major event in life.]{.class_3527}

[2. ]{.class_3527}["Disobedient."]{.class_3528}[ He threw out any one of the Ten Commandments with which he didn't agree. He did not believe on "the Son" though commanded to (1 John 3:23). He disobeyed his parents, his teachers, and the law, and probably his officers in the army when he could get away with it.]{.class_3527}

[3. ]{.class_3527}["Deceived."]{.class_3528}[ He thought the Catholic Church was PRO-JEWISH during World War II. ]{.class_3527}[He thought Einstein was a brilliant intellect.]{.class_3526}[ He thought science and philosophy would solve man's problems. He thought computers would help man out. ]{.class_3527}[He thought Mary was sinless.]{.class_3526}[ He thought this was the New Age of "Aquarius." He thought he would go to purgatory when he died. He thought a ten o'clock newscast was giving him the truth. He thought the Bible was full of myths and legends and had "contradictions" in it. He thought sprinkling babies had something to do with salvation. He thought the American people had control of their own money and banking situations. He thought World Wars I and II were fought to "set man free in the free world." He thought blacks and whites were equal and should be mixed in the bedroom. He thought segregation was "NON-CHRISTIAN" and that no Christian would think of telling a man he was going to Hell. He was ]{.class_3527}["deceived."]{.class_3528}

[4. ]{.class_3527}["Serving divers lusts and pleasures,"]{.class_3528}[ which varied from caffeine and nicotine to alcohol and barbituates. With many it was sex, sex deviation, and sex perversion. With others it was just sun bathing or golf. One hang-up was as good as another. With some, the sun rose and set on bowling, and with others it rose and set on Scrabble. Gourmets stuffed their guts with exotic dishes, drunkards filled their beer bellies with mixed drinks, and where they couldn't get "Bloody Marys" and "Zombies" and "Slow Gin Fizzes," they settled for cheap wine, hair tonic, aftershave lotion, rifle bore cleaner, and "torpedo juice."]{.class_3527}

[5. ]{.class_3527}["Living in malice."]{.class_3528}[ Living with a hateful attitude (bad attitude) towards everything and everybody. Not loving God, not loving God's Book, and not loving God's people, and many times not loving mother, father, wife, husband, or children (see 2 Tim. 3:1--2).]{.class_3527}

[6. ]{.class_3527}["Envy"]{.class_3528}[ (see comments under Prov. 27:4 in that commentary). Jealousy is "the rage of man" (see Mark 15:10; 1 John 3:12; Acts 13:45; and Isa. 14:10--15). ]{.class_3527}[All unsaved men are Marxists]{.class_3526}[ (1 Tim. 6:10) if they don't have what they want. Karl Marx was simply a typical lost sinner reduced to man's primitive and basic nature: "I want what I want when I want it, and I have a right to]{.class_3527}[ take it]{.class_3526}[ from anyone who has it if I don't have it." That is the "scientific philosophy" of Communism in a nutshell, and since you know a thing by its ]{.class_3527}[fruits,]{.class_3526}[ all you have to do is study history from 1917 to 1989 to see how Communism operates in Europe, Cuba, Asia, Africa, or anywhere else. It is just a larger form of the Mafia or Cosa Nostra than the Sicilian original. All Communists are driven by ENVY.]{.class_3527}

[7. ]{.class_3527}["Hateful,"]{.class_3528}[ as a basic tenet of nature; not merely hateful acts and thoughts, but hateful in essence. "[Love of God our Saviour toward MAN appeared"]{.class_3528} (vs. 4), not the ELECT. "[Not by works of righteousness which we have done"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5). "[But according to his mercy He saved us"]{.class_3528} (vs. 5). Mercy, then, with ["kindness and love"]{.class_3529} (vs. 4), are the elements in saving a sinner. Artemas or Tychicus will do the job, and ]{.class_3527}[Tychicus ]{.class_3526}[is a good bet, as he is mentioned in Ephesians 6:21 and 2 Timothy 4:12 as being sent by Paul from Rome. ]{.class_3527}["Nicopolis"]{.class_3528}[ can be Nicopolis of Epirus or Thrace (there is another city by the same name in Cilicia). Compare "Greenville" or "Brownsville"---South Carolina, Alabama, Ohio, Georgia, Tennessee, Maryland, Pennsylvania, Indiana, Illinois, Texas, or anywhere else.]{.class_3527}

"[For I have determined there to winter"]{.class_3528} (vs. 12). He will spend the winter there. (See Acts 27:12 for the use of the expression and in 1 Cor. 16:6.) The "archaic" way of putting it in the [King James Holy Bible]{.class_3530} ([AV]{.class_3530}) does not need to be "updated" any more than the words found in 1:9 or 2:10. After all (!), "not one single archaic word in any edition of the [AV ]{.class_3530}obscures ONE FUNDAMENTAL OF THE FAITH," and "ALL OF THE FUNDAMENTALS OF THE FAITH can be found in any edition," etc.

[See how the snow drifts, kiddies? (We thought you would like it. ]{.class_3527}[You get to try out your new sled.]{.class_3526}[)]{.class_3527}

"[Zenas the lawyer and Apollos" ]{.class_3528}(vs. 13), showing that not all lawyers are to be classified with those found in Luke 11:52, just as all doctors are not like those mentioned in Mark 5:26. All lawyers are not engaged in taking ["away the key of knowledge,"]{.class_3529} and Luke was a ["beloved physician"]{.class_3529} (Col. 4:14), not a "quack."

"[Zenas"]{.class_3528} is a "dark horse" up until here, but ["Apollos"]{.class_3529} is well-known as the Alexandrian orator of Acts 18:25 who got his seminary education straightened out by a man and his wife (Acts 18:26) and who became a powerful instrument in the hands of God in "helping the brethren" (Acts 18:28). Fortunately, God sent a couple of common, ordinary tent-makers to the Greek seminarian to clear up his understanding of the Scriptures. (This is often the case.)

Titus is told to help Zenas and Apollos out with food, funds, or whatever, that they may "want nothing."

"[And let ours also learn to maintain good works for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful."]{.class_3528} ["Ours" ]{.class_3529}will be a reference to "our people" (the saved on Crete), who are told to help out the missionary travellers as well as Titus himself. The pastor is not to bear the burden of "care and maintenance" alone. When something comes up that is[ "necessary" ]{.class_3529}(see vs. 14), the Christian congregation is to "chip in" and supply it so that they can be rewarded for "fruit-bearing." This financial help is called ["FRUIT"]{.class_3529} in Romans 15:28 and in Philippians 4:17. It is part of the FRUIT of the Holy Spirit (Gal. 5:22), for it is a [manifestation ]{.class_3530}of the first fruit of the Spirit (["love,"]{.class_3529} Gal. 5:22). The verse reinforces verse 8, which mentioned maintaining[ "good works."]{.class_3529} They are to be "kept up." The Cretian Christians are not to ["be weary in well doing"]{.class_3529} (see 2 Thess. 2:17 for another comment on the passage).

[Verse 15 is self-explanatory. ]{.class_3527}["Greet them that love us in the faith."]{.class_3528}[ The ]{.class_3527}[Dead Dodo]{.class_3526}[ has a monumental boo-boo here for all the ages to behold. It says "Please say hello to all of the Christian friends there." (Original Greek: "Hey, deah! How you?" How about, "Have them Jesus freaks give you some skin, baby"? I mean, if you are going to "update" it, why not do a "bang-up" job?)]{.class_3527}

[In spite of the fact that the article is missing from ]{.class_3527}["the faith,"]{.class_3528}[ the ]{.class_3527}[NIV ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[RSV violate their own rules]{.class_3526}[ for adding articles and insert the article, so did the ]{.class_3527}[NASV]{.class_3526}[. This is "backsliding" (see article in the ]{.class_3527}[Bible Believers' Bulletin]{.class_3526}[), for the ]{.class_3527}[ASV ]{.class_3526}[(1901) ]{.class_3527}[more than fifty years ago ]{.class_3526}[omitted the article, as did the grossly corrupt ]{.class_3527}[RV ]{.class_3526}[of 1885. But backsliding is nothing new (see Appendix XXII), for Nestle himself backslid after holding to a false position for more than eighty years (Appendix V). ]{.class_3527}["Them that love us IN THE FAITH"]{.class_3528}[ are those church members that Titus is pastoring who love him and Paul ]{.class_3527}["IN THE FAITH,"]{.class_3528}[ not "in the flesh" or "in the church" or "in some heresy." First Timothy 1:2, 4:1, 6:21; 2 Timothy 4:7; and Titus 1:1 are the cross references.]{.class_3527}

"[Grace be with you all. Amen."]{.class_3528}

Sermons from Titus:

[A. ]{.class_3527}[THE FAITH OF GOD'S ELECT:]{.class_3526}[ (Titus 1:1--3)]{.class_3527}

1. It includes the Truth of God, which is to be accepted.

2. It includes the Holiness of God, which is to be followed (vs. 1).

3. It includes the Eternal Life which is in God, which is given (vs. 2).

4. It includes the Promises of God, which are to be claimed (vs. 2).

5. It includes the Preaching of those things which were believed (vs. 3).

[B. ]{.class_3527}[THE ORDAINED ELDER:]{.class_3526}[ (Titus 1:5)]{.class_3527}

1. Must have a regulated family life (vs. 6)

2. Must have a controlled temperment (vs. 7).

3. Must have a Biblical "lifestyle" (vs. 8).

4. Must know the Book and know how to use it (vs. 9).

[C. ]{.class_3527}[PEOPLE WHOSE MOUTHS MUST BE\
  STOPPED:]{.class_3526}[ (Titus 1:11).]{.class_3527}

[1. People who correct and attack the Holy Bible (]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[1611).]{.class_3527}

[2. People who destroy the faith of young men and women in the Holy Bible (]{.class_3527}[AV ]{.class_3526}[1611).]{.class_3527}

3. People whose motive in teaching or preaching is "the love of money" (vs. 11).

4. People who teach the fables of Rome and the fables of Alexandria (Catholic popes and Protestant popes).

5. People who spend their time being experts in other peoples' business.

6. People who seek to split congregations by raising up another authority equal to the pastor or the Bible.

7. People who split churches by demanding loyalty to a SCHOOL instead of the Bible.

[D. ]{.class_3527}[THE PURE MIND:]{.class_3526}[ (Titus 1:15).]{.class_3527}

[1. Rejoices ]{.class_3527}["in the truth"]{.class_3528}[ (1 Cor. 13:6).]{.class_3527}

2. Rejoices in God's judgments (Psa. 97:8).

[3. Sets]{.class_3527}[ "no wicked thing"]{.class_3528}[ before the eyes (Psa. 101:3).]{.class_3527}

4. Flees youthful lusts (2 Tim. 2:22).

5. Refuses to believe an evil report without confirmation (Deut. 17:2--4).

6. Refuses to dwell on fleshy lusts (2 Cor. 7:1 and 1 Pet. 2:11).

7. Refuses to conspire, plot, deceive, or mislead (2 Cor. 11:1--4).

[E. ]{.class_3527}[THE DEFILED MIND:]{.class_3526}[ (Titus 1:15).]{.class_3527}

1. Cannot discern Biblical truth.

2. Cannot comprehend the things of God.

3. Cannot cease to imagine evil (Jude 8, 18 and 2 Peter 2:14).

4. Will dwell on pornography or demonology.

5. Will invent means of self-justification to justify sin.

6. Will listen to a"naughty doer" (Prov. 17:4) and pass the information on (Prov. 20:19).

[F. ]{.class_3527}[THE THINGS THAT BECOME SOUND]{.class_3526}

  [DOCTRINE:]{.class_3526} (Titus 2:1).

1\. Elderly men that behave themselves (vs. 2).

2\. Elderly women that behave themselves (vs. 3).

3\. Young women that behave themselves (vs. 4).

4\. Young men that behave themselves (vs. 6).

Behavior should be characterized by:

A. Minds occupied with God and the Bible and the things in the Bible.

B. Mouths occupied with witnessing and telling the truth.

C. Activities occupied with getting along with other Christians and maintaining Christian households.

D. Life given to witnessing for Christ and manifesting Christ to the community and neighborhood.

[G. ]{.class_3527}[GOD TEACHING MAN:]{.class_3526}[ (Titus 2:11).]{.class_3527}

1. God teaches man what real Kindness and Mercy are.

2. God teaches man what Love is capable of doing.

3. God teaches man that the present world system is temporary.

4. God teaches man that He is going to make an appearance.

5. God teaches man that he should take care how he lives.

6. God teaches man that self-denial is right and self-indulgence is wrong.

[H. ]{.class_3527}[OUR PAST, PRESENT, AND FUTURE:]{.class_3526}

  [(Titus 2:1--13).]{.class_3527}

1. Our Past (vs. 3).

2. Our Present (vss. 7--8).

3. Our Future (vs. 13).

[I. ]{.class_3527}[THE DIM, DARK PAST: ]{.class_3526}[(Titus 3:3).]{.class_3527}

1. We were foolish, so we needed wisdom.

2. We were disobedient, so we needed righteousness.

3. We were deceived, so we needed light.

4. We were sensual, so we needed spiritual strength.

5. We were hateful, so we needed LOVE.

[J. ]{.class_3527}[AVOIDING THE HERETICKS: ]{.class_3526}[(Titus 3:10).]{.class_3527}

1. They should be avoided because they will not accept an admonition.

2. They should be avoided because they will overthrow the faith of some.

3. They should be avoided because they are self-condemned already.

4. They should be avoided because their sins are contagious.

[K. ]{.class_3527}[GOOD WORKS ]{.class_3526}["FOR NECESSARY USES"]{.class_3528}[:]{.class_3526}

  [(Titus 3:14).]{.class_3527}

1. It will be necessary to support a minister and his family; this is a good work.

2. It will be necessary to help poor brothers in Christ financially at times; this is a good work.

3. It will be necessary to supply the need of missionaries on the field; this is a good work.

4. It may be necessary to give large offerings in emergencies; this is a good work.

5. It may be necessary to take time to help aged Christians or crippled Christians; this is a good work.

6. It may be necessary to provide room and board, or transportation, to some of the saints; this is a good work.

[7. ]{.class_3527}[MAINTAIN GOOD WORKS.]{.class_3526}

"[You speak of a letter from a layman that was very ]{.class_3527}["kind"]{.class_3530} and ["gracious"]{.class_3530}; I wonder just how kind and gracious you think it is to call A BROTHER IN CHRIST 'THAT NUT' in Florida?"

[Nothing complicated. Nothing difficult to answer. Any Christian could answer all three questions in five minutes by writing less than ]{.class_3527}[twenty lines ]{.class_3526}[of answers.]{.class_3527}

[But my, my! When old "Terrible Timothy" posits 1 Timothy 6:10 in front of your face (!), ]{.class_3527}["THE LOVE OF MONEY IS THE ROOT OF ALL EVIL,"]{.class_3528}[ oh boy, do these three questions ever get "complicated"!]{.class_3527}

APPENDIX 3

The King James Version Cop Out

[The book that Jerry Falwell wanted all of his students to read to be "champions for Christ" was not ]{.class_3527}[Which Bible ]{.class_3526}[by David Otis Fuller or ]{.class_3527}[Believing Bible Study]{.class_3526}[ by Edward Hills, nor was it]{.class_3527}[ The Identity of the New Testament Text]{.class_3526}[ by Pickering. It was an evasive, side-stepping, irrational work called ]{.class_3527}[The King James Version Debate,]{.class_3526}[ written by D. A. Carson. The book professed to prove fourteen points (theses) for all of the students at Liberty University so that they could abandon the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version]{.class_3526}[ as the "final authority in all matters of faith and practice" (while still ]{.class_3527}["using"]{.class_3526}[ it, of course; of course!), and taking, instead, the humanistic position of the Alexandrian Cult: i.e., the final authority is the opinions and ]{.class_3527}[preferences ]{.class_3526}[of the Cult.]{.class_3527}

[The problem to which Carson professes to address himself is first of all, which Greek text is best; and secondly, which translation is best? Naturally, he doesn't deal with ]{.class_3527}[the issue,]{.class_3526}[ which is, has been, and always will be ]{.class_3527}[Absolute and Final Authority]{.class_3526}[. There is no such discussion of this found anywhere in the entire ]{.class_3527}["Debate." ]{.class_3526}[Carson pretends that this matter was never brought up, so his conclusion is so easy to forecast, we have simply printed it in every issue of the ]{.class_3527}[Bible Believers' Bulletin]{.class_3526}[ since 1979. It is called "THE CREED OF THE ALEXANDRIAN CULT" and contains a complete summary of ALL the material in Carson's book.]{.class_3527}

[Carson's first "thesis" is that "there is no evidence that the Byzantine ]{.class_3527}[text type]{.class_3526}[ was known before the middle of the fourth century" (p. 44). This is Westcott and Hort's position, ]{.class_3527}[debunked in 1890 by Burgon and in 1960 by Pickering and in 1970 by Hills.]{.class_3526}[ Since it had nothing to do with "THE KING JAMES VERSION" debate, it was rather stupid to bring it up in the first place. Carson side-stepped (copped out) once.]{.class_3527}

[Origen cites Byzantine ]{.class_3527}[readings ]{.class_3526}[(and so do Cyprian and Irenaeus) many, many years before A.D. 300. Carson just gave his readers the shaft by adopting Griesbach's terminology: "text TYPE" rather than "text."]{.class_3527}

[Cute, isn't it? Carson, as Griesbach (more than a hundred and fifty years ago), eliminated ALL OF THE BYZANTINE ]{.class_3527}[READINGS ]{.class_3526}[in line with the ]{.class_3527}[Authorized Version]{.class_3526}[ on the grounds that they had to be a "family TYPE." Just being ]{.class_3527}[words ]{.class_3526}[in a ]{.class_3527}[verse ]{.class_3526}[wouldn't allow them to "qualify." That was Griesbach and company, and it had nothing to do with any "debate." The "family classification" of manuscripts was found to be, and proved to be, a HOAX by Pickering and Colwell, and they ]{.class_3527}[listed the evidence]{.class_3526}[ in Pickering's work. ]{.class_3527}[Carson didn't mention Pickering's work.]{.class_3526}

He copped out twice.

[Now, this is how Carson's "debate" gets started. ]{.class_3527}[It starts with two lies.]{.class_3526}[ One: "a reading cannot be Byzantine" unless it is found in a manuscript that accepts Byzantine "readings" throughout as a ]{.class_3527}["family type."]{.class_3526}[ Two: no such text showed up until the time of Chrysostom; this is the Westcott and Hort theory, ]{.class_3527}[soundly debunked by Dean Burgon nearly ninety years ago.]{.class_3526}

[The second "thesis" is that you cannot accept the "Byzantine type text just because its manuscripts comprise the vast majority of manuscripts." Since Byzantine ]{.class_3527}[type texts]{.class_3526}[ have nothing to do with the ]{.class_3527}[King James VERSION]{.class_3526}[ debate, the thesis was immaterial. ]{.class_3527}[He copped out thrice.]{.class_3526}[ Following Westcott and Hort, Carson says that the Byzantine manuscripts were preserved by "accident" and were the product of "conflation."]{.class_3527}

[We listed the eight verses Westcott and Hort used to prove "conflation" (]{.class_3527}[Christian's Handboook of Manuscript Evidence, ]{.class_3526}[1997) more than fifteen years ago and gave forty-two verses to prove they didn't know what they were talking about. If the reader would like a list of places where the ]{.class_3527}[Alexandrian ]{.class_3526}[conflated the ]{.class_3527}[Western ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[Byzantine ]{.class_3526}[readings, we will be glad to send it to him. We also found ten more places of "conflation" ]{.class_3527}[that Westcott and Hort couldn't find. ]{.class_3526}[The "conflate theory" had no more Scriptural evidence behind it than an Old Testament in Greek before A.D. 130.]{.class_3527}

Carson didn't know where the verses were that proved "conflation," so he couldn't even back up his own theory.

He copped out four times in a row.

On page 52, Carson says that the "Alexandrian type has better CREDENTIALS."

[He means "]{.class_3527}[recommendations ]{.class_3526}[from the Alexandrian Cult." Since this had nothing to do with any "debate about the ]{.class_3527}[King James Version]{.class_3526}[," there was no need of mentioning it. If you want to know what the "credentials" are for א]{.class_3527}[ ]{.class_3544}[and B (Alexandrian type texts), we will be glad to list them. Here are their ]{.class_3527}[associates ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[fruits]{.class_3526}[: Eusebius, Jerome, Origen, Pamphilus, Constantine, Clement of Alexandria, Augustine, the Donatists' killers, the Montanists' killers, the Waldenses' killers, the popes, Bloody Mary, the Jesuits, the killers of the Albigenses, the economic and social conditions of South Ireland, Central America, and Spain, ]{.class_3527}[Hitler]{.class_3526}[, Castro, Mussolini, Napoleon, Ted Kennedy, Pope John Paul II, Heinrich Himmler, ]{.class_3527}[Al Capone]{.class_3526}[, and FOUR DOZEN STUPID CONSERVATIVE SCHOLARS IN AMERICA THAT DIDN'T HAVE A SENSE OF HISTORY EQUAL TO A BLIND GNAT.]{.class_3527}

[Carson side-steps the COMPANY and FRUITS of the "Alexandrian type text," after being told by his Saviour that a thing could be ]{.class_3527}[known ]{.class_3526}[by its ]{.class_3527}["FRUITS"]{.class_3528}[ (Matt. 7:16, 20). Carson, however, ]{.class_3527}[ignores ]{.class_3526}[the fruits and says if they are "closer to the originals" they have to have better ]{.class_3527}[credentials]{.class_3526}[. They don't, for the closer to the originals they were (according to Eusebius, ]{.class_3527}[Ecclesiastical History,]{.class_3526}[ Vol. 28), ]{.class_3527}[the more they got tampered with.]{.class_3526}[ Hills shows that the most corrupt manuscripts we have are Papyri 46 and 75 and ]{.class_3527}[Sinaiticus ]{.class_3526}[and ]{.class_3527}[Vaticanus]{.class_3526}[. Carson ignores the evidence. He side-stepped again; ]{.class_3527}[that is five times in fifty-two pages.]{.class_3526}

[On page 54, Carson says it is "dangerous to go by" what the majority of Christians believe about the ]{.class_3527}[King James Bible]{.class_3526}[, since the textual critics are better able to find the truth than common Christians. Carson questions the opinion of the majority of believers as being wrong since "a majority was never right on ]{.class_3527}[anything]{.class_3526}[." 2. He selected material from Westcott and Hort that is not relevant to ANYTHING.

[We were ]{.class_3527}[eighty years ahead ]{.class_3526}[of the German scholars in Stuttgart before they made the changes.]{.class_3527}
